themes.title: Chemistry transition pace
themes.prior_rationale: The chemistry-transition force is currently observable, not forward-looking speculation. SNE Research confirms LFP >50% of global EV battery deployment in 2025; ACEA Q1 2026 shows BEV share at 19.4% with Chinese-pool brands (predominantly LFP) gaining; Mercedes, VW PowerCo and BMW have all formally adopted multi-chemistry platform strategies that include LFP for entry/mid-range. The high prior reflects that the substitution is already past the inflection point on Western mass-market and has begun penetrating premium. Residual uncertainty (~22%) reflects whether Korean cell-makers + Euro-built NMC capacity stabilise a defensible long-range premium NMC niche above €50k or whether the LFP wave continues to compress that floor toward €60-70k.
themes.thesis: The central question is whether premium nickel-rich NMC chemistry retains a defensible Western profit pool through 2028-2030, or whether LFP/LMFP migrates further up-market into premium-OEM platforms and Korean cell-makers convert enough lines that external Western NMC-CAM producers lose qualified offtake faster than IRA/CRMA localisation policy can compensate. As of Q1 2026, LFP exceeds 50% of global EV battery deployments (SNE Research), CATL holds 42.1% global cell share, and even Mercedes' new MMA platform launched with both LFP (entry CLA) and NMC (long-range CLA) variants — confirming LFP has crossed into Western premium architectures. The unresolved question is whether this is a permanent re-stratification (NMC niches into >€55k long-range only) or whether Korean cell-maker NMC defence + Euro 7 + EU Battery Regulation traceability rebuild a stable mid-premium NMC pool.
themes.definition: T1 tracks the world-state question of how fast the global EV cathode profit pool migrates away from nickel-rich NMC toward LFP and LMFP, with high-nickel NMC retreating to a narrowing premium-and-long-range niche. The theme covers Western (EU + North America) demand-side mix shifts on premium-OEM platforms, Chinese-OEM share of European BEV sales, the LFP-vs-NMC retail-price boundary, and the supply-side qualification chain through Korean cell-makers (LG Energy Solution, Samsung SDI, SK On) plus their external CAM dependence. It is universal: the chemistry-transition force exists independent of any single Western CAM producer.

named_watch_metrics.name: Western premium-OEM NMC mix on >€40k BEV registrations (Mercedes, BMW, Audi, Porsche, Volvo)
named_watch_metrics.source: ACEA monthly + JATO Dynamics + OEM disclosures
named_watch_metrics.current_value: ~70-75% NMC on >€40k Western premium BEV registrations as of Q1 2026 (estimate; Mercedes MMA split is the inflection point)

thresholds.bull: >80% NMC sustained for two consecutive quarters

named_watch_metrics.name: Chinese-OEM EU BEV pool share (BYD, MG/SAIC, Leapmotor, Nio, Geely-pool)
named_watch_metrics.source: ACEA monthly registrations
named_watch_metrics.current_value: BYD pool 55% BEV share within its registrations Jan 2026; BYD +165% YoY; combined Chinese-OEM EU BEV share roughly 10-12% of total BEV registrations

thresholds.bull: Chinese-OEM EU BEV share stalls <10% for three consecutive months (e.g., post-tariff)
thresholds.bear: Chinese-OEM EU BEV share >18% in any single quarter

named_watch_metrics.name: Korean cell-maker external CAM reliance (LG/Samsung/SK On NMC volumes sourced from external Western suppliers vs in-house / POSCO Future M / L&F / Ecopro)
named_watch_metrics.source: SNE Research quarterly + Korean cell-maker disclosures (LGES, Samsung SDI, SK On)
named_watch_metrics.current_value: Mostly Korean integrated supply (POSCO Future M, L&F, Ecopro) for NMC; external Western non-Korean CAM share <15% of Korean cell-maker NMC volumes (estimate)

thresholds.bull: Korean cell-makers add named external Western NMC CAM offtake >€500m total in any 12-month window
thresholds.bear: Korean cell-makers announce a second wave of LFP-line conversions in 2027-2028

named_watch_metrics.name: LFP-vs-NMC pack-level retail-price boundary on EU BEV trim ladders
named_watch_metrics.source: OEM price lists + Benchmark Mineral Intelligence Cell Price Forecast
named_watch_metrics.current_value: Mercedes CLA: LFP variant from €46,500 (58 kWh) vs NMC long-range €52-58k (85 kWh); ~€6-12k effective premium for NMC long-range

thresholds.bull: NMC premium narrows to <€4k or NMC offered on entry trim of any premium platform
thresholds.bear: NMC premium widens to >€15k as LFP enters >€50k segment

named_watch_metrics.name: Premium-OEM platform chemistry decisions (BMW Neue Klasse follow-on, Audi PPE-2, Mercedes EQS replacement, Porsche SSP, Volvo SPA3)
named_watch_metrics.source: OEM Capital Markets Days + Volta Battery Report quarterly + electrive.com
named_watch_metrics.current_value: BMW Neue Klasse i3/iX3 confirmed high-nickel NMC cylindrical (Q1 2026 launch); Audi PPE 8:1:1 NMC; Mercedes MMA dual-chemistry; VW unified-cell mostly LFP from 2027

thresholds.bull: Two or more premium-OEM follow-on platforms confirm high-nickel NMC-only for full range
thresholds.bear: Any premium-OEM (BMW, Audi, Mercedes, Porsche, Volvo) adopts LFP/LMFP for non-entry trim before 2028

named_watch_metrics.name: Western (non-Chinese) NMC-CAM utilisation rate at Korean and EU plants
named_watch_metrics.source: Benchmark CAM Daily + plant-level disclosures (POSCO Future M, EcoPro BM, L&F, Umicore Nysa, Targray, BASF/Shanshan, Northvolt-derived)
named_watch_metrics.current_value: Estimated 50-65% utilisation across Western non-Chinese NMC-CAM nameplate; Umicore deferred 150 kt EU recycling tied to underloaded CAM capacity

thresholds.bull: >80% sustained for two consecutive quarters at named Korean and EU NMC plants
thresholds.bear: <50% utilisation at any Western NMC plant for two consecutive quarters or any plant placed on care-and-maintenance

named_watch_metrics.name: Global LFP share of EV cathode tonnage (vs NMC, NCA, LMFP)
named_watch_metrics.source: SNE Research monthly + Benchmark Mineral Intelligence Cathode forecast
named_watch_metrics.current_value: LFP >50% globally 2025 (SNE); CATL 42.1% of cell market Jan-Feb 2026 (~70-80% LFP within CATL)

thresholds.bull: Global LFP share stalls or retreats below 48% for two consecutive months
thresholds.bear: Global LFP share >60% in any month

baseline_research_note.summary: The cathode chemistry profit pool is bifurcating along a price-and-range axis. On the demand side, LFP took >50% global share of EV battery deployments by 2025 (SNE Research) and crossed into Western premium platforms in 2026: Mercedes' new MMA-platform CLA launched with an entry LFP variant (58 kWh, €46,500) alongside the long-range NMC variant (85 kWh), and VW PowerCo's unified-cell roadmap explicitly schedules LFP production at Valencia from 2027 with the stated mix of '80% of cells LFP for lower-range, High-Mn no-Cobalt for mid, NMC for high-range'. ACEA Q1 2026 shows BEV share at 19.4% (vs 15.2% a year earlier) with Chinese-pool brands — predominantly LFP-equipped — at 55% BEV share within their pool registrations and BYD volumes up 165% YoY in January 2026. On the supply side, Korean cell-makers (LG Energy Solution, Samsung SDI, SK On) — historically the qualified Western offtake counterparties for high-nickel NMC CAM — are formally adding LFP lines in North America: LG-GM and Samsung SDI-GM joint LFP plants are confirmed, and SK On signed an L&F cathode supply MoU for North-American LFP from 2026. POSCO Future M is converting NCM lines at Pohang to LFP from H2 2026, and Benchmark Mineral Intelligence projects LFP at 75% of Chinese CAM capacity by 2030. The premium-NMC pool persists — BMW Neue Klasse i3/iX3 launched in 2026 with 6th-gen high-nickel NMC cylindrical cells, Audi PPE remains 8:1:1 NMC, and the Mercedes long-range CLA is NMC — but the addressable cathode tonnage has narrowed substantially compared to the 2022-2023 capacity-build assumption that NMC would scale linearly with BEV volumes. Western non-Chinese NMC CAM growth is therefore decoupled from BEV-unit growth.

key_evidence.snippet: By last year, LFP batteries accounted for more than half of global EV battery deployments... LFP almost 30% cheaper per kilowatt-hour than NMC.
key_evidence.implication: LFP is the dominant global EV chemistry; NMC's structural growth is now niche-bounded.

key_evidence.snippet: Battery-electric cars accounted for 19.4% of the EU market share in Q1 2026, an increase from 15.2% one year earlier.
key_evidence.implication: BEV penetration is accelerating in Europe; chemistry mix within that growing share is the live battleground.

key_evidence.snippet: Mercedes will use LFP cells in the entry-level version to offer a lower price, while the larger version for maximum range will use NMC cells.
key_evidence.implication: LFP has crossed the premium threshold via MMA platform; NMC is being explicitly priced as the long-range premium-only option.

key_evidence.snippet: 80% of the cells would include LFP for lower-range EVs, High Manganese no Cobalt chemistry for mid-range EVs and NMC for high-range EVs.
key_evidence.implication: VW's unified-cell architecture explicitly relegates NMC to the top of its mix; PowerCo Salzgitter NMC ramp now defines a smaller share than the original 2022 plan implied.

key_evidence.snippet: LG Energy Solution and Samsung SDI plan to install LFP battery production lines at their respective US plants co-owned with General Motors.
key_evidence.implication: The Korean cell-maker qualification chain — the mainstream Western-compatible NMC offtake counterparty — is bifurcating its CAM demand between high-nickel NMC and LFP.

key_evidence.snippet: LFP is set to account for three-quarters of China's CAM capacity by 2030; outside China, NCM still 60% in 2030.
key_evidence.implication: Outside China NMC remains the majority cathode capacity, but the trajectory and pricing power are set by Chinese LFP.

key_evidence.snippet: BMW Neue Klasse Gen6 round cells: nickel content higher on the cathode side, while cobalt content is reduced... 50% lower cost per pack.
key_evidence.implication: Premium-OEM NMC defence is real but cost-competitive only via cell redesign; chemistry stays high-nickel where range is the brand promise.

key_evidence.snippet: LFP has become the dominant EV chemistry globally in 2025, and is moving up-market into Western platforms via cost-led OEM decisions; NMC demand still grows in absolute GWh, but its share of incremental Western demand is shrinking faster than the premium-platform narrative implies.
key_evidence.implication: Macro stream confirms the structural compression of Western NMC demand even as BEV volumes grow.

key_evidence.snippet: Accelerating LFP adoption on Western mass-market EV platforms erodes the addressable market for nickel-rich NMC cathode assets... 18-36m timing window, strong strength.
key_evidence.implication: Scenarios stream classifies this as a strong, near-term force; aligns with theme prior at 0.78.

key_evidence.snippet: An LFP-centered trend spread globally, while Chinese battery manufacturers strengthened their influence through overseas local production.
key_evidence.implication: SNE confirms the trend is no longer China-only; export of Chinese cell capacity is the transmission mechanism into Western markets.

what_would_move_it_up: Premium-OEM platform refresh decisions (BMW Neue Klasse follow-on, Audi PPE-2, Mercedes EQS replacement) confirming high-nickel NMC for full-range >€55k segment
Euro 7 final rule + EU Battery Regulation carbon-footprint thresholds creating a measurable price premium for low-CO2 NMC made in Europe
Korean cell-maker NMC line capex announcements (vs LFP retrofits) — LG/Samsung/SK On committing new high-nickel NMC capacity in 2027-2028
LMFP commercialisation slipping past 2028 — keeping mid-range demand inside NMC-622/811 rather than ceding to manganese-rich variants
Chinese OEM EU-share growth stalling below 12% (e.g. via EU CBAM-like tariff escalation), reducing LFP transmission into Western mass-market
what_would_move_it_down: BMW Neue Klasse follow-up models or Audi PPE-2 adopting LFP/LMFP for any non-base trim
Korean cell-makers (LG, Samsung, SK On) announcing a second wave of LFP retrofits in Korea or US converting existing NMC lines
Chinese-OEM EU BEV pool share crossing 18-20% in any quarter (currently rising fast off ~10-12%)
Cell-pack-level LFP price below €70/kWh while NMC stays above €100/kWh — widening the chemistry retail-price gap permanently
VW PowerCo accelerating LFP at Valencia from H2 2026 instead of 2027, and reallocating Salzgitter NMC capacity to lower-nickel chemistries
Solid-state pilot deployments hitting volume thresholds before 2029 — would compress remaining NMC economic life

cross_cutting: T2 EU industrial cost / EU Battery Regulation carbon-footprint moat (interacts with NMC defensibility on a CO2-per-kWh basis)
T4 Compliance moat / EU CRMA / IRA FEOC localisation premium (the policy stack that partially offsets LFP cost advantage)
T6 Western EV demand trajectory (BEV total volume; T1 is the chemistry-mix slice of T6's volume slice)
T-Solid-state horizon theme (handles post-2030; T1 explicitly stops at 2030 to avoid double-counting)
T-PGM-and-precious-metals (chemistry shift indirectly affects nickel and cobalt demand which feed back into mining-and-refining margin pools)
expected_projection_topics: Western premium-OEM (BMW, Mercedes, Audi, Porsche, Volvo) high-nickel NMC mix on >€40k BEV registrations through 2028-2030
Chinese-OEM (BYD, MG/SAIC, Leapmotor, Nio, Geely-pool) EU BEV pool share trajectory 2026-2030
Korean cell-maker (LG Energy Solution, Samsung SDI, SK On) external Western CAM reliance vs Korean-integrated supply (POSCO Future M, L&F, Ecopro)

LFP-vs-NMC retail-price boundary on EU BEV trim ladders, plus discrete premium-OEM follow-on platform chemistry decisions (BMW Neue Klasse-2, Audi PPE-2, Mercedes EQS replacement, Porsche SSP, Volvo SPA3)
LMFP and manganese-rich NMC commercialisation timeline (2027-2030) and its compression of mid-range NMC demand
Western non-Chinese NMC-CAM utilisation and rationalisation (which plants close, which retrofit to LMFP, which hold NMC)

themes.title: EU industrial cost stack

current_state.trajectory: two-sided / mildly widening on molecule + carbon side, stable on power day-ahead, asymmetrically relieved by national German scheme

themes.prior_rationale: The EU industrial cost disadvantage versus Asia and the US Gulf Coast is a directly observable, quantified condition in May 2026, not a forward-looking event. Industrial electricity in the EU H1 2025 averaged ~USD 90/MWh against ~USD 48/MWh in the US (gap widened since 2019); EU gas remains 3-5x US levels; chemical-sector closures jumped from 2.9 Mt (2022) to 17.2 Mt (2025) per Cefic with INEOS, Dow, Evonik and BASF all citing energy + carbon. Within the 18-30 month resolution window, persistence is supported by: (a) NW EU 1-year power forwards stuck at EUR 74-95/MWh, (b) EU LCI +3.7% YoY with Polish manufacturing wages +6.6% (intra-EU labour-relief valve narrowing), (c) CBAM permanent phase active but certificate sales deferred to Feb 2027 (cash-impact lagged), (d) German Industriestrompreis is asymmetric national relief that helps German competitors more than it helps the EU industrial baseline, (e) China holding ~46% global chemicals share with continued capacity adds. Pockets of relief (NW EU baseload at EUR 32/MWh day-ahead, German EUR 50/MWh scheme, EUR 426M Spanish Hydrogen Bank scheme cleared April 2026, Aurora Line transmission addition Nov 2025) prevent the worst-case widening but do not symmetrically close the gap. The persistence prior sits at 0.66 — clearly above 0.50 and slightly above the v1 0.63, reflecting the May 2026 information added: TTF rebound to EUR 47, ETS rally to EUR 75.5, German Industriestrompreis fragmentation, Polish fee stack rising, CBAM cash deferral.
themes.thesis: The EU industrial cost stack is in a fragmented, asymmetrically relieved regime in mid-2026. Five layers move on different clocks: (1) NW European baseload day-ahead power has held the 2024 gain at EUR 32-50/MWh, but 1-year forwards remain EUR 74-95/MWh, embedding persistence of the cost gap into 2027; (2) TTF gas has rebounded to ~EUR 47/MWh on Iran-blockade headlines, well off the EUR 25-35 normalisation band of 2024; (3) EU ETS has rallied to EUR 75.5/tCO2 with 2026 supply cuts of ~8% mechanically tightening the market; (4) CBAM permanent phase started 1 Jan 2026 at EUR 75.36/cert, but actu
themes.definition: T2 captures the joint trajectory of NW European baseload power, TTF gas, EU ETS / CBAM carbon pricing, automatic wage indexation in Belgium and CEE wage convergence in Poland, Finnish Nordic-pool power and carbon path, and EU-level industrial-energy-relief execution (German Industriestrompreis, Clean Industrial Deal, Hydrogen Bank). The world-state question is whether these layers settle into a competitive baseline that allows European energy-intensive processors to defend margin against Chinese, Korean and US Gulf Coast peers, or whether the cost stack hardens into a structural disadvantage that drives further capacity rationalisation, plant closures and FCF compression across the EU industrial base.

named_watch_metrics.name: TTF month-ahead gas price (EUR/MWh)
named_watch_metrics.threshold_escalation: Sustained above EUR 60/MWh for more than one quarter
named_watch_metrics.source: ICE Endex TTF futures (front-month + 12-month strip)
named_watch_metrics.notes: Rebounded to EUR 47/MWh on Iran blockade preparation reports (May 2026). Was EUR 50.35 in April 2026 v1 scan; mean-reverted slightly but still well above EUR 25-35 normalisation band of 2024. -8.55% MoM, +40.3% YoY.

named_watch_metrics.name: NW European baseload day-ahead power (EUR/MWh)
named_watch_metrics.threshold_escalation: Sustained above EUR 80/MWh, or 1-year forward above EUR 100/MWh
named_watch_metrics.threshold_deescalation: Sustained below EUR 30/MWh with 12-month forward strip below EUR 60
named_watch_metrics.source: EPEX SPOT day-ahead auctions + ENTSO-E transparency platform (Belgium / Netherlands / France / Germany bidding zones)
named_watch_metrics.notes: Day-ahead remains the cleanest tailwind in the stack (EUR 32 Apr 2026), but 1-year forwards stuck around EUR 74-95/MWh (per EEX German calendar futures and IEA Electricity 2026 outlook). Forward curve embeds persistence of the cost gap into 2027.

named_watch_metrics.name: EU ETS December settlement (EUR/tCO2)
named_watch_metrics.threshold_escalation: Sustained above EUR 100/tCO2 for two consecutive auctions
named_watch_metrics.source: EEX EUA auctions + EU Commission ETS registry + ICE EUA futures
named_watch_metrics.notes: Rallied +13% in 4 weeks to EUR 75.5 (highest since Feb 2026). 2026 supply ~8% lower than 2025 mechanically tightening market. Brussels MSR proposal scrapping invalidation of certain permits seen as supportive but not bearish-flooding.

named_watch_metrics.name: CBAM quarterly certificate reference price (EUR/tCO2)
named_watch_metrics.threshold_escalation: Quarterly reference price above EUR 90/tCO2 sustained, or DG TAXUD enforcement gap publicly flagged
named_watch_metrics.threshold_deescalation: Material enforcement gaps or postponement of CBAM permanent phase, or quarterly reference below EUR 60
named_watch_metrics.source: DG TAXUD CBAM portal (taxation-customs.ec.europa.eu) + EU Commission Official Journal
named_watch_metrics.notes: Q1 2026 first official price set 7 Apr 2026 at EUR 75.36/cert. Certificate SALES postponed from 1 Jan 2026 to 1 Feb 2027 — declarants acquire certs in 2027 for 2026 imports. Cash impact for 2026 is therefore deferred but liability is accruing. From 2027, weekly pricing replaces quarterly.

named_watch_metrics.name: German Industriestrompreis execution status
named_watch_metrics.current_value_2026_05: approved 16 Apr 2026 by EU Commission; EUR 50/MWh cap; 91 sectors; 2026-2028 window; retroactive applications from 2027 for 2026 consumption; up to 50% of annual consumption eligible
named_watch_metrics.threshold_escalation: Scheme extended beyond 2028 or expanded to >50% of consumption — widens asymmetric relief; OR equivalent French / Spanish / Italian schemes fail to materialise (locks in German-only advantage)
named_watch_metrics.threshold_deescalation: Belgian / Polish / Finnish equivalent state-aid schemes approved by Commission under expanded TCTF; OR EU-level Clean Industrial Deal symmetric relief mechanism adopted
named_watch_metrics.source: BMWE press releases + Bundesanzeiger + EU Commission state-aid decisions database (DG COMP)
named_watch_metrics.notes: Asymmetric national relief — meaningful for German chemical / steel / mechanical-engineering peers, NOT available to Belgian (Hoboken / Olen), Polish (Nysa) or Finnish (Kokkola-linked) processing assets. Funded via Climate and Transformation Fund. Companies must invest 50% of subsidies in efficiency / sustainability.

named_watch_metrics.name: Belgian wage indexation pace (chemical sector + cross-economy health index)
named_watch_metrics.current_value_2026_05: Joint committee 111/207 chemical-sector indexation: 2% step expected May/June 2026 (next scheduled fixed-percentage variable-date adjustment). Federal cent-index cap reform effective 1 June 2026: full indexation for salaries below EUR 4,000/month, capped indexation for excess. 2025-2026 pay norm set at 0% by Royal Decree (negotiated wage growth frozen, automatic indexation continues).
named_watch_metrics.threshold_escalation: Health-index YoY above +3.5%, or chemical-sector indexation cumulative above +6% over 12 months — pressure on Hoboken / Olen unit labour cost
named_watch_metrics.threshold_deescalation: Health-index YoY below +1.5%, or government extends pay-norm freeze beyond 2026
named_watch_metrics.source: Statbel health index series + Joint Committee 111 / 207 minutes + Belgian Federal Public Service Employment + Royal Decrees on pay norm
named_watch_metrics.notes: Belgian automatic indexation is the single largest distinguishing feature of the Belgian cost stack vs Germany and Poland. Pay-norm 0% means negotiated pay growth is frozen, but automatic indexation continues mechanically. Cent-index cap (1 June 2026) reduces but does not eliminate indexation drag for higher-paid technical staff.

named_watch_metrics.name: Polish industrial power + labour cost composite
named_watch_metrics.current_value_2026_05: Industrial wholesale power ~PLN 540/MWh for 2026 (Enea CEO guidance, regulated cap at PLN 500/MWh unlikely to extend beyond 2025); Polish manufacturing LCI +6.6% YoY (Sept 2025); 2026 minimum wage +3.0% (vs Germany +8.4%); capacity / RES / cogeneration fees rising in 2026 with cogeneration fee alone +~50%; ETS allowances drive up to 50% of energy price.
named_watch_metrics.threshold_escalation: Polish industrial wholesale power above PLN 600/MWh sustained one quarter, OR Polish manufacturing LCI above +7% YoY four consecutive quarters, OR composite Polish-vs-German manufacturing labour-cost gap (currently EUR 17.30/h vs EUR 48.30/h) narrowing below 2.5x
named_watch_metrics.threshold_deescalation: Polish industrial wholesale power below PLN 450/MWh sustained, OR Polish CRMA / battery-sector localisation incentives disbursing in full (offsetting fee stack)
named_watch_metrics.cadence: monthly (URE wholesale prints) + quarterly (Statistics Poland LCI)
named_watch_metrics.source: Statistics Poland (GUS) labour cost index + URE (Polish Energy Regulatory Office) industrial tariffs + Trading Economics PPI electricity series + Bloomberg URE coverage
named_watch_metrics.notes: Poland is third-fastest EU electricity-price rise. CEE near-shoring premium narrowing. Direct read-across to Nysa unit economics and to any CAM / battery investment thesis predicated on Polish cost advantage.

named_watch_metrics.name: Finnish Nordic-pool power + carbon path (Kokkola feed)
named_watch_metrics.current_value_2026_05: Finnish Q1 2026 area-price derivative ~EUR 70/MWh (winter peak); Q2 2026 derivative ~EUR 35/MWh (seasonal shoulder). Energy mix 42.6% nuclear, 23.7% hydro, 20.9% wind — diversified low-carbon mix. Industrial electricity for businesses ~EUR 0.102/kWh (Sept 2025). Aurora Line transmission to Sweden commissioned 13 Nov 2025, expected to moderate prices.
named_watch_metrics.threshold_escalation: Finnish full-year average area price above EUR 60/MWh, OR Aurora Line outage, OR major Olkiluoto / Loviisa nuclear unavailability event sustained more than one month
named_watch_metrics.threshold_deescalation: Finnish full-year average area price below EUR 40/MWh, OR Nordic hydro reservoir levels above 5-year average sustained
named_watch_metrics.source: Nord Pool day-ahead + Fingrid + Statistics Finland (stat.fi/en/statistics/ehi) + Energiateollisuus annual electricity report
named_watch_metrics.notes: Kokkola-linked refining (precursor / cobalt-nickel feed for IONWAY) sits in the FI bidding zone. Diversified low-carbon mix is supportive vs Continental EU on carbon-intensity (CBAM-relevant), but seasonal volatility (winter Q1 prints) is real. Indirectly relevant to Umicore via IONWAY supply chain, not a primary site.

propagation_to_bets_seed.bet_label: BMS Olen / Nysa unit economics — can European CAM + cobalt/nickel refining reach competitive break-even by FY2028?
propagation_to_bets_seed.transmission_mechanism: Olen (Belgian Co/Ni refining) and Nysa (Polish CAM) sit outside German Industriestrompreis relief. Olen exposed to Belgian automatic wage indexation + ETS / CBAM compliance overhead + EUR 47 TTF feeding into chemical / electrolytic processing energy mix. Nysa exposed to Polish industrial power (~PLN 540/MWh + rising fees), Polish manufacturing LCI +6.6%, and ETS allowances driving ~50% of Polish energy price. Composite effect: unit-cost floor for European CAM / refining is being lifted by ~EUR 5-15/MWh-equivalent vs 12 months ago at exactly the moment BMS needs break-even economics.
propagation_to_bets_seed.reading_rule: -1 magnitude per +EUR 10/MWh in TTF above EUR 50 sustained one quarter; -0.5 if Polish industrial power above PLN 600/MWh sustained; +0.5 if Belgian or Polish equivalent state-aid scheme matching German EUR 50/MWh approved; -0.5 if EU ETS sustained above EUR 100/tCO2.

propagation_to_bets_seed.bet_label: Catalysis EU plant economics — can European autocatalyst footprint defend margin through ICE structural decline?
propagation_to_bets_seed.transmission_mechanism: Catalysis EU sites (Florange, Bad Säckingen, Nowa Ruda) are exposed to: (i) German Industriestrompreis ENABLES Bad Säckingen if eligible under the 91-sector list (mechanical engineering / specialty chemicals plausible), creating relative advantage vs Florange (France) and Nowa Ruda (Poland); (ii) ETS / CBAM enforcement supportive on imported-catalyst competition; (iii) PGM-bearing process energy intensity less than refining but meaningful. Net: asymmetric national relief could intra-EU favour the German node, while the segment as a whole has Euro 7 content uplift offsetting volume decline.
propagation_to_bets_seed.reading_rule: -0.5 magnitude per second energy shock; +0.5 if Bad Säckingen confirmed eligible under Industriestrompreis; -0.5 if Polish Nowa Ruda site loses cost competitiveness via combined power + LCI moves; +0.5 if Clean Industrial Deal symmetric relief adopted.

propagation_to_bets_seed.bet_label: Recycling Hoboken energy + carbon costs — can the EUR 400m 2025-2028 capex programme deliver expected payback?
propagation_to_bets_seed.transmission_mechanism: Hoboken is the highest-weight asset for T2 because: (i) it is a pyrometallurgical complex feeding off TTF gas + NW EU power directly; (ii) it sits inside Belgian automatic wage indexation; (iii) it is fully exposed to ETS direct emissions on smelter operations and gets no German Industriestrompreis relief; (iv) CBAM is supportive on competitive side (penalises non-EU imported PGM-bearing material) but cash impact pushed to Feb 2027; (v) the 2026 scheduled maintenance shutdown overlaps with elevated input cost regime. Combined energy + carbon + labour drag compresses the EUR 400m capex payback — a +EUR 10/MWh sustained move in TTF or +EUR 20/tCO2 in ETS materially extends payback period.
propagation_to_bets_seed.reading_rule: -1 magnitude per +EUR 10/MWh in TTF above EUR 50 sustained; -0.5 per +EUR 20/tCO2 in ETS above EUR 80 sustained; -0.3 if Belgian health-index YoY above +3.5%; +0.5 if NW EU baseload day-ahead anchors below EUR 30 sustained four quarters; +0.3 if CBAM Q3/Q4 2026 reference price holds above EUR 80 (competitive moat deepens).

propagation_to_bets_seed.bet_label: Cash conversion via FCF compression — does the EU industrial cost stack make CORE plan cash gates realistic?
propagation_to_bets_seed.transmission_mechanism: Group-level FCF translation: CORE plan rests on Catalysis + Recycling generating ~EUR 679m foundation EBIT to fund BMS losses + EUR 400m Hoboken capex + dividend rebuild. T2 hits the FCF line at three points: (i) Hoboken energy cost + ETS direct compliance compresses Recycling EBITDA; (ii) Belgian wage indexation drags unit cost across Hoboken / Olen; (iii) Polish + Finnish input cost pressure lifts BMS site operating cost floor. FY2025 underlying FCF was only EUR 145m — sensitivity to a 5-10% energy + carbon + labour drag is non-linear at this conversion ratio. A second energy shock or accelerating Belgian indexation could push underlying FCF below EUR 100m, breaching CORE's quarterly cash gate logic.
propagation_to_bets_seed.reading_rule: -1 magnitude per second energy shock realised; -0.5 if Belgian health-index YoY above +3.5% AND chemical-sector indexation triggers in May/June 2026; -0.3 if EU ETS sustained above EUR 90/tCO2; +0.5 if Clean Industrial Deal symmetric relief adopted; +0.3 if NW EU 1-year forward repriced below EUR 70/MWh.

cross_cutting.overlay_mechanism: Decoupling tightens / loosens commodity flows that price into TTF (LNG redirection), Brent (Middle East risk premium), and PGM / battery-precursor competitive landscape (CBAM bite vs Chinese CAM). A Chinese retaliation extending to LNG cargo allocation or cobalt / nickel export licensing would lift T2 input-cost layer through the molecule channel; conversely, a managed-trade carve-out would loosen molecule prices. T3 escalation paths (rare-earth / antimony / battery-equipment tariff intensification) directly intersect with T2's escalation_paths.second_brent_or_ttf_shock and carbon_market_stress branches.

Hoboken energy cost feeds the conversion margin alongside PGM basket pricing. T2's ETS + TTF moves directly compress Recycling EBITDA at any given PGM basket level. Conversely, a PGM price collapse would convert T2 cost-stack drag into a more acute margin event because there would be no PGM-tailwind cushion. The interaction is multiplicative on Hoboken FCF, not additive.

T2 raises the cost-floor for European NMC CAM (Nysa / Olen / Kokkola) at the same time chemistry mix is shifting toward LFP. The two themes compound: higher EU input cost + LFP substitution = sharper realised-margin compression for NMC CAM than either theme alone implies. Reading rule: any quarter where T2 escalates AND new LFP-platform announcements emerge from VW / Stellantis / Mercedes is a high-conviction negative for B1.

themes.title: US-China decoupling permanence

current_state.trajectory: holding with upward bias (escalation pipeline > de-escalation pipeline)

themes.prior_rationale: Anchored slightly above the v1 0.62 because the OBBBA July 2025 codification of FEOC, the 12 Feb 2026 IRS Notice 2026-15 with MACR framework, and the 11 Mar 2026 USTR overcapacity-investigation initiation each materially raise the friction cost of reversal vs the v1 baseline. Pulled below 0.75 by the dated 10 Nov 2026 Busan / MOFCOM-70 expiry which creates a binary near-term re-anchoring event and a credible (though not consensus) Phase-2 carve-out path. The 0.66 anchor reflects: durable legal infrastructure (favours +), active escalation pipeline (favours +), dated expiry and Phase-2 leverage point (favours -), historical base rate of industrial-policy reversal within 18 months (low, favours +).
themes.thesis: The US-China relationship sits in a layered, dual-track state as of May 2026. Layer 1 (the surface): the Busan truce of October 2025 suspends heightened reciprocal tariffs, halves the fentanyl tariff to 10% and pauses MOFCOM Announcements 55–62 (rare-earth / permanent-magnet / battery-grade graphite controls) until 10 November 2026 via MOFCOM/GAC Announcement 70/2025. Layer 2 (the durable stack, untouched by Busan): USTR Section 301 tariffs from FRN 89 FR 76581 step up on schedule on 1 January 2026 — non-EV lithium-ion batteries to 25%, natural graphite to 25%; the cumulative US tariff rate on
themes.definition: Captures whether the structural framing of US-China decoupling — Section 301 tariff stack, MOFCOM critical-minerals export controls, IRA / OBBBA Foreign Entity of Concern (FEOC) localization rules, EU Critical Raw Materials Act (CRMA) project pipeline, and battery-passport-style extra-territorial chemistry rules — holds through the 10 November 2026 Busan-truce expiry and the July 2026 USTR overcapacity-investigation determination, or whether a Phase-2 deal / political cycle relaxes it. The world-state question is whether the policy stack hardens into permanent regionalisation (compliant Western suppliers monetise a durable premium) or partially reverses through negotiated carve-outs and managed-trade quotas. T3 is the universal political/legal pillar that supports B1 (BMS compliance moat), B4 (compliance moat half-life across the portfolio) and B5 (Specialty germanium pricing) — four of the five Crow's Nest portfolio bets carry exposure to it.

cross_cutting.with_theme_label: Chemistry mix and Chinese-OEM EU share dynamics
cross_cutting.interaction: If T3 strengthens via overcapacity-tariff extension to pCAM and separators, Chinese-OEM EU import share is suppressed by both the cost wall and FEOC-sympathy procurement decisions by Western OEMs, materially supporting the regionalised NMC / NMx pricing window. If T3 weakens via Phase-2 carve-out, Chinese-OEM EU share accelerates and chemistry-mix risk on T1 amplifies (LFP / Chinese-LFP price competition reaches Western OEM platforms faster).
cross_cutting.joint_propagation: T3 outcome conditions T1's chemistry-share trajectory; the two themes are positively correlated in their downside (carve-out + LFP migration) and in their upside (overcapacity remedy + premium-NMC retention).

cross_cutting.interaction: T3 is the single largest determinant of T4. OBBBA-codified FEOC + IRS Notice 2026-15 MACR + CRMA Strategic Project status together extend the compliance-moat half-life from a 2027 expiry to a 2030+ horizon. A Phase-2 carve-out or material softening of MACR safe harbours via forthcoming proposed regulations would compress T4 by 18–24 months.
cross_cutting.joint_propagation: T3 → T4 weight is high (~0.9 via direct legislative and regulatory channel); B4 should be re-evaluated within 30 days of any T3 named-watch-metric breach.

cross_cutting.with_theme_label: Specialty germanium PGM regime via export controls
cross_cutting.interaction: The 2023 MOFCOM germanium / gallium regime is the operative T3 sub-component for T5. T5's pricing-power thesis depends on this regime persisting; a voluntary suspension as Phase-2 goodwill gesture would compress germanium spot premia and Umicore Electro-Optic franchise economics. The 2025 MOFCOM 55–62 package (currently suspended) is a separate, additive layer whose snap-back on 10 November 2026 would be incremental upside for T5.
cross_cutting.joint_propagation: T3 → T5 weight ~0.7; T5 is asymmetric on the upside (two layers of MOFCOM controls available) and exposed only to a specific Phase-2 carveout that names germanium / gallium.

themes.title: Compliance-moat half-life
themes.prior_rationale: Two-sided observable today: regulatory framework is at widest legal aperture (CRMA designations, Battery Passport guidelines, NDAA 842, 30D 70% threshold, 127% CAM tariff) — supportive of high persistence prior — BUT three concrete erosion vectors are visible on the calendar: (1) Treasury 'impracticable-to-trace' carve-out expires end-2026 (binary test of enforcement teeth, replay-risk of May 2024 softening playbook); (2) Asian compliant-capacity ramp inside EU/US perimeter is operational now (EcoPro Debrecen live Dec 2025 at 54 kt/yr, expanding to 108 kt/yr in 2026; LG Chem Tennessee from 2026 H1 at half-original-scale; POSCO Future M North America announcements); (3) demand-side bifurcation — defence (NDAA 842, biting 2028+) and ESG-fund mandates pay premium, but mass-market OEMs are actively renegotiating compliance-pricing language (POSCO/GM/Ultium precedent visible in Umicore FY2025 EUR -91m BMS Adjusted EBIT, EUR 261m WC build). Compliance-mandate-vs-uptake split anchors the prior at 0.42 — well below the 0.62 anchor of T3 (decoupling permanence) — reflecting that the moat persists but its monetisation rate is structurally compressing.
themes.thesis: The compliance moat consists of four mutually reinforcing layers, each with a different half-life: (1) the headline tariff wall (~127% on Chinese CAM via Section 301 + MFN + IRA stack) — long-lived because reversal requires explicit USTR action and consumer-price politics has not bitten; (2) the IRA 30D FEOC eligibility regime — partially operational, with the 25% ownership threshold and battery-component domestic-content step-up to 70% in 2026, BUT softened by the 'impracticable-to-trace' carve-out that expires end-2026 and creates a Q1 2027 enforcement-teeth test; (3) the EU CRMA + Battery R
themes.definition: Captures the half-life of the regulatory compliance moat that today protects Western battery-materials suppliers — specifically the bundle of EU Critical Raw Materials Act (CRMA) strategic-project designations, EU Battery Regulation 2023/1542 / Battery Passport carbon-footprint and recycled-content thresholds, IRA Section 30D Foreign-Entity-of-Concern (FEOC) sourcing rules, and the cumulative ~127% US Section 301/232/IRA-stacked tariff wall on Chinese cathode active material (CAM). T4 is distinct from T3 (decoupling permanence) in that it does NOT ask whether the legal framework persists; it asks whether the framework, while persisting, ACTUALLY produces a measurable price premium for compliant suppliers AND whether that premium is monetisable on the demand side — through premium consumer ASP differentials, fleet/B2B RFP language, defence procurement (NDAA Section 842), public procurement floors, and ESG-mandated fund flows. The compliance-mandate-vs-uptake split is the load-bearing distinction: the moat can be legally durable but commercially shallow if (a) Asian competitors localise compliant capacity in EU/US faster than the OEM willingness-to-pay extends, (b) impracticable-to-trace transition rules and FEOC carve-outs survive past 2026, or (c) demand-side actors treat compliance as a binary qualifier rather than a price-bearing differentiator.

baseline_research_note.summary: T4 isolates the variable that separates regulatory persistence from commercial monetisation. The legal infrastructure is operational at its widest aperture: EU CRMA has designated 47 Strategic Projects (May 2024), EU Battery Regulation 2023/1542 has set the Feb 2027 Battery Passport hard date with operational guidelines published Aug 2025, IRA Section 30D battery-component threshold steps to 70% in 2026, FY26 NDAA Section 842 prohibits DoD procurement of FEOC batteries (effective 2028+), and the cumulative US Section 301/MFN/IRA tariff wall on Chinese CAM stands at ~127.2%. Yet the demand-side translation rate is bifurcated: defence procurement (NDAA 842) and ESG-mandated fleet/B2B buyers (Ayvens 90 g/km 2026 target, ESG-RFP exclusion language) pay a measurable compliance premium, while mass-market OEMs replay the POSCO/GM/Ultium contract-renegotiation precedent to compress it. Asian localisers — EcoPro Debrecen (54 kt/yr live Dec 2025, 108 kt/yr by 2026), LG Chem Tennessee (commissioning from 2026 at half-original-scale), POSCO Future M Quebec — are ramping compliant capacity inside the EU/US perimeter faster than the OEM willingness-to-pay premium expands, capturing moat rent for themselves rather than ceding it to incumbent Western suppliers. Treasury's May 2024 'impracticable-to-trace' carve-out (expires end-2026) is the canary on enforcement-teeth posture: a renewal would replay the soften-via-guidance playbook; a clean expiry would convert moat from notional to enforceable. Net: persistence high, depth eroding — T4 prior anchored at 0.42.

key_evidence.snippet: Treasury / IRS Final Rule on 30D establishes 25% FEOC ownership threshold and creates impracticable-to-trace transition through end-2026 for battery materials including graphite, electrolyte salts, and certain trace minerals.
key_evidence.implication: Soften-via-guidance playbook is operational and dated — Q4 2026 is the binary test of enforcement teeth.

key_evidence.snippet: EcoPro BM commercial cathode-material production starts at Debrecen, Hungary (54 kt/yr nameplate, ~600,000 EVs equivalent); expansion to 108 kt/yr planned for 2026, NCA / NCM high-nickel chemistry.
key_evidence.implication: Asian-promoter compliant capacity is live inside the EU CRMA perimeter — direct erosion vector for incumbent Western CAM suppliers' localisation premium.

key_evidence.snippet: LG Chem Tennessee cathode plant production launch slid to 2026 from original 2025 H2 target; capacity reset to ~60 kt/yr (half of original 120 kt/yr 2027 target) covering 600,000 EVs.
key_evidence.implication: Pace and scale are slipping — partial offset to localisation-erosion thesis but still adds compliant capacity inside US IRA perimeter.

key_evidence.snippet: FY26 NDAA Section 842 prohibits DoD procurement of advanced batteries (cells + key components) from FEOCs; phased compliance: 1 Jan 2028 (new contracts), 1 Jan 2029 (standard batteries), 1 Jan 2031 (legacy programmes).
key_evidence.implication: Defence-demand pillar of compliance moat formalised — premium-tier monetisation channel anchored by statute, biting 2028+.

key_evidence.snippet: ALD-LeasePlan (Ayvens) PowerUP 2026 strategic plan: target running-fleet CO2 <90 g/km by 2026 (vs 112 g in 2022); ESG and risk management 'at the core' of strategy; world's largest multi-brand EV fleet at 428,000 vehicles.
key_evidence.implication: Largest multi-brand fleet operator has internalised ESG/compliance as procurement assumption — supports demand-side monetisation thesis on B2B / fleet channel.

key_evidence.snippet: European Commission first list of 47 CRMA Strategic Projects (May 2024) covers extraction, processing, recycling; Umicore-promoted GePETO (germanium processing) and ReGAIN (germanium substitution) included.
key_evidence.implication: CRMA enforcement architecture is operational — first-cycle designations live, but funding disbursement pace remains the binding constraint on whether designations translate into capacity.

key_evidence.snippet: Cumulative US tariff rate on Chinese CAM has reached 127.2% per context_pack — the highest sectoral wall in the modern bilateral relationship.
key_evidence.implication: Headline tariff layer of compliance moat is at maximum aperture — supportive of moat-PERSISTENCE prior but does not directly speak to moat-DEPTH (premium translation).

key_evidence.snippet: Battery Materials Solutions FY2025 Adjusted EBIT EUR -91 m; WC build EUR 261 m; A2 strategic verdict MIXED — buyer-power examples show contracts ARE renegotiable.
key_evidence.implication: Direct financial evidence that moat-depth is eroding even where moat-persistence holds — supports T4 prior anchoring well below T3.

what_would_move_it_up: Treasury allows 'impracticable-to-trace' transition to expire on schedule end-2026 without renewal AND issues hard-edge tracing guidance for graphite/electrolyte salts in Q1 2027
First DG GROW or member-state Battery Passport non-compliance penalty case published with monetary threshold post Feb 2027
DFARS implementing rule for NDAA Section 842 published ahead of statutory 2028 date, demonstrating defence-demand pull-forward
OEM Q-call ASP commentary materially widens (>5pp) the price spread between compliant and non-compliant CAM in 2026-2027
Major fleet operator (Ayvens, Element, ARI) publishes RFP language explicitly excluding non-FEOC-compliant batteries with attestation requirement
what_would_move_it_down: Treasury extends 'impracticable-to-trace' transition rule beyond end-2026 (replay of May 2024 carve-out playbook)
Asian compliant-capacity build inside EU/US perimeter exceeds 250 kt/yr cumulative by year-end 2027 (EcoPro Phase 2, LG Chem TN ramp, POSCO Future M, additional SK On / Sumitomo announcements)
Public news of GM, Ford, Stellantis, BMW, VW renegotiating CAM offtake terms with compliant suppliers in 2026-2027 (POSCO/GM/Ultium replay at scale)
Platts CAM Daily index shows sustained <5% premium for compliant vs Chinese-origin CAM for >2 quarters
DFARS Section 842 implementing rule slips beyond 2028 statutory date OR pilot procurements use commercial-COTS exemption broadly

propagation_to_bets_seed.label: BMS compliance pricing
propagation_to_bets_seed.channel: Compliance-mandate-vs-uptake split: BMS conversion premium depends on the ASP-translation rate, not just on regulatory persistence. Asian-localisation pace inside EU/US is the dominant erosion channel.
propagation_to_bets_seed.directionality: TL up → BMS conversion premium holds; TL down → BMS premium compresses toward cost-plus, accelerating BP-4 commoditization risk.

propagation_to_bets_seed.label: Whole compliance-moat bet (Umicore-level franchise persistence)
propagation_to_bets_seed.channel: Direct — B4 IS the bet on moat depth and monetisation across Catalysis, BMS, Recycling and Specialty. T4 is the load-bearing macro driver.
propagation_to_bets_seed.directionality: TL up → whole-franchise re-rating supportable; TL down → segment-by-segment defensible-niche re-scoping required (per convergence stream's S-3-1 forced-decision flag).

propagation_to_bets_seed.label: Specialty premium driven by China germanium / antimony / gallium export-control regime
propagation_to_bets_seed.channel: Indirect — Specialty premium is anchored more on T3 (decoupling permanence) than T4, but compliance-moat enforcement on critical minerals (CRMA strategic-project designations on germanium recycling, MOFCOM regime persistence) overlaps the demand-side monetisation channel for defence/space-solar.
propagation_to_bets_seed.directionality: TL up → Specialty premium reinforced by enforcement teeth; TL down → Specialty premium remains anchored to T3 alone, with compliance contributing only marginal demand-pull.

cross_cutting.label: Chemistry mix (NMC vs LFP/LMFP)
cross_cutting.interaction: Compliance moat affects which chemistry survives: NMC retains higher compliance-pricing leverage (premium platforms, defence demand) while LFP localisation in EU/US (e.g. ProLogium, Verkor LFP, BYD Hungary) tests whether compliance moat extends to commodity-grade chemistry. Asian localisation pace under T4 directly accelerates chemistry-mix shifts.

cross_cutting.label: Decoupling permanence
cross_cutting.interaction: T3 governs whether the legal framework persists; T4 governs whether the framework, while persisting, translates into price premium. The two are SEPARABLE — T3 can hold high TL while T4 erodes. Equity-market consensus systematically conflates them; the wedge IS the alpha.

themes.title: PGM regime + recycling stream capture
themes.prior_rationale: The PGM regime is overwhelmingly an observable-today condition rather than a forward-looking event. WPIC has now confirmed a 4th consecutive year of platinum deficit (1.082 Moz in 2025, 240 koz forecast 2026), repeatedly revising UP not DOWN; Norilsk Q1 2026 production is -18% Pd / -26% Pt YoY hard data; gold spot at USD 4,592/oz (~EUR 4,000/oz) is empirically above the EUR 3,500/oz tailwind threshold; Aurubis Q1 2025/26 confirms refiner pricing power intact (PGM volume +27%, metal-results 45% of group gross margin); EU WSR digital procedures go operational 21 May 2026 and WSR full export rules 21 May 2027. The bet is therefore not whether the supportive regime exists but whether it persists through an 18-month resolution window. Persistence prior sits at 0.70 rather than 0.80+ because three components carry meaningful tail risk: (a) gold is cyclical not structural and a 15-25% correction takes the EUR-denominated price below the EUR 3,500/oz threshold, (b) Russian PGM normalisation in a Ukraine-ceasefire scenario is a low-probability but high-impact shock that consensus is not pricing, (c) trader-vs-refiner feedstock competition is a 12-18 month dynamic with Glencore Li-Cycle and Trafigura Nth Cycle commitments still ramping. A 0.70 prior captures both the strongly observable current state and the asymmetric downside tail.
themes.thesis: The PGM regime in May 2026 is in a multi-layered supportive regime for Western complex-feed refiners, but the components have different durabilities. Layer 1 (primary mining tightness): structural — JM forecasts a 3rd-then-4th consecutive year of platinum deficit, Norilsk is guiding -10-11% palladium and -5-8% platinum FY2026 (Q1 already -18% Pd / -26% Pt YoY), and South African producers are still working off pre-2024 grade and shaft-depth constraints even as Eskom power-side eases (341 days without load-shedding, EAF 98.9%). The Eskom recovery is genuinely meaningful but it eases one of thre
themes.definition: Captures whether the precious-metals regime — Pt + Pd + Rh primary mining tightness, Russian PGM containment, gold-price tailwind, and Western secondary-feed accessibility — stays supportive of integrated complex-feed refining economics. Specifically tracks (a) WPIC/JM platinum market deficit persistence (4th consecutive year forecast for 2026), (b) Southern African primary supply constraint via Eskom power and Anglo Platinum / Sibanye / Implats production, (c) Russian PGM normalisation risk (Norilsk sanctions and grade decline), (d) refiner-vs-trader feed competition (Glencore, Trafigura, Aurubis, Heraeus, JM), (e) Western recycling stream Western-capturability under EU Waste Shipment Regulation (WSR) and Critical Raw Materials Act (CRMA), and (f) working-capital regime staying inside ±EUR 250m/cycle. Gold price (currently ~EUR 4,000-4,100/oz on May 2026 spot, well above the EUR 3,500/oz threshold) is explicitly part of the theme: it is the dominant cyclical tailwind on Recycling reported margin and on the inventory-leaseback economics, but it is a cyclical not a structural property of the regime.

named_watch_metrics.threshold: Direct Norilsk parent or PJSC MMC Norilsk Nickel SDN designation = escalation; OFAC sectoral relief = de-escalation
named_watch_metrics.source: OFAC SDN list + EU sanctions packages + LME advisory notes

named_watch_metrics.name: Aurubis quarterly refining-margin / metal-result share of gross margin
named_watch_metrics.current_value: 45% of group gross margin (Q1 2025/26); PGM production +27% YoY
named_watch_metrics.threshold: Sustained metal-result share > 40% = refiner pricing power intact; below 30% = trader-diverted feedstock biting
named_watch_metrics.source: Aurubis quarterly reports (Feb / May / Aug / Nov)

named_watch_metrics.name: EU Waste Shipment Regulation enforcement status
named_watch_metrics.current_value: Digital procedures applicable 21 May 2026; export rules 21 May 2027; Environmental Crime Directive in force
named_watch_metrics.threshold: Enforcement statistics showing > 90% retention of EU PGM/e-scrap = Western capture executing; major export-rule postponement = de-supports
named_watch_metrics.source: European Commission DG ENV bulletins + national customs authorities + BIR / EERA / Recycling Today data

named_watch_metrics.name: Norilsk PGM production (Pd + Pt, Moz annualised)
named_watch_metrics.current_value: FY2026 guidance: Pd -10-11%, Pt -5-8%; Q1 2026 Pd -18% YoY, Pt -26% YoY
named_watch_metrics.threshold: Sustained YoY decline > 5% = Russian containment intact; flat or YoY recovery = normalisation risk
named_watch_metrics.source: Nornickel quarterly production releases (nornickel.com investor relations)

baseline_research_note.summary: The PGM regime in May 2026 is supportive across multiple independently-observable layers: WPIC confirms 4th consecutive year of platinum deficit (240 koz 2026 after 1.082 Moz 2025, repeatedly revised UP); Johnson Matthey 2025 PGM Market Report shows 3rd consecutive year of platinum deficit, palladium moving from deficit to balance, rhodium remaining in deficit; Norilsk Q1 2026 production printed Pd -14% QoQ / -18% YoY and Pt -26% QoQ / -26% YoY with full-year guidance of Pd -10-11% and Pt -5-8%; Eskom EAF at 98.9% with 341 consecutive days without load-shedding eases the power-side of SA primary supply but does not reverse the structural mine decline; gold at USD 4,592/oz (May 1 2026) translates to ~EUR 4,000/oz at EUR/USD ~1.15, comfortably above the EUR 3,500/oz threshold below which Recycling EBITDA compresses by EUR 70-100m per EUR 500/oz; Aurubis Q1 2025/26 reported PGM production +27% YoY with metal-results now 45% of group gross margin (vs 36% prior year), confirming refiner pricing power despite trader incursion; EU Waste Shipment Regulation digital procedures apply from 21 May 2026 and most export rules from 21 May 2027, complemented by the Environmental Crime Directive; CRMA names PGMs strategic with 6 dedicated projects, 25% secondary-supply target by 2030, and 15-month permitting for recycling. The two layers carrying the most asymmetric downside are the gold tailwind (cyclical, not structural — EUR 500/oz cushion above threshold) and Russian PGM normalisation risk (currently low but tail-shaped on a Ukraine ceasefire path).

key_evidence.ref: WPIC Platinum Quarterly Q1 2026 release (28 April 2026)
key_evidence.snippet: WPIC announces 240 koz platinum deficit forecast for 2026 (4th consecutive year), following 1.082 Moz deficit in 2025 — revised UP from earlier 'balanced' forecast on geopolitical disruption. Total supply +2% (mine flat 5.553 Moz, recycling +10% to 1.664 Moz), demand -8% to 7.619 Moz.
key_evidence.implication: Structural deficit is empirically confirmed across multiple WPIC report cycles with progressive UPWARD revisions. Persistence prior must sit well above 0.50.

key_evidence.snippet: Platinum 3rd consecutive year of significant deficit (mine supply -3% on SA constraints); palladium expected to move from deficit (2012-2024) to balance on Pt-for-Pd substitution + Chinese auto recycling growth; rhodium remains in deficit with SA decline balanced by autocatalyst demand fall.
key_evidence.implication: Independent corroboration of WPIC. Bifurcating PGM economics: Pt + Rh structurally tight, Pd rebalancing — supportive of complex-feed refiner pricing power, less supportive of palladium-heavy autocatalyst inventory values.

key_evidence.ref: Nornickel Q1 2026 production release (28 April 2026) + FY2026 guidance (28 January 2026)
key_evidence.snippet: Q1 2026: nickel -28% QoQ, copper -12%, palladium -14% QoQ / -18% YoY, platinum -26% QoQ / -26% YoY. FY2026 guidance: Pd -10-11%, Pt -5-8%, Ni -3%, Cu -5%. Western sanctions disrupt payments, logistics, equipment imports despite Norilsk parent + main subs not OFAC-designated.
key_evidence.implication: Russian PGM containment is empirically intact and worsening, not normalising. Direct read-across to global Pt deficit persistence.

key_evidence.snippet: PGM production +27% YoY to 1.55t; silver +17% to 270t; metal results now 45% of group gross margin (was 36%); EBT EUR 105m; 2025/26 forecast upgraded. Treatment + refining charges share of gross margin declined from 31% to 22% — pricing-power shift FROM TC/RCs TO METAL RESULTS.
key_evidence.implication: Refiner pricing power and feedstock access intact at the largest European complex-feed peer. Direct comparable for Hoboken economics — supportive read-across.

key_evidence.ref: EU Waste Shipment Regulation revision (in force 20 May 2024) + Environmental Crime Directive
key_evidence.snippet: Digital procedures applicable from 21 May 2026; most export rules from 21 May 2027; plastic-waste export ban to non-OECD from 21 November 2026. Stronger enforcement and cooperation in fighting waste trafficking. Authorities will require evidence that scrap goes to properly permitted smelters/refiners, not loosely regulated yards.
key_evidence.implication: Western recycling stream Western-capturability becomes a regulatory lock from late 2026 onward, structurally favouring permitted refiners (Aurubis, Heraeus, Hoboken) over export-dependent Asian destination yards.

key_evidence.ref: EU Critical Raw Materials Act (CRMA) — 25% secondary-supply target by 2030, 6 PGM-specific strategic projects
key_evidence.snippet: CRMA prioritises PGMs as strategically essential; 6 strategic projects target PGM extraction/recovery/recycling; permitting accelerated to 15 months for processing/recycling and 27 months for extraction; 25% of critical-mineral supply from recycled sources by 2030.
key_evidence.implication: CRMA + WSR jointly create a structural Western-capture regime for PGM secondary supply, materially supportive of integrated complex-feed refining economics through 2027-2030.

key_evidence.ref: Eskom Power System Status (April 2026) + FY25/26 System Adequacy Outlook
key_evidence.snippet: 341 consecutive days without load-shedding (as of 26 April 2026); FY25/26 EAF at 98.9%; unplanned losses reduced 7.1 GW (from 16.5 GW to 9.1 GW); FY2026 winter projected load-shedding-free; ~6 GW reserve capacity over winter.
key_evidence.implication: Eskom power-side constraint on SA primary supply is materially eased — but does not reverse mine grade/depth/labour structural decline. SA mine output 2026 still flat-to-down per WPIC.

key_evidence.ref: May 2026 spot prices (Pt USD 1,999.70/oz, Pd USD 1,545.50/oz, gold USD 4,592/oz, EUR/USD ~1.15)
key_evidence.snippet: Gold ~EUR 4,000/oz at May 2026 EUR/USD ~1.15, vs Umicore-disclosed EUR 3,500/oz threshold below which Recycling EBITDA compresses by EUR 70-100m per EUR 500/oz of decline. Platinum re-rated above USD 2,000 from sub-USD 1,000 levels in 2024. Palladium structurally lower at USD 1,500 reflecting JM-confirmed supply-rebalancing.
key_evidence.implication: Gold tailwind is fully active with ~EUR 500/oz cushion above threshold; platinum re-rating reflects deficit pricing the market; palladium normalisation reflects supply-rebalancing. Mixed but net supportive for integrated complex-feed economics.

key_evidence.ref: Glencore Metallium binding agreement (5 January 2026) + Li-Cycle assets acquisition (August 2025); Trafigura USD 1.1bn Nth Cycle deal (April 2026)
key_evidence.snippet: Glencore: 2,400 t/yr feedstock for Metallium Texas; expanded into US complex-feed via Li-Cycle Rochester hub. Trafigura: 10-year USD 1.1bn deal starting 2028 for 2,000t Ni + 1,500t Li carbonate from recycled feedstock.
key_evidence.implication: Trader incursion into refiner feedstock territory is concrete and ramping. Aurubis pricing-power data confirms refiners holding for now, but 2027-2028 contract pricing is the contested period.

what_would_move_it_up: WPIC 2027 forecast extending platinum deficit to a 5th consecutive year above 200 koz
JM May 2026 PGM Market Report confirming platinum and rhodium deficit horizon to 2028 with palladium maintaining tightness
Norilsk FY2026 actual production undershooting -10-11% Pd guidance with grade-decline acceleration
EU WSR enforcement statistics through Q3-Q4 2026 showing >90% retention of EU PGM/e-scrap; major customs seizure cases publicised
CRMA strategic-project disbursement on schedule with first PGM project FIDs
Gold sustained above USD 5,000/oz / EUR 4,500/oz on continued geopolitical / real-rate tailwinds
what_would_move_it_down: Ukraine ceasefire framework + OFAC sectoral relief for Russian non-ferrous metals — Russian PGM normalisation shock
Gold correction below USD 3,800/oz / EUR 3,300/oz on real-rates re-rating or coordinated central-bank gold sales
WPIC mid-2026 quarterly REVISING DEFICIT DOWN with Pt market flipping to balanced/surplus
Aurubis Q3-Q4 2025/26 metal-results gross-margin share falling below 30% indicating refiner pricing power compression
EU member-state postponement or weakening of WSR export rules ahead of 21 May 2027 go-live
Major OEM hybrid Pt-loading reduction announcement materially compressing autocatalyst PGM demand

propagation_to_bets_seed.name: Recycling franchise (Hoboken complex-feed PGM refining)
propagation_to_bets_seed.rule: +1 magnitude per supportive layer (Pt deficit, Russian containment, gold > EUR 3,500/oz, refiner pricing power, WSR/CRMA capture); -1 per failing layer; weighted to current 5-of-6 supportive state.
propagation_to_bets_seed.rationale: Direct exposure: Recycling EBITDA is the single business unit fully captured by the PGM regime + gold tailwind + Western capture lock. The EUR 525m gold-inventory sale-and-leaseback is contingent on this regime. T5 truth-likelihood maps almost 1:1 onto B3 viability.

propagation_to_bets_seed.name: Catalysis input cost / PGM-loop integration
propagation_to_bets_seed.rule: +0.5 magnitude when integrated Catalysis-Recycling PGM loop benefits from refiner-side feedstock access advantage; -0.5 when PGM thrifting + substitution accelerates; +0.5 when Pt-for-Pd substitution still has runway (current state).
propagation_to_bets_seed.rationale: Secondary exposure: Catalysis benefits from the integrated loop with Recycling (input-cost pass-through, secured feed) but is partially exposed to thrifting / substitution risk in the demand layer. Magnitude smaller and two-sided.

propagation_to_bets_seed.name: Cash conversion via working-capital regime
propagation_to_bets_seed.rule: -1 magnitude per +EUR 100m WC build above EUR 250m/cycle envelope; +1 per WC release from PGM/gold price moderation; current state pricing-driven WC build at upper envelope.
propagation_to_bets_seed.rationale: Indirect exposure: gold + PGM rally drives WC build (commodity inventory revaluation + customer-deposit dynamics); a price spike in either Pt or gold would push WC consumption above the EUR 250m/cycle envelope and constrain dividend / capex headroom even while reported EBITDA remains strong. The two-sidedness explains the moderate weight and current modest negative contribution.

themes.title: Western EV demand trajectory
themes.prior_rationale: The world-state question — does Western EV demand evolve at a pace that sustains premium-NMC CAM volumes through 2028 — is observable today on multiple dimensions and the observation is mixed-to-negative for the Umicore-relevant slice. EU aggregate BEV demand is healthy (19.4% Q1 2026, +4.2pp YoY) but Q1 2026 ACEA splits show HEV at 38.6% remains the larger share, indicating European consumers still favour hybrid over pure electric even under binding CO2 compliance. US BEV demand has compressed from 10.6% Q3 2025 peak to 5.8% Q1 2026 (-45% relative) following the OBBBA termination of Section 30D, with hybrid stepping in to capture displaced demand (Q4 2025 hybrid 19.7%, Q1 2026 14%). Premium-segment BEV (>EUR 40k retail), the load-bearing segment for nickel-rich NMC, is performing worse than aggregate: BMW BEV+PHEV -50% YoY in US Q1 2026, Mercedes EU BEV +7% off a low base, Audi/VW Group BEV-mix targets repeatedly cut. The regulatory pull-through that was supposed to sustain BEV demand has been weakened on both sides: EU 2035 100% target diluted to 90% in Dec 2025, US 30D credit terminated Sep 2025. Chinese-brand EU BEV share at ~7-8% growing despite 17.8-45.3% combined countervailing duties, with BYD Hungary localisation set to bypass tariffs. Hybrid is more durable than 2023-vintage forecasts assumed: Toyota's Feb 2026 commitment to grow hybrid output ~30% to 6.7m units by 2028 implies hybrid is a destination, not a transition. Net: the prior that 'Western EV demand sustains premium-NMC CAM volumes through 2028' sits clearly below 0.50 because (a) the premium slice is compressing faster than aggregate BEV, (b) hybrid is taking material share from the BEV TAM that nickel-rich NMC needs, (c) regulatory tailwinds have weakened on both sides in H2 2025-Q1 2026, (d) Chinese-OEM share of EU BEV market is dilutive to Western suppliers. The 0.42 prior reflects that demand is not collapsing (EU compliance pull is real and persistent) but is also not delivering the premium-NMC-supportive trajectory that consensus and Umicore's contracted book implicitly assumed.
themes.thesis: Western EV demand is bifurcating along three axes. First geography: EU BEV share has reaccelerated to ~19-20% in Q1 2026 driven by binding CO2 fleet targets (carmakers chasing 2025-2027 compliance), while US BEV share has collapsed from 10.6% Q3 2025 to 5.8% Q1 2026 after Section 30D termination — a 45% relative compression in one quarter. Second segment: premium-segment BEV demand (>EUR 40k retail), the load-bearing segment for Umicore-relevant nickel-rich NMC, is performing materially worse than mass-market BEV demand (BMW BEV+PHEV -50% YoY in US Q1 2026; Mercedes Europe BEV +7% YoY off a lo
themes.definition: Captures whether Western (EU + UK + US + Canada) electric-vehicle demand evolves at a pace and chemistry-mix that sustains premium-segment NMC cathode volumes through 2028. Five sub-questions: (a) does Western premium-OEM BEV demand stay net-flat-or-up through 2027 (>EUR 40k retail price band, the load-bearing segment for nickel-rich NMC); (b) does the ICE+HEV portion of the Western fleet decline gradually rather than cliff-down, preserving Catalysis runway; (c) does US hybrid share remain above 19% (current Q1 2026: ~14% per Cox; Q4 2025 peak: 19.7%) — i.e. is hybrid truly persistent or a transition-phase artefact; (d) does Chinese-OEM EU BEV penetration stay bounded by tariffs and minimum-price agreements (currently 7.4% share Sep 2025, growing); (e) does the Western EV TAM survive regulatory rollback shocks (EU 2035 ICE-ban dilution to 90% Dec 2025; US Section 30D credit termination effective Oct 2025). Theme is universal world-state: it answers whether demand exists at all, leaving chemistry-within-demand to T1.

named_watch_metrics.name: ACEA monthly EU BEV market share (%)
named_watch_metrics.threshold: Sustained below 17% for two consecutive quarters (compliance erosion) or above 25% (compliance success and Umicore-positive); split BEV/HEV/PHEV/petrol/diesel monthly required
named_watch_metrics.source: ACEA monthly press releases (Passenger Car Registrations) + ICCT European Car Market Monitor

named_watch_metrics.threshold: Sustained below 5% (structural retreat) or recovery above 8% (post-30D resilience); HEV share split required

named_watch_metrics.name: ICCT EU + NA premium-segment (>EUR 40k) BEV share (%)
named_watch_metrics.threshold: Sustained below 22% in EU premium / 6% in US premium = premium-NMC demand compression flag
named_watch_metrics.source: ICCT European Car Market Monitor + ICCT US Vehicle Markets Briefing

named_watch_metrics.threshold: 2027 BEV production forecast cut > 10% from prior quarter = TAM contraction signal
named_watch_metrics.source: S&P Global Mobility Light Vehicle Production Forecasts (quarterly revision)
