named_watch_metrics.threshold: Above USD 4.00/gal sustained = EV-switching tailwind; below USD 3.00 = EV-switching headwind
named_watch_metrics.source: EIA Weekly Petroleum Status Report + AAA daily fuel gauge

named_watch_metrics.name: OEM premium BEV-mix CY guidance (BMW + Mercedes + Audi + Porsche, blended %)
named_watch_metrics.threshold: Blended Q-call BEV-mix CY2026 guidance below 18% = premium demand compression confirmed; revisions downward across 2 consecutive Q-calls = escalation
named_watch_metrics.source: Quarterly earnings calls and OEM investor-day decks (BMW, Mercedes-Benz Group, Audi/VW, Porsche AG)

named_watch_metrics.name: Chinese-OEM EU BEV share (% of new BEV registrations)
named_watch_metrics.threshold: Sustained above 10% = market-share dilution for Western premium suppliers; above 12% = structural breach of countervailing-duty barrier
named_watch_metrics.source: ACEA + JATO Dynamics + Rhodium Group Chinese-EV Europe tracker

baseline_research_note.summary: Western EV demand is bifurcated and the Umicore-relevant premium-NMC slice is structurally weaker than aggregate BEV indicators suggest. EU BEV share at 19.4% Q1 2026 (ACEA) under binding CO2 fleet compliance pull is healthy in aggregate but masks that HEV at 38.6% remains the European consumer's preferred electrified choice. US BEV share has compressed from 10.6% Q3 2025 peak to 5.8% Q1 2026 after OBBBA terminated Section 30D effective Oct 1 2025, with hybrid stepping in (Q4 2025 19.7%, Q1 2026 14% per Cox). Premium-segment BEV — the load-bearing pool for nickel-rich NMC — is underperforming aggregate by 5-10 percentage points (BMW BEV+PHEV -50% YoY US Q1 2026, Mercedes EU BEV +7% off a low base, Audi BEV programmes repeatedly re-baselined). Regulatory pull-through is weaker than 12 months ago: EU 2035 100% target diluted to 90% in Dec 2025, US 30D credit terminated. Chinese-OEM EU BEV share at 7-8% and rising despite 17.8-45.3% countervailing duties because BYD Hungary and Chery Barcelona localisation bypass tariff incidence. Toyota's Feb 2026 commitment to grow hybrid output ~30% to 6.7m units by 2028 (~60% of total Toyota production) signals hybrid is a destination not a transition — supportive for Umicore Catalysis runway, structurally negative for the BEV TAM that BMS needs. Net world-state: Western EV demand exists and grows in aggregate, but the premium-NMC slice for 2027-2028 is materially weaker than the 190 GWh / 270 GWh contracted-book implied volumes.

key_evidence.ref: ACEA Q1 2026 New Car Registrations press release; ICCT European Car Market Monitor Mar 2026
key_evidence.snippet: EU Q1 2026: 546,937 BEV (19.4% share, +4.2pp YoY), 1,089,421 HEV (38.6%), 268,344 PHEV (9.4%), petrol+diesel combined 30.3% (down from 38.2% Q1 2025). Top-5 markets BEV +36% YoY. Germany 22%, France 27% BEV share Jan-Feb 2026.
key_evidence.implication: EU aggregate BEV demand is on track and reaccelerating under binding 2025-2027 CO2 fleet compliance pull. But the HEV share at 38.6% is the larger slice and continues to grow, telling us European consumers still default to hybrid where given the choice.

key_evidence.ref: Cox Automotive Q1 2026 EV Sales Report Commentary; Cox Industry Insights call Mar 25 2026
key_evidence.snippet: US Q1 2026 EV sales 216,399 (-27% YoY), 5.8% share (unchanged from Q4 2025, down from Q3 2025 peak of 10.6%). Hybrid Q1 2026 record 14%, Q4 2025 19.7% peak. Used EV sales surge 12%. Sales drop after 30D termination 'has slowed' but not reversed.
key_evidence.implication: US BEV demand has structurally retrenched post-30D. Hybrid is absorbing displaced BEV demand. The 5.8% US BEV share is closer to a floor than a transition point — without policy or fuel-price re-acceleration, US BEV does not return to 10%+ in 2026-2027.

key_evidence.ref: BMW Q1 2026 US sales (BMWBlog Apr 10 2026); Mercedes-Benz Group Q1 2026 sales release; Stellantis Q1 2026 EU30 release
key_evidence.snippet: BMW BEV+PHEV US Q1 2026: 9,856 units (~12% of BMW US sales) vs 19,761 Q1 2025 (-50% YoY). Mercedes-Benz Q1 2026 global EV +11% to 50,400 (passenger 44,300) — Europe +7%, US +20% off a low base. Stellantis EU30 +5%, hybrid leadership 21.6%.
key_evidence.implication: Premium-segment BEV demand (BMW, Mercedes premium SUVs and sedans) is materially weaker than aggregate BEV in both regions. The Umicore-relevant load-bearing slice — premium nickel-rich NMC — is contracting where the broader market is holding.

key_evidence.ref: EU Commission CO2 regulation amendment proposal Dec 16 2025; OBBBA Section 30D termination effective Oct 1 2025 (IRS guidance)
key_evidence.snippet: EU 2035 target diluted from 100% to 90% CO2 reduction; ICE, range-extenders, mild hybrids, PHEVs permitted past 2035 with low-carbon fuels offsetting residual 10%. US OBBBA terminates 30D Clean Vehicle Credit for vehicles acquired after Sep 30 2025.
key_evidence.implication: Regulatory pull-through has weakened on both sides of the Atlantic in H2 2025-Q1 2026. Trilogue negotiations starting Jan 2026 under Cypriot Presidency present risk of further EU dilution. The OEM compliance pressure that was the main driver of CY2027-2030 BEV-mix forecasts has lost a significant chunk of its force.

key_evidence.ref: Toyota Motor Corporation Feb 2026 production guidance update
key_evidence.snippet: Toyota signaled plans Feb 2026 to grow hybrid and PHEV production by ~30% to 6.7 million units by 2028, positioning hybrids as the core of electrification strategy and ~60% of total vehicle output. RAV4 2026 model exclusively hybrid.
key_evidence.implication: Toyota's hybrid commitment is a strong structural signal that hybrid is a destination, not a transition. This is positive for Umicore Catalysis runway through 2030+ but structurally negative for BMS — every hybrid vehicle Toyota produces is one that does not consume nickel-rich NMC cathode material.

key_evidence.ref: EIA Short-Term Energy Outlook Apr 2026; AAA fuel gauge Mar 2026
key_evidence.snippet: US national average gasoline March 2026 USD 3.54/gal (+43c WoW on Middle East escalation). EIA 2026 forecast USD 3.70/gal vs USD 3.10 2025; 2027 USD 3.46/gal. Cox flags rising EV shopping intent correlated with gas-price spikes.
key_evidence.implication: Fuel-price path is at upper end of recent range, providing modest tailwind to EV-switching consideration. But not yet sustained at levels that reverse the post-30D US BEV compression. EIA 2027 forecast shows fuel-cost driver weakening into 2027 as oil supply normalises.

key_evidence.ref: Rhodium Group / JATO Dynamics Chinese-EV Europe tracker; EU Commission countervailing duties Oct 30 2024
key_evidence.snippet: Chinese-brand EU BEV share crossed 7.4% by Sep 2025 (record); BYD H1 2025 +311% YoY, MG holding ~1.9% share at 45.3% combined tariff. BYD Hungary plant ramping to bypass tariffs; Chery Barcelona FID active. EU minimum-price agreement framework Jan 2026 guidance.
key_evidence.implication: Chinese-OEM EU BEV penetration is climbing despite tariffs and is set to accelerate via localisation. By 2027 Chinese brands could hold 12-15% of EU BEV market, structurally diluting the addressable Western premium-NMC pool. This is incremental to the demand-cliff risk for Umicore.

key_evidence.ref: S&P Global Mobility 2026 Light Vehicle Production Forecast; PwC Strategy& EV Sales Review Q1 2026
key_evidence.snippet: S&P cut Europe LV production outlook by 114k 2026 / 143k 2027; North America by 63k 2026 / 235k 2027. NA combined BEV+PHEV share 8.9% 2026, 10.1% 2027. EU BEV share expected 23% 2026, 28% 2027 driven by 2025-2027 CO2 target.
key_evidence.implication: S&P numbers confirm bifurcated path: Europe BEV reaccelerating under CO2 compliance, North America remaining structurally lower with modest 2027 recovery only. Premium-segment splits within these aggregates are not separately published but our channel reads suggest premium underperforms aggregate by 5-10pp.

what_would_move_it_up: BMW Neue Klasse iX3, iX5, iX7 launch trajectory above plan with cumulative volumes >200k by end-2027
EU trilogue holds 90% line on 2035 CO2 target and adds binding 2030 ratchet
US gasoline sustained above USD 4.00/gal driving Cox EV shopping intent above 2024 peak
Premium OEM Q-call language returning to BEV-mix expansion in two consecutive cycles
Mercedes EQ-replacement platform delivering range/charging parity at >60k units/quarter
Chinese-OEM EU BEV share capped below 8% via enforced minimum-price agreements
ACEA monthly EU BEV share crossing 25% sustained on premium-segment-led growth
what_would_move_it_down: Premium OEM (BMW + Mercedes + Audi + Porsche) blended BEV-mix CY2026 reported below 18% vs ~22% guidance
EU 2035 trilogue dilutes target below 90% with PHEVs explicitly counted as compliant
US Cox HEV share above 22% sustained for 3 consecutive quarters confirming hybrid lock-in
S&P Global Mobility Western 2027 BEV production forecast revised down >10% from current
Chinese-brand EU BEV monthly share above 10% sustained through Hungary/Barcelona localisation
PowerCo Salzgitter ramp slip > 9 months past current schedule with knock-on AESC/ACC delays
Toyota CY2027 hybrid production confirmed above 5.5m units cementing hybrid as destination
EPA Phase 3 GHG rules formally weakened or California ZEV waiver federal-court reversal

propagation_to_bets_seed.bet_label: BMS / Battery Materials Solutions — premium-NMC CAM volumes through 2028
propagation_to_bets_seed.rule: Direct demand floor. -1 magnitude per 5pp compression in EU+NA premium-segment BEV share below 22%; -1 per 100k unit cut to S&P Global Mobility 2027 Western BEV production forecast; +0.5 per BMW Neue Klasse cumulative production milestone above plan. T6 is the single largest consumer-demand input to BMS impairment risk and to the credibility of the 190 GWh 2027 / 270 GWh 2030 contracted book translating into realised volumes.

propagation_to_bets_seed.bet_label: Catalysis — ICE/HEV runway and 2028+ cliff timing
propagation_to_bets_seed.rule: Two-sided. +1 magnitude when US HEV share above 19% sustained or EU HEV share above 38% sustained (hybrid is a Catalysis-positive destination). -1 magnitude per 2pp acceleration in BEV share above 22% in EU because that compresses ICE+HEV catalyst demand floor faster than current Catalysis depreciation schedule assumes. T6 is the most direct macro determinant of Catalysis cash-engine durability.

propagation_to_bets_seed.bet_label: Group cash conversion and dividend sustainability via group volume
propagation_to_bets_seed.rule: Indirect. -0.5 magnitude when premium BEV cliff scenario probability rises above 0.30 (because BMS losses widen and force capital reallocation away from cash-return). +0.3 when bifurcated_holding probability exceeds 0.45 sustained. T6 transmits to B7 via the BMS impairment and dividend-defence pathway rather than directly.

cross_cutting.interaction: T6 captures whether demand exists at all; T1 captures which chemistry wins within demand. Joint state matters: high T6 BEV TAM + adverse T1 chemistry mix (LFP dominance) is still bad for Umicore. The Umicore-relevant joint condition is: T6 premium-segment BEV demand AND T1 nickel-rich NMC retention in premium platforms.

T4 sets the regulatory pull-through that determines OEM willingness to push BEV mix above natural consumer demand. T6 measures the consumer-pull signal independent of compliance. EU CO2 binding targets are amplifying T6 in Q1 2026 (BEV share 19.4% reflects compliance push as much as consumer pull); US has lost regulatory amplification post-30D termination.

T2 affects EV affordability through (i) cell-pack production cost in European gigafactories (Olen, Nysa, Salzgitter, Douvrin, Kaiserslautern) and (ii) consumer electricity costs that determine TCO advantage of BEV vs ICE. Adverse T2 (high power, high CBAM) raises European BEV sticker prices, which feeds back negatively into T6 premium-segment demand.

bets.label: BMS NMC viability through FY2028

intermediate_gates.name: Q3 2027 BMS proof-gate
intermediate_gates.what_resolves: CEO non-negotiable commitment (per pos_strategic_commitment_q3_2027_proof_gate): BMS must demonstrate positive unit economics on shipped volumes (positive segment free cash flow, not EBITDA break-even) verified by an independent commercial audit. If gate is missed, a structured partnership or partial divestiture process begins before year-end 2027. This is the single load-bearing readable signal between today (May 2026) and the FY2028 resolution; it converts the bet from path-dependent persistence into a binary observable.

intermediate_gates.name: Q4 2026 Treasury 'impracticable-to-trace' transition expiry
intermediate_gates.what_resolves: T4-coupled gate: Treasury / IRS either lets the May 2024 carve-out expire (enforcement-teeth tightening, BMS premium widens) or extends it (replay of the soften-via-guidance playbook, premium compresses further). Sets the FY2027 ASP-translation backdrop for the Loyalist/AESC volumes.

intermediate_gates.name: First IONWAY/PowerCo Salzgitter call-off cycle (H2 2026 - H1 2027)
intermediate_gates.what_resolves: First sustained quarter of physical call-off volumes from IONWAY against the 190 GWh 2027 contracted-book schedule; tests whether the contracted-book-to-realised-volume slippage is at Macro's '6-18 month' base case or worse. Single largest BMS customer concentration.

intermediate_gates.name: Quarterly segment-level FCF disclosure (target Q1 2026 trading update onwards)
intermediate_gates.what_resolves: Per pos_governance_segment_fcf_disclosure_commitment, all five Convergence debate participants demanded quarterly segment FCF disclosure starting with the 24 April 2026 Q1 update. Implementation makes the proof-gate path independently auditable; non-implementation is itself a negative governance signal that re-prices the prior.

bets.prior_rationale: Five observable inputs anchor the prior well below 0.50. (1) T1 (chemistry transition pace) prior 0.78 with falling trajectory (LFP >50% global EV deployments, Mercedes MMA dual-chemistry crossing premium threshold, VW PowerCo unified-cell roadmap relegating NMC to high-range, Korean cell-makers committing LFP capex at LG-GM and Samsung SDI-GM joint plants) is the load-bearing macro driver and points one-way against premium-NMC volume retention. (2) T6 (Western EV demand) prior 0.42 with bifurcated trajectory and premium slice deteriorating: BMW BEV+PHEV -50% YoY in US Q1 2026, Mercedes EU BEV +7% off a low base, Audi/VW BEV-mix targets repeatedly cut, US Section 30D terminated Sep 2025, EU 2035 target diluted to 90% in Dec 2025 - premium-NMC demand floor is materially weaker than the contracted-book-implied 190 GWh 2027 / 270 GWh 2030 volumes. (3) T4 (compliance-moat half-life) prior 0.42 with moat-persists-premium-eroding trajectory: regulatory framework at widest legal aperture (CRMA, Battery Passport, NDAA 842, 30D 70%, 127% CAM tariff) but Asian compliant-capacity ramping inside the perimeter (EcoPro Debrecen 54 kt/yr live Dec 2025, expanding to 108 kt/yr; LG Chem Tennessee from H1 2026) compresses the ASP-translation rate that BMS needs to monetise. (4) Financial baseline is severe: FY2025 BMS revenue EUR 436m at -19.5% Adjusted EBIT margin, capital employed EUR 2.0bn (56% of group), -4.8% ROCE, EUR 1.7bn FY2024 impairment already booked, FY2024 revenue down 30% YoY despite the same contract book - establishing that contracted GWh and realised GWh are separable variables. (5) Two structural constraints further weight against: pCAM externally sourced from CNGR / Eco&Dream (dilutes localisation cost-curve advantage) and the 2028 capacity-vs-commitment mismatch (115 GWh capacity vs 133 GWh commitments per pos_strategic_commitment_bms_capital_ceiling) means utilisation upside is also tolling-dependent. Mildly offsetting: T3 (decoupling permanence) prior 0.66 with upward bias provides FEOC-driven regional regionalisation underwriting on AESC/Loyalist and the EUR 50m AESC ramp; T2 prior 0.66 is two-sided but Belgian (Olen) and Polish (Nysa) sites sit OUTSIDE the German Industriestrompreis relief approved April 2026, so the asymmetric national relief actively disadvantages BMS unit economics. Net-net the prior settles at 0.32 - clearly above zero because (a) the EUR 679m foundation cushion gives Umicore four years of patience capital, (b) the offtake book is contracted not aspirational, and (c) the Loyalist/AESC corridor is a defensible North American niche - but well below 0.50 because the load-bearing macro driver (T1) is one-way, the demand floor (T6 premium slice) is compressing in real time, and the compliance premium (T4) is being captured by Asian localisers rather than incumbents. The 0.32 prior reflects the asymmetry: the bet has many ways to break (chemistry shift, demand cliff, compliance commoditisation, IONWAY/PowerCo restructuring, AESC slippage, second impairment) and few independent ways to clear all the gates simultaneously by Q3 2027.
bets.thesis_resolves_true: By Q3 2027 BMS demonstrates positive segment free cash flow on shipped volumes against an independent commercial audit, and by FY2028 reports a positive Adjusted EBIT margin (recovery from -19.5% FY2025) with capital employed held at or below the implicit EUR 2.0bn ceiling. The path requires: (i) the 190 GWh 2027 contracted book translates into call-offs near 150-180 GWh (Macro's central case minus or near base slippage), (ii) IONWAY/PowerCo Salzgitter ramps without VW chemistry pivot or PowerCo restructuring, (iii) AESC's North American factories ramp on or near 2026 commitment, (iv) Korean cell-maker NMC demand from Loyalist + Korea CAM base remains a defensible niche even as Korean cell-makers add LFP lines, (v) Asian localisation inside the EU/US perimeter (EcoPro Hungary, LG Chem Tennessee) does not fully clear the compliant-CAM market by 2028 so a 5-10% ASP premium survives, (vi) the EU Battery Passport (Feb 2027) and CRMA traceability rules translate into preferred-supplier procurement language at IONWAY, AESC, ACC, SK On rather than table-stakes attestation, and (vii) the EUR 679m Catalysis+Recycling foundation cushion holds (no PGM/gold collapse, no Hoboken operational interruption beyond the 2026 maintenance shutdown) long enough to fund the ~EUR 100-200m of incremental BMS losses through FY2027 without forcing the kill criteria to fire. Resolution-true does NOT require BMS to deliver target margins on the original 270 GWh 2030 plan - the bet is calibrated to Q3 2027 proof-gate clearance plus FY2028 break-even-or-better, not to the failed RISE-strategy growth path.
bets.thesis_resolves_false: The bet resolves false in any of three structurally different ways. Most likely: a slow-burn miss where contracted-book slippage runs at the upper end of Macro's 6-18 month range, IONWAY call-offs print 20-30% below the 2027 schedule, Korean cell-maker LFP conversions accelerate (Macro's bear: second wave in 2027-2028), and BMS prints a third year of EUR 50-150m EBIT losses through FY2027 - Q3 2027 proof-gate is missed, structured partnership or partial divestiture is initiated, and a second impairment of EUR 400-800m is recognised against the EUR 2.0bn capital base. Less likely but more abrupt: a chemistry-driven cliff where BMW Neue Klasse follow-on or Audi PPE-2 confirms LFP/LMFP for any non-entry trim before 2028 (T1 bear threshold), Mercedes shifts long-range CLA/EQS-replacement strategy toward Korean-LFP, and VW PowerCo accelerates LFP at Valencia from H2 2026 - the 270 GWh 2030 book is renegotiated POSCO/GM/Ultium-style, IONWAY capex is partially stranded, and BMS is formally re-scoped to 'surviving offtakes' (probably AESC/Loyalist plus residual Korean premium NMC). Tail-but-non-trivial: governance failure where the foundation cushion compresses before Q3 2027 - gold corrects to USD 1,800-2,000/oz, Catalysis hits an unexpected ROCE wall, or the Hoboken EUR 400m capex slides - and the board is forced to fire BMS kill criteria pre-emptively because the patience capital simply runs out before the proof-gate matures.

transmission_mechanisms.reading_rule: Reading T1 named-watch metric 'Western premium-OEM NMC mix on >EUR 40k BEV registrations'. -1 magnitude on B1 prior per 5pp compression of premium-NMC mix below 70%; +0.5 per BMW Neue Klasse premium-OEM platform refresh confirming high-nickel NMC for full range. Bear threshold: <55% NMC in any single quarter on >EUR 40k Western premium BEVs. T1 prior 0.78 falling trajectory implies the contracted 190 GWh 2027 / 270 GWh 2030 book is structurally over-sized vs realised premium-NMC demand.

Reading T1 named-watch metric 'Western (non-Chinese) NMC-CAM utilisation rate at Korean and EU plants'. -1 magnitude per quarter Nysa or Korean CAM utilisation prints <50% (consistent with Macro's call that Umicore deferred 150 kt EU recycling tied to underloaded CAM capacity); +1 magnitude per two consecutive quarters >80%. The Q3 2027 proof-gate cannot mathematically clear without sustained European CAM utilisation above ~70%.
transmission_mechanisms.note: Folded from T1's mislabelled B2 entry (weight 0.9 LB 'CAM utilisation / Nysa break-even') per brief's adjudication rule - see metadata.adjudication_notes. This is now a SECOND B1 transmission channel under T1, raising T1's effective coupling to B1 to its load-bearing role across both demand-side mix and supply-side utilisation.

transmission_mechanisms.reading_rule: Reading T1 named-watch metric 'Korean cell-maker external CAM reliance'. SK On / LG ES / Samsung SDI moves matter directly because Loyalist + Korea CAM base depend on Korean cell-maker NMC capex direction. Bear: second wave of LFP-line conversions in 2027-2028. Bull: Korean cell-makers add named external Western NMC CAM offtake >EUR 500m total in any 12-month window.

Reading T1 named-watch metric 'LFP-vs-NMC pack-level retail-price boundary on EU BEV trim ladders'. Mercedes CLA reference: ~EUR 6-12k effective premium for NMC long-range. Bear: NMC premium widens to >EUR 15k as LFP enters >EUR 50k segment (compression of NMC niche from below). Bull: NMC premium narrows to <EUR 4k OR NMC offered on entry trim of any premium platform (NMC re-stratifies wider).

T6 prediction-layer named indicator 'ICCT EU + NA premium-segment (>EUR 40k) BEV share'. -1 magnitude per 5pp compression below 22% in EU premium / 6% in US premium; T6 propagation rule confirms: '-1 magnitude per 5pp compression in EU+NA premium-segment BEV share below 22%'. Current Q1 2026 reading: BMW BEV+PHEV US -50% YoY, Mercedes EU BEV +7% off low base, Audi/VW BEV-mix repeatedly cut. T6 central path expects premium slice to compress 3-7pp below aggregate BEV through 2026.

T6 named indicator 'S&P Global Mobility Western Europe + North America BEV production forecast 2027'. -1 magnitude per 100k unit cut in S&P 2027 Western BEV production forecast in any quarterly revision. Current S&P call: cut Europe LV production outlook by 114k 2026 / 143k 2027; NA combined BEV+PHEV share 8.9% 2026, 10.1% 2027.

T6 named indicator (escalation/deescalation): BMW Neue Klasse iX3, iX5, iX7 cumulative production above 200k by end-2027. +0.5 magnitude per Neue Klasse milestone above plan; this is the single largest premium-NMC demand floor, anchored on Korean cell-maker NMC supply. Bear: BMW BEV+PHEV CY2026 mix below 18% vs 22% guidance.

T6 named indicator: 'Chinese-OEM EU BEV share'. Currently 7-8%. Bear threshold: sustained >10% (market-share dilution for Western premium suppliers), >12% structural breach of countervailing-duty barrier via BYD Hungary + Chery Barcelona localisation. Each 1pp of Chinese-OEM EU BEV share gain is direct displacement of the addressable Western premium-NMC pool.

T4 named indicator 'Asian compliant-capacity build inside EU/US perimeter (kt/yr CAM commissioned)'. EcoPro Debrecen live Dec 2025 at 54 kt/yr, scaling to 108 kt/yr in 2026; LG Chem Tennessee from 2026 H1 at half-original-scale. Bear threshold: cumulative >250 kt/yr Asian-promoter compliant CAM capacity inside EU + US by year-end 2027 (oversupply trigger collapsing premium to commodity-with-attestation pricing). Per T4 propagation rule, this is the dominant erosion channel for B1's compliance-pricing premium.

T4 named indicator 'Compliant-CAM ASP premium vs Chinese-origin equivalent'. Currently estimated 8-15% per Platts CAM Daily indications. Bear: sustained <5% premium for >2 quarters (moat-depth collapse). The Q3 2027 proof-gate path requires the premium to stay above ~5% to reach positive segment FCF; collapse to commodity pricing makes break-even mathematically impossible without offsetting volume surprise.

T4 dated escalation: Treasury 'impracticable-to-trace' transition rule expires end-2026. If Treasury extends (replays May 2024 carve-out playbook), B1's regional underwriting compresses (T4 alternate scenario 2 'erosion via Asian-localisation oversupply' fires, T4 TL re-rates to 0.20-0.30). If Treasury allows clean expiry + hard-edge tracing guidance, B1's premium widens (T4 alternate scenario 'enforcement-teeth episode' fires, premium 15-25%, T4 TL 0.65-0.75).

T4 deescalation channel: POSCO/GM/Ultium precedent visible in Umicore FY2025 EUR -91m BMS Adjusted EBIT and EUR 261m WC build. Reading rule: any public news of GM, Ford, Stellantis, BMW, VW renegotiating CAM offtake terms with compliant suppliers in 2026-2027 = direct read-across to B1's contracted-book book value. The 8-10 year tenor + indexation is renegotiable when chemistry pivots.

T3 propagation rule for B1: '+1 magnitude per persistence cycle of structural framing; -2 magnitude on Phase-2 CAM carve-out below 100%'. OBBBA-codified FEOC + IRS Notice 2026-15 MACR mechanism + 127% CAM tariff wall underwrite the Loyalist/AESC corridor as a regionalised premium pool. Bear: Phase-2 carve-out below 100% (8% probability per T3 scenarios) which would erode the premium and accelerate B1's commoditisation case.

T3 dated gate: USTR Section 301 overcapacity-investigation remedy by 24 July 2026. If remedy extends Section 301 coverage upstream (pCAM, separators, processing equipment) or to transshipment corridors (Korea, Mexico) - supportive for B1 because it widens the wall around Loyalist's North American supply position. If investigation closed without remedy - mildly negative for B1's regional underwriting.

T3 dated gate: 10 November 2026 Busan / MOFCOM-70 expiry. Either snap-back of MOFCOM 55-62 (rare-earth / permanent-magnet / battery-graphite controls) which is mildly supportive for Western suppliers - or Phase-2 framework deal with carve-outs below 100% which is the only T3 outcome that materially erodes B1's regional position. T3 central path: 0.55 'structural decoupling holds with extension' + 0.20 'Busan renewed status quo' = 0.75 cumulative supportive of B1.

theme_dependencies.theme_trajectory: persistent / mildly widening on molecule + carbon, asymmetrically relieved by Germany only

transmission_mechanisms.reading_rule: T2 propagation rule for B1: 'Olen (Belgian Co/Ni refining) and Nysa (Polish CAM) sit OUTSIDE German Industriestrompreis relief.' German competitors benefit from EUR 50/MWh capped power (5 ct/kWh, 91 sectors) for 50% of consumption from 2026; Belgian + Polish + Finnish nodes pay market rates. -0.5 magnitude per quarter the gap persists; +0.5 if Belgian / Polish equivalent state-aid scheme matching German EUR 50/MWh approved.

T2 propagation rule for B1: '-1 magnitude per +EUR 10/MWh in TTF above EUR 50 sustained one quarter; -0.5 if Polish industrial power above PLN 600/MWh sustained; -0.5 if EU ETS sustained above EUR 100/tCO2'. Current state: TTF EUR 47.4/MWh (rebound on Iran blockade), EU ETS EUR 75.5/tCO2 (rallying), Polish industrial power PLN 540/MWh with cogeneration fee +50% YoY. Composite cost-floor lift visible at exactly the moment BMS needs break-even economics.

T2 named indicator: Belgian Joint Committee 111/207 chemical-sector indexation 2% step expected May/June 2026; cent-index cap reform 1 June 2026. Olen exposure direct. -0.3 magnitude if Belgian health-index YoY above +3.5% sustained. Indexation drag is mechanically additive on top of energy + carbon costs.

scenarios.name: central_path_partial_book_translation_proof_gate_marginally_clears
scenarios.narrative: 190 GWh 2027 contracted book translates into ~150-170 GWh of physical call-offs (Macro central case minus base slippage). IONWAY/PowerCo Salzgitter ramps with 6-9 month delay but no chemistry pivot. AESC Loyalist commissioning slips ~6 months past 2026 commitment but stabilises. EcoPro Debrecen Phase 2 (108 kt/yr) commissioned on schedule but compliant-CAM ASP premium holds at ~6-8% (T4 alternate path between erosion-via-localisation and persists-and-translates). EU Battery Passport (Feb 2027) delivers modest preferred-supplier procurement language at IONWAY + AESC. BMS exits FY2027 at -EUR 30 to -EUR 60m EBIT loss (improvement from -EUR 91m), Q3 2027 proof-gate marginally clears via positive segment FCF on the shipped sub-set of volumes (auditor verification on the AESC + Korean premium NMC sub-book), FY2028 EBIT prints between EUR -20m and EUR +30m. EUR 2.0bn capital ceiling held; no second impairment. Foundation cushion holds (gold + PGM stable, Catalysis hits durability target).
scenarios.what_makes_this_path_break: Korean cell-maker LFP second-wave conversion (T1 bear) firing in 2027-2028; OR PowerCo restructuring stranding IONWAY capex; OR Treasury extending impracticable-to-trace transition (compresses regional ASP premium below 5%); OR Belgian indexation + Polish power composite lifting unit cost floor by EUR 10-15/MWh-equivalent over 2026-2027 (T2 escalation).

scenarios.name: slow_burn_proof_gate_missed_partial_divestiture
scenarios.narrative: The most likely path. Contracted-book slippage runs at upper end of Macro 6-18 month range; 2027 call-offs land closer to 130-150 GWh. T6 premium-NMC slice continues to compress 5-10pp below aggregate BEV (BMW BEV+PHEV remains -30 to -50% YoY range, Mercedes/Audi BEV-mix 18% vs 22% guidance). Korean cell-maker LFP-line conversions continue (LG, Samsung SDI add second wave). Asian compliant-capacity (EcoPro 108 kt/yr + LG Chem TN ramp + POSCO Future M Quebec) fills 250+ kt/yr inside the perimeter by year-end 2027 - compliant-CAM ASP premium compresses to <5% (T4 erosion-via-Asian-localisation scenario fires). BMS prints third year of EUR -50 to -120m EBIT through FY2027. Q3 2027 proof-gate is missed (positive segment FCF not demonstrable on full book). Board fires structured partnership / partial divestiture process. Second impairment of EUR 400-800m recognised against the EUR 2.0bn capital base. BMS continues at smaller scale anchored on AESC/Loyalist + Korean premium NMC niche (~80-130 GWh effective realised volume). FY2028 EBIT prints EUR -100 to -50m.
scenarios.what_makes_this_path_break: AESC/Loyalist commissions ahead of schedule with full FEOC compliance; OR Treasury hard-edge tracing guidance (T4 enforcement-teeth episode) delivers a 15-25% ASP premium that mechanically widens BMS conversion margin; OR BMW Neue Klasse Phase 2 beats plan and Mercedes EQS-replacement confirms high-nickel NMC for full range (T1 bull). Any of those pulls the path toward the central case.

scenarios.name: chemistry_cliff_book_renegotiated_bms_re_scoped
scenarios.narrative: T1 bear scenario fires: BMW Neue Klasse follow-on or Audi PPE-2 confirms LFP/LMFP for any non-entry trim before 2028; Mercedes shifts long-range CLA / EQS-replacement strategy toward Korean-LFP; VW PowerCo accelerates LFP at Valencia from H2 2026 instead of 2027. Korean cell-makers reverse NMC line capex. T6 premium-NMC slice compresses sharply (BMW BEV/PHEV CY2026 below 18% guidance, S&P Mobility 2027 Western BEV production cut >15%). 270 GWh 2030 book is renegotiated POSCO/GM/Ultium-style at scale. IONWAY capex partially stranded; ACC offtake renegotiated; SK On book shrinks via LFP redirect. BMS formally re-scoped to 'surviving offtakes' (AESC/Loyalist + Korean residual premium NMC ~50-80 GWh effective). Second impairment of EUR 700-1,200m. Q3 2027 proof-gate decisively missed; structured partnership or partial sale executed by year-end 2027. Foundation cushion still funds the wind-down. T1 TL re-rates toward 0.92.
scenarios.what_makes_this_path_break: Premium-OEM chemistry decisions consolidate one-way around high-nickel NMC (T1 bull move-up triggers); OR T3 escalation pulls Western premium pool back via tariff intensification on pCAM/separators (USTR overcapacity July 2026 remedy + MOFCOM-70 expiry without renewal). Even on these triggers, the chemistry decisions are typically locked 18-24 months in advance, so a true cliff path has limited reversibility within the 2027-2028 window.

scenarios.name: regulatory_underwriting_premium_widens_marginal_break_even
scenarios.narrative: T4 enforcement-teeth episode fires: Treasury allows impracticable-to-trace clean expiry end-2026 AND issues hard-edge FEOC tracing guidance Q1 2027; first DG GROW Battery Passport penalty cases in H2 2027; DFARS Section 842 implementing rule pulled forward to 2027. Compliant-CAM ASP premium widens to 12-20%. T3 USTR overcapacity remedy (24 July 2026) extends Section 301 coverage upstream to pCAM and to transshipment corridors, narrowing Asian-localiser arbitrage. Combined effect: BMS conversion margin widens 6-10pp on regional book; offsets 6-12 month customer slippage. IONWAY ramps as scheduled; AESC delivers near 2026 commitment; Korean premium NMC niche stabilises. BMS prints FY2027 EBIT in EUR -20 to +20m range; Q3 2027 proof-gate clears with margin; FY2028 prints EUR +30 to +90m. EUR 2.0bn ceiling held; foundation cushion strong.
scenarios.what_makes_this_path_break: Treasury extends impracticable-to-trace (T4 alternate scenario 2 fires); Phase-2 Busan-2 carve-out below 100% on CAM (T3 alternate scenario fires); LFP migration into premium ahead of regulatory tailwind realisation; Belgian/Polish energy stack escalation absorbs the premium widening on the cost side.

scenarios.name: tail_governance_failure_premature_kill
scenarios.narrative: Foundation cushion compresses before Q3 2027: gold corrects to USD 1,800-2,000/oz (CFO stress-test reference) AND Catalysis hits unexpected ROCE wall (early premium-OEM ICE programme loss or PGM substitution acceleration) AND/OR Hoboken EUR 400m capex slides materially. Patience capital runs out. Board fires kill criteria pre-emptively in 2026-Q1 2027 to protect group ROCE. BMS is forced into structured partnership / sale BEFORE the proof-gate matures - effectively conceding the bet without testing the contracted-book translation. T2 second-energy-shock concurrent (TTF >EUR 60 sustained, ETS >EUR 100/tCO2) exacerbates. Second impairment EUR 800-1,500m. BMS effectively wound down by FY2028 with residual AESC tolling structure.
scenarios.what_makes_this_path_break: Foundation cushion holds (gold + PGM + Catalysis margins) AND board declines to fire kill criteria pre-emptively. The path is conditional on simultaneous foundation-side stress, which has only modest probability in an 18-30 month window.

named_watch_metrics.metric: Quarterly BMS segment free cash flow (post-implementation of segment-level FCF disclosure target Q1 2026 update on 24 April 2026)
named_watch_metrics.source: Umicore quarterly trading updates and half-year results; cross-checked against group FCF reconciliation per Convergence stream demand
named_watch_metrics.current_value: Not separately disclosed today; Convergence stream prescribed implementation starting Q1 2026 trading update on 24 April 2026. Reverse-derivation from FY2025 EUR -91m EBIT + EUR 2.0bn capital + ~EUR 261m WC build implies negative FCF.
named_watch_metrics.threshold_implications: Bull: BMS segment FCF prints positive for two consecutive quarters before Q3 2027 proof-gate (clears CEO non-negotiable). Bear: BMS segment FCF prints below EUR -80m in any single quarter through 2026-2027 OR full-year FY2026 BMS FCF below EUR -300m (breaches implicit capital ceiling glide-path). Critical gate: positive BMS segment FCF for the four-quarter rolling window ending Q3 2027 = bet-truth resolution. Note: implementation itself is a leading governance signal; non-implementation by Q3 2026 is bear.

named_watch_metrics.metric: Nysa CAM utilisation rate and IONWAY/PowerCo Salzgitter call-off volumes
named_watch_metrics.source: Umicore quarterly disclosures (plant-level utilisation if disclosed); Benchmark CAM Daily; PowerCo/VW Group Q-call commentary on Salzgitter ramp; IONWAY governance review (60-day Convergence-prescribed review)
named_watch_metrics.current_value: Position-map estimate: 50-65% utilisation across Western non-Chinese NMC-CAM nameplate; Umicore-specific Nysa data not separately published; H1 2025 status 'gradual ramp-up'; PowerCo Salzgitter cell-plant in commissioning
named_watch_metrics.threshold_implications: Bull: Nysa utilisation prints >70% sustained for two consecutive quarters with named IONWAY call-offs at ~70% of contracted-schedule cadence. Bear: Nysa utilisation prints <50% in any quarter through 2026-2027, OR PowerCo Salzgitter ramp slips >9 months past current schedule (T6 escalation trigger fires), OR IONWAY 60-day review surfaces material termination-protection gaps.

named_watch_metrics.metric: AESC North American factory commissioning schedule and Loyalist Ontario production ramp
named_watch_metrics.source: AESC press releases; Nissan/Renault group disclosures; Umicore Loyalist site updates; Section 232 / FEOC compliance filings; USMCA rules-of-origin documentation
named_watch_metrics.current_value: Loyalist site in planned/ramp status; AESC long-tenor 10-year agreement 2026-2035 with ~50 GWh annual ramp ambition by decade-end; AESC parent ownership currently Envision
named_watch_metrics.threshold_implications: Bull: AESC plant achieves first commercial cell production in 2026 with Umicore CAM share confirmed at >90%, Loyalist hits operational milestones on or near schedule. Bear: AESC slips >12 months past 2026 ramp commitment (Macro flag: 'credible second-impairment trigger') OR AESC parent Envision restructuring affects offtake terms OR USMCA rules-of-origin exposes Loyalist-to-US flows to duty stack.

named_watch_metrics.metric: Compliant-CAM ASP premium vs Chinese-origin equivalent (Platts CAM Daily index proxy)
named_watch_metrics.source: Platts CAM Daily (Korea/Japan-domiciled NMC vs China-origin); Fastmarkets Battery Materials; Umicore/BASF/LG Chem/EcoPro Q-call ASP commentary

named_watch_metrics.threshold_implications: Bull: sustained >15% premium across two consecutive quarters (T4 enforcement-teeth episode realising). Bear: sustained <5% premium for >2 quarters (T4 erosion scenario - moat-depth collapse fires; B1 conversion margin compresses below break-even structurally). The Q3 2027 proof-gate path mathematically requires the premium to stay above ~5%.

named_watch_metrics.threshold_implications: Bull: Korean cell-makers announce >EUR 500m of named external Western NMC CAM offtake in any 12-month window (T1 named-watch threshold). Bear: second wave of LFP-line conversions in 2027-2028 (T1 bear threshold) - direct read-across to Loyalist + Korea CAM base + SK On offtake resilience; this is the single strongest leading indicator of the chemistry-cliff scenario firing.

Bear: NMC mix <55% in any single quarter OR premium OEM BEV-mix CY2026 reported below 18% blended OR any premium-OEM (BMW, Audi, Mercedes, Porsche, Volvo) adopts LFP/LMFP for non-entry trim before 2028.

190 GWh 2027 / 270 GWh 2030 contracted-book translation to realised call-offs by customer (IONWAY/PowerCo, AESC, ACC, SK On) with slippage tracking
Nysa + Korea CAM base utilisation rate and unit-economics convergence on European premium-NMC
AESC North American ramp execution and Loyalist Ontario commissioning under Section 232/FEOC compliance
Compliant-CAM ASP premium evolution vs Asian compliant-capacity ramp (EcoPro Hungary, LG Chem Tennessee, POSCO Future M Quebec)
Premium-OEM platform chemistry decisions through 2028 (BMW Neue Klasse follow-on, Audi PPE-2, Mercedes EQS replacement, Porsche SSP, Volvo SPA3) and Korean cell-maker LFP-line second-wave watch
falsification_criteria: Q3 2027 proof-gate (positive BMS segment FCF on shipped volumes per independent commercial audit) is decisively missed AND a structured partnership / partial divestiture process is initiated by year-end 2027 - the bet's load-bearing readable signal failing.

BMS segment FCF prints below EUR -80m in any single quarter through 2026-2027 OR full-year FY2026 BMS FCF below EUR -300m, breaching the implicit ~EUR 2.0bn capital ceiling glide-path before the proof-gate matures.
Second impairment of EUR 400m+ recognised against the EUR 2.0bn capital base before FY2028 (Macro flags EUR 400-800m as base-case bear, not tail risk).

Any premium-OEM (BMW, Audi, Mercedes, Porsche, Volvo) adopts LFP/LMFP for non-entry trim before 2028 (T1 bear threshold) AND/OR Korean cell-makers (LG ES, Samsung SDI, SK On) announce a second wave of LFP-line conversions in 2027-2028 - confirming the chemistry-cliff path on the supply side.

Compliant-CAM ASP premium prints sustained <5% for >2 quarters per Platts CAM Daily proxy AND/OR Treasury extends 'impracticable-to-trace' transition rule beyond end-2026 - moat-depth collapse making break-even mathematically unreachable on the contracted book.

VW PowerCo material restructuring affecting IONWAY JV terms OR AESC parent (Envision) ownership transition stranding the long-tenor supply agreement OR SK On book shrinks via formal LFP redirect on Hyundai-Kia / Ford BlueOval programmes - any single major customer-side break of the four-customer concentration.
Nysa utilisation prints <50% in any quarter through 2026-2027 OR PowerCo Salzgitter ramp slips >9 months past current schedule (T6 escalation trigger).

Foundation cushion compresses before Q3 2027: gold corrects to USD 1,800-2,000/oz AND/OR Catalysis ROCE moves materially below 20% AND/OR Hoboken EUR 400m capex slides materially - patience capital runs out before proof-gate, board fires kill criteria pre-emptively.

Board declines to formalise binding kill criteria by Q1 2027 AND/OR quarterly segment FCF disclosure not implemented by Q3 2026 - governance failure mode that allows indefinite 're-baselining' under guise of patience, which itself functionally falsifies the bet's binary-resolution mechanism.
bets.breakage_shape: When B1 breaks, it does NOT typically break sharply on a single date. The most likely break-shape is a 12-18 month slow grind: 2026 reports BMS revenue tracking ~10-15% below internal plan as IONWAY/PowerCo call-offs slip and AESC commissioning delays; FY2026 EBIT prints EUR -90 to -150m (no improvement on FY2025); compliant-CAM ASP indices show 4-8% premium not the 8-15% needed; Korean cell-maker LFP capex announcements continue (a second-wave headline lands in mid-2027); Q3 2027 proof-gate auditor verification finds positive segment FCF only on the AESC + Korean premium NMC sub-book (~30-50% of total volumes), not on the full book; board converts the structured-partnership clause into a formal carve-out / sale process for the IONWAY-tied European pCAM-CAM activities; second impairment of EUR 400-700m is recognised against capital employed; CORE plan is re-cast as 'Catalysis + Recycling + Specialty foundation focus with residual Battery Materials niche'. The sharper alternative break-shape (chemistry cliff) compresses this to 6-9 months around a single inflection event - typically a premium-OEM platform announcement (BMW Neue Klasse follow-on or Audi PPE-2 going LFP/LMFP for non-entry trim) cascading to renegotiation of the 270 GWh 2030 book within 12 weeks of disclosure. Either path culminates in BMS being formally re-scoped to surviving offtakes (typically AESC/Loyalist + Korean premium NMC) at ~50-130 GWh effective realised volume - materially below the 190 GWh 2027 / 270 GWh 2030 contracted-book size. The bet's own definition of 'true' (positive segment FCF AND positive Adjusted EBITA by FY2028 AND no kill-criteria event) is not satisfiable on a re-scoped book.

baseline_research_note.claim_under_test: BMS reaches Q3 2027 proof-gate clearance (positive segment free cash flow on shipped volumes per independent commercial audit) AND exits FY2024 EUR -91m EBITA loss / -19.5% margin to positive Adjusted EBITA by FY2028, all while staying inside the implicit EUR 2.0bn capital ceiling and avoiding a board kill-criteria event - i.e. the European premium-NMC CAM franchise (Olen pCAM + Nysa CAM + Korea CAM + offtake book to AESC/IONWAY/PowerCo/SK On + ACC) reaches contracted volumes (190 GWh 2027 / 270 GWh 2030) at margins that defend the EUR 400m+ capex base.
baseline_research_note.what_is_known_today: FY2025 BMS reported EUR 436m revenue (+13.0% YoY off the depressed FY2024 base, recovering some of the prior FY2024 -30% YoY collapse), EUR -91m Adjusted EBIT (-19.5% margin, EUR -84m corporate corporate-line drag separate), capital employed EUR 2,003m (56% of group), -4.8% ROCE; FY2024 EUR 1.7bn impairment already recognised (the prior NMC growth thesis abandoned). Contracted CAM book is 190 GWh 2027 / 270 GWh 2030 across IONWAY/PowerCo (cornerstone European JV, EUR ~500m committed by Umicore alongside Salzgitter cell plant), AESC (>90% CAM share for AESC's NA factories from 2026, 50 GWh annual run-rate by decade-end, anchored at Loyalist Ontario), ACC (Stellantis/Mercedes/TotalEnergies JV, multiple LFP signals), SK On (substantial portion of book, Korean cell-maker exposure to LFP conversion), plus the AESC long-tenor 10-year supply agreement 2026-2035. BMS chemistry concentration is nickel-rich NMC (622, 811, Ni-rich) plus manganese-rich and solid-state R&D - NO disclosed material LFP/LMFP position. pCAM is externally sourced from CNGR / Eco&Dream (~20 kT in-house European footprint plus IONWAY-tied co-refining at Kokkola). CORE plan March 2025 narrowed BMS capex to Nysa + Korea, deferred 150 kt EU recycling plant to 2032+, halved dividend EUR 0.80 -> EUR 0.25 (partially restored to EUR 0.50), delivered EUR 100m FY2025 efficiency. Foundation cushion EUR 679m FY2025 Adjusted EBIT (Catalysis 383 + Recycling 296) plus Specialty 76 = EUR 755m. CEO non-negotiable Q3 2027 proof-gate (positive segment FCF, independent commercial audit, structured partnership/exit if missed) - but board kill-criteria not yet formalised. Quarterly segment-level FCF disclosure unanimously demanded by Convergence debate, target Q1 2026 trading update on 24 April 2026. Macro central case: contracted-to-realised slippage of 6-18 months, 2027 landing closer to 150 GWh than 190 GWh, 2030 between 220-270 GWh.
baseline_research_note.contradictions_in_evidence: Several genuine cross-stream tensions exist. (1) Contracted-book vs realised volumes: strategic stream stresses the 190/270 GWh book and 8-10 year tenor as a strength; macro stream stresses that FY2024 revenue dropped 30% YoY despite the same book, that POSCO/GM/Ultium showed long-term cathode contracts CAN be sharply downsized, and that take-or-pay terms / cancellation penalties / minimum-volume floors are NOT publicly disclosed - so the book's enforceability is materially less certain than headline tenor implies. (2) Closed-loop CAM+Recycling moat: position-map pos_capability_integrated_data_traceability flags 'There is no moat, only table stakes' (Glencore, Li-Cycle, Aurubis, OEMs all pursuing similar circularity) but strategic stream lists 'integrated CAM + recycling supports circular supply and customer stickiness' as strength. The 150 kt EU recycling plant deferral to 2032+ confirms the macro view that the integrated story is partial today. (3) Chemistry diversification: position-map pos_segment_battery_materials_solutions explicitly notes 'a single-chemistry-family bet, NOT a multi-chemistry portfolio' and 'no material disclosed LFP/LMFP position'. The solid-state BMW/Solid Power partnership is option-value not core earnings (strategic A12 confidence rating 49%). (4) ASP-translation rate: T4 deep-read confirms the compliance moat is at widest legal aperture but ASP-translation is bifurcated - defence + ESG-fund channels pay premium but mass-market OEMs renegotiate; this contradicts any thesis that EU Battery Regulation alone delivers a price premium for compliant CAM. (5) Cost stack: T2 deep-read confirms Olen + Nysa sit outside the German Industriestrompreis relief, contradicting any thesis that EU localisation alone provides a cost-curve advantage; pCAM external dependence further dilutes the localisation benefit. (6) ROCE optics: pos_balance_sheet_gold_leaseback flags that the EUR 525m one-off gold sale flatters FY2025 cash flow and the leverage improvement; without it FY2025 cash conversion would have been visibly weaker - the foundation cushion's 'patience capital' is more fragile than headline numbers suggest.
open_questions: What is the current realised utilisation rate at Nysa and at the Korean CAM base (the position-map estimate is 50-65% across Western non-Chinese NMC-CAM nameplate, but Umicore-specific quarterly data is not disclosed)? The Q3 2027 proof-gate cannot mathematically clear without sustained European CAM utilisation above ~70%.

What share of the 190/270 GWh contracted book is firm-committed (take-or-pay / minimum-volume-floor protected) versus flexible? Position-map explicitly flags this as a transparency gap - resolving it would materially re-rate the book's enforceability and the prior on the bet.

Does the IONWAY JV with PowerCo include termination protections that survive a PowerCo restructuring or VW chemistry pivot? Convergence stream demanded a 60-day legal/commercial review; outcome not yet visible. EUR ~500m capital-at-risk.

What is Umicore's actual share of the contracted book that translates into IRA-30D-compliant (FEOC-clean) volumes? pCAM external dependence on CNGR (Chinese) creates an FEOC-adjacency risk for Loyalist/AESC volumes that depends on Treasury's impracticable-to-trace decision (Q4 2026 gate).

Is Bad Säckingen (German Catalysis site) eligible under the German Industriestrompreis 91-sector list? If yes, it widens the asymmetric intra-EU disadvantage for Belgian/Polish BMS sites; if no, it neutralises that channel. The EU Commission state-aid decisions database has not yet published site-level confirmations.

What is the FY2026 incremental BMS funding requirement (working capital + maintenance capex) if customer call-offs slip 6-18 months as Macro forecasts? The implicit ~EUR 2.0bn ceiling is cumulative; an additional EUR 100-300m in WC build before Q3 2027 would risk breaching the ceiling.

Has the board formalised binding kill criteria (Convergence debate Round 2 unanimous demand)? If not formalised before Q1 2027, the CEO's stated Q3 2027 proof-gate may not be enforceable as a kill mechanism - meaning BMS could continue burning capital under 're-baselining' framing rather than triggering structured partnership/exit.

What are the contractual terms of the AESC long-tenor 10-year supply agreement 2026-2035 - specifically AESC's parent ownership transition risk (currently Envision)? Long-tenor commitment is most concrete dated commitment in the book; counterparty health is the binding constraint.

adjudication_notes: T1's B2-labelled propagation entry ('CAM utilisation / Nysa break-even', weight 0.9, role load-bearing) was folded into B1's T1 theme_dependency as a SECOND transmission channel ('european_CAM_unit_utilisation_nysa_korea'). Justification: (a) all other themes (T2/T5/T6) consistently label B2 as Catalysis, but T1's 'CAM utilisation / Nysa break-even' is unambiguously BMS-domain framing about European NMC-CAM capacity utilisation across Nysa + Korea, not autocatalyst capacity in Florange/Bad Säckingen/Nowa Ruda; (b) the position-map's pos_strategic_commitment_q3_2027_proof_gate explicitly uses 'positive unit economics on shipped volumes' which IS the CAM utilisation / break-even gate; (c) Macro identifies Nysa plant-loading disclosure ('utilization percentage and break-even commentary') as the single most important leading indicator for BMS, not for Catalysis. Decision rule from B1 brief Step 2 review applied: treat as second B1 channel under T1, fold weight + reading rule under B1's T1 theme_dependency, surface explicitly here for the user. T1 effective coupling to B1 therefore spans both demand-side mix (premium NMC share) and supply-side utilisation (Nysa/Korea CAM).
factual_corrections: BMS chemistry portfolio is concentrated nickel-rich NMC (622, 811, Ni-rich variants) plus manganese-rich and solid-state R&D; NOT a multi-chemistry portfolio. Solid-state captured only via the BMW + Solid Power tail-hedge (pos_technology_solid_state_bmw_partnership), strategic A12 confidence rating 49% - option value, not core earnings.

pCAM is externally sourced from CNGR (Chinese) and Eco&Dream; Umicore retains ~20 kT in-house European footprint plus IONWAY-tied co-refining at Kokkola - pos_capability_pcam_external_dependence flags this as one of the reasons Right-to-Play scores only 'credible' (3/5) for BMS.

Closed-loop CAM+Recycling moat is overstated: 150 kt European battery recycling deferred to 2032+ under CORE plan; pos_capability_integrated_data_traceability flags 'There is no moat, only table stakes' (Glencore, Li-Cycle, Aurubis, OEMs all pursuing similar circularity).

Western cost curve is unverified: Olen (Belgium) and Nysa (Poland) sit OUTSIDE the German Industriestrompreis relief approved April 2026; the asymmetric national relief actively disadvantages BMS unit economics (T2 propagation rule).

CORE 'cash generation pivot' is not yet visible: FY2025 underlying FCF only EUR 145m; WC build EUR 261m; sub-20% FCF conversion; EUR 525m gold sale-and-leaseback flatters reported cash flow (pos_balance_sheet_gold_leaseback / Convergence earnings-quality verdict 'mixed').
Recycling 208% ROCE is artefactual (collapsed CE from gold sale-and-leaseback); normalised high-teens / mid-20s. Touches B1 only via the dividend / foundation cushion durability path.

bets.label: Catalysis cash-engine durability through FY2028

intermediate_gates.name: FY2026 Catalysis EBITA print + Bad Sackingen Industriestrompreis eligibility confirmation
intermediate_gates.what_resolves: First post-baseline annual print of the Catalysis cash engine. Bull confirmation: FY2026 revenue >= EUR 1,600m and EBITA >= EUR 360m at margin >= 22.5% (i.e. status-quo trajectory holds, with FY2025 EUR 1,668m / EUR 383m / 23.0% drifting only as far as the trajectory stream's year-1 path EUR 1,635m / EUR 376m / 23.0%). Concurrent confirmation by EU Commission state-aid decisions database that Bad Sackingen sits inside the German Industriestrompreis 91-sector list (mechanical engineering / specialty chemicals plausible) materially supports the German node vs Florange (France) and Nowa Ruda (Poland) which are excluded from German relief. Single highest-conviction year-1 readable signal because both inputs land in the same FY2026 results cycle.

intermediate_gates.name: OEM platform-life award decisions cycle (2026-H1 2027) for 2028-2033 ICE/HEV programmes
intermediate_gates.what_resolves: Catalysis cash flows from FY2028 to early 2030s are mechanically locked in by OEM platform-life award decisions taken in 2026-H1 2027. Bull: Umicore-led wins on at least one major BMW / Mercedes / VW Group / Stellantis / Toyota / Hyundai-Kia hybrid powertrain programme spanning 2028-2033 with confirmed EBITA contribution. Bear: visible market-share losses to Johnson Matthey post-dismemberment-restructure or to BASF ECMS carve-out on >= 2 platform tenders; Heraeus / Cataler / Solvay encroachment on Asian platforms. Resolution-true requires the three-way oligopoly framework to hold.

intermediate_gates.name: T6 mid-2027 hybrid-vs-pure-BEV crossover read (Cox quarterly + ACEA monthly)
intermediate_gates.what_resolves: Two-sided framing inflection point. Cox US HEV share readings across Q1-Q2 2027 averaging >= 19% (Toyota-driven hybrid-as-destination) is positive for Catalysis volume floor. Equivalently negative read: Cox HEV share averaging <= 14% with US BEV share recovering above 8% AND/OR ACEA EU BEV share averaging > 25% with HEV share falling below 35%. Either side of the inflection re-rates B2 forward projection probabilities materially because the central path requires hybrid-as-destination not as transition.

intermediate_gates.name: FY2027 Catalysis EBITA print + foundation cushion confirmation
intermediate_gates.what_resolves: Penultimate annual print before FY2028 resolution. The combined Catalysis + Recycling foundation cushion (EUR 679m FY2025 baseline) must remain intact: Catalysis EBITA in EUR 350-380m band per trajectory year-2 path (EUR 363m central) AND Recycling EBITA holding above EUR 250m (i.e. gold above EUR 3,500/oz threshold and PGM regime persists per T5). Combined foundation EBITA below EUR 600m for FY2027 is the cross-subsidy-math break point at which BMS tail-governance scenarios accelerate, per pos_balance_sheet_foundation_cushion analysis.

bets.prior_rationale: Six observable inputs anchor the prior clearly above 0.50. (1) FY2025 baseline is concretely strong: revenue EUR 1,668m, EBITA EUR 383m at 23.0% margin, ROCE 40.8%, contributing 66.1% of group segment EBITA on a largely depreciated brownfield footprint with low capex intensity (Florange, Bad Sackingen, Nowa Ruda in EU; Tulsa + Mexico in NA; Suzhou + Jiangmen in China; Korea + Thailand + India + Japan + Brazil) - this is a wasting asset with a defined endpoint, but the endpoint is into early 2030s not by FY2028. (2) Trajectory stream year-by-year status-quo path lands FY2028 at EUR 1,545m / EUR 347m EBITA / 22.5% margin / 38.3% ROCE - a -7% revenue decline and -EUR 36m EBITA delta versus FY2025, comfortably inside the +/-15% bet definition envelope (which would tolerate revenue down to EUR 1,418m and EBITA down to EUR 326m). (3) T6 (Western EV demand) prior 0.42 with two-sided trajectory is net-supportive for Catalysis: the load-bearing positive channel is hybrid persistence (EU HEV share 38.6% Q1 2026 ACEA, US Q4 2025 19.7% Cox peak with Q1 2026 14% reflecting volatility around a high baseline, Toyota Feb 2026 commitment to grow hybrid output ~30% to 6.7m units by 2028 - the single strongest signal that hybrid is a destination not a transition); T6 propagation rule for B2 is explicit '+1 magnitude when US HEV share above 19% sustained or EU HEV share above 38% sustained, -1 per 2pp acceleration in BEV share above 22% in EU' and the current-contribution figure is +0.3, supportive. (4) T5 (PGM regime) prior 0.70 with supportive_with_gold_cyclical_fragility trajectory delivers a meaningful Catalysis benefit via two channels: input-cost loop integration with Recycling (Hoboken complex-feed PGM loop is one of the operational moats explicitly cited in pos_technology_hoboken_complex_feed_loop) AND Pt-for-Pd substitution runway (JM 2025 PGM Market Report confirms palladium is moving from deficit to balance while platinum is in 4th consecutive year of deficit, and Catalysis benefits from Pt-for-Pd substitution because Pt loadings in TWC are increasing) - T5 propagation rule for B2 is +0.3 current contribution, supportive. (5) T2 (EU industrial cost) prior 0.66 with two-sided trajectory is asymmetrically relieving for the German Bad Sackingen node IF eligible under the 91-sector list (mechanical engineering / specialty chemicals plausible per position-map note) - this is a standing open question. T2 propagation rule for B2 is current contribution -0.1 (mildly negative on aggregate), with the asymmetry between Bad Sackingen (potentially relieved) vs Florange (France, no relief) and Nowa Ruda (Poland, no relief) being the active variable. (6) Regulatory tailwinds within window: Euro 7 content uplift flows through European platforms in 2026-2028 (incremental revenue per vehicle); EPA 2027 heavy-duty SCR demand provides additional pull for Tulsa; EU 2035 target diluted to 90% Dec 2025 means range-extenders, mild hybrids, PHEVs permitted past 2035 - all of which carry catalyst content. Mildly offsetting: chemistry transition acceleration on the BEV side (T6 escalation path 'premium_bev_demand_cliff' would compress Catalysis revenue floor faster than current 3-4% ICE production decline assumes, although in T6's own scoring this scenario sits at 25% probability and is partially offset by US hybrid lock-in at 20%); Russian PGM normalisation tail (T5 alternate scenario at 10% probability) could compress loaded-catalyst inventory values; PGM thrifting and Pt-for-Pd substitution running ahead of JM expectations could erode autocatalyst pricing power; three-way oligopoly competitive stress (JM dismemberment, BASF ECMS carve-out) could pull share. Net-net the prior settles at 0.60 - clearly above 0.50 because (a) the trajectory stream's status-quo year-by-year path lands FY2028 well inside the +/-15% bet envelope, (b) the +/-15% envelope is generous (allows EBITA to drop to EUR 326m vs EUR 347m central case), (c) the Catalysis runway is into early 2030s on management framing not 2028, (d) T5 + T6 combined contribute +0.6 magnitude per propagation rules vs T2's -0.1 - net +0.5 supportive, (e) pos_capability_efficiency_program_100m delivered EUR 100m EBITDA contribution FY2025 ahead of EUR 70m target with continuation pipeline, (f) the brownfield footprint is largely depreciated so capex intensity is low (cash conversion per unit of EBITA is structurally higher than for BMS). The 0.60 prior reflects the asymmetry inverse to B1: B2 has many quiet ways to clear the gate (ICE decline at the lower end of 2-4%, hybrid persistence above 19%, Euro 7 content uplift, oligopoly stability, Bad Sackingen relief eligibility) and a smaller set of break paths (premium-BEV cliff faster than expected, Russian PGM normalisation shock, OEM-share loss on platform tenders, Catalysis-specific impairment on technology obsolescence). The prior is below 0.70 because two-sided risk is genuine - this bet is unique in B-set in that BOTH too-fast pure-BEV adoption AND too-slow HEV adoption hurt - and because the load-bearing role for the foundation cushion means even a partial break (Catalysis EBITA in EUR 270-320m band vs +/-15% envelope-floor of EUR 326m) cascades into BMS tail-governance scenarios firing faster.
bets.thesis_resolves_true: By FY2028 Catalysis reports revenue within +/-15% of the FY2025 EUR 1,668m baseline (i.e. EUR 1,418-1,918m, with the trajectory-stream central path EUR 1,545m comfortably inside) AND EBITA within +/-15% of the EUR 383m baseline (i.e. EUR 326-440m, with central path EUR 347m comfortably inside) AND EBITA margin within +/-15% of 23.0% (i.e. 19.6-26.5%, with central path 22.5% comfortably inside). The path requires: (i) global ICE+HEV light-vehicle production declines no faster than the depreciation schedule of the global Catalysis footprint - i.e. roughly 3-4% CAGR not 6-8% CAGR through 2028 (per trajectory stream's key_drivers_held_constant); (ii) hybrid share holds near 19-20% of US LDV mix (Cox quarterly, supported by Toyota's 6.7m unit commitment) AND EU HEV share holds at 35-39% (ACEA monthly) - hybrids still require autocatalysts, so two-sided protection here is the Catalysis volume-floor mechanism; (iii) Euro 7 content uplift flows through European platforms as scheduled, supporting revenue per vehicle through 2026-2028; (iv) EPA 2027 heavy-duty SCR demand pulls Tulsa volumes; (v) gasoline catalyst share gains continue at the current modest pace, partially offsetting industry volume loss; (vi) the three-way oligopoly framework (Umicore + JM + BASF + Heraeus + Cataler) holds without major share loss to a restructured JM Catalyst Technologies (post-Honeywell deal) or BASF ECMS spin; (vii) PGM regime stays supportive (T5 prior 0.70) with no Russian PGM normalisation shock that would mark down loaded-catalyst inventory values, AND with Pt-for-Pd substitution continuing as a tailwind not running so fast that JM 2027 PGM Market Report flips platinum to balanced/surplus before FY2028 (T5 escalation 'pgm_thrifting_and_substitution_bites' has limited time-to-bite within this window); (viii) Bad Sackingen secures Industriestrompreis eligibility OR EU Clean Industrial Deal symmetric relief is adopted, neutralising the asymmetric national disadvantage for Florange (France) and Nowa Ruda (Poland); (ix) PEM fuel cell catalyst (pos_technology_pem_fuel_cell_catalyst) remains contained as option-value capex (modest in absolute terms per macro framing) without forcing material write-downs that would break the foundation-cushion EBITA arithmetic; (x) the foundation cushion EUR 679m FY2025 (Catalysis 383 + Recycling 296) holds combined above EUR 600m through FY2027 to underwrite BMS patience capital. Resolution-true does NOT require Catalysis to grow - the bet is calibrated to harvest-mode durability inside +/-15% bands, consistent with the segment's role as cash_engine_to_harvest under CORE.
bets.thesis_resolves_false: The bet resolves false in any of three structurally different ways. Most likely: a slow-burn erosion that breaches the -15% EBITA floor (EBITA below EUR 326m for FY2028 / margin below 19.6%) caused by an acceleration of the ICE production decline curve from 3-4% CAGR toward 6-8% CAGR - either because EU 2035 trilogue holds the 90% line firmly (T6 deescalation 'regulatory_recommitment') accelerating BEV pull AND US hybrid share fails to lock in above 19% (T6 escalation 'premium_bev_demand_cliff' fires while 'us_hybrid_lock_in' does not). In this slow-burn path, Catalysis FY2028 lands at EUR 1,300-1,400m revenue / EUR 270-310m EBITA / 19-21% margin, which still leaves the segment cash-generative but breaches the +/-15% bet envelope and forces foundation-cushion arithmetic below the BMS cross-subsidy threshold. Less likely but more abrupt: a chemistry-side cliff where premium-OEM platform-life award decisions in the 2026-H1 2027 window go materially against Umicore on 2028-2033 ICE+HEV programmes (BMW Neue Klasse follow-on emphasising BEV-only platforms cutting hybrid-platform attach rates, Mercedes EQS-replacement platform de-emphasising hybrid, VW Group consolidating MEB+ on BEV-only, Toyota awarding North American hybrid catalyst growth to Cataler / BASF rather than Umicore) - share-loss not volume-loss as the dominant channel, with FY2028 revenue compressing 18-25% and EBITA falling to EUR 240-300m. Tail-but-non-trivial: PGM-loop disruption where (a) Russian PGM normalisation shock (T5 escalation, 10% probability) compresses Pt and Pd prices simultaneously, marking down loaded-catalyst inventory values and unwinding the integrated Catalysis-Recycling working-capital cushion; OR (b) PGM thrifting and substitution running materially faster than JM expects (T5 escalation 'pgm_thrifting_and_substitution_bites') with Toyota / Honda announcing hybrid Pt-loading reduction step-change before FY2028 - this would compress Catalysis pricing power and revenue per vehicle simultaneously. In any of these false paths, the cascade into BMS is direct: foundation-cushion compression below EUR 600m combined accelerates the BMS tail-governance scenario (B1's 'tail_governance_failure_premature_kill', currently 0.05 probability in B1) into the 0.10-0.15 range and may force board kill criteria pre-emptively before BMS Q3 2027 proof-gate matures.

transmission_mechanisms.reading_rule: T6 propagation rule for B2 is explicit two-sided: '+1 magnitude when US HEV share above 19% sustained or EU HEV share above 38% sustained (hybrid is a Catalysis-positive destination); -1 magnitude per 2pp acceleration in BEV share above 22% in EU because that compresses ICE+HEV catalyst demand floor faster than current Catalysis depreciation schedule assumes.' Current Q1 2026 readings: ACEA EU HEV 38.6% (above threshold, supportive), Cox US HEV 14% Q1 2026 vs 19.7% Q4 2025 peak (volatile around 17% midpoint, marginally below threshold). Toyota's Feb 2026 commitment to grow hybrid output ~30% to 6.7m units by 2028 (~60% of Toyota total production) is the strongest single anchor for the hybrid-as-destination thesis. T6 current_contribution to B2 is +0.3 in T6's propagation_to_bets_seed.

Reading S&P Global Mobility Light Vehicle Production Forecasts and ACEA / Cox monthly registrations splits. Bear: cumulative S&P Western LV production cuts >300k 2026-2027 combined AND ICE+HEV production decline accelerating from 3-4% CAGR to 6-8% CAGR through 2028. Bull: ICE+HEV production decline at lower end of 2-3% CAGR with hybrid lock-in above 22% US sustained (T6 escalation 'us_hybrid_lock_in' fires positively for B2). The trajectory stream's central path explicitly assumes 'global ICE light-vehicle production declining at roughly 3-4% CAGR through 2028' as a key driver held constant - the pace assumption is the single biggest variable under this channel.

T6 escalation path 'premium_bev_demand_cliff' (T6 named-watch indicator: BMW BEV+PHEV CY2026 mix reported below 18% vs 22% guidance) cuts both ways for B2 - bad if premium-OEM customers re-baseline toward fully BEV-only follow-on platforms that don't carry hybrid-catalyst attach rates, neutral-to-good if it accelerates hybrid retention as a 'safer' interim platform. Current Q1 2026 print: BMW BEV+PHEV US -50% YoY, Mercedes EU BEV +7% off low base, Audi/VW BEV-mix repeatedly cut - consistent with hybrid-shift-not-BEV-shift, which is supportive for B2.

EU Commission Dec 2025 decision diluting 2035 target from 100% to 90% (range-extenders, mild hybrids, PHEVs permitted past 2035 with low-carbon fuels offsetting residual 10%) is structurally Catalysis-positive because it preserves the catalyst content-per-vehicle on a meaningful slice of post-2030 fleet. Bull: EU trilogue under Cypriot Presidency (Jan 2026 onwards) further dilutes target below 90% with PHEVs explicitly counted as compliant. Bear: EU trilogue tightens back toward 100% with binding 2030 ratchet (T6 deescalation 'regulatory_recommitment') - this is the load-bearing risk that consensus under-weights for B2. Treat trilogue outcome as a material gate event before FY2028.

T5 propagation rule for B2 is '+0.5 magnitude when integrated Catalysis-Recycling PGM loop benefits from refiner-side feedstock access advantage' with current_contribution +0.3. Hoboken complex-feed PGM loop (pos_technology_hoboken_complex_feed_loop) is one of few complex-feed PGM smelter-refinery assets globally; spent autocatalysts are collected -> Hoboken refining -> Catalysis re-formulation. Bull: WPIC 2027 forecast extending Pt deficit to 5th consecutive year above 200 koz; Aurubis Q3-Q4 2025/26 refining-margin metal-result share holding above 40%. Bear: WPIC mid-2026 quarterly REVISING DEFICIT DOWN with Pt market flipping to balanced/surplus; Aurubis metal-results gross-margin share falling below 30%.

T5 propagation rule for B2 includes '+0.5 when Pt-for-Pd substitution still has runway (current state)' - reflecting that JM 2025 PGM Market Report shows palladium moving from deficit (2012-2024) to balance while platinum stays in 4th consecutive year of deficit. Pt-for-Pd substitution in TWC is a Catalysis-positive cost optimisation lever. Bull: JM May 2026 PGM Market Report extending Pt deficit horizon to 2028 with Pd maintaining tightness. Bear: T5 escalation 'pgm_thrifting_and_substitution_bites' fires - Toyota / Honda announcing hybrid Pt-loading reduction step-change OR DOE Hydrogen Shot achieving USD 2/kg with sub-0.5 mg/cm2 PGM loading - this would compress Catalysis pricing power.

T5 escalation 'russian_pgm_normalisation_shock' (10% probability per T5 scenarios): Ukraine ceasefire framework + OFAC sectoral relief allows Norilsk to restore palladium (current Q1 2026 -18% YoY) and platinum (Q1 2026 -26% YoY) flows to Western markets; Pd reverts toward USD 1,000/oz, Pt below USD 1,500/oz. Direct read-across to B2: loaded-catalyst inventory values mark down across the integrated PGM loop; pos_capability_metal_management_hedging is exposed (FY2025 WC build EUR 261m was the visible failure mode). Bear threshold: any combination of (a) Ukraine ceasefire framework agreed at Geneva or Istanbul level, (b) OFAC sectoral relief for Russian non-ferrous metals, (c) LME re-listing of Russian PGM warrants.

T5 escalation 'gold_correction_to_2500_eur_oz' transmits to B2 indirectly via the foundation cushion arithmetic (Recycling EBITDA compresses EUR 70-100m per EUR 500/oz of decline below the EUR 3,500/oz threshold). Direct B2 read-across is small but material via the foundation-cushion role: a gold correction that pulls Recycling EBITDA below EUR 250m for FY2027 combined with even mild Catalysis erosion below EUR 350m EBITA breaches the EUR 600m foundation floor that underwrites BMS patience capital.

theme_dependencies.theme_trajectory: persistent / mildly widening on molecule + carbon, asymmetrically relieved by Germany only

transmission_mechanisms.reading_rule: T2 propagation rule for B2 is current_contribution -0.1 with explicit asymmetry: 'Catalysis EU sites (Florange, Bad Sackingen, Nowa Ruda) are exposed to: (i) German Industriestrompreis ENABLES Bad Sackingen if eligible under the 91-sector list (mechanical engineering / specialty chemicals plausible), creating relative advantage vs Florange (France) and Nowa Ruda (Poland); (ii) ETS / CBAM enforcement supportive on imported-catalyst competition.' Reading rule: '+0.5 if Bad Sackingen confirmed eligible under Industriestrompreis; -0.5 if Polish Nowa Ruda site loses cost competitiveness via combined power + LCI moves; +0.5 if Clean Industrial Deal symmetric relief adopted; -0.5 magnitude per second energy shock'. EU Commission state-aid decisions database has not yet published site-level confirmations - this is a load-bearing open question for B2.

Catalysis EU pyrometallurgical and chemical processing energy intensity is meaningful (less than Hoboken refining but not zero). Composite cost-floor lift visible: TTF EUR 47.4/MWh May 2026 (rebound on Iran blockade), EU ETS EUR 75.5/tCO2 (highest since Feb 2026, +13% in 4 weeks), CBAM Q1 2026 reference price EUR 75.36/tCO2. T2 reading rule for B2: -0.5 magnitude per second energy shock realised; +0.5 if Bad Sackingen Industriestrompreis confirmed; CBAM is supportive on competitive side (penalises non-EU imported catalyst material) but cash impact pushed to Feb 2027.

CBAM permanent phase started 1 Jan 2026 at EUR 75.36/cert is supportive on the competitive side for EU Catalysis sites because it penalises non-EU imported catalyst material entering the EU. Bull: CBAM Q3/Q4 2026 reference price holds above EUR 80 (competitive moat deepens). Bear: DG TAXUD bulletin flagging material enforcement gaps OR major export-rule postponement. Net effect on B2 is positive but small in magnitude vs the Bad Sackingen relief variable.

scenarios.name: central_path_status_quo_trajectory_holds_within_envelope
scenarios.narrative: The trajectory stream's status-quo path lands FY2028 at EUR 1,545m / EUR 347m EBITA / 22.5% margin / 38.3% ROCE - comfortably inside the +/-15% bet envelope. Global ICE light-vehicle production declines at the assumed 3-4% CAGR (T6 'bifurcated_holding' scenario at 40% probability dominates). EU HEV share holds above 35% (ACEA), US HEV share averages 17-19% across 2026-2028 with Cox quarterly cycle volatility absorbed (Toyota's 6.7m unit hybrid commitment to 2028 anchors the destination thesis). Euro 7 content uplift flows through European platforms as scheduled from H2 2027. EPA 2027 heavy-duty SCR demand pulls Tulsa volumes. Three-way oligopoly framework holds with Umicore retaining share on at least one major BMW / Mercedes / VW / Toyota platform-life award in 2026-H1 2027. PGM regime supportive (T5 'regime_holds_supportive' at 50% probability) - Pt deficit 5th consecutive year, Pt-for-Pd substitution continuing as a tailwind, integrated Catalysis-Recycling PGM loop fully functional, Hoboken 2026 maintenance shutdown absorbed without lasting feedstock disruption. Bad Sackingen Industriestrompreis eligibility resolved positively in 2026 (mechanical engineering / specialty chemicals confirmed in 91-sector list); Florange and Nowa Ruda absorb mild T2 cost pressure but offset by the EUR 100m FY2025 efficiency program delivering continuation effects. Foundation cushion holds combined Catalysis + Recycling above EUR 600m through FY2027 - underwrites BMS patience capital through Q3 2027 proof-gate per B1 thesis.
scenarios.what_makes_this_path_break: EU 2035 trilogue tightens back toward 100% with binding 2030 ratchet (T6 deescalation 'regulatory_recommitment') accelerating BEV pull faster than hybrid-as-destination supports; OR Toyota CY2027 hybrid production prints below 5.5m units (hybrid-as-transition not destination); OR major platform-life award loss to JM Catalyst Technologies post-restructuring on >=2 BMW/Mercedes/VW programmes; OR Russian PGM normalisation shock (T5 alternate scenario 10% probability) marking down loaded-catalyst inventory.

scenarios.name: hybrid_lock_in_durability_overshoots_central_path
scenarios.narrative: T6 escalation 'us_hybrid_lock_in' fires positively for B2: Cox quarterly US HEV share locks in above 22% sustained for 3 consecutive quarters (Toyota / Honda / Hyundai-Kia hybrid waitlists extend; gas prices at EIA 2026 forecast USD 3.70/gal sustained through Middle East risk premium); EU HEV share 38-42% range (ACEA); Toyota CY2027 hybrid production above 5.5m units confirms hybrid-as-destination. EU 2035 trilogue under Cypriot Presidency further dilutes target toward 80-85% with PHEVs explicitly counted as compliant - Catalysis content-per-vehicle preserved on a wider slice of post-2030 fleet. ICE+HEV production decline at lower end of 2-3% CAGR. Catalysis FY2028 prints EUR 1,600-1,720m revenue / EUR 380-415m EBITA / 23-24% margin - bet clears comfortably AND foundation cushion strengthens (EUR 700m+ combined Catalysis + Recycling FY2027) underwriting BMS proof-gate path with margin. Pt-for-Pd substitution continues as tailwind without thrifting acceleration. Three-way oligopoly stable. The path is conditional on simultaneous T6 'us_hybrid_lock_in' AND T5 'regime_holds_supportive' AND favourable EU regulatory-rollback - which is upside-skewed probability not modal.
scenarios.what_makes_this_path_break: EU 2035 target tightens back toward 100% (T6 deescalation 'regulatory_recommitment'); OR US gas prices fall sustained below USD 3.20/gal removing the fuel-cost EV/HEV switching driver (T6 escalation 'us_hybrid_lock_in' named-trigger 'EIA US gasoline 2027 forecast revised below USD 3.20/gal'); OR Pt-for-Pd substitution running ahead of JM expectations and triggering 'pgm_thrifting_and_substitution_bites' scenario (T5).

scenarios.name: premium_bev_pull_forward_compresses_floor_within_envelope
scenarios.narrative: T6 escalation 'premium_bev_demand_cliff' fires (25% probability in T6's own scoring) but with the offsetting hybrid mechanism still partially absorbing displaced volume rather than wholesale BEV-only conversion. BMW Neue Klasse iX3/iX5/iX7 ramp ahead of plan, Mercedes EQ-replacement platform delivers range/charging parity at scale, Audi A6 e-tron / Q6 e-tron PPE platform achieving scale - premium-OEM CY2026 BEV mix exceeds 22% guidance. PowerCo Salzgitter ramp on schedule. Premium platform attach rate for autocatalysts compresses faster than central path assumes (10% revenue decline by FY2028 not 7%). BUT US hybrid share holds at 17-19% (Cox), absorbing displaced mass-market volumes not premium. ICE+HEV production decline accelerates from 3-4% CAGR toward 5% CAGR. Catalysis FY2028 prints EUR 1,420-1,500m revenue / EUR 320-345m EBITA / 21.5-22.5% margin - bet clears at the lower end of the +/-15% envelope; foundation cushion compresses to EUR 580-620m combined (Catalysis + Recycling) which sits at the BMS cross-subsidy break point.
