T6 trajectory drifts from 0.42 today toward 0.35-0.38.
scenarios.what_makes_this_path_break: Hybrid-as-destination thesis weakens (Toyota CY2027 hybrid below 5.5m units AND Cox US HEV below 14% sustained); OR EU 2035 trilogue tightens to 100% accelerating BEV-only attach rates and pulling Catalysis decline curve faster - either pulls the path toward the 'slow_burn_envelope_breach' scenario.

scenarios.name: slow_burn_envelope_breach_foundation_cushion_compresses
scenarios.narrative: Combined T6 'premium_bev_demand_cliff' + EU regulatory recommitment fires: EU trilogue under Cypriot Presidency holds 90% line firmly with binding 2030 ratchet; California ZEV waiver upheld at federal court; EPA Phase 3 GHG rules confirmed without delay. Western premium-OEM CY2026 BEV mix prints below 18% (escalation trigger met) but premium platforms shift toward BEV-only follow-on architecture rather than hybrid retention. ICE+HEV production decline accelerates to 5-7% CAGR through 2028. Toyota's 6.7m unit hybrid commitment partially honoured but skewed toward Asia rather than EU/NA, reducing hybrid attach-rate transmission to Catalysis. Major platform-life award loss to JM Catalyst Technologies post-restructuring on at least 1-2 BMW / Mercedes / VW Group programmes (three-way oligopoly stress). Catalysis FY2028 prints EUR 1,300-1,400m revenue / EUR 270-325m EBITA / 20-22% margin - breaches the -15% EBITA floor (EUR 326m). Foundation cushion combined Catalysis + Recycling FY2027 sits at EUR 540-590m, below the EUR 600m BMS cross-subsidy break point per pos_balance_sheet_foundation_cushion analysis. Bad Sackingen Industriestrompreis eligibility resolved negatively (mechanical engineering / specialty chemicals not in final 91-sector list interpretation). T6 truth-likelihood drifts toward 0.30-0.35; T5 may also weaken modestly on PGM thrifting acceleration. Critical cascade: B1's 'tail_governance_failure_premature_kill' scenario (currently 5%) re-rates to 10-15% probability because BMS patience capital runs out earlier than Q3 2027 proof-gate.
scenarios.what_makes_this_path_break: T6 'us_hybrid_lock_in' fires concurrently absorbing displaced volumes (escalation paths in T6 are not mutually exclusive); OR Bad Sackingen eligibility resolved positively under expanded TCTF state-aid; OR Clean Industrial Deal symmetric relief adopted neutralising EU asymmetry; OR T5 enforcement-teeth episode on PGM regime widens loop integration value enough to offset volume decline.

scenarios.name: platform_award_share_loss_competitive_cliff
scenarios.narrative: Three-way oligopoly competitive stress fires sharply: Johnson Matthey emerges from Honeywell-deal restructuring as a more-aggressive standalone bidder with restructured cost base; BASF ECMS carve-out delivers a leaner, capital-light competitor on EU platforms; Heraeus, Cataler, and Solvay win share on Asian platforms (China + Japan + Korea + India). Umicore loses on >=2 major BMW / Mercedes / VW Group / Stellantis / Toyota / Hyundai-Kia platform-life award decisions in the 2026-H1 2027 window covering 2028-2033 cash flows. Share-loss not volume-loss is the dominant break channel. Catalysis FY2028 prints EUR 1,250-1,350m revenue / EUR 240-300m EBITA / 19-22% margin - decisively breaches the -15% EBITA floor. Foundation cushion combined falls to EUR 500-560m FY2027 - BMS Q3 2027 proof-gate is impacted because patience capital math no longer supports the EUR 100-200m incremental BMS losses through that window. The path requires concurrent T6 erosion AND competitive failure, hence lower probability than slow-burn-envelope-breach. Most directly tested by Q3 2027 platform-award disclosure cycle; can be partially confirmed before FY2028 resolution by FY2027 EBITA print landing below EUR 350m without other observable T6/T5/T2 driver moves.
scenarios.what_makes_this_path_break: JM Catalyst Technologies post-Honeywell deal disrupted by integration friction (option-value rather than aggressive bidding); OR Umicore wins on at least one anchor BMW Neue Klasse follow-on or Mercedes hybrid programme tender; OR three-way oligopoly remains stable with no major share-loss event.

scenarios.name: tail_pgm_loop_disruption_inventory_markdown
scenarios.narrative: T5 escalation 'russian_pgm_normalisation_shock' fires (10% probability in T5 mapped to ~2% impact on B2 via the loaded-catalyst inventory channel): Ukraine ceasefire framework agreed at Geneva or Istanbul level in H2 2026; OFAC sectoral relief for Russian non-ferrous metals; LME re-listing of Russian PGM warrants. Norilsk restores 50-70% of degraded throughput by mid-2027. Pt re-prices toward USD 1,500/oz, Pd below USD 1,200/oz. Loaded-catalyst inventory across the Catalysis-Recycling PGM loop marks down materially; pos_capability_metal_management_hedging absorbs incomplete protection (FY2025 WC build EUR 261m was the visible failure mode). Catalysis FY2028 prints EUR 1,400-1,500m revenue / EUR 290-335m EBITA / 20.5-22.5% margin (revenue holds because volume is stable but EBITA compresses on inventory revaluation + lower pass-through pricing). Concurrent T2 second-energy-shock or T2 ETS sustained above EUR 100/tCO2 exacerbates. Foundation cushion compresses sharply because Recycling EBITDA is also compressed in this scenario (T5 alternate scenario 'russian_normalisation_partial_compression' tl 0.45-0.55, Recycling EBITDA -25-40% on price-driven inventory revaluation). The path is conditional on simultaneous Russian shock plus partial Pt-for-Pd substitution acceleration plus oligopoly stable, hence low probability.
scenarios.what_makes_this_path_break: Russian PGM containment intact (Norilsk Q1 2026 -26% Pt YoY trend continues); OR EU sanctions packages 21st/22nd progressing rather than reversing; OR sectoral relief gated by Ukraine ceasefire that does not materialise within window.

named_watch_metrics.metric: Catalysis quarterly segment revenue + Adjusted EBIT + EBIT margin (post-implementation of segment-level FCF disclosure target Q1 2026 update on 24 April 2026)
named_watch_metrics.source: Umicore quarterly trading updates and half-year results; cross-checked against trajectory stream year-by-year status-quo path (FY2026 EUR 1,635m / EUR 376m / 23.0%; FY2027 EUR 1,595m / EUR 363m / 22.8%; FY2028 EUR 1,545m / EUR 347m / 22.5%)
named_watch_metrics.current_value: FY2025 baseline: EUR 1,668m revenue (+0.1% YoY), EUR 383m EBITA, 23.0% margin, 40.8% ROCE on EUR 938m capital employed. Per Convergence stream demand, segment-level FCF disclosure starting Q1 2026 trading update on 24 April 2026 is a unanimous prescription.
named_watch_metrics.threshold_implications: Bull: any single quarter with Catalysis revenue tracking >2% YoY positive AND/OR EBITA margin >23.5% (above central path) - confirms hybrid-lock-in scenario. Bear: any single quarter with Catalysis revenue tracking >5% YoY negative AND/OR EBITA margin <22.0% sustained for two quarters - tracks toward slow-burn envelope breach. Critical gate: FY2027 full-year EBITA print below EUR 350m would put FY2028 at risk of breaching the -15% floor (EUR 326m) and the EUR 600m foundation cushion floor for BMS cross-subsidy.

named_watch_metrics.metric: Cox Automotive US quarterly HEV share + ACEA monthly EU HEV share
named_watch_metrics.source: Cox Automotive Kelley Blue Book quarterly EV report + Cox Industry Insights call; ACEA monthly Passenger Car Registrations press releases; ICCT European Car Market Monitor + ICCT US Vehicle Markets Briefing
named_watch_metrics.current_value: Cox US Q1 2026: HEV share 14% (down from Q4 2025 19.7% peak). ACEA EU Q1 2026: HEV share 38.6% (1,089,421 HEV registered, +pp YoY). Toyota Feb 2026 commitment: grow hybrid output ~30% to 6.7m units by 2028 (~60% of total Toyota production).
named_watch_metrics.threshold_implications: Bull (T6 propagation rule for B2): Cox US HEV share >19% sustained 3 consecutive quarters AND ACEA EU HEV share >38% sustained four monthly prints (T6 escalation 'us_hybrid_lock_in' fires positively for B2, +1 magnitude per propagation rule). Bear: Cox US HEV share <14% sustained 2 consecutive quarters AND ACEA EU HEV share <35% sustained four monthly prints AND/OR Toyota CY2027 hybrid production confirmed below 5.5m units - hybrid-as-transition not destination, B2 prior re-rates to 0.45-0.50.

named_watch_metrics.metric: S&P Global Mobility Western (EU + NA) ICE+HEV light-vehicle production forecast 2026-2028 (units)
named_watch_metrics.source: S&P Global Mobility Light Vehicle Production Forecasts (quarterly revision); cross-checked against ACEA + EPA registration data
named_watch_metrics.current_value: S&P Apr 2026 reading: cut Europe LV production outlook by 114k 2026 / 143k 2027; NA combined BEV+PHEV share 8.9% 2026, 10.1% 2027; EU BEV share expected 23% 2026, 28% 2027. ICE+HEV implied complement = ~77% EU 2026, ~72% EU 2027; ~91% NA 2026, ~90% NA 2027. Trajectory stream key driver: 'Global ICE light-vehicle production declining at roughly 3-4% CAGR through 2028.'
named_watch_metrics.threshold_implications: Bull: ICE+HEV Western production decline at lower end of 2-3% CAGR sustained through 2027 (S&P quarterly revisions stable or upgraded). Bear: cumulative S&P Western ICE+HEV production cuts >300k 2026-2027 combined AND/OR ICE+HEV decline acceleration to >5% CAGR. T6 named indicator 'S&P Global Mobility Western 2027 BEV production forecast' - any cut >10% from prior quarter is the T6 escalation trigger; for B2 the inverse (ICE+HEV upgrade) would be supportive.

named_watch_metrics.metric: Bad Sackingen Industriestrompreis eligibility status under German 91-sector list
named_watch_metrics.source: EU Commission state-aid decisions database (DG COMP) + Bundesanzeiger + BMWE press releases + Umicore investor disclosure
named_watch_metrics.current_value: T2 propagation rule for B2 currently models -0.1 contribution with explicit asymmetry: Bad Sackingen ELIGIBLE if confirmed under 91-sector list (mechanical engineering / specialty chemicals plausible per position-map note); ELIGIBLE worth +0.5 magnitude on B2 prior. Florange (France) and Nowa Ruda (Poland) categorically excluded from German relief. EU Commission state-aid decisions database has not yet published site-level confirmations.
named_watch_metrics.threshold_implications: Bull: Bad Sackingen confirmed eligible under Industriestrompreis 91-sector list - relative cost advantage vs Florange and Nowa Ruda, +0.5 B2 magnitude. Bull alt: Belgian / Polish / French equivalent state-aid scheme matching German EUR 50/MWh approved under expanded TCTF, neutralising the asymmetry. Bear: Bad Sackingen ineligibility confirmed AND no symmetric Clean Industrial Deal package adopted - asymmetric national disadvantage compounds for Florange + Nowa Ruda.

named_watch_metrics.metric: OEM platform-life award decisions cycle 2026-H1 2027 (BMW Neue Klasse follow-on, Mercedes EQS-replacement, VW Group hybrid programmes, Toyota NA hybrid awards, Hyundai-Kia hybrid programmes)
named_watch_metrics.source: OEM Q-call commentary + Capital Markets Day decks; Umicore Catalysis platform-win disclosure; Johnson Matthey / BASF / Heraeus / Cataler competitor IR commentary; Reuters / Automotive News tracking
named_watch_metrics.current_value: Three-way oligopoly Umicore + JM + BASF + Heraeus + Cataler holding for now. Johnson Matthey Catalyst Technologies post-Honeywell deal restructuring underway - directional uncertainty on competitive stance. BASF ECMS carve-out path ongoing. Position-map pos_site_catalysis_global_footprint notes the depreciated brownfield footprint already captures OEM platform-life award lock-in.
named_watch_metrics.threshold_implications: Bull: Umicore confirms wins on >=2 major BMW / Mercedes / VW Group platform-life awards covering 2028-2033 cash flows. Bear: Umicore loses on >=2 major BMW / Mercedes / VW / Stellantis / Toyota / Hyundai-Kia hybrid programme tenders to JM Catalyst Technologies post-restructuring or BASF ECMS - share-loss as dominant break channel, fires the 'platform_award_share_loss_competitive_cliff' scenario. Bear alt: any major OEM Q-call language shifting from 'Umicore as preferred Catalysis supplier' to 'multi-supplier strategy' on hybrid programmes.

named_watch_metrics.metric: WPIC Platinum Quarterly deficit estimate + JM PGM Market Report Pt-for-Pd substitution outlook + Norilsk PGM production
named_watch_metrics.source: World Platinum Investment Council (WPIC) Platinum Quarterly; Johnson Matthey PGM Market Report (twice-annual May/November); Nornickel quarterly production releases (nornickel.com investor relations)
named_watch_metrics.current_value: WPIC 28 April 2026: 240 koz platinum deficit forecast 2026 (4th consecutive year); JM 2025 PGM Market Report: Pt 3rd consecutive year of significant deficit, Pd moving from deficit to balance, Rh remains in deficit. Norilsk Q1 2026: Pd -14% QoQ / -18% YoY, Pt -26% QoQ / -26% YoY; FY2026 guidance Pd -10-11%, Pt -5-8%. Pt USD 1,999.70/oz, Pd USD 1,545.50/oz May 2026 spot.
named_watch_metrics.threshold_implications: Bull (T5 propagation +0.5 to B2): WPIC 2027 forecast extending Pt deficit to 5th consecutive year above 200 koz AND/OR JM May 2026 Market Report extending deficit horizon to 2028 - integrated PGM loop value strengthens. Bear: WPIC mid-2026 quarterly REVISING DEFICIT DOWN with Pt market flipping to balanced/surplus AND/OR Norilsk Q3 2026 production showing flat-or-recovery YoY (Russian normalisation shock starting) - direct B2 read-across via loaded-catalyst inventory revaluation. Bear alt: T5 escalation 'pgm_thrifting_and_substitution_bites' - Toyota / Honda announcing hybrid Pt-loading reduction step-change, compressing autocatalyst pricing power.

named_watch_metrics.metric: Foundation cushion EBITA combined (Catalysis + Recycling FY2025 baseline EUR 679m) per Umicore semi-annual + annual reports
named_watch_metrics.source: Umicore consolidated financial statements (segment_analysis); cross-checked against pos_balance_sheet_foundation_cushion definition; Convergence-prescribed quarterly segment-level FCF disclosure starting 24 April 2026
named_watch_metrics.frequency: semi-annual (FY + H1 reports) + quarterly (post-implementation of segment FCF disclosure)
named_watch_metrics.current_value: FY2025 combined Catalysis + Recycling EBITA EUR 679m (Catalysis 383 + Recycling 296). Specialty Materials adds 76 for combined foundation EUR 755m. BMS drag FY2025 -91, Corporate -84. Trajectory stream year-by-year: FY2026 Catalysis EUR 376m + Recycling assumed broadly stable; FY2027 Catalysis EUR 363m; FY2028 Catalysis EUR 347m. Recycling 208% reported ROCE distortion is a separate watch metric for B3.
named_watch_metrics.threshold_implications: Bull: combined Catalysis + Recycling FY2026 EBITA prints above EUR 660m (status-quo path) AND H1 2027 trending toward >EUR 600m for FY2027 - foundation cushion underwrites BMS patience capital through Q3 2027 proof-gate per B1 thesis. Bear (LOAD-BEARING for cross-bet cascade): combined Catalysis + Recycling FY2026 EBITA prints below EUR 600m AND/OR FY2027 trends below EUR 580m - the BMS cross-subsidy break point at which B1's tail-governance scenarios accelerate; board may be forced to fire BMS kill criteria pre-emptively before Q3 2027 proof-gate matures. Critical gate: cumulative 3-year combined foundation EBITA below EUR 1,800m over FY2025-2027 forces dividend-dilution + leverage breach concurrently.

expected_projection_topics: Catalysis quarterly + annual revenue / EBITA / margin / ROCE trajectory through FY2028 against +/-15% bet envelope and trajectory stream year-by-year status-quo path
US Cox HEV share quarterly + ACEA EU HEV monthly path 2026-2028 against T6 two-sided propagation rule (positive at >=19% US / >=38% EU sustained, negative at >2pp BEV acceleration above 22% EU)
S&P Global Mobility Western ICE+HEV light-vehicle production forecast 2026-2028 vs trajectory-assumed 3-4% CAGR decline and Euro 7 / EPA 2027 content uplift schedule
Bad Sackingen Industriestrompreis eligibility resolution under German 91-sector list AND EU Clean Industrial Deal symmetric relief progression for Florange (France) and Nowa Ruda (Poland)

OEM platform-life award decisions cycle 2026-H1 2027 covering 2028-2033 BMW / Mercedes / VW Group / Stellantis / Toyota / Hyundai-Kia hybrid programmes against three-way oligopoly competitive stress (JM dismemberment post-Honeywell deal, BASF ECMS carve-out, Heraeus / Cataler / Solvay encroachment)

WPIC Platinum Quarterly deficit persistence + JM 2026 PGM Market Report Pt-for-Pd substitution runway + Norilsk PGM production trajectory against T5 escalation paths (Russian normalisation shock, PGM thrifting acceleration)
Foundation cushion combined Catalysis + Recycling EBITA quarterly / annual trajectory through FY2027 against EUR 600m BMS cross-subsidy break point
Hoboken 2026 maintenance shutdown impact on integrated Catalysis-Recycling PGM loop and Pt-for-Pd substitution timing
falsification_criteria: Catalysis FY2028 EBITA prints below EUR 326m (i.e. below the -15% bet envelope floor against the EUR 383m FY2025 baseline) - this is the headline falsification trigger and is reachable via slow-burn ICE+HEV decline acceleration, OEM platform-share loss, OR concurrent T6 + T2 + T5 stress.

Catalysis FY2028 revenue prints below EUR 1,418m (-15% vs EUR 1,668m baseline) AND/OR EBITA margin prints below 19.6% (-15% vs 23.0% baseline) - either supplementary breach of the +/-15% envelope falsifies even if EBITA absolute number stays above EUR 326m via off-margin volume mix.

Catalysis EBITA below EUR 350m for any two consecutive years before FY2028 (i.e. FY2026 + FY2027 both <EUR 350m) - the breakage shape is clearly visible before resolution date and should re-rate B2 prior to Vulnerable tier.

Combined foundation cushion (Catalysis + Recycling) EBITA below EUR 600m for FY2026 OR FY2027 - the load-bearing role for B1 (BMS bet) breaks; B1's tail-governance scenario re-rates from 5% to 10-15% probability. This is the cross-bet cascade falsification.

Toyota CY2027 hybrid production confirmed below 5.5m units AND Cox US HEV share averaging below 14% sustained for 3 consecutive quarters AND ACEA EU HEV share averaging below 35% for two consecutive quarters - hybrid-as-transition not destination, T6 propagation rule for B2 flips from +0.3 to negative.

Umicore loses platform-life awards to Johnson Matthey Catalyst Technologies (post-Honeywell deal) or BASF ECMS on >=2 major BMW / Mercedes / VW Group / Stellantis / Toyota / Hyundai-Kia 2028-2033 ICE+HEV programmes - share-loss as dominant break channel, fires 'platform_award_share_loss_competitive_cliff' scenario.

Russian PGM normalisation shock realised: any combination of (a) Ukraine ceasefire framework agreed at Geneva or Istanbul level, (b) OFAC sectoral relief for Russian non-ferrous metals, (c) LME re-listing of Russian PGM warrants - direct read-across to loaded-catalyst inventory mark-down across Catalysis-Recycling PGM loop AND concurrent Recycling EBITDA compression breaches the foundation cushion floor.

Bad Sackingen confirmed ineligible under German Industriestrompreis 91-sector list AND no Belgian / Polish / French equivalent state-aid scheme approved under expanded TCTF AND no symmetric Clean Industrial Deal relief adopted by end-2027 - asymmetric national disadvantage compounds across the EU footprint without offset.

Quarterly segment-level FCF disclosure (per pos_governance_segment_fcf_disclosure_commitment) NOT implemented by Q3 2026 - governance failure mode that allows the foundation cushion arithmetic to be opaque, prevents independent assessment of the BMS cross-subsidy break point, and itself functionally falsifies the bet's readable-signal mechanism.

Pt-for-Pd substitution running materially faster than JM expects (T5 escalation 'pgm_thrifting_and_substitution_bites'): Toyota / Honda announcing hybrid Pt-loading reduction step-change AND/OR JM 2027 PGM Market Report flipping platinum to balanced/surplus - compresses autocatalyst pricing power before FY2028, breaching the -15% revenue floor via mix erosion.
bets.breakage_shape: When B2 breaks, the most likely shape is a 24-30 month slow grind not a sharp cliff - this is structurally different from B1's chemistry-cliff alternative because Catalysis runway is into early 2030s on management framing and the brownfield footprint is largely depreciated (low fixed-cost erosion from any single year's volume hit). The grinding break-shape: FY2026 Catalysis revenue tracks ~3-5% YoY below the trajectory-stream EUR 1,635m central path because S&P Global Mobility revises Western ICE+HEV production forecasts down >150k cumulative through Q3 2026; Cox US HEV share averages 13-15% across Q1-Q3 2026 (volatile around the 14% midpoint, materially below the 19% T6 threshold); ACEA EU HEV share holds at 36-38% but BEV acceleration accelerates beyond 22% in EU (T6 negative trigger fires at -1 magnitude per 2pp acceleration). FY2026 Catalysis EBITA prints EUR 360-370m vs EUR 376m central path - first visible margin compression. By FY2027 the grind compounds: Bad Sackingen Industriestrompreis eligibility resolved negatively in mid-2026; combined T2 EU cost stack drag adds 30-50 bps margin compression to the depreciation-curve effect; PGM regime mildly weakens (T5 'gold_correction_partial_compression' scenario fires at 20% probability with EUR 200-300/oz gold correction taking Recycling EBITDA -EUR 50-70m); FY2027 Catalysis EBITA prints EUR 330-345m vs EUR 363m central path; combined foundation cushion FY2027 falls to EUR 580-600m - at or just below the BMS cross-subsidy break point. Board-level governance response materialises in early 2027: quarterly segment FCF disclosure (if implemented per pos_governance_segment_fcf_disclosure_commitment) makes the cushion compression auditable; Convergence-prescribed binding kill criteria for BMS may be activated pre-emptively. By FY2028 Catalysis revenue lands EUR 1,400-1,500m (vs EUR 1,545m central path), EBITA EUR 300-340m (vs EUR 347m central path) - the +/-15% envelope is breached by ~1-5pp on EBITA and 5-10pp on revenue. The sharper alternative break-shape (platform-award-share-loss cliff) compresses this to 12-18 months around the 2026-H1 2027 OEM platform-life award cycle - typically a single competitive-loss event (Umicore loses BMW Neue Klasse follow-on hybrid catalyst tender to JM Catalyst Technologies, or Mercedes hybrid programme to BASF ECMS) cascading to >=2 follow-on awards over the next 6 months given OEM portfolio-supplier consolidation logic. The tail-PGM-loop disruption shape (Russian normalisation shock) is faster still (3-9 months) but limited to inventory-revaluation impact of EUR 100-200m one-off rather than a structural break. Either path culminates in Catalysis being formally re-framed under CORE from 'foundation business managed for cash extraction' to 'managed decline with explicit harvest schedule' - and the cascade into BMS is direct because foundation cushion compression accelerates the BMS tail-governance scenario from B1's currently-modelled 5% probability into the 10-15% range, potentially forcing board kill criteria pre-emptively before BMS Q3 2027 proof-gate matures. The asymmetry that makes B2 unique among B-set: the bet's two-sided exposure (too-fast pure-BEV adoption AND too-slow HEV adoption both hurt) means there is no single direction of T6 movement that is unambiguously positive - even the central path requires hybrid persistence threading the needle between BEV pull-forward and ICE collapse.

baseline_research_note.claim_under_test: Catalysis revenue + EBITA holds the FY2025 EUR 1,668m / EUR 383m / 23.0% margin profile within +/-15% by FY2028 despite the structural ICE -> BEV transition, with ICE+HEV catalyst volumes declining no faster than the depreciation schedule of the global Catalysis footprint (Florange, Bad Sackingen, Nowa Ruda, Tulsa, Mexico, Suzhou, Jiangmen, Korea, Thailand, India, Japan, Brazil), Euro 7 and EPA 2027 content uplift flowing through as scheduled, and the segment continuing to fund the foundation cushion that underwrites the BMS bet (B1) through Q3 2027 proof-gate.
baseline_research_note.what_is_known_today: FY2025 Catalysis reported EUR 1,668m revenue (+0.1% YoY, essentially flat), EUR 383m Adjusted EBIT at 23.0% margin, 40.8% ROCE on capital employed of EUR 938m, contributing 46.8% of group revenue mix and 66.1% of group segment EBIT mix - the dominant cash engine of the foundation businesses. Brownfield footprint largely depreciated with low capex intensity per pos_site_catalysis_global_footprint. Trajectory stream year-by-year status-quo path: FY2026 EUR 1,635m / EUR 376m / 23.0% / 40.4% ROCE; FY2027 EUR 1,595m / EUR 363m / 22.8% / 39.5%; FY2028 EUR 1,545m / EUR 347m / 22.5% / 38.3%. Cumulative FY2025-FY2028 revenue decline -7%, EBITA decline -EUR 36m (-9.4%), margin compression -50 bps. T6 propagation rule for B2 is two-sided with +0.3 current contribution (hybrid persistence positive, premium-BEV cliff bidirectional). T5 propagation rule for B2 is +0.3 current contribution (integrated PGM loop + Pt-for-Pd substitution runway). T2 propagation rule for B2 is -0.1 current contribution (asymmetric EU cost pressure with Bad Sackingen relief eligibility unresolved). FY2025 group efficiency program delivered EUR 100m EBITDA contribution ahead of EUR 70m original target, supporting cost discipline through 2026-2028. Foundation cushion EUR 679m FY2025 (Catalysis 383 + Recycling 296) plus Specialty 76 = EUR 755m. CORE plan March 2025 explicitly framed Catalysis as 'foundation business' to be managed for maximum cash extraction with low incremental capex.
baseline_research_note.contradictions_in_evidence: Several genuine cross-stream tensions exist. (1) Wasting-asset framing vs durability: macro stream's segment_views/automotive_catalysts.decision_guidance is 'Harvest aggressively - monetize the cash annuity while it lasts and redirect proceeds to higher-return uses elsewhere in the group'; convergence Round 2 frames Catalysis as 'a runway, not an ice cube' but explicitly acknowledges it is a non-renewable resource. The +/-15% bet envelope by FY2028 is a far gentler test than the long-run wasting-asset framing implies, but the load-bearing role for the foundation cushion means even gentle erosion in that direction matters. (2) Hybrid as destination vs transition: Toyota's Feb 2026 commitment to grow hybrid output ~30% to 6.7m units by 2028 supports hybrid-as-destination thesis (positive for B2); BUT US Q1 2026 hybrid 14% vs Q4 2025 19.7% peak shows hybrid share is volatile around the threshold not stable above it. ACEA EU HEV 38.6% Q1 2026 is more clearly above threshold, but the China and Asia-Pacific footprint exposure (which is not separately disclosed in trajectory) is a transparency gap. (3) Premium-BEV cliff: T6 escalation 'premium_bev_demand_cliff' (25% probability in T6) cuts both ways for B2 - bad if premium-OEM customers re-baseline toward fully BEV-only follow-on platforms that don't carry hybrid attach rates; neutral-to-good if it accelerates hybrid retention as a safer interim platform. The dual-direction sensitivity is what makes T6 the load-bearing two-sided driver. (4) Russian PGM normalisation shock vs deficit persistence: T5 prior 0.70 reflects 5-of-6 supportive layers but the Russian normalisation shock (10% probability) is an asymmetric tail that consensus is not pricing - direct read-across to Catalysis via loaded-catalyst inventory mark-downs and PGM-loop working-capital unwind. (5) Bad Sackingen eligibility: position-map flags this as an OPEN QUESTION not a resolved fact; T2 reading rule for B2 carries +/-0.5 magnitude conditional on resolution, which is material relative to the +/-15% EBITA envelope. (6) PEM fuel cell adjacency: pos_technology_pem_fuel_cell_catalyst is explicitly 'option value, not core earnings' per macro framing; the position-map factual_corrections explicitly note that 'Hyundai Changshu PEM, Loyang Singapore, Cheonan Korea are NOT named in any of the four input streams' - any thesis invoking those specific assets is unsupported. (7) Three-way oligopoly competitive stress: Johnson Matthey dismemberment (post-Honeywell deal) and BASF ECMS carve-out are active variables for 2026-H1 2027 platform-life award decisions covering 2028-2033 cash flows - share-loss is a material break path that the +/-15% envelope alone does not capture if Umicore loses on >= 2 major platform tenders.
open_questions: Is Bad Sackingen (German Catalysis site) confirmed eligible under the German Industriestrompreis 91-sector list? Position-map explicitly flags this as a transparency gap - mechanical engineering / specialty chemicals are plausible but not confirmed. Resolution worth +/-0.5 magnitude on B2 prior per T2 reading rule. EU Commission state-aid decisions database has not yet published site-level confirmations.

What is the regional split of Catalysis revenue / EBITA / capital employed across (a) EU footprint Florange + Bad Sackingen + Nowa Ruda, (b) NA Tulsa + Mexico, (c) China Suzhou + Jiangmen, (d) Asia-Pacific ex-China + South America? The trajectory stream uses aggregate global figures only; site-level economics matter for the asymmetric T2 cost stack and for the platform-life award analysis. Position-map flags this as a non-disclosed factual variable.

What is Umicore's expected catalyst content uplift per vehicle from Euro 7 (operative for new types from 1 July 2027 for cars/vans, 29 May 2028 for trucks/buses) and EPA 2027 (heavy-duty SCR)? Trajectory stream cites 'Euro 7 content uplift flowing through European platforms as scheduled' as a key driver but does not quantify the uplift magnitude.

What is the expected 2026-H1 2027 platform-life award outcome on the major BMW / Mercedes / VW Group / Stellantis / Toyota / Hyundai-Kia hybrid programmes covering 2028-2033 cash flows? The three-way oligopoly competitive stress (JM dismemberment post-Honeywell deal, BASF ECMS carve-out) is a material variable but not separately disclosed by Umicore.

What is the FY2025 Catalysis exposure to PGM working-capital build (separate from the EUR 261m group-level FY2025 WC build)? pos_capability_metal_management_hedging flags WC build as the visible failure mode for hedging; the Catalysis-specific component vs the BMS / Recycling components is not separately disclosed.

Has the board or CEO formally signalled intent to harvest Catalysis distributions to fund BMS losses through FY2027? Macro segment_views/automotive_catalysts.decision_guidance prescribes 'Harvest aggressively - monetize the cash annuity while it lasts' but this is a sell-side analytical recommendation not a stated Umicore commitment - the explicit cash-allocation policy from Catalysis to BMS through Q3 2027 proof-gate is a governance gap.

What is the Hoboken 2026 maintenance shutdown impact on the integrated Catalysis-Recycling PGM loop? The shutdown is named in pos_site_hoboken as 'meaningful one-off EBITDA hit (lost fixed-cost absorption)' but the Catalysis-side knock-on (delayed feedstock recycling cycle, Pt-for-Pd substitution timing) is not separately quantified.

Does the FY2025 revenue +0.1% YoY (essentially flat) print conceal underlying volume decline of 3-4% being offset by Euro 7 / pricing / mix? The trajectory stream assumes 'Euro 7 content uplift flowing through European platforms as scheduled' as forward driver but the FY2025 baseline disclosure does not split price/mix/volume.

factual_corrections: T1 (chemistry transition) is excluded from B2 theme dependencies. T1's mislabelled 'B2' propagation entry ('CAM utilisation / Nysa break-even', weight 0.9) was reassigned to B1 in Step 3.1 as a SECOND B1 transmission channel under T1; T1 had no B2 entry once that adjudication was made. Re-introducing T1 weight under B2 would double-count.

Hyundai Changshu PEM, Loyang Singapore, and Cheonan Korea are NOT named in any of the four input streams. The disclosed Asian Catalysis sites are Suzhou + Jiangmen (China), Korea (un-specified), Thailand, India, Japan. PEM fuel cell catalyst capability (pos_technology_pem_fuel_cell_catalyst) is a separate adjacent technology position underwritten as 'option value, not core earnings' per macro framing - capital at risk modest in absolute terms.

Catalysis FY2025 baseline is EUR 1,668m revenue / EUR 383m EBITA / 23.0% margin / 40.8% ROCE per Financial analyse.json segment_analysis. The +/-15% envelope therefore translates to EUR 1,418-1,918m revenue, EUR 326-440m EBITA, 19.6-26.5% margin.

Foundation cushion EUR 679m FY2025 (Catalysis 383 + Recycling 296) is the load-bearing combined cushion underwriting BMS patience capital. B2 is the LOAD-BEARING half of that cushion via EBITA ranking (66.1% of group segment EBITA mix); if B2 breaks, B1's tail-governance scenario fires faster.

Trajectory stream year-by-year status-quo path: FY2026 EUR 1,635m / EUR 376m / 23.0% / 40.4% ROCE; FY2027 EUR 1,595m / EUR 363m / 22.8% / 39.5%; FY2028 EUR 1,545m / EUR 347m / 22.5% / 38.3% ROCE - all within the +/-15% envelope.

ICE+HEV light-vehicle production decline pace assumed at 3-4% CAGR per trajectory stream key_drivers_held_constant; cliff dates beyond 2030 are NOT named in trajectory stream or T6 named-watch indicators and would be fabrication if introduced.

bets.label: Recycling franchise (Hoboken complex-feed PGM refining) durability + monetisation through FY2028

intermediate_gates.name: Hoboken 2026 scheduled maintenance shutdown completion + restart performance
intermediate_gates.what_resolves: The 2026 maintenance shutdown is the single largest operational gate in the bet horizon. Resolves whether (a) the shutdown completes on schedule and within budgeted EBITDA-impact envelope, (b) restart throughput recovers to pre-shutdown complex-feed tonnes within Q1 2027, (c) any permitting / lead-emission step-change emerges from the shutdown window (Belgian 2 μg/dl dispute is the watching tail-risk). Lost fixed-cost absorption during the shutdown is a real one-off EBITDA hit, not cosmetic; magnitude flagged by Macro at EUR ~30-60m range. A shutdown over-run extending into H1 2027 would directly compress the FY2027 normalised EBITA range and re-rate B3.

intermediate_gates.name: EU Waste Shipment Regulation digital-procedure go-live (21 May 2026) + first enforcement evidence
intermediate_gates.what_resolves: T5 dated regulatory catalyst. WSR digital procedures became applicable 21 May 2026 (already crossed at as_of 2026-05-04); what resolves through 2026 is whether enforcement bites (DG ENV bulletins, member-state customs seizures, BIR/EERA EU-internal scrap retention statistics). Bull: >85% EU-internal retention rates by Q4 2026, refiner-side feedstock contract pricing improves +5-10% on Q3-Q4 2026 renewals. Bear: enforcement gap visible (large Asian destination-yard flows persist), Glencore Metallium / Trafigura Nth Cycle deals divert Western scrap streams ahead of WSR full-export-rule date (21 May 2027).

intermediate_gates.name: Treasury Q4 2026 'impracticable-to-trace' transition expiry (CBAM and WSR full export rules in 2027 corridor)
intermediate_gates.what_resolves: Cluster of dated regulatory catalysts in H1 2027: (a) WSR full export rules apply 21 May 2027 (Western capture lock complete), (b) CBAM certificate sales begin 1 Feb 2027 with weekly pricing (cash-impact on imported-PGM material competition), (c) EU Battery Passport 18 Feb 2027 (recycled-content traceability). Together these crystallise the structural Western-capture lock. Bear: any material postponement of WSR export rules or CBAM weakening = T5 'trader_diverts_western_scrap' scenario fires.

intermediate_gates.name: Q1 2026 segment-level FCF disclosure implementation (Recycling)
intermediate_gates.what_resolves: Per pos_governance_segment_fcf_disclosure_commitment, Convergence demanded segment-FCF disclosure starting Q1 2026 trading update on 24 April 2026 (already crossed at as_of). What resolves: whether the disclosure (i) strips out the gold sale-and-leaseback gain cleanly, (ii) reconciles Recycling underlying cash conversion separately from gold price effect, (iii) makes the artefactual-vs-normalised ROCE distinction independently auditable. This is the readable signal between today and resolution that tells the market whether B3's normalised economics are durable below the headline.

intermediate_gates.name: Hoboken EUR 400m capex programme mid-cycle progress milestone (FY2027 H1)
intermediate_gates.what_resolves: EUR ~300m of the EUR 400m envelope is concentrated in 2027-2028. By H1 2027 the programme should report (a) cumulative capex spent at ~EUR 200-250m run-rate, (b) execution against schedule with no major slippage, (c) first throughput uplift evidence on commissioned capacity. Bull: on-schedule execution preserves the 2030-2031 payback path. Bear: cost over-run >15% or schedule slippage >9 months pushes payback into 2032+ and re-rates the capex-vs-foundation-cushion logic (B7 territory).

bets.prior_rationale: Six observable layers anchor the prior comfortably above 0.50, with one cyclical fragility pulling it back from 0.70+. T5 is load-bearing direct (weight 1.0) at theme prior 0.70 with current_contribution 0.85 to B3 — the regime is supportive across 5 of 6 layers TODAY: (1) Pt deficit confirmed 4th consecutive year (WPIC 240 koz 2026, repeatedly revised UP not DOWN); (2) Russian PGM containment empirically intact and worsening (Norilsk Q1 2026 Pd -18% / Pt -26% YoY hard data, FY2026 guidance Pd -10-11% / Pt -5-8%); (3) gold spot ~EUR 4,000/oz on May 2026 spot, ~EUR 500/oz cushion above the EUR 3,500/oz EBITDA-compression threshold; (4) refiner pricing power INTACT at the largest comparable peer (Aurubis Q1 2025/26 PGM volume +27%, metal-results 45% of group gross margin vs 36% prior year); (5) EU WSR digital procedures operational from 21 May 2026 + WSR full export rules from 21 May 2027 + Environmental Crime Directive — Western-capture infrastructure live INSIDE the bet horizon; (6) one fragile layer: gold tailwind is cyclical not structural — a 12-15% gold correction still leaves the regime supportive, but the CFO-stress-test reference at USD 1,800-2,000/oz is the explicit fragility flag (~50-55% downside from current spot). Hoboken is one of few complex-feed PGM smelter-refinery assets globally with a real operational moat (pos_technology_hoboken_complex_feed_loop) and EUR 400m 2025-2028 capex defends the franchise (pos_strategic_commitment_hoboken_400m_capex). FY2025 reported Recycling EBITA of EUR 296m on EUR 947m revenue (31.2% margin, 51.1% of group EBITA) — the foundation cushion is already delivered, not promised. Three structural risks pull the prior back from a 0.70+ shape: (a) the EUR 525m gold sale-and-leaseback creates contingent margin-call exposure if gold corrects to USD 1,800-2,000/oz (CFO stress reference) — this is the tail-but-non-trivial scenario that would simultaneously compress reported EBITA and force a leverage / cash-conversion stress (B7 territory); (b) trader-vs-refiner feedstock competition is a 12-18 month dynamic, with Glencore Li-Cycle (Aug 2025) + Glencore Metallium binding agreement (Jan 2026) + Trafigura USD 1.1bn Nth Cycle deal (April 2026) representing concrete trader balance-sheet capacity that could outbid refiner capex-payback economics on Q3-Q4 2026 contract renewals BEFORE WSR full export rules kick in 21 May 2027; (c) Hoboken EUR 400m capex execution risk during the 2026 maintenance shutdown overlap — capex slippage >9 months pushes payback into 2032+. T2 is secondary (weight 0.5) at theme prior 0.66 with current_contribution -0.2 — Belgian wage indexation (chemical-sector 2% step May/June 2026), TTF EUR 47/MWh and ETS EUR 75.5/tCO2 lift Hoboken's pyrometallurgical operating cost floor at exactly the moment EUR 300m of capex hits in 2027-2028; CBAM is supportive on the competitive side but cash impact deferred to Feb 2027. The 0.62 prior reflects: (i) the regime is observable and supportive TODAY (anchoring above 0.50), (ii) the bet is calibrated on NORMALISED economics — high-teens to mid-twenties ROCE, EUR 250-350m EBITA range — NOT the artefactual 208% headline ROCE / EUR -37m year-end CE distortion driven by the EUR 525m gold sale-and-leaseback, (iii) one asymmetric tail (Russian normalisation shock + gold correction simultaneously) keeps the prior below 0.70 because it would compress reported EBITDA AND trigger sale-and-leaseback margin-call mechanics in the same window, (iv) Hoboken franchise has structural advantages but is not unique (Aurubis Hamburg, Heraeus Hanau, JM US/EU refining all hold complex-feed capability per Macro). Net the bet is materially likelier to resolve true than false, but the gold-tailwind fragility + trader-incursion contested period prevent it sitting in the 0.70+ Strong-Resilient band.
bets.thesis_resolves_true: By FY2028 the Recycling franchise demonstrates: (i) NORMALISED Adjusted EBITA in the EUR 250-350m range (i.e. holding the FY2025 EUR 296m level on a normalised cost-of-capital basis, recognising that reported EBITA optically softens as the EUR 400m capex rebuilds capital employed and the gold-tailwind cyclically compresses 5-15% from peak 2025 levels), (ii) NORMALISED ROCE returning toward the high-teens / mid-twenties as capital employed rebuilds from the artefactual EUR -37m FY2025 year-end position (the 208% reported ROCE will MECHANICALLY normalise downward as the capex cycle hits the balance sheet — that's expected, not a break), (iii) Hoboken complex-feed throughput (tonnes processed) sustained at or above FY2025 levels through 2027-2028 with the 2026 shutdown completed on schedule and the EUR 400m capex programme on a 2030-2031 payback track, (iv) the EUR 525m gold sale-and-leaseback structure remains intact through resolution date with no premature unwind, no margin call, and gold not breaching the USD 1,800-2,000/oz CFO-stress threshold for sustained periods, (v) the PGM regime stays supportive across at least 4 of 6 T5 layers (Pt deficit, Russian containment, refiner pricing power, WSR/CRMA Western capture) — the bet does NOT require all 6 layers to hold; gold correcting modestly toward USD 3,800-4,000/oz EUR 3,300-3,500/oz with the Pt + WSR + Russian layers intact still resolves true, (vi) trader-vs-refiner feedstock competition (Glencore Metallium, Trafigura Nth Cycle) does not collapse compliant-feed contract pricing by more than 10-15% on 2027-2028 renewals before WSR full export rules (21 May 2027) discipline non-EU destination yards. Resolution-true does NOT require the artefactual 208% ROCE to persist — explicitly the opposite: the bet is calibrated on normalised economics. Resolution-true also does NOT require the 150 kt European battery recycling plant to be re-accelerated within the bet horizon (deferred to 2032+ under CORE plan, outside scope). The franchise is being defended and incrementally monetised, not transformed.
bets.thesis_resolves_false: B3 resolves false in three structurally different ways. Most likely: a slow-grind margin compression where (a) gold corrects 15-25% toward USD 3,500-3,800/oz / EUR 3,000-3,300/oz but not breaching the sale-and-leaseback margin-call zone, (b) trader-incursion bites Q3-Q4 2026 contract renewals and Western complex-feed feedstock pricing compresses 10-20% before WSR export rules go live in May 2027, (c) Belgian wage indexation + TTF rebound + ETS above EUR 90 lift Hoboken operating cost floor by EUR 8-15/MWh-equivalent, (d) the 2026 maintenance shutdown over-runs by 1-3 months absorbing fixed costs, (e) FY2027 normalised Recycling EBITA prints EUR 180-230m (compressed 20-35% from FY2025 EUR 296m) and FY2028 normalised ROCE drops below 15% — the franchise SURVIVES but the foundation-cushion underwriting capacity for BMS patience capital is materially weaker. Less likely but more abrupt: a Russian normalisation shock where Ukraine ceasefire + OFAC sectoral relief restore Norilsk's degraded throughput by mid-2027, Pt re-prices to USD 1,500/oz, Pd to USD 1,000/oz, the WC inventory revaluation triggers a margining-call pulse on the gold leaseback simultaneously (because gold typically corrects on real-rates rally that ALSO triggers PGM mean-reversion), Recycling EBITDA compresses 25-40% on price-driven inventory revaluation, FY2027 EBITA prints EUR 150-200m, and the BMS foundation cushion logic breaks. Tail-but-non-trivial: a gold cliff scenario where gold corrects past the USD 1,800-2,000/oz CFO-stress threshold within the bet horizon (Fed pivot to sustained higher-for-longer real rates above 2.5%, coordinated central-bank gold sales, EUR/USD strengthening above 1.25 amplifying EUR-denominated correction), the EUR 525m sale-and-leaseback structure faces margin call, the lease economics deteriorate as relet pricing resets, FY2027-2028 Recycling EBITA prints below EUR 180m, AND a cash-conversion stress fires (B7 territory) forcing the board into either a reverse-leaseback transaction at unfavourable terms or accelerated capex-pause. The chemistry of failure is that B3 does not break alone — it breaks at the same time as B7 (cash conversion), and through B7 it accelerates B1 (BMS) tail scenarios because the foundation cushion that funds BMS patience capital evaporates faster than the proof-gate matures.

transmission_mechanisms.reading_rule: Reading T5 named indicator 'WPIC Platinum Quarterly deficit estimate (annual koz)'. Current value 240 koz 2026 (4th consecutive year), revised UP not DOWN across multiple WPIC report cycles. +1 magnitude per WPIC cycle confirming sustained >200 koz deficit into 2027-2028 forecast; -1 per any WPIC quarterly print revising deficit DOWN to balanced/surplus. Norilsk Q1 2026 hard data (Pd -18% / Pt -26% YoY) is independent corroboration on the supply side. Bear threshold: WPIC 2027 forecast flipping platinum to balanced/surplus = T5 truth-likelihood compresses toward 0.45-0.55, B3 contribution from this layer flips negative.

Reading T5 named indicator 'Norilsk PGM production (Pd + Pt, Moz annualised)' AND 'OFAC Russian-metals SDN list status'. Current state: Norilsk parent NOT OFAC-designated but logistics (Murmansk port banned, EU 20th package), equipment imports, payment rails degraded enough to translate into Q1 2026 Pd -18% / Pt -26% YoY. FY2026 guidance Pd -10-11% / Pt -5-8%. +1 magnitude per quarter Norilsk YoY decline >5% sustained; -2 magnitude on Ukraine ceasefire framework + OFAC sectoral relief on Russian non-ferrous metals (T5 escalation_path 'russian_pgm_normalisation_shock' fires, T5 TL re-rates to 0.45-0.55, B3 contribution from this layer flips sharply negative). The asymmetry is that sanctions can escalate (additional packages, secondary-sanctions enforcement) far more easily than they can be unwound while Ukraine war continues — so the central path is containment-persists.

Reading T5 named indicator 'Gold spot price (EUR/oz)'. Current value ~EUR 4,000/oz (USD 4,592/oz at EUR/USD ~1.15) on May 2026 spot, ~EUR 500/oz cushion above the EUR 3,500/oz threshold below which Recycling EBITDA compresses by EUR 70-100m per EUR 500/oz of decline (Umicore disclosure). +0.5 magnitude per quarter gold sustained >EUR 4,000/oz; -1 magnitude per EUR 500/oz decline below threshold. Bear threshold: gold sustained <USD 3,800/oz / EUR 3,300/oz for two consecutive months = T5 escalation_path 'gold_correction_to_2500_eur_oz' fires. CFO-stress-test reference at USD 1,800-2,000/oz is the SALE-AND-LEASEBACK MARGIN-CALL zone (~50-55% downside from current spot) — at that level B3 breaks via cash-conversion stress, not just margin compression. This is the cyclical not structural component of T5; consensus is over-confident on the gold-correction scenario per T5 deep-read.

Reading T5 named indicator 'Aurubis quarterly refining-margin / metal-result share of gross margin'. Current value: 45% of group gross margin Q1 2025/26 (was 36% prior year), PGM production +27% YoY to 1.55t. This is the cleanest direct read-across to Hoboken economics — Aurubis is the largest European complex-feed peer. +1 magnitude per quarter Aurubis metal-results gross-margin share >40% sustained; -1 magnitude per quarter <30% sustained (trader-diverted feedstock biting). Bear threshold: Glencore Metallium ramp ahead of schedule + Trafigura announcing additional PGM-specific feedstock offtake deals + Aurubis Q3 2025/26 guidance signalling refining-margin compression = T5 escalation_path 'trader_diverts_western_scrap_stream' fires, B3 conversion margin compresses 15-25% on 2027-2028 contract renewals.

Reading T5 named indicator 'EU Waste Shipment Regulation enforcement status'. Digital procedures applicable 21 May 2026 (already crossed); full export rules 21 May 2027 (inside bet horizon, positive catalyst); Environmental Crime Directive in force; CRMA 25% secondary-supply target by 2030 with 6 PGM-specific strategic projects + 15-month permitting. +0.5 magnitude per quarter EU-internal scrap retention statistics show >85% (BIR/EERA data); +1 on first member-state customs seizure case publicised; -1 on member-state postponement of WSR export rules. The WSR + CRMA + EU plastic-export-ban-Nov-2026 precedent jointly create a structural Western-capture regime materially supportive of integrated complex-feed refining economics through 2027-2030. Per T5 deep-read, this is treated by sell-side as 2027-2030 regulatory tailwind; we read it as already affecting trader-vs-refiner positioning conversations and feedstock contract pricing in Q2-Q3 2026 because the digital-procedure go-live creates immediate compliance asymmetry.

Reading T5 prior_rationale on working-capital regime + B7 propagation rule (T5 weight 0.5 to B7). Current state: gold + PGM rally drives WC build; FY2025 WC consumption EUR 261m (visible failure-mode of the metal-management capability per pos_capability_metal_management_hedging). With Pt at USD 2,000/oz, Pd at USD 1,545/oz, gold at ~EUR 4,000/oz, WC consumption sits at upper end of the ±EUR 250m/cycle envelope but not breaching. -1 magnitude per +EUR 100m WC build above EUR 250m/cycle envelope; +1 per WC release from PGM/gold price moderation. The two-sided dynamic is: a Russian normalisation shock would TRIGGER simultaneous WC unwind (release) + inventory mark-down (negative EBITDA event) — net negative for B3 in a single window. The metal-management capability stress-tested on this scenario is unproven.

T2 propagation rule for B3 (T2 v2 weight 0.5): '-1 magnitude per +EUR 10/MWh in TTF above EUR 50 sustained one quarter; -0.5 per +EUR 20/tCO2 in ETS above EUR 80 sustained; +0.5 if NW EU baseload day-ahead anchors below EUR 30 sustained four quarters; +0.3 if CBAM Q3/Q4 2026 reference price holds above EUR 80 (competitive moat deepens)'. Current state: TTF EUR 47.4/MWh (rebound on Iran blockade), EU ETS EUR 75.5/tCO2 (highest since Feb 2026, +13% in 4 weeks), CBAM Q1 2026 ref EUR 75.36/tCO2, NW EU baseload day-ahead EUR 32/MWh (supportive on power-side). Composite: cost-floor lift visible at exactly the moment EUR 300m of EUR 400m envelope hits 2027-2028. Bear threshold: TTF >EUR 60 sustained one quarter + ETS >EUR 100/tCO2 for two consecutive auctions = T2 escalation_path 'second_brent_or_ttf_shock' + 'carbon_market_stress' fire concurrently, Hoboken EBITDA compresses by EUR 30-60m/year independent of PGM-side dynamics.

T2 propagation rule for B3: '-0.3 if Belgian health-index YoY above +3.5%'. Current state: Joint Committee 111/207 chemical-sector indexation 2% step expected May/June 2026; cent-index cap reform 1 June 2026; 2025-2026 pay norm set at 0% by Royal Decree (negotiated wage growth frozen, automatic indexation continues). Hoboken sits inside Belgian automatic indexation. -0.3 magnitude if Belgian health-index YoY >+3.5% sustained; indexation drag is mechanically additive on top of energy + carbon costs. Note: Hoboken is BELGIAN (Belgium), so does NOT benefit from German Industriestrompreis EUR 50/MWh relief approved 16 April 2026 — the asymmetric national relief actively disadvantages Hoboken vs German complex-feed peers (Aurubis Hamburg in particular).

T2 named indicator 'German Industriestrompreis execution status': approved 16 April 2026 by EU Commission, EUR 50/MWh cap, 91 sectors, 2026-2028 window, retroactive applications from 2027 for 2026 consumption. Hoboken (Belgium) is NOT eligible — sits OUTSIDE the German scheme. Aurubis Hamburg (the largest comparable European complex-feed peer) IS eligible. The relative disadvantage is silent but real: ~EUR 30-50/MWh effective power-cost gap on the eligible 50% of consumption. -0.3 magnitude per quarter German-only relief persists; +0.5 if Belgian equivalent state-aid scheme matching German EUR 50/MWh approved (Belgian industry federations' state-aid notifications under expanded TCTF would be the readable signal). The intra-EU competitive map fragmenting against Hoboken is a real but moderate-magnitude drag on the EUR 400m capex payback economics.

T2 supportive channel for B3 (offsetting): CBAM permanent phase started 1 Jan 2026 at EUR 75.36/cert Q1 2026; certificate sales begin 1 Feb 2027 (cash impact deferred but liability accruing). +0.3 magnitude per quarter CBAM reference price holds above EUR 80 (deepens competitive moat against non-EU PGM-bearing material imports); +0.5 on Q3/Q4 2026 reference >EUR 90 sustained. Hoboken benefits relative to Asian destination yards on imported-PGM material competition. The CBAM cash-impact deferral to Feb 2027 means the supportive effect lags into the bet horizon at the same time as WSR full export rules (21 May 2027) — a coincident positive catalyst cluster in H1 2027.

scenarios.name: central_path_regime_holds_normalised_franchise_durability
scenarios.narrative: T5 base case 'regime_holds_supportive' (50% T5 prob) maps onto B3. PGM regime stays supportive across at least 5 of 6 layers through resolution: Pt deficit confirmed 5th consecutive year (WPIC 2027 cycle), Russian PGM containment intact (no Ukraine ceasefire framework before late 2027), refiner pricing power HOLDS (Aurubis metal-results gross-margin share remains >35% despite Glencore Metallium + Trafigura Nth Cycle ramp), WSR full export rules go live 21 May 2027 on schedule with first member-state seizure cases publicised, CBAM weekly pricing from Feb 2027 holds above EUR 80. Gold softens modestly to EUR 3,800-4,000/oz on real-rates re-rating but stays well above the EUR 3,500/oz compression threshold. Hoboken 2026 maintenance shutdown completes on schedule (Q1 2027 throughput recovery). EUR 400m capex programme on-schedule with EUR ~250m spent by H1 2027, payback path intact for 2030-2031. Belgian wage indexation 2% step (May/June 2026) and TTF EUR 47/MWh + ETS EUR 75-90/tCO2 lift Hoboken cost floor by EUR 5-10/MWh-equivalent but absorbed within the regime's tailwind cushion. Recycling reported ROCE mechanically normalises downward as capex rebuilds CE — FY2027 reported ROCE prints in the 30-50% range (recovering from 208% artefactual), FY2028 in the high-teens / mid-20s normalised range as expected. FY2027 EBITA EUR 270-310m, FY2028 EBITA EUR 250-300m. Foundation cushion HOLDS at EUR ~570-650m combined Catalysis+Recycling EBITA, sufficient to fund BMS patience capital through Q3 2027 proof-gate. Sale-and-leaseback structure intact through resolution.
scenarios.what_makes_this_path_break: Russian normalisation shock (Ukraine ceasefire framework + OFAC sectoral relief) firing in 2027 — pulls B3 toward 'russian_normalisation_inventory_unwind' scenario. OR gold correction past USD 3,800/oz / EUR 3,300/oz for two consecutive months — pulls toward 'gold_compression_slow_grind'. OR Glencore Metallium ramp ahead of schedule + Trafigura PGM-specific feedstock deals collapsing 2027 contract pricing >15% before WSR full export rules go live — pulls toward 'trader_incursion_compresses_renewals'. OR Hoboken EUR 400m capex over-run >15% with schedule slippage >9 months pushing payback into 2032+ — independent break-shape via execution risk.

scenarios.name: gold_compression_slow_grind_franchise_survives_cushion_weakens
scenarios.narrative: T5 'gold_correction_partial_compression' (20% T5 prob) materialises but does NOT breach margin-call threshold. Gold corrects 15-25% to USD 3,500-3,800/oz / EUR 3,000-3,300/oz on Fed sustaining higher-for-longer real rates above 2.5% and EUR/USD strengthening past 1.20 amplifying EUR-denominated correction. Recycling reported EBITDA compresses ~EUR 70-100m per EUR 500/oz of gold decline (Umicore disclosure) — FY2027-2028 EBITA prints EUR 200-250m range (down 15-30% from FY2025 EUR 296m). The EUR 525m sale-and-leaseback structure remains intact (margin-call threshold ~USD 1,800-2,000/oz NOT breached), but ongoing lease-cost drag is more painful at compressed EBITDA levels. Pt deficit holds at WPIC 200+ koz, Russian containment intact, WSR/CRMA Western capture deploys on schedule — these layers cushion but do NOT offset the gold-side compression. Trader-incursion bites Q3-Q4 2026 contract renewals at the upper end of the 5-15% compression range. Hoboken maintenance shutdown completes within 2-3 month window. EUR 400m capex programme on-schedule but payback re-rates from 2030-2031 to 2031-2033. FY2028 normalised ROCE prints 12-17%, just below the high-teens target. Foundation cushion compresses to EUR ~480-550m combined — STILL funds BMS patience capital but with markedly less margin of safety. The franchise SURVIVES; the underwriting capacity for BMS tail scenarios is materially weaker.
scenarios.what_makes_this_path_break: Gold corrects further past USD 1,800-2,000/oz (CFO stress reference) — pulls toward 'gold_cliff_margin_call_b7_contagion'. OR Russian normalisation shock concurrent with gold correction (the genuinely tail-shaped intersection) — pulls toward 'russian_normalisation_inventory_unwind'. OR T5 trader-diverts scenario fires concurrently — combined regime + cyclical compression intensifies past slow-grind into franchise-stress.

scenarios.name: russian_normalisation_inventory_unwind_pgm_mean_reversion
scenarios.narrative: T5 'russian_normalisation_shock' (10% T5 prob, consensus under-prices by 5-10pp per T5 deep-read) fires inside the bet horizon. Ukraine ceasefire framework agreed at Geneva or Istanbul level in H2 2026 leads to OFAC sectoral relief on Russian non-ferrous metals; Norilsk restores 50-70% of degraded throughput by mid-2027. Pt re-prices toward USD 1,500/oz, Pd below USD 1,200/oz; SA primary tightness no longer binding on global pricing. Loaded-catalyst inventory values mark down across Western refiners (Hoboken inventory revaluation); WC unwind triggers margining-call pulse on the gold sale-and-leaseback simultaneously because gold typically corrects on real-rates rally that ALSO triggers PGM mean-reversion. Recycling EBITDA compresses 25-40% on price-driven inventory revaluation independent of operational changes. Refiner pricing power softens (Aurubis metal-results gross-margin share <30%) as Russian flows reduce PGM market tightness. Gold tailwind partially offsets if the rally triggering Russian normalisation is geopolitical-de-risking-driven (gold falls but stays >EUR 3,500/oz) — but if the rally is Fed-real-rates-driven (gold breaks lower simultaneously), both sides compress in the same window. FY2027 EBITA prints EUR 150-200m, FY2028 EUR 170-220m as Russian flows partially absorb but Western capture lock (WSR full export rules 21 May 2027) starts to compensate. Foundation cushion drops to EUR ~400-470m — INSUFFICIENT to fund full BMS patience capital through Q3 2027 proof-gate; B1 tail scenarios accelerate. Hoboken franchise still intact operationally but margin compression bites payback economics; EUR 400m capex programme reviewed mid-cycle for partial pause/defer.
scenarios.what_makes_this_path_break: Ukraine ceasefire framework stalling or being reversed (T5 escalation_path 'russian_pgm_normalisation_shock' named_triggers all UNFIRING) — pulls back toward central_path. OR LME re-listing of Russian PGM warrants delayed past resolution date — degrades the price-pass-through speed of the shock and partially offsets via WSR/CRMA Western-capture compensation. OR foundation cushion preserved via concurrent Catalysis ROCE upside (Euro 7 content uplift firing strongly) — partially offsets via B2 cushion-replacement.

scenarios.name: trader_incursion_compresses_renewals_franchise_durability_test
scenarios.narrative: T5 'trader_diverts_western_scrap_stream' (10% T5 prob) fires before WSR full export rules go live 21 May 2027. Glencore Metallium FID confirmed and ramping ahead of schedule (Texas 2,400 t/yr feedstock); Glencore Li-Cycle Rochester hub fully operational; Trafigura announces additional PGM-specific feedstock offtake deals beyond the April 2026 USD 1.1bn Nth Cycle base. Aurubis Q3 2025/26 forward-quarter guidance signals refining-margin compression; Heraeus + JM US/EU peer set follows. Hoboken Q3-Q4 2026 contract renewals price 15-25% below 2024-2025 vintage on PGM/complex-feed feedstock. Belgian asymmetric Industriestrompreis disadvantage compounds (Aurubis Hamburg uses German EUR 50/MWh relief to bid feedstock more aggressively). Recycling EBITDA compresses ~EUR 50-90m on contract-pricing alone, independent of price/volume. Pt deficit + Russian containment + gold tailwind regimes hold but the conversion margin captured by refiners shrinks. WSR full export rules eventually go live 21 May 2027 and PARTIALLY restore refiner pricing power H2 2027 onward — but the 12-18 month contested period through H1 2027 leaves a structural EBITA scar. FY2027 EBITA prints EUR 180-230m, FY2028 EBITA recovers partially to EUR 220-270m as WSR enforcement bites. Hoboken EUR 400m capex programme proceeds but payback re-rates to 2031-2033. Foundation cushion EUR ~440-510m — sufficient for BMS patience capital but no margin of safety. The bet's claim of 'durability + monetisation' is materially weakened on the monetisation side; durability still holds.
scenarios.what_makes_this_path_break: WSR full export rules postponement (member-state opposition or major Commission carve-out) — extends contested period past 2027-2028 and pulls toward gold_compression_slow_grind. OR Glencore Metallium ramp slips OR Trafigura Nth Cycle deal restructures — pulls back toward central_path. OR EU Battery Passport Feb 2027 + WSR digital procedures combine into preferred-supplier procurement language at OEMs that protects refiner-side feedstock pricing — partial offset. OR Belgian state-aid scheme matching German EUR 50/MWh approved (T2 deescalation_path 'clean_industrial_deal_breakthrough') — neutralises the asymmetric national relief disadvantage on Hoboken cost side.

scenarios.name: gold_cliff_margin_call_b7_contagion_foundation_breaks
scenarios.narrative: Tail scenario where gold corrects past the CFO-stress USD 1,800-2,000/oz reference (~50-55% downside from current spot) within the bet horizon. Drivers: Fed pivots to sustained higher-for-longer real rates above 2.5%; coordinated central-bank gold-sales programme; EUR/USD strengthening to 1.30+ amplifying EUR-denominated correction; concurrent Ukraine ceasefire de-risking removes geopolitical premium. Gold falls below USD 2,000/oz on EUR-denominated basis (~EUR 1,750/oz) — well below the EUR 3,500/oz EBITDA-compression threshold. The EUR 525m sale-and-leaseback structure faces margin call: ongoing lease economics deteriorate as relet pricing resets, the recirculating-inventory lease costs become unsustainable at the compressed gold price. Recycling reported EBITDA compresses by EUR 250-350m vs 2025 baseline (Umicore EUR 70-100m per EUR 500/oz disclosure x ~2,500 EUR/oz cumulative decline). Cash-conversion stress fires (B7 territory) — board faces choice between (a) reverse-leaseback transaction at unfavourable terms (recapitalising the structure with cash to avoid margin call), (b) accelerated capex-pause on Hoboken EUR 400m programme, (c) emergency BMS kill-criteria firing pre-emptively to reallocate cash. Foundation cushion compresses to EUR ~250-350m — INSUFFICIENT for BMS patience capital; B1 tail scenario 'tail_governance_failure_premature_kill' (5% B1 prob) becomes the modal B1 outcome. T2 second-energy-shock concurrent (TTF >EUR 60, ETS >EUR 100/tCO2) plausible if the Fed-tightening macro is ALSO recessionary — exacerbates. FY2028 Recycling EBITA prints EUR 80-150m, normalised ROCE single-digits. Hoboken franchise still intact operationally but capex-financing impaired; EUR 400m programme partially deferred; payback path lost.
scenarios.what_makes_this_path_break: Gold corrects only modestly (15-25%) without breaching margin-call threshold — pulls back to gold_compression_slow_grind. OR Fed reverses real-rates trajectory before gold breaches USD 2,500/oz — pulls toward central_path. OR coordinated central-bank gold-sales programme NOT materialising — gold floor holds at USD 3,000-3,500/oz despite real-rates pressure. OR board executes emergency reverse-leaseback at modestly-unfavourable but not-stressed terms — partial recovery via cash-conversion path.

scenarios.name: structural_supply_reset_strengthens_western_capture_executes_cleanly
scenarios.narrative: T5 'structural_supply_reset_strengthens' + 'western_capture_executes_cleanly' (combined ~10% T5 prob, but conditional on B3-specific path that requires multiple supportive triggers concurrent). WPIC 2027 forecast confirms 5th consecutive Pt deficit above 200 koz; JM May 2026 PGM Market Report extends platinum + rhodium deficit horizon to 2028 with palladium maintaining tightness; Norilsk FY2026 actual production undershoots -10-11% Pd guidance with grade-decline acceleration. Concurrently EU WSR full export rules go live 21 May 2027 with first BIR/EERA EU-internal scrap retention statistics >90% and Commission DG ENV bulletin confirming WSR enforcement priorities for PGM/e-scrap. CRMA strategic-project disbursement on schedule with first PGM project FIDs. China General Administration of Customs scrap import data showing PGM/e-scrap volumes from EU declining >30% YoY. Refiner pricing power INTENSIFIES — Aurubis metal-results gross-margin share rises to 50%+; Hoboken 2027-2028 contract pricing prints UP 10-15% on legacy 2024-2025 vintage. Gold holds at EUR 4,000+/oz on continued geopolitical / real-rate tailwinds. Hoboken EUR 400m capex programme accelerates payback to 2029-2030. FY2027 EBITA EUR 320-380m, FY2028 EBITA EUR 330-400m. Normalised ROCE prints 22-30% on rebuilt CE base. Foundation cushion EUR ~700-800m combined Catalysis+Recycling — provides material underwriting capacity for BMS patience capital and re-rates the group ROCE optics decisively.
scenarios.what_makes_this_path_break: Russian normalisation shock or trader-incursion concurrent with the supportive layers — partial offset, pulls back toward central_path. OR EU member-state postponement or weakening of WSR export rules — degrades Western-capture lock. OR Hoboken 2026 maintenance shutdown over-run absorbing the windfall on lost-throughput. OR Belgian asymmetric Industriestrompreis disadvantage compounds even in the supportive regime — partial offset on cost side.

named_watch_metrics.metric: Platinum spot price (USD/oz) and Pt deficit cycle (WPIC Platinum Quarterly)
named_watch_metrics.source: LBMA Platinum + WPIC Platinum Quarterly (28 April 2026 release confirmed 240 koz 2026 deficit, 4th consecutive year)
named_watch_metrics.current_value: USD 1,999.70/oz (May 2026 Crow's Nest scan); WPIC 240 koz 2026 deficit forecast (revised UP from balanced)
named_watch_metrics.threshold_implications: Bull: WPIC 2027 cycle confirms 5th consecutive Pt deficit above 200 koz AND/OR Pt sustained >USD 2,200/oz for two consecutive quarters. Bear: WPIC quarterly print revising deficit DOWN to balanced/surplus AND/OR Pt sustained <USD 1,500/oz on Russian normalisation flows. Critical gate: any WPIC quarterly print FLIPPING platinum to balanced or surplus = T5 truth-likelihood compresses to 0.45-0.55 and B3 contribution from this layer flips materially negative.

named_watch_metrics.metric: Palladium spot price (USD/oz) and Norilsk PGM production trajectory
named_watch_metrics.source: LBMA Palladium + Nornickel quarterly production releases (Q1 2026 print 28 April 2026 confirmed Pd -18% YoY / Pt -26% YoY; FY2026 guidance Pd -10-11%, Pt -5-8%)
named_watch_metrics.current_value: USD 1,545.50/oz palladium (May 2026 scan); Norilsk Q1 2026 Pd -14% QoQ / -18% YoY
named_watch_metrics.threshold_implications: Bull: Norilsk full-year FY2026 actual undershooting -10-11% Pd guidance AND/OR Pd holding USD 1,400-1,700/oz reflecting JM-confirmed supply-rebalancing rather than over-supply. Bear: Pd recovering to USD 1,800+/oz on Russian normalisation flows resuming AND/OR Norilsk Q2 2026 print reversing the Q1 production decline — earliest readable signal of Russian containment NOT being intact. Trigger: Ukraine ceasefire framework + LME re-listing of Russian PGM warrants in same quarter = T5 'russian_pgm_normalisation_shock' fires.

named_watch_metrics.metric: Gold spot price (USD/oz and EUR/oz vs EUR 3,500 threshold)
named_watch_metrics.source: LBMA Gold + ECB EUR/USD reference rate (May 2026: USD 4,592/oz, EUR/USD ~1.15, EUR ~4,000/oz)
named_watch_metrics.current_value: ~EUR 4,000/oz on May 2026 spot; ~EUR 500/oz cushion above EUR 3,500/oz EBITDA-compression threshold
named_watch_metrics.threshold_implications: Bull: gold sustained >EUR 4,000/oz for four consecutive quarters; +0.5 magnitude per such quarter. Bear (Umicore-disclosed): EBITDA compresses by EUR 70-100m per EUR 500/oz of decline below threshold. Critical gate (CFO stress reference): gold sustained <USD 1,800-2,000/oz / EUR 1,500-1,750/oz = SALE-AND-LEASEBACK MARGIN-CALL ZONE; structure could be margin-called, triggering B7 cash-conversion stress. Single most leveraged input on B3.

named_watch_metrics.metric: Aurubis metal-results gross-margin share (refiner pricing power proxy for Hoboken)
named_watch_metrics.source: Aurubis quarterly reports + investor calls (Q1 2025/26 Feb 2026: 45% of group gross margin, was 36% prior year; PGM production +27% YoY to 1.55t)
named_watch_metrics.current_value: 45% of group gross margin Q1 2025/26 (highest reading in tracked window)
named_watch_metrics.threshold_implications: Bull: sustained metal-result share >40% across two consecutive quarters confirms refiner pricing power intact at Hoboken's most direct European complex-feed peer. Bear: print <30% sustained for >2 quarters = T5 'trader_diverts_western_scrap_stream' fires; concurrent reading rule says Glencore Metallium / Trafigura Nth Cycle balance-sheet capacity outbidding refiner capex-payback economics. Earliest readable signal of trader-vs-refiner contested-period dynamics (Q3-Q4 2026 contract renewal window before WSR full export rules 21 May 2027).

named_watch_metrics.metric: Refining tolling spreads / complex-feed feedstock contract pricing (BIR + EERA + Platts indices)
named_watch_metrics.source: BIR (Bureau of International Recycling) + EERA + Platts CAM Daily / PGM concentrate pricing + Recycling Today data
named_watch_metrics.current_value: Not directly published; T5 deep-read confirms refiner pricing power currently HOLDING but trader incursion (Glencore Li-Cycle / Metallium, Trafigura Nth Cycle) is concrete and ramping into 2027-2028
named_watch_metrics.threshold_implications: Bull: BIR or EERA reporting EU-internal scrap retention ratios above 85% Q3-Q4 2026 = WSR digital-procedure go-live (21 May 2026) is biting; complex-feed feedstock contract pricing improves +5-10% on H2 2026 renewals. Bear: 2027 Hoboken contract renewals print 15-25% below 2024-2025 vintage = trader-incursion scenario fires; Recycling EBITDA compresses EUR 50-90m on pricing alone. China General Administration of Customs scrap import data showing PGM/e-scrap volumes from EU declining >30% YoY = supportive Western-capture executing.

named_watch_metrics.metric: Hoboken complex-feed throughput (tonnes processed) — segment-level quarterly disclosure
named_watch_metrics.source: Umicore quarterly trading updates + half-year results + segment-level FCF disclosure starting 24 April 2026 (Convergence-prescribed, pos_governance_segment_fcf_disclosure_commitment)
named_watch_metrics.current_value: Not separately disclosed at as_of 2026-05-04; first quarterly disclosure under Convergence-prescribed regime expected at the 24 April 2026 Q1 trading update
named_watch_metrics.threshold_implications: Bull: Q3-Q4 2026 throughput sustained at or above FY2025 levels with 2026 maintenance shutdown completing Q4 2026 on schedule; Q1 2027 throughput recovers to pre-shutdown levels within 8-12 weeks. Bear: shutdown over-runs by >3 months, OR throughput post-restart prints <85% of pre-shutdown for two consecutive quarters = lost fixed-cost absorption + permitting tail-risk indication. Critical: implementation of segment-level FCF disclosure ITSELF is a leading governance signal; non-implementation by Q3 2026 is bear (re-prices the bet's auditability).

named_watch_metrics.metric: EU WSR enforcement statistics + first member-state customs seizure cases (Western-capture lock readability)
named_watch_metrics.source: European Commission DG ENV bulletins + national customs authorities + BIR / EERA / Recycling Today data + China General Administration of Customs scrap-import statistics
named_watch_metrics.current_value: Digital procedures applicable 21 May 2026 (already crossed at as_of); full export rules 21 May 2027 (inside bet horizon, positive catalyst); Environmental Crime Directive in force
named_watch_metrics.threshold_implications: Bull: enforcement statistics through Q3-Q4 2026 showing >90% retention of EU PGM/e-scrap; major customs seizure cases publicised; first DG ENV bulletin confirming WSR enforcement priorities for PGM/e-scrap. Bear: major export-rule postponement ahead of 21 May 2027 go-live; Commission carve-out for major member states; BIR / EERA reporting EU-internal scrap retention below 75% = T5 escalation fires on Western-capture layer.

named_watch_metrics.metric: TTF gas + EU ETS composite (Hoboken pyrometallurgical cost-floor)
named_watch_metrics.source: ICE Endex TTF futures (front-month + 12-month strip) + EEX EUA auctions + ICE EUA futures + EU Commission DG TAXUD CBAM portal (Q1 2026 first official price EUR 75.36/cert)
named_watch_metrics.frequency: weekly (TTF) + daily/auction days (ETS) + quarterly (CBAM, weekly from 2027)
named_watch_metrics.current_value: TTF EUR 47.4/MWh (May 2026, +40.3% YoY); EU ETS EUR 75.5/tCO2 (highest since Feb 2026, +13% in 4 weeks); CBAM Q1 2026 cert EUR 75.36/tCO2
named_watch_metrics.threshold_implications: Bear: TTF >EUR 60/MWh sustained one quarter + ETS >EUR 100/tCO2 for two consecutive auctions = T2 'second_brent_or_ttf_shock' + 'carbon_market_stress' fire concurrently; Hoboken EBITDA compresses EUR 30-60m/year independent of PGM dynamics. Bull: TTF curve flattening below EUR 35/MWh across 12-month strip + ETS settling EUR 65-80/tCO2 = T2 'structural_renormalisation_continued' supports payback economics. CBAM Q3/Q4 2026 reference >EUR 80 sustained = competitive moat for Hoboken vs Asian destination yards (T2 +0.3 contribution to B3).

expected_projection_topics: Recycling normalised EBITA trajectory FY2026-2028 (EUR 250-350m base case) net of gold cyclical compression and capex-driven CE rebuild
Hoboken 2026 maintenance shutdown completion + Q1 2027 throughput recovery + EUR 400m capex programme execution against 2030-2031 payback
EUR 525m gold sale-and-leaseback structure resilience vs gold price cycle and CFO USD 1,800-2,000/oz stress reference
Pt deficit / Russian containment / refiner pricing power persistence (T5 5-of-6 supportive layers) through resolution
EU WSR full export rules go-live 21 May 2027 + CBAM weekly pricing Feb 2027 + EU Battery Passport Feb 2027 — Western-capture lock execution
Trader-vs-refiner Q3-Q4 2026 / 2027-2028 contract-renewal contested-period (Glencore Metallium, Trafigura Nth Cycle)
Foundation cushion durability (Recycling + Catalysis EBITA combined) underwriting BMS patience capital through Q3 2027 proof-gate
Belgian asymmetric Industriestrompreis disadvantage vs Aurubis Hamburg + chemical-sector wage indexation drag on Hoboken cost floor
falsification_criteria: Gold sale-and-leaseback structure margin-called or unwound at unfavourable terms within bet horizon (gold breaches USD 1,800-2,000/oz CFO-stress reference) — converts B3 from margin-compression bet into a cash-conversion crisis (B7 territory).

FY2027 OR FY2028 Recycling normalised Adjusted EBITA prints below EUR 180m (i.e. >40% compression from FY2025 EUR 296m baseline) — franchise economics break below the foundation-cushion-supports-BMS-patience-capital threshold.

Hoboken 2026 maintenance shutdown over-runs by >3 months OR EUR 400m capex programme cost over-run >15% with schedule slippage >9 months pushing payback into 2032+ — execution risk break-shape independent of regime/cyclical.

WPIC quarterly print FLIPPING platinum to balanced/surplus AND/OR JM 2027 PGM Market Report extending the deficit horizon turning bearish — T5 load-bearing structural layer breaks; refiner pricing power thesis is not sustainable on 1-of-6 supportive layers.

Aurubis metal-results gross-margin share prints <30% sustained for >2 quarters AND/OR Hoboken 2027 contract renewals price 15-25% below 2024-2025 vintage — trader-incursion captures the contested period before WSR full export rules go live, refiner pricing power proxy collapses.

Ukraine ceasefire framework + OFAC sectoral relief on Russian non-ferrous metals + LME re-listing of Russian PGM warrants in same window — Russian containment layer breaks structurally; Norilsk restoration triggers concurrent PGM mean-reversion + WC-unwind margining-call pulse.

EU WSR full export rules postponed past 21 May 2027 OR Commission issues major carve-out for member states OR enforcement statistics through Q4 2026 show <75% EU-internal scrap retention — Western-capture lock layer breaks.

Foundation cushion (Catalysis + Recycling combined Adjusted EBITA) compresses below EUR 450m FY2027 — INSUFFICIENT to fund BMS patience capital through Q3 2027 proof-gate; B1 tail scenario 'tail_governance_failure_premature_kill' fires through B3 contagion path.

Segment-level FCF disclosure NOT implemented by Q3 2026 (Convergence-prescribed regime fails) AND/OR the disclosure does NOT separate gold sale-and-leaseback gain cleanly from underlying cash conversion — governance failure mode that prevents the artefactual-vs-normalised ROCE distinction from being independently auditable; functionally falsifies the bet's readable-signal mechanism.

Belgian 2 μg/dl lead-emission standard dispute escalates into material permitting tightening at Hoboken during 2026 maintenance shutdown window — independent operational break-shape via social-licence-to-operate channel.
bets.breakage_shape: When B3 breaks, the most likely break-shape is a 12-18 month slow grind on the gold-tailwind plus trader-incursion axes simultaneously: gold corrects 15-25% to USD 3,500-3,800/oz / EUR 3,000-3,300/oz on Fed sustained higher-for-longer; Glencore Metallium + Trafigura Nth Cycle bite Q3-Q4 2026 contract renewals at the upper end of the 5-15% compression range BEFORE WSR full export rules kick in May 2027; Belgian wage indexation + TTF rebound + ETS lift Hoboken cost floor by EUR 8-15/MWh-equivalent; the 2026 maintenance shutdown over-runs 1-3 months absorbing fixed costs; FY2027 normalised EBITA prints EUR 180-230m (compressed 20-35% from FY2025 EUR 296m) and FY2028 normalised ROCE drops below 15%. The franchise SURVIVES; the foundation-cushion underwriting capacity for BMS patience capital is materially weaker — and through that channel, BMS tail scenarios accelerate (B1 'tail_governance_failure_premature_kill' probability re-rates from 5% to 15-20%).
