The sharper alternative break-shape (Russian normalisation shock + concurrent gold cliff) compresses this to 6-9 months around two coincident inflection events: a Ukraine ceasefire framework agreed at Geneva or Istanbul (typical 4-8 week pricing-in window) cascades to Pt re-pricing toward USD 1,500/oz, Pd toward USD 1,000/oz, simultaneous WC-inventory unwind triggering margining-call pulse on the EUR 525m gold sale-and-leaseback. Recycling EBITDA compresses 25-40% on price-driven inventory revaluation independent of operational changes; if gold ALSO breaks past USD 1,800-2,000/oz in the same window (Fed pivot to sustained higher-for-longer being the typical co-trigger), the structure faces margin call and the bet converts from margin-compression failure into cash-conversion crisis (B7 territory). Either path culminates in foundation cushion (Catalysis + Recycling combined EBITA) compressing from EUR 679m baseline toward EUR 400-500m — enough to keep the Hoboken franchise operational at scale but insufficient to provide margin of safety for the BMS Q3 2027 proof-gate window. The tightest-coupling failure mode through the bet network is: B3 breaks → foundation cushion compresses → BMS patience capital evaporates faster than proof-gate matures → board fires kill criteria pre-emptively → B1 'tail_governance_failure_premature_kill' becomes the modal B1 outcome. B3 + B2 jointly underwrite the BMS bet: failure of either compresses BMS optionality independent of BMS-specific dynamics.

baseline_research_note.claim_under_test: Through FY2028 the Recycling franchise (Hoboken-anchored complex-feed PGM refining + integrated Catalysis-Recycling PGM loop) sustains complex-feed throughput, the EUR 525m gold sale-and-leaseback structure remains intact (no premature unwind, no margin call), the EUR 400m Hoboken 2025-2028 capex programme completes on plan with EUR ~300m hitting 2027-2028, and Recycling normalised Adjusted EBITA holds in the EUR 250-350m range at high-teens / mid-20s normalised ROCE — i.e. the foundation cushion that jointly with B2 underwrites the BMS patience capital does NOT compress before the BMS Q3 2027 proof-gate matures.
baseline_research_note.what_is_known_today: FY2025 Recycling reported revenue EUR 947m (+4.4% YoY, 26.6% of group), Adjusted EBITA EUR 296m (31.2% margin, 51.1% of group EBITA), reported ROCE 208% on EUR -37m year-end capital employed — the 208% figure is ARTEFACTUAL (collapsed CE post EUR 525m gold sale-and-leaseback, EUR 486m EBITDA impact / EUR 365m EBIT impact / EUR 383m after-tax gain in FY2025). Macro stream explicitly notes 'normalized ROCE is in the high-teens to mid-twenties' and Convergence stream confirms 'The 208% ROCE is distorted by the gold inventory sale, which reduced Recycling's capital employed to -EUR 37 million at year-end.' Hoboken EUR 400m 2025-2028 capex programme is committed with EUR ~300m concentrated in 2027-2028 and ~6-year payback (window 2030-2031). FY2026 brings a scheduled Hoboken maintenance shutdown (one-off EBITDA hit EUR ~30-60m, magnitude estimate). The integrated Catalysis-Recycling PGM loop is the most named structural moat in the portfolio (pos_technology_hoboken_complex_feed_loop). Foundation cushion: Recycling EUR 296m + Catalysis EUR 383m = EUR 679m FY2025 Adjusted EBIT, the load-bearing financial position underwriting BMS losses. Net debt fell EUR 1,425m → EUR 1,357m FY2025 (1.6x leverage) — but Convergence flags this is 'underwritten by the gold sale; without that one-time cash inflow, leverage would likely have deteriorated.' Underlying operating cash flow ex-gold was ~EUR 324m vs EUR 869m prior year. T5 regime supportive across 5 of 6 layers as at 2026-05-04; T2 is two-sided with asymmetric German Industriestrompreis disadvantaging Hoboken vs Aurubis Hamburg. The 150 kt European battery recycling plant deferred to 2032+ under CORE — explicitly OUTSIDE the bet horizon. Hoboken EUR 400m capex is for the existing Cu/PGM circuit, NOT battery recycling.
baseline_research_note.contradictions_in_evidence: Genuine cross-stream tensions exist on Recycling. (1) ROCE optics: Financial stream reports 208% headline ROCE; Macro deep-read flags this as 'normalized ROCE in the high-teens to mid-twenties'; Convergence earnings-quality verdict 'mixed' precisely on the gold sale-and-leaseback. The bet must be calibrated on NORMALISED economics, not headline — which means resolution-true is consistent with reported ROCE MECHANICALLY normalising downward as the EUR 400m capex rebuilds capital employed. (2) Foundation cushion durability: Convergence stresses 'world-class foundation businesses funding a Battery Materials division'; Macro/Convergence both stress durability depends on three simultaneous conditions (gold/PGM stays elevated, Catalysis ROCE holds, BMS no further capital) — failure of any one collapses the financial underpinning. The gold-tailwind layer is cyclical not structural. (3) Hoboken moat persistence: pos_technology_hoboken_complex_feed_loop notes 'Heraeus demonstrates that large-scale refining capability exists outside Umicore — the moat is real but not unique'; pos_capability_integrated_data_traceability flags 'There is no moat, only table stakes' on the data-traceability side. Compare to T5 deep-read which confirms refiner pricing power is currently HOLDING (Aurubis +27% PGM volume, metal-results 45% gross margin) but trader incursion (Glencore Li-Cycle / Metallium, Trafigura Nth Cycle) is concrete and ramping into 2027-2028 contract pricing windows. (4) Sale-and-leaseback margin-call exposure: pos_balance_sheet_gold_leaseback notes ongoing lease costs on recirculating inventory will reduce future EBITDA; pos_balance_sheet_net_debt_leverage references CFO stress-test target gold correction to USD 1,800-2,000/oz — the structure could be margin-called at that level, triggering cash-conversion stress (B7 territory). Underlying operating cash flow ex-gold was EUR 324m vs EUR 869m prior year — the sale-and-leaseback is masking weaker underlying cash generation. (5) Belgian asymmetric energy disadvantage: T2 propagation rule confirms Hoboken sits OUTSIDE German Industriestrompreis EUR 50/MWh relief; Aurubis Hamburg (the cleanest European complex-feed peer comparison) IS eligible — silently degrading Hoboken's relative cost position even as PGM regime is supportive. (6) The 150 kt European battery recycling deferral to 2032+ is a material capability gap — the integrated CAM+Recycling moat thesis is partial today, not closed-loop within the bet horizon.
open_questions: What is the ACTUAL margin-call trigger price embedded in the EUR 525m gold sale-and-leaseback contract? CFO stress-test references USD 1,800-2,000/oz but the contract-specific trigger price is not disclosed; resolving this would re-rate the tail-scenario probability materially.

What is Hoboken's Q1 2026 complex-feed throughput (tonnes processed) and is segment-level FCF disclosure starting 24 April 2026 separating the gold sale-and-leaseback gain cleanly from underlying cash conversion? The first quarterly disclosure under the Convergence-prescribed regime is the readable signal.

Is there contract-by-contract vintage data on Hoboken's 2026-2028 PGM feedstock pricing book — what share is locked in long-tenor contracts (>2 years) vs spot-renegotiated annually? This determines exposure to the trader-incursion 2027-2028 contract-renewal window before WSR full export rules kick in 21 May 2027.

What is Aurubis Hamburg's effective power-cost benefit under German Industriestrompreis (approved 16 April 2026), and does it translate into Q3-Q4 2026 metal-pricing competitive intensity vs Hoboken on overlapping feedstock contracts? The asymmetric relief's competitive impact is theoretical until Aurubis Q3 2025/26 + Q4 2025/26 disclosures land.

Is the Belgian 2 μg/dl lead-emission standard dispute resolved or escalating? A material permitting tightening at Hoboken during the 2026 maintenance shutdown window would compound the operational gate (pos_site_hoboken 'what_it_exposes_to' flags 'Belgian / EU environmental permitting and lead-emission standards (2 μg/dl dispute flagged)').

What share of Hoboken's working-capital build (FY2025 group WC +EUR 261m) is attributable to PGM/gold inventory revaluation vs operational drivers? This determines the WC-unwind risk in a Russian normalisation shock scenario where simultaneous PGM mean-reversion + gold correction would trigger a margining-call pulse on the leaseback in the same window.

Has the board reviewed reverse-leaseback / unwind options for the EUR 525m gold structure, and at what cost? CFO non-negotiable from Convergence is 'one-time levers are exhausted' — meaning the structure is contingent on the regime continuing, with no graceful exit at current spot.

Is Hoboken's scheduled 2026 maintenance shutdown sequenced to allow EUR 400m capex Phase 1 commissioning to coincide with restart? Schedule overlap could compress lost-throughput to Q1 2027 only, but a poorly-sequenced overlap could extend EBITDA disruption through H1 2027.

factual_corrections: Recycling 208% reported ROCE on EUR -37m year-end capital employed is ARTEFACTUAL — collapsed CE post EUR 525m gold sale-and-leaseback (FY2025 EUR 486m EBITDA impact / EUR 365m EBIT impact / EUR 383m after-tax gain). Normalised ROCE per Macro deep-read is 'high-teens to mid-twenties'. The bet is calibrated on NORMALISED economics (EUR 250-350m EBITA at high-teens / mid-20s ROCE), NOT the headline figures. ROCE optics will MECHANICALLY normalise downward as the EUR 400m capex cycle rebuilds capital base — that's expected, not a break.

150 kt European battery recycling deferred to 2032+ under CORE plan — explicitly OUTSIDE the bet horizon. The 'closed-loop CAM+Recycling moat' thesis is partial today, not closed-loop within the bet horizon. Per pos_capability_integrated_data_traceability: 'There is no moat, only table stakes' on the data-traceability side (Glencore, Li-Cycle, Aurubis, OEMs all pursuing similar circularity).

Hoboken EUR 400m 2025-2028 capex programme is for the EXISTING Cu/PGM circuit (defends complex-feed PGM smelter-refinery moat), NOT for battery recycling. EUR ~300m concentrated 2027-2028, ~6-year payback with window 2030-2031.

EUR 525m gold sale-and-leaseback is contingent on the PGM/gold price regime continuing. Per pos_balance_sheet_net_debt_leverage / Convergence CFO non-negotiable: 'one-time levers are exhausted' — the next leverage improvement must come from operational cash conversion. CFO stress-test reference at gold USD 1,800-2,000/oz is the explicit margin-call zone (~50-55% downside from May 2026 spot ~USD 4,592/oz).

FY2025 underlying operating cash flow ex-gold was EUR 324m vs EUR 869m prior year — the sale-and-leaseback is masking weaker underlying cash generation. Headline net debt EUR 1,425m → EUR 1,357m and leverage 1.87x → 1.6x improvement is GOLD-SALE-DRIVEN; without it 'leverage would likely have deteriorated' (Convergence verdict).

Hoboken complex-feed metallurgical capability is a real operational moat (one of few globally) but NOT unique — Aurubis Hamburg, Heraeus Hanau, Johnson Matthey US/EU refining all hold complex-feed capability. The moat is real but not exclusive; trader-vs-refiner feedstock competition (Glencore Li-Cycle / Metallium, Trafigura Nth Cycle) is the contested period through 2027-2028 contract renewals.

Hoboken (Belgium) sits OUTSIDE German Industriestrompreis EUR 50/MWh relief approved 16 April 2026; Aurubis Hamburg (the cleanest European complex-feed peer) IS eligible. The asymmetric national relief silently degrades Hoboken's relative cost position by ~EUR 30-50/MWh effective on the eligible 50% of consumption — direct intra-EU competitive disadvantage.

Belgian 2 μg/dl lead-emission standard dispute is a tail-risk on Hoboken social-licence-to-operate (pos_site_hoboken 'what_it_exposes_to') — a material permitting tightening during the 2026 maintenance shutdown window would compound the operational gate.

bets.label: Compliance moat half-life (cross-segment) through FY2028

intermediate_gates.name: USTR Section 301 overcapacity-investigation remedy determination
intermediate_gates.what_resolves: T3-anchored gate (T3 weight 0.9 to B4): if USTR remedy extends Section 301 coverage upstream (pCAM, separators, processing equipment) or to transshipment corridors (Korea, Mexico, Vietnam, Indonesia, Malaysia), the regulatory underwriter widens and the FEOC-eligible Western pool is structurally walled — supportive of B4 Layer 1 (regulatory persistence). If the investigation closes without remedy or with carve-out language, the underwriting compresses and the layered moat reads as one-sided. Hearings 5-8 May 2026 are the determinative pre-remedy block.

intermediate_gates.name: Busan / MOFCOM-70 expiry
intermediate_gates.what_resolves: Binary near-term re-anchor of T3 (the underwriter): MOFCOM Announcements 55-58 and 61-62 (rare-earth / permanent-magnet / battery-grade graphite controls) suspended via MOFCOM/GAC Announcement 70/2025 expire on 10 Nov 2026. Either (i) snap-back of the controls (mildly supportive for the Western compliant pool, additive to T5/germanium-side moat depth via the 2025 layer; B5 channel uplift), (ii) extension status quo (hold), or (iii) Phase-2 framework deal with carve-outs below 100% on CAM (T3 alternate scenario fires, B4 Layer 1 compresses materially). Pre-positioning signals readable from September 2026.

intermediate_gates.name: Treasury 'impracticable-to-trace' transition expiry decision
intermediate_gates.what_resolves: Single largest direct-T4 calendared gate. Treasury / IRS either lets the May 2024 'impracticable-to-trace' carve-out (graphite, electrolyte salts, trace minerals) expire on schedule end-2026 and issues hard-edge tracing guidance (T4 enforcement-teeth episode, ASP premium widens to 15-25%, T4 TL re-rates to 0.65-0.75) — or extends it under OEM lobbying (replay of May 2024 soften-via-guidance playbook, T4 alternate scenario 2 fires, T4 TL re-rates to 0.20-0.30). The downstream Q1 2027 hard-edge FEOC tracing guidance window depends on this decision.

intermediate_gates.name: EU Battery Passport hard date and first 6-month enforcement window
intermediate_gates.what_resolves: EU Battery Regulation 2023/1542 Article 77 Battery Passport requirement enters binding enforcement on 18 Feb 2027. Operational guidelines published Aug 2025 are now testable. The bet's commercial-monetisation Layer 2 reads on (a) whether DG GROW or member-state market-surveillance authorities (Germany BAUA, France DGCCRF, Netherlands ILT) issue first non-compliance penalty cases or market-access denials in H1-H2 2027 with monetary thresholds, and (b) whether OEM Q-call commentary references passport-driven re-sourcing decisions. First-cycle monitoring runs Feb-Aug 2027.

intermediate_gates.name: Q1 2027 hard-edge FEOC tracing guidance window
intermediate_gates.what_resolves: Conditional on the Treasury impracticable-to-trace expiry path: if expiry held, Q1 2027 IRS / DOE FEOC interpretive guidance is the first window where hard-edge tracing methodology is operationalised in IRS Bulletin form. First IRS denial of Section 30D credit on FEOC-traced grounds is the leading enforcement-teeth indicator. If the carve-out was extended, this window slips and the Layer 1 erosion path becomes more likely.

intermediate_gates.name: 30D battery-component domestic-content step-up to 80% (CY2027) and 85% (CY2028)
intermediate_gates.what_resolves: Annual tightening of 30D thresholds: 70% in CY2026 (live), 80% in CY2027, 85% in CY2028, 90% in CY2029. This is the demand-side underwriter mechanically tightening on schedule absent legislative reversal. Each step-up raises the OEM cost of non-compliance and tests whether the compliance gate translates into incremental ASP differential or remains a binary qualifier. Reading-rule: -1 magnitude on B4 prior if any step-up is delayed via legislative action; +0.5 if step-up is paired with an enforcement-action precedent.

intermediate_gates.name: DFARS Section 842 implementing rule publication
intermediate_gates.what_resolves: FY26 NDAA Section 842 prohibits DoD procurement of advanced batteries from FEOCs with phased compliance: 1 Jan 2028 (new contracts), 1 Jan 2029 (standard batteries), 1 Jan 2031 (legacy programmes). The Defense Acquisition Regulations Council publishes the implementing rule in the Federal Register pre-2028. Pull-forward of the rule (publication ahead of statutory date) signals defence-demand pull-forward and widens the premium-tier monetisation channel; slippage past 2027 confirms commercial monetisation lag.

bets.prior_rationale: B4 is the most ambiguous of the seven bets and has an asymmetric two-input prior structure that pulls in opposite directions. T4 (the load-bearing direct theme, weight 1.0) is anchored at 0.42 with 'moat-persists-premium-eroding' trajectory: regulatory framework at widest legal aperture (47 CRMA Strategic Projects designated May 2024 first list including Umicore's GePETO + ReGAIN, EU Battery Passport hard date Feb 2027 with operational guidelines published Aug 2025, NDAA Section 842 enacted Dec 2025, IRA 30D 70% threshold live Jan 2026, ~127% cumulative US tariff stack on Chinese CAM) but three concrete erosion vectors are visible on the calendar (Treasury impracticable-to-trace expiry end-2026 binary test, Asian compliant-capacity ramp inside the perimeter — EcoPro Debrecen 54 kt/yr live Dec 2025 expanding to 108 kt/yr in 2026, LG Chem Tennessee in commissioning H1 2026 at half-original-scale ~60 kt/yr by 2027, POSCO Future M Quebec announcements pending — and demand-side bifurcation where defence and ESG-fund channels pay premium but mass-market OEMs replay the POSCO/GM/Ultium renegotiation precedent visible in Umicore FY2025 EUR -91m BMS Adjusted EBIT and EUR 261m WC build). T3 (regulatory underwriter, weight 0.9) is anchored at 0.66 with 'holding with upward bias': OBBBA-codified FEOC + IRS Notice 2026-15 MACR mechanism + 127% CAM tariff wall + active USTR overcapacity-investigation track (16 economies, 24 Jul 2026 remedy target) all raise the friction cost of legal-framework reversal materially above consensus. The two priors interact along the load-bearing compliance-mandate-vs-uptake split: T3 anchors Layer 1 (regulatory persistence likely high — P(framework persists through 2030) ~0.70-0.80 per T4 deep-read), T4 anchors Layer 2 (commercial monetisation uncertain — P(framework translates into >5% sustained ASP premium for incumbent Western suppliers through 2030) ~0.35-0.45 per T4 deep-read). Resolution-true requires BOTH layers; the multiplicative structure of joint-resolution at full strength is approximately 0.30-0.36, but B4 is calibrated to a softer 'persistence + measurable ASP-premium retention >5% on at least the BMS + Specialty perimeters' rather than full enforcement-teeth realisation across all four segments. Five further anchoring inputs nudge the prior. (1) Cross-segment exposure is asymmetric: Specialty Materials' germanium / electro-optic franchise is anchored more on T3 (MOFCOM Aug 2023 regime) than T4 and remains the strongest monetised compliance-moat node today (FY2025 11.1% ROCE, 13.6% margin, 'capturable but time-limited' per Macro with 3-5 year Western substitution horizon); Catalysis's compliance exposure is via Euro 7 + EPA 2027 content uplift and PGM-loop closed-loop traceability — high and durable through early 2030s but distinct from FEOC/Battery-Passport; Recycling's compliance exposure is via EU recycling-efficiency thresholds (90% Ni/Co/Cu/Pb, 50% Li by 31 Dec 2027) and CRMA secondary-supply rules — supportive but the integrated CAM+Recycling moat is overstated per pos_capability_integrated_data_traceability ('There is no moat, only table stakes' — Glencore, Li-Cycle, Aurubis, OEM closed-loop programs all pursuing similar circularity); BMS exposure via pos_capability_eu_battery_passport_readiness is the most-direct ASP-monetisation channel and is precisely where the Asian-localisation oversupply erosion is concentrated. (2) CRMA Strategic Project status is a designation that signals priority in permitting, financing access (EIB / EIF), and member-state alignment but does NOT by itself convert to ASP premium; Umicore's GePETO (germanium processing) and ReGAIN (germanium substitution) are on the May 2024 first list and reinforce the Specialty perimeter but do not extend to CAM/pCAM. (3) Convergence stream concludes the compliance premium 'degrades from competitive advantage to table stakes within 3-5 years' — direct read-across that this bet is structurally a half-life bet, not a persistence bet, with the 2028 horizon falling within the table-stakes-conversion window. (4) Strategic stream A4 (regulatory premium) is rated VALIDATED with net-positive signal score +4.44 BUT failure scenarios explicitly include 'Governments soften localization rules; OEMs prioritize lowest-cost supply amid EV affordability push; ESG/traceability becomes table-stakes with no premium' — i.e. the validation is on existence-of-framework, not on premium-translation. (5) Equity-market consensus systematically conflates moat-persistence with moat-depth, pricing closer to {moat_persists_and_translates: 0.50, premium_erodes_via_localisation: 0.25, treasury_softens: 0.15, enforcement_deepens: 0.10}; our distribution shifts ~20pp from 'persists_and_translates' into 'premium_erodes_via_localisation' on Asian-localisation pace under-pricing. Net-net the prior settles at 0.40 — clearly above zero because (a) the regulatory infrastructure is operationally widest at exactly the moment of resolution, (b) Specialty's germanium-anchored sub-bet provides a structurally insulated minority of B4 exposure largely independent of the BMS Asian-localisation erosion vector, and (c) defence (NDAA 842) and ESG-fund channels are durable premium-tier monetisation paths — but well below 0.50 because the load-bearing channel (BMS ASP-translation rate) is being compressed by Asian-localiser capacity inside the perimeter in real time, the convergence stream's 3-5 year table-stakes timeline mathematically lands inside the resolution horizon, and the multiplicative structure of joint-resolution (Layer 1 AND Layer 2) penalises an asymmetric input (T4 0.42 * T3 0.66 ≈ 0.28 at full strength; B4 reads above this because the bet's resolution-true definition is softer than full joint-realisation). The 0.40 prior reflects the asymmetry: persistence is likely high but monetisation rate is structurally compressing, and the 2028 horizon catches the half-life mid-cycle.
bets.thesis_resolves_true: By FY2028 the regulatory framework underwriting Western premium pricing for compliant, traceable, FEOC-clean materials persists at full or near-full aperture — Treasury impracticable-to-trace transition expired on schedule end-2026 with at least moderate hard-edge tracing guidance issued in Q1 2027, EU Battery Passport (Feb 2027) is operational with at least one DG GROW / member-state penalty case or market-access denial published, USTR Section 301 overcapacity remedy (24 Jul 2026) extended Section 301 coverage upstream or to transshipment corridors, OBBBA FEOC + IRS Notice 2026-15 MACR mechanism survives without material safe-harbour widening through Q3 2026 proposed regulations, MOFCOM Aug 2023 germanium / gallium licensing regime remains operational (with the 2025 MOFCOM 55-62 layer providing optional incremental upside via post-Busan snap-back or Phase-2 carve-out below 100% NOT firing) — AND that framework translates into measurable, monetised ASP premium realisation on at least the BMS + Specialty Materials perimeters through 2028. Specifically: (i) compliant-CAM ASP premium vs Chinese-origin equivalent holds at 5-10% sustained (per Platts CAM Daily proxy), defending the BMS conversion margin needed for the Q3 2027 proof-gate path, (ii) Asian compliant-capacity build inside EU/US perimeter does NOT exceed the 250 kt/yr T4-bear threshold by year-end 2027 (EcoPro Hungary Phase 2 commissions on schedule but LG Chem TN ramps at the half-original-scale pace and POSCO Future M Quebec slips), (iii) Specialty Materials germanium franchise retains FY2027-2028 EBIT margin >12% with germanium spot premia anchored by MOFCOM regime persistence (T5 channel), (iv) NDAA Section 842 DFARS implementing rule is published ahead of or at the 1 Jan 2028 statutory date with first DoD pilot procurement RFP using FEOC-free language, anchoring the defence-tier premium channel, (v) major fleet operators (Ayvens, Element, ARI) publish RFP language explicitly excluding non-FEOC-compliant batteries with attestation requirement, anchoring the B2B/ESG-fund tier premium channel, and (vi) Umicore's GePETO + ReGAIN CRMA Strategic Project designations advance through funding disbursement (EIB / EIF) by year-end 2027, signalling the EU institutional infrastructure is operational for incumbent Western suppliers. Resolution-true does NOT require the moat to deepen materially across all four segments, nor does it require the consensus 50% probability of 'moat_persists_and_translates' to be vindicated; it requires the cross-stream Layer 1 (legal persistence) AND Layer 2 (commercial monetisation, as expressed via measurable ASP premium retention >5% on the most-exposed segments) to both clear. The bet is calibrated to a partial-persistence-with-asymmetric-monetisation outcome where Specialty captures durable rent and BMS captures defendable-but-eroding rent, NOT to the full-aperture 'compliance_moat_enforcement_teeth_episode' realising.
bets.thesis_resolves_false: The bet resolves false in any of four structurally different ways. Most likely: a 'moat persists / premium erodes to cost-plus' path (T4 erosion-via-Asian-localisation scenario fires) — the legal framework persists as scheduled (CRMA designations expand, Battery Passport binding Feb 2027, NDAA 842 enacted, 30D thresholds step on schedule, 127% CAM tariff intact) BUT Asian compliant-capacity inside the EU/US perimeter exceeds 250-300 kt/yr cumulative by year-end 2027 (EcoPro Hungary 108 kt/yr commissioned on schedule, LG Chem Tennessee ramps to 60+ kt/yr, POSCO Future M Quebec or PoscoChemTech US plant announcements cross 50 kt/yr, additional SK On / Sumitomo Metal Mining moves), OEMs (GM, Ford, Stellantis, VW, BMW) replay the POSCO/GM/Ultium contract-renegotiation precedent at scale, and the compliant-CAM ASP premium compresses to <5% sustained for >2 quarters per Platts CAM Daily proxy. T4 TL re-rates to 0.20-0.30, BMS Q3 2027 proof-gate becomes mathematically unreachable on the contracted book (B1 propagation channel weight 0.6), Specialty Materials germanium niche remains insulated (B5 channel weight 0.4 partially holds via T3 alone), Catalysis and Recycling traceability exposure remains commercially neutral. Less likely but distinctly different: a 'moat softens via Treasury carve-outs replay' path — Treasury extends the impracticable-to-trace transition rule beyond end-2026 (e.g. to 2028) under OEM lobbying citing supply-chain readiness, replays the May 2024 soften-via-guidance playbook; IRS issues forthcoming proposed regulations under OBBBA that materially soften MACR thresholds or expand safe harbours to Korea-, Mexico- and Morocco-routed material; T3 USTR overcapacity investigation closes without remedy or with carve-out language; T3 Phase-2 Busan-2 carve-out below 100% on CAM (currently 8% probability per T3 scenarios) fires post 10 Nov 2026 expiry. Layer 1 itself compresses; Layer 2 follows mechanically. Less likely still: a 'moat fragments / asymmetric' path where the BMS perimeter erodes commercially (per central bear) BUT Specialty's germanium / electro-optic franchise simultaneously suffers a Phase-2 MOFCOM carve-out (germanium / gallium regime suspended as goodwill gesture, T3 -2 magnitude on B5), AND DFARS Section 842 implementing rule slips beyond 2028 statutory date OR pilot procurements use commercial-COTS exemption broadly — the segment-by-segment defensible-niche re-scoping flagged in convergence's S-3-1 forced-decision becomes the actual operating posture, with no full-aperture monetisation channel surviving. Tail-but-non-trivial: a 'moat fragments / commercial Phase-2 grand bargain' tail where a negotiated grand bargain (T3 2% probability scenario) restructures the entire stack — managed-trade quotas paired with Chinese unilateral relaxation of germanium / gallium controls — collapsing both the BMS-pCAM regional underwriting AND the Specialty-germanium pricing power simultaneously. In any of the four false paths, Umicore's compliance-premium thesis collapses to 'license-to-operate-only' framing within the FY2028 horizon, the convergence-flagged S-3-1 forced-decision (JV, tolling, partial exit) on BMS becomes pre-determined irrespective of customer execution, and the cross-stream Convergence verdict that the compliance premium 'degrades from competitive advantage to table stakes within 3-5 years' validates within the resolution window.

transmission_mechanisms.reading_rule: Reading T4 named indicator 'Asian compliant-capacity build inside EU/US perimeter (kt/yr CAM commissioned)'. EcoPro Debrecen live Dec 2025 at 54 kt/yr, scaling to 108 kt/yr 2026; LG Chem Tennessee H1 2026 at half-original-scale (~60 kt/yr by 2027); POSCO Future M Bécancour Quebec announcements pending. Bear threshold (T4 propagation rule): cumulative >250 kt/yr Asian-promoter compliant CAM capacity inside EU + US by year-end 2027 = oversupply trigger collapsing premium to commodity-with-attestation pricing. Per T4 deep-read, this is the dominant erosion channel for B4 Layer 2 (commercial monetisation) and the load-bearing transmission for the cross-stream 'moat persists / premium erodes to cost-plus' resolution outcome. -1 magnitude per 50 kt/yr commissioned above central path; -2 magnitude on cumulative breach of 250 kt/yr threshold.

Reading T4 named indicator 'Compliant-CAM ASP premium vs Chinese-origin equivalent'. Currently estimated 8-15% per Platts CAM Daily indications; T4 velocity_per_cycle -0.05. Bear: sustained <5% premium for >2 quarters (T4 erosion threshold; B4 Layer 2 collapses; B1 propagation channel weight 0.6 fires). Bull: sustained >15% premium for >2 quarters (T4 enforcement-teeth episode realising; B4 TL re-rates to 0.65-0.75). The compliance-mandate-vs-uptake split is operationally visible exactly here — moat-persistence (Layer 1) does not imply premium-translation (Layer 2). Per T4 propagation rule: 'TL up → BMS conversion premium holds; TL down → BMS premium compresses toward cost-plus, accelerating BP-4 commoditization risk.'

T4 dated escalation: Treasury 'impracticable-to-trace' transition rule expires end-2026 (2026-12-31). If Treasury extends (replays May 2024 carve-out playbook), B4 Layer 1 compresses (T4 alternate scenario 2 'erosion via Asian-localisation oversupply' fires concurrently with carve-out, T4 TL re-rates to 0.20-0.30, B4 prior re-anchors below 0.30). If Treasury allows clean expiry + hard-edge tracing guidance Q1 2027, B4 Layer 2 widens for the BMS + Recycling perimeters (T4 enforcement-teeth episode fires, premium 15-25%, T4 TL 0.65-0.75, B4 prior re-anchors above 0.55). Reading rule: -2 magnitude on extension confirmation; +2 magnitude on clean expiry + hard-edge guidance pairing.

T4 escalation path 'battery_passport_market_access_denial': member-state market-surveillance authorities (Germany BAUA, France DGCCRF, Netherlands ILT) issuing battery-passport non-compliance market-access denials post Feb 2027. Reading rule: each published DG GROW / member-state penalty case with monetary threshold = +0.5 magnitude on B4 Layer 2 monetisation reading; absence of any published case through H2 2027 = -0.5 magnitude (table-stakes confirmation). Anchors directly to pos_capability_eu_battery_passport_readiness.

T4 escalation path 'ndaa_section_842_demand_pull_acceleration': DFARS amendment publishing FEOC-battery exclusion language ahead of 1 Jan 2028 statutory date; major defence prime (Lockheed, RTX, GD) Q-call commentary on compliant-battery sourcing premium; Federal Register notice of DoD pilot procurement for FEOC-free advanced batteries. Reading rule: pull-forward = +1 magnitude on B4 defence-tier monetisation channel; slippage past 2028 = -0.5 magnitude. Defence-tier is the most insulated premium channel because consumer-price politics does not bind.

T4 named indicator 'Fleet RFP language requiring FEOC-free / Battery-Passport-ready batteries (% of EU+US fleet RFPs)'. Ayvens (ex ALD-LeasePlan) PowerUP 2026 plan targets <90 g/km fleet CO2 by 2026; ESG-RFP exclusion language increasingly standard. Bear: <30% of major-fleet RFPs requiring compliance attestation by 2027 (table-stakes confirmation). Bull: sustained >50% by 2027 (T4 named threshold). Reading rule: each major fleet operator (Ayvens, Element, ARI / Holman) publishing exclusion-language RFP = +0.5 magnitude on B4 B2B/ESG-fund tier monetisation channel.

T4 deescalation channel: POSCO/GM/Ultium precedent visible in Umicore FY2025 EUR -91m BMS Adjusted EBIT and EUR 261m WC build. Reading rule: any public news of GM, Ford, Stellantis, BMW, VW renegotiating CAM offtake terms with compliant suppliers in 2026-2027 = direct read-across to B4 Layer 2 reading; Platts CAM Daily index posting persistent negative spread to compliant-CAM benchmark = -1 magnitude. Specific contract amendments visible in supplier 10-Q / annual report disclosure intensify the read.

T3 propagation rule for B4: '+1 magnitude per OBBBA / MACR tightening event; -1 magnitude per safe-harbour widening or Phase-2 carve-out'. OBBBA July 2025 codification of FEOC into statute + IRS Notice 2026-15 (12 Feb 2026) MACR framework + safe-harbour publication + 127% CAM tariff stack jointly raise the friction cost of legal-framework reversal materially above v1 baseline. Reading rule: forthcoming Treasury proposed regulations under OBBBA expected Q3 2026 — preserving MACR + tightening transshipment safe harbours = T3 supportive (B4 Layer 1 reading holds); widening safe harbours (Korea / Mexico / Morocco) or softening MACR = T3 erosion (B4 Layer 1 compresses 18-24 months per T3 cross-cutting overlay).

T3 dated gate: USTR Section 301 overcapacity-investigation remedy by 24 July 2026 (16 economies investigated since 11 Mar 2026; hearings 28 Apr - 8 May 2026). If remedy extends Section 301 coverage upstream (pCAM, separators, processing equipment) or to transshipment corridors (Korea, Mexico, Vietnam, Indonesia, Malaysia) — supportive for B4 Layer 1 (BMS + Recycling perimeters) and reinforces T4 'tariff_intensification_to_battery_value_chain' escalation path. If investigation closed without remedy — mildly negative for B4 Layer 1 underwriting. Per T3 central-path scenario probability 0.55 'structural decoupling holds with extension', the upside path is meaningfully more likely than consensus.

T3 dated gate: 10 November 2026 Busan / MOFCOM-70 expiry. Either snap-back of MOFCOM 55-58 + 61-62 (rare-earth / permanent-magnet / battery-graphite controls) which is mildly supportive for Western compliant suppliers (B4 + adds optional incremental upside on Specialty / germanium via the 2025 layer if it interacts with the August 2023 regime) — OR extension status quo (T3 scenario 0.20, B4 hold) — OR Phase-2 framework deal with carve-outs below 100% on CAM (T3 alternate scenario 0.08, the only T3 outcome that materially erodes B4 Layer 1; B4 prior re-anchors to 0.20-0.25). T3 central path: 0.55 'structural holds + extension' + 0.20 'Busan renewed status quo' = 0.75 cumulative supportive of B4 Layer 1.

T3 propagation rule for B5 carries directly into B4 Specialty perimeter (B5 channel weight 0.4 to B4): MOFCOM Aug 2023 germanium / gallium licensing regime is the operative T3 sub-component for the Specialty / germanium franchise; T5 pricing-power thesis depends on this regime persisting. Reading rule: '+1 magnitude per MOFCOM tightening or extension to additional metals (tungsten, indium, niobium); -2 magnitude on regime suspension as Phase-2 goodwill'. The 2023 regime is DISTINCT from the suspended 2025 MOFCOM 55-62 package and was untouched by Busan; voluntary suspension as Phase-2 goodwill gesture would compress germanium spot premia and Umicore Electro-Optic franchise economics — direct erosion of the most-insulated B4 monetisation node.

T3 named-watch metric 'EU CRMA Strategic Project pipeline — second-round selections'. 47 first-round Strategic Projects approved 2025 including Umicore-promoted GePETO (germanium processing) + ReGAIN (germanium substitution) — anchored on the May 2024 first list, do NOT invent additional designations. Second-round >160 applications (75 battery, 21 RE-magnet) under assessment since 19 Jan 2026. Reading rule: second-round selections finalised + Umicore-promoted designations advance through EIB / EIF funding disbursement = supportive for B4 (institutional infrastructure operational); EU policy softens against China-content rules or stalls Strategic Project designations = -0.5 magnitude. CRMA designation is a signal but does not by itself convert to ASP — the designation reads on Layer 1 (legal infrastructure persistence) not Layer 2 (commercial monetisation).

scenarios.name: central_path_moat_persists_premium_holds_5_to_10_pct
scenarios.narrative: Through end-2027 the regulatory framework persists at full aperture. Treasury allows the impracticable-to-trace transition to expire on schedule end-2026 but issues moderate-rather-than-hard-edge tracing guidance Q1 2027 (partial enforcement-teeth tightening). USTR overcapacity remedy on or around 24 Jul 2026 extends Section 301 coverage upstream to pCAM and processing equipment AND to transshipment corridors (Korea, Mexico). Busan / MOFCOM-70 suspension is renewed in some form (full extension, partial roll-forward, or successor framework). IRS proposed regulations under OBBBA preserve MACR and tighten transshipment safe harbours. EU Battery Passport binding Feb 2027 with operational guidelines live; first DG GROW or member-state penalty case lands in H2 2027 with measurable monetary threshold. NDAA Section 842 DFARS implementing rule published Q3-Q4 2027 ahead of 1 Jan 2028 statutory date. EcoPro Hungary Phase 2 (108 kt/yr) commissions on schedule but LG Chem TN ramps at half-original-scale pace; cumulative Asian compliant-capacity inside perimeter lands ~200-230 kt/yr by year-end 2027 (below T4 bear threshold of 250 kt/yr). Compliant-CAM ASP premium compresses from 8-15% today to 5-10% sustained through FY2028 (T4 central path). Specialty / germanium franchise retains EBIT margin >12% with MOFCOM Aug 2023 regime operational and 2025 MOFCOM 55-62 layer providing optional incremental upside via post-Busan snap-back. Defence (NDAA 842) and ESG-fund channels continue paying clear premium. Mass-market OEMs replay POSCO/GM/Ultium-style renegotiations on a modest scale but the 5-10% premium floor holds. T4 TL re-rates to 0.42-0.48; T3 holds at 0.66-0.70. B4 resolves true at marginal-clearance: Layer 1 + Layer 2 both clear but at the lower end of the 'persists_and_translates' band, with BMS premium defendable but eroding and Specialty rent durable.
scenarios.what_makes_this_path_break: Treasury extending impracticable-to-trace beyond end-2026 (T4 alternate scenario 2 fires); OR EcoPro Phase 2 + LG Chem TN both commissioning ahead of schedule pushing Asian capacity above 250 kt/yr; OR Phase-2 Busan-2 carve-out below 100% on CAM (T3 0.08 probability); OR mass-market OEM renegotiations spreading from one-off to systematic across GM/Ford/Stellantis/VW/BMW pulling the premium below 5% sustained.

scenarios.name: moat_persists_premium_erodes_to_cost_plus
scenarios.narrative: The most likely path. The regulatory framework persists at full aperture as scheduled (CRMA designations expand including GePETO + ReGAIN funding disbursement; EU Battery Passport binding Feb 2027; NDAA Section 842 enacted; 30D thresholds step on schedule 80% 2027 / 85% 2028; 127% CAM tariff intact; OBBBA-codified FEOC + MACR survives Q3 2026 proposed regs without material safe-harbour widening; MOFCOM Aug 2023 germanium / gallium regime persists). Treasury allows impracticable-to-trace clean expiry but Q1 2027 hard-edge tracing guidance is moderate. USTR overcapacity remedy extends Section 301 coverage but stops short of transshipment-corridor closure. BUT Asian compliant-capacity inside the EU/US perimeter exceeds 250-300 kt/yr cumulative by year-end 2027 (EcoPro Hungary 108 kt/yr commissioned on schedule, LG Chem Tennessee ramps to 60+ kt/yr by H2 2027, POSCO Future M Quebec / PoscoChemTech US plant announcements cross 50 kt/yr each, additional SK On / Sumitomo Metal Mining moves). OEMs (GM, Ford, Stellantis, VW, BMW) replay POSCO/GM/Ultium contract-renegotiation precedent at scale. Compliant-CAM ASP premium compresses to <5% sustained for >2 quarters per Platts CAM Daily proxy by H2 2027 — moat-depth collapse on the BMS perimeter. T4 TL re-rates to 0.20-0.30; T4 erosion-via-Asian-localisation scenario fires. BMS Q3 2027 proof-gate becomes mathematically unreachable on the contracted book (B1 propagation channel weight 0.6 cascades). Specialty / germanium franchise remains insulated (T3-anchored, B5 channel weight 0.4 holds independently with germanium spot premia anchored by MOFCOM Aug 2023 persistence, FY2028 EBIT margin >12% holds). Catalysis + Recycling traceability exposure remains commercially neutral (Euro 7 / EPA 2027 content uplift on Catalysis durable through early 2030s but distinct from FEOC/Battery-Passport; integrated CAM+Recycling is table stakes per pos_capability_integrated_data_traceability). B4 Layer 1 holds but Layer 2 fails on the BMS perimeter — bet resolves false on the load-bearing channel.
scenarios.what_makes_this_path_break: EcoPro Phase 2 OR LG Chem TN ramp slips materially (T4 named-watch threshold not breached); OR Treasury hard-edge tracing guidance Q1 2027 widens premium to 15-25% (T4 enforcement-teeth episode fires); OR USTR overcapacity remedy extends to transshipment corridors AND to processing equipment, narrowing Asian-localiser arbitrage; OR DFARS Section 842 rule pulls forward to 2027 with first DoD pilot procurement RFP — any single trigger pulls the path toward central case.

scenarios.name: compliance_moat_enforcement_teeth_episode
scenarios.narrative: T4 enforcement-teeth episode fires: Treasury allows impracticable-to-trace clean expiry end-2026 AND issues hard-edge FEOC tracing guidance Q1 2027 with first IRS denial of Section 30D credit on FEOC-traced grounds in IRS Bulletin H1 2027. First DG GROW or member-state Battery Passport non-compliance penalty cases land in H2 2027 with monetary thresholds; OEM Q-call commentary references passport-driven re-sourcing. DFARS Section 842 implementing rule pulls forward to 2027 with first DoD pilot procurement RFP under Section 842 language; major defence prime (Lockheed, RTX, GD) Q-call commentary on compliant-battery sourcing premium. USTR overcapacity remedy extends Section 301 coverage to pCAM, separators, processing equipment AND to transshipment corridors (Korea, Mexico, Vietnam), narrowing Asian-localiser arbitrage materially. Treasury Q3 2026 proposed regulations under OBBBA preserve MACR + tighten transshipment safe harbours. Compliant-CAM ASP premium widens to 15-25% sustained. T4 TL re-rates to 0.65-0.75; T3 re-rates to 0.75-0.80. Asian localisation continues but at structural premium-tier rather than commodity tier. B4 resolves true with margin: Layer 1 + Layer 2 both clear strongly across BMS + Specialty + Catalysis + Recycling perimeters. BMS Q3 2027 proof-gate clears with margin (B1 pulled toward bull); Specialty franchise retains pricing power with FY2028 EBIT margin >15%; Catalysis Euro 7 / EPA 2027 content uplift compounds with traceability premium; Recycling 31 Dec 2027 efficiency-threshold compliance becomes a procurement gate not just attestation.
scenarios.what_makes_this_path_break: Treasury extends impracticable-to-trace (T4 alternate scenario 2 fires); OR Phase-2 Busan-2 carve-out on CAM (T3 0.08 probability); OR consumer-price politics forces administrative tariff suspensions; OR LFP migration pace inside premium platforms accelerates ahead of regulatory tailwind (T1 bear threshold pulls demand-side floor below the regulatory ceiling).

scenarios.name: moat_softens_via_treasury_carveouts_replay
scenarios.narrative: Treasury extends the 'impracticable-to-trace' transition rule beyond end-2026 (e.g. to 2028) under OEM lobbying and Auto Innovators / Alliance for Automotive Innovation public filings citing supply-chain readiness — replays the May 2024 soften-via-guidance playbook directly. Bipartisan Senate letter to Treasury supports extension. IRS issues Q3 2026 proposed regulations under OBBBA that materially soften MACR thresholds OR expand safe harbours to Korea-, Mexico- and Morocco-routed material — reducing the friction cost of Asian-promoter compliant-capacity routing through transshipment corridors. T3 USTR overcapacity remedy closes without remedy or with carve-out language (T3 alternate scenario fires). T4 alternate scenario 2 'erosion via Asian-localisation oversupply' fires concurrently with carve-out. Compliant-CAM ASP premium collapses to <3% sustained (commodity-with-attestation pricing). T4 TL re-rates to 0.20-0.30; T3 re-rates to 0.40-0.50. Layer 1 itself compresses; Layer 2 follows mechanically. Specialty / germanium franchise still relatively insulated through MOFCOM Aug 2023 regime persistence (B5 channel partial hold) but Catalysis emissions-compliance path is lightly affected (Euro 7 / EPA 2027 are EU/US sovereign frameworks not bilateral). Convergence-flagged S-3-1 forced-decision (JV, tolling, partial exit) on BMS becomes pre-determined irrespective of customer execution. B4 resolves false on Layer 1 compression — the underwriting itself softens before the monetisation lag has time to test.
scenarios.what_makes_this_path_break: Bipartisan Senate trade-relief blocked OR Auto Innovators lobby filing rejected; OR Treasury issues clean-expiry guidance overruling extension lobby; OR USTR remedy lands ahead of Treasury extension decision and pre-empts the soften path; OR consumer-price politics fails to materialise (auto-affordability remains tolerable through 2026-2027) reducing political pressure for carve-outs.

scenarios.name: moat_fragments_asymmetric_specialty_collapse
scenarios.narrative: BMS perimeter erodes commercially per central bear (Asian-localisation oversupply >300 kt/yr, ASP premium <3%) BUT simultaneously the Specialty / germanium franchise suffers a Phase-2 MOFCOM carve-out — voluntary suspension of the August 2023 germanium / gallium licensing regime as goodwill gesture in a Phase-2 Busan-2 deal post 10 Nov 2026 expiry. T3 → B5 propagation fires '-2 magnitude on regime suspension as Phase-2 goodwill'. Germanium spot premia compress as Western feedstock scarcity narrative collapses; Umicore Electro-Optic franchise economics erode (FY2028 Specialty EBIT margin <10%). DFARS Section 842 implementing rule slips beyond 2028 statutory date OR pilot procurements use commercial-COTS exemption broadly (defence-tier premium channel fails to materialise). EU Battery Passport produces no published penalty cases through H2 2027 (table-stakes confirmation). The segment-by-segment defensible-niche re-scoping flagged in convergence's S-3-1 forced-decision becomes the actual operating posture across BMS + Specialty simultaneously, with no full-aperture monetisation channel surviving. Catalysis Euro 7 / EPA 2027 content uplift remains durable as the only insulated Umicore compliance-pricing node (sovereign EU/US frameworks unaffected by bilateral US-China dynamics). Recycling traceability is commercially neutral. T4 TL re-rates to 0.18-0.25; T3 re-rates to 0.35-0.45 on Phase-2 deal framing. B4 resolves false on Layer 1 + Layer 2 fragmentation across the load-bearing nodes — Umicore's compliance-premium thesis collapses to 'license-to-operate-only' framing.
scenarios.what_makes_this_path_break: MOFCOM Aug 2023 regime survives Phase-2 negotiations (T3 named-watch metric on germanium / gallium / antimony approval rate stays low / declining); OR Phase-2 Busan-2 deal collapses entirely (return to status quo); OR DFARS Section 842 implementing rule arrives ahead of 2028 statutory date overriding defence-tier slippage; OR Western germanium substitution timeline slips (5N Plus / Teledyne / AXT / JX Advanced Metals qualification lags the policy-relaxation window).

scenarios.name: tail_grand_bargain_full_premium_collapse
scenarios.narrative: Tail-but-non-trivial: a negotiated grand bargain (T3 'negotiated_grand_bargain' 0.02 probability scenario) restructures the entire stack — managed-trade quotas paired with Chinese unilateral relaxation of germanium / gallium / antimony controls, partial Section 301 rollback to <80%, OBBBA MACR safe-harbour widening via legislative trade-relief vehicle. Headline framing shifts from 'structural' to 'managed competition' across ALL bilateral channels simultaneously. Treasury extends impracticable-to-trace through 2028; IRS proposed regulations soften MACR; USTR Section 301 exclusion process re-opens at scale; MOFCOM voluntarily suspends both 2023 and 2025 layers. Compliance-CAM ASP premium collapses to <2% (commodity with paper-trail). Germanium spot premia normalise toward Asian floor. EU Battery Passport produces no published penalty cases; DFARS Section 842 effective date slips. Catalysis Euro 7 / EPA 2027 remains the sole insulated channel (sovereign frameworks). T4 TL re-rates to <0.15; T3 re-rates to <0.30. B4 resolves false across all four segments simultaneously — Umicore's compliance-premium thesis collapses entirely; the convergence-flagged S-3-1 forced-decision fires across BMS AND Specialty franchise re-scoping.
scenarios.what_makes_this_path_break: Base-rate frequency of grand-bargain industrial-policy reversal within 18-30 months is structurally very low (consensus ~0.10, our read ~0.02); requires simultaneous political alignment in Beijing AND Washington that is contradicted by USTR overcapacity-investigation framing language and OBBBA codification of FEOC into statute (raising friction cost of legislative reversal materially).

named_watch_metrics.metric: Compliant-CAM ASP premium vs Chinese-origin equivalent (Platts CAM Daily index proxy)
named_watch_metrics.source: Platts CAM Daily (Korea/Japan-domiciled NMC vs China-origin); Fastmarkets Battery Materials; OEM Q-call ASP commentary (Umicore, BASF, LG Chem, EcoPro)
named_watch_metrics.current_value: Estimated 8-15% premium per Platts CAM Daily indications (T4 named indicator); not directly published. T4 velocity_per_cycle -0.05.
named_watch_metrics.threshold_implications: Bull: sustained >15% premium for >2 quarters (T4 enforcement-teeth episode realising; B4 Layer 2 widens; B4 TL re-rates to 0.65-0.75). Bear: sustained <5% premium for >2 quarters (T4 erosion threshold; moat-depth collapse; B4 Layer 2 fails on BMS perimeter; B1 propagation channel weight 0.6 fires; Q3 2027 BMS proof-gate becomes mathematically unreachable). Critical gate: 5-10% sustained through FY2028 = central-path resolution-true at marginal clearance.

named_watch_metrics.metric: Asian compliant-CAM capacity inside EU/US perimeter (cumulative kt/yr commissioned)
named_watch_metrics.source: BNEF Battery Materials Tracker; Wood Mackenzie EV & Battery Supply Chain Outlook; EcoPro / LG Chem / POSCO Future M / SK On / Sumitomo Metal Mining IR press releases
named_watch_metrics.current_value: EcoPro Debrecen 54 kt/yr live Dec 2025, expansion to 108 kt/yr planned 2026; LG Chem Tennessee in commissioning H1 2026 at half-original-scale (~60 kt/yr by 2027); POSCO Future M Bécancour Quebec announcements pending. Cumulative ~75-90 kt/yr live as of May 2026.
named_watch_metrics.threshold_implications: Bear (T4 erosion threshold, B4 load-bearing): cumulative >250 kt/yr Asian-promoter compliant CAM capacity inside EU + US by year-end 2027 = oversupply trigger collapsing premium to commodity-with-attestation pricing. Central-path: 200-230 kt/yr (below threshold). Bull: EcoPro Phase 2 slips OR LG Chem TN ramp delays beyond H2 2027 OR POSCO Future M Bécancour announcements stall. The single most readable supply-side leading indicator for the central-vs-bear path bifurcation.

named_watch_metrics.metric: Treasury 'impracticable-to-trace' transition expiry decision (binary, Q4 2026)
named_watch_metrics.source: Treasury Federal Register + IRS Internal Revenue Bulletin; Auto Innovators / Alliance for Automotive Innovation public lobby filings; bipartisan Senate trade-relief letters
named_watch_metrics.current_value: In force through 2026-12-31; no extension proposed as of May 2026; Auto Innovators / Alliance lobby pre-positioning under tracking
named_watch_metrics.threshold_implications: Bull (T4 enforcement-teeth episode): clean expiry + hard-edge tracing guidance Q1 2027 (T4 TL re-rates 0.65-0.75; B4 prior re-anchors above 0.55). Bear (T4 alternate scenario 2): Treasury Federal Register NPRM proposing extension OR confirmed extension by Q4 2026 (replay May 2024 carve-out playbook; T4 TL re-rates to 0.20-0.30; B4 prior re-anchors below 0.30; concurrent firing of 'erosion via Asian-localisation oversupply' scenario). The single most consequential calendared decision in the 2026 window.

named_watch_metrics.metric: DG GROW / member-state Battery Passport non-compliance penalty cases (count, monetary thresholds)
named_watch_metrics.source: DG GROW press releases; Official Journal of the EU; member-state market-surveillance bulletins (Germany BAUA, France DGCCRF, Netherlands ILT)
named_watch_metrics.current_value: 0 published cases as of May 2026 (regulation enforcement still pre-Feb-2027 passport deadline). T4 named indicator velocity_per_cycle 0.0.
named_watch_metrics.threshold_implications: Bull: first published penalty case or market-access denial with monetary threshold in H1 2027 (T4 escalation 'battery_passport_market_access_denial' fires; +0.5 magnitude per case on B4 Layer 2 monetisation reading). Bear: 0 published cases through H2 2027 (table-stakes confirmation; -0.5 magnitude on B4 Layer 2; signals that the EU Battery Passport functions as binary qualifier rather than premium driver, supporting the Convergence stream verdict that traceability 'becomes table-stakes with no premium').

named_watch_metrics.metric: NDAA Section 842 / DFARS battery-FEOC exclusion implementation pace
named_watch_metrics.source: Defense Acquisition Regulations Council Federal Register notices; DOD Section 1742 supply-chain disclosures; GAO defense-supply-chain reports; major defence prime (Lockheed, RTX, GD) Q-call commentary
named_watch_metrics.current_value: Statute enacted Dec 2025; DFARS implementing rule pending; statutory hard date 1 Jan 2028 (new contracts), 1 Jan 2029 (standard), 1 Jan 2031 (legacy). T4 named indicator velocity_per_cycle 0.0.
named_watch_metrics.threshold_implications: Bull (T4 escalation 'ndaa_section_842_demand_pull_acceleration'): DFARS proposed rule published in Federal Register OR pilot procurement RFP under Section 842 language pre-2028 (+1 magnitude on B4 defence-tier monetisation channel; defence is most insulated premium channel because consumer-price politics does not bind). Bear: DFARS rule slips beyond 2028 statutory date OR pilot procurements use commercial-COTS exemption broadly (-0.5 magnitude on B4; defence channel fails to materialise as premium tier).

named_watch_metrics.metric: MOFCOM germanium / gallium / antimony export-licence regime status (Aug 2023 anchor)
named_watch_metrics.source: MOFCOM monthly bulletins (mofcom.gov.cn); USGS Mineral Commodity Summaries; CRU germanium / gallium price feeds; The Oregon Group critical-minerals tracker
named_watch_metrics.current_value: Aug 2023 regime in force; specific licence-grant counts limited transparency. Distinct from suspended 2025 MOFCOM 55-62 package. Sept 2024 antimony controls operational.
named_watch_metrics.threshold_implications: Bull (T3 escalation): approval rate stays low / declining; licence scope extends to tungsten, indium, niobium (+1 magnitude on B5 channel weight 0.4 to B4; reinforces Specialty / germanium franchise as most-insulated B4 monetisation node, FY2028 Specialty EBIT margin >15%). Bear (T3 deescalation): voluntary suspension as Phase-2 goodwill gesture in Busan-2 deal post 10 Nov 2026 expiry (-2 magnitude per T3 → B5 propagation rule; Specialty franchise economics erode toward commodity floor; FY2028 Specialty EBIT margin <10%; the 'moat fragments / asymmetric' scenario fires).

named_watch_metrics.metric: Fleet RFP language requiring FEOC-free / Battery-Passport-ready batteries (% of major-fleet RFPs)
named_watch_metrics.source: Ayvens (ex ALD-LeasePlan) press releases + Element Fleet + ARI / Holman + Federal Fleet Management Bulletins (DOE/FEMP); ESG-RFP exclusion language tracking
named_watch_metrics.current_value: Rising — Ayvens PowerUP 2026 plan targets <90 g/km fleet CO2 by 2026 (vs 112 g 2022); ESG-RFP exclusion language increasingly standard. T4 velocity_per_cycle 0.10.
named_watch_metrics.threshold_implications: Bull (T4 named threshold): sustained >50% of major-fleet RFPs requiring compliance attestation by 2027; major fleet operator (Ayvens, Element, ARI / Holman) publishes RFP language explicitly excluding non-FEOC-compliant batteries with attestation requirement (+0.5 magnitude per operator on B4 B2B/ESG-fund tier monetisation channel). Bear: <30% of major-fleet RFPs requiring compliance attestation by 2027 (table-stakes confirmation; B2B channel fails to translate into measurable premium).

expected_projection_topics: Compliant-CAM ASP premium evolution (Platts CAM Daily proxy) through FY2028 with bear-threshold tracking <5% sustained
Asian compliant-capacity build inside EU/US perimeter cumulative kt/yr vs T4 250 kt/yr threshold (EcoPro Hungary Phase 2, LG Chem TN, POSCO Future M, SK On, Sumitomo)
Treasury impracticable-to-trace expiry decision Q4 2026 + Q1 2027 hard-edge FEOC tracing guidance window
10 Nov 2026 Busan / MOFCOM-70 expiry path (snap-back / status-quo extension / Phase-2 carve-out below 100%) and downstream 2025 layer reactivation
EU Battery Passport (Feb 2027) first 6-month enforcement window: DG GROW / member-state penalty case publication and OEM Q-call passport-driven re-sourcing commentary
DFARS Section 842 implementing rule publication and DoD pilot procurement RFP under FEOC-free language
MOFCOM Aug 2023 germanium / gallium / antimony regime persistence vs Phase-2 goodwill suspension scenarios — Specialty franchise pricing power
Umicore-promoted GePETO + ReGAIN CRMA Strategic Project funding disbursement (EIB / EIF) progression through 2027
Cross-segment compliance-premium translation rate: BMS vs Catalysis (Euro 7 / EPA 2027) vs Recycling (31 Dec 2027 efficiency thresholds) vs Specialty (germanium) — segment-level read
falsification_criteria: Compliant-CAM ASP premium prints sustained <5% for >2 quarters per Platts CAM Daily proxy AND/OR <2% in any single quarter through 2026-2027 — moat-depth collapse on the BMS perimeter making Layer 2 unreachable on the load-bearing channel.

Asian compliant-capacity inside EU/US perimeter exceeds 250 kt/yr cumulative by year-end 2027 (EcoPro Phase 2 + LG Chem TN ramp + POSCO Future M + SK On / Sumitomo additional moves) — T4 erosion-via-Asian-localisation scenario fires definitively; the 'moat persists / premium erodes to cost-plus' resolution outcome locks in.

Treasury extends 'impracticable-to-trace' transition rule beyond end-2026 (NPRM filing, Federal Register notice, or confirmed extension by Q4 2026) — Layer 1 itself compresses; the May 2024 soften-via-guidance playbook replays and B4 prior re-anchors below 0.30.

Treasury Q3 2026 proposed regulations under OBBBA materially soften MACR thresholds OR expand safe harbours to Korea-, Mexico- and Morocco-routed material — T3 → B4 propagation re-rates 18-24 months; transshipment-corridor closure fails and Asian-localiser arbitrage widens.

Phase-2 Busan-2 carve-out below 100% on CAM post 10 Nov 2026 expiry (T3 alternate scenario fires) — the single T3 outcome that materially erodes B4 Layer 1; managed-trade quotas restructure the stack.

MOFCOM Aug 2023 germanium / gallium licensing regime voluntarily suspended OR materially loosened as Phase-2 goodwill gesture — Specialty / germanium franchise pricing power collapses; the 'moat fragments / asymmetric' scenario fires; B5 channel weight 0.4 to B4 also erodes.

EU Battery Passport (Feb 2027) produces 0 published DG GROW / member-state penalty cases or market-access denials through H2 2027 — direct table-stakes confirmation; B4 Layer 2 reads as binary qualifier not premium driver; convergence stream's 'degrades to table stakes within 3-5 years' verdict validates within resolution window.

DFARS Section 842 implementing rule slips beyond 1 Jan 2028 statutory date OR pilot procurements use commercial-COTS exemption broadly — defence-tier premium channel fails to materialise; B4 monetisation channel mix narrows to Specialty / germanium only.

USTR Section 301 overcapacity-investigation remedy (24 Jul 2026) closes without remedy or with carve-out language — T3 underwriter compresses; B4 Layer 1 reading degrades; the structural escalation pipeline that supports our +25pp wedge vs consensus on T3 fails to deliver.

Umicore's GePETO + ReGAIN CRMA Strategic Project designations stall at funding-disbursement stage through 2027 (no EIB / EIF disbursement, no member-state co-financing) — institutional infrastructure signal degrades; designation stays nominal rather than commercial.

Major fleet operators (Ayvens, Element, ARI / Holman) <30% RFP penetration of FEOC-free / Battery-Passport-ready language by 2027 — B2B/ESG-fund tier monetisation channel fails to translate into measurable premium; only defence + premium-OEM channels remain potentially viable but each has its own falsification path.
bets.breakage_shape: When B4 breaks, the most likely break-shape is a 12-18 month asymmetric grind concentrated on the BMS perimeter: through 2026 the regulatory framework persists at full aperture exactly as scheduled (CRMA designations advance, EU Battery Passport guidelines firm up, NDAA 842 enacted, 30D 70% threshold biting, USTR overcapacity hearings 5-8 May 2026 produce structural framing language) — the Layer 1 reading appears strong and consensus 'persists_and_translates' framing initially holds. Then through H2 2026 the calendared erosion gates fire one by one: EcoPro Hungary Phase 2 commissions on schedule taking cumulative Asian compliant capacity to ~165 kt/yr, Treasury announces extension of impracticable-to-trace OR issues moderate Q1 2027 guidance (not hard-edge), the 24 Jul 2026 USTR remedy lands without transshipment-corridor closure, and Q3 2026 OBBBA proposed regulations widen safe harbours to Mexico-routed material. By Q1 2027 the Platts CAM Daily proxy prints 5-7% premium (down from 8-15%); by Q3 2027 LG Chem TN ramps and POSCO Future M Quebec announcements take Asian capacity to ~220 kt/yr; by Q4 2027 sustained <5% premium reads are visible AND the EU Battery Passport's first 6 enforcement-window months produce 0 published penalty cases. The Convergence stream's 'degrades to table stakes within 3-5 years' verdict validates exactly here. Cross-segment monitoring shows Specialty / germanium franchise holds (T3-anchored, B5 channel partial hold), Catalysis Euro 7 / EPA 2027 content uplift compounds normally (sovereign-framework-protected), Recycling traceability proves commercially neutral. The bet's resolution-true definition (Layer 1 + Layer 2 both clear with measurable premium retention >5% on the BMS + Specialty perimeters) fails because the BMS load-bearing channel collapses to cost-plus while Specialty rent holds — asymmetric realisation but the cross-segment definition does not satisfy. The sharper alternative break-shape is a 6-9 month compression triggered by the Treasury extension decision in late Q4 2026 (the 'moat softens via Treasury carveouts replay' scenario): once Treasury extends impracticable-to-trace, Auto Innovators lobby filings cite supply-chain readiness, IRS Q3 2026 proposed regs widen safe harbours, Asian compliant-capacity routes through Korea/Mexico transshipment with reduced friction, ASP premium collapses to <3% by mid-2027 — the sequence runs from Layer 1 directly into Layer 2 with no monetisation lag. The tail break-shape is the 'moat fragments / asymmetric' path where BMS erosion pairs with a Phase-2 MOFCOM germanium suspension post 10 Nov 2026 — the rare two-node simultaneous break that would produce no surviving full-aperture monetisation channel. In every break path, Umicore's compliance-premium thesis collapses to license-to-operate-only framing; the convergence-flagged S-3-1 forced-decision (JV, tolling, partial exit) on BMS becomes pre-determined irrespective of customer execution; CRMA Strategic Project designations remain nominal but are no longer load-bearing for the equity story. The bet's own resolution-true definition is not satisfiable on a license-to-operate-only operating posture.

baseline_research_note.claim_under_test: By FY2028 the regulatory framework underwriting Western premium pricing for compliant, traceable, FEOC-clean materials persists at full or near-full aperture (CRMA Strategic Project status holding, EU Battery Passport binding from Feb 2027, IRA OBBBA FEOC + MACR mechanism intact, NDAA Section 842 implementing rule live or pulled forward, MOFCOM Aug 2023 germanium / gallium regime operational), AND that framework translates into measurable, monetised ASP-premium realisation on at least the BMS + Specialty Materials perimeters — not just into table-stakes attestation requirements. The cross-segment scope spans BMS (Olen + Nysa + Korea CAM + Loyalist + offtake book), Catalysis (Euro 7 + EPA 2027 content uplift via PGM-loop traceability), Recycling (EU recycling-efficiency thresholds + CRMA secondary supply), and Specialty (germanium / electro-optic franchise anchored on MOFCOM Aug 2023). Resolution requires both Layer 1 (regulatory persistence) AND Layer 2 (commercial monetisation, expressed via measurable ASP premium retention >5% on the most-exposed segments) to clear.
baseline_research_note.what_is_known_today: The legal infrastructure is operational at its widest aperture as of May 2026. EU CRMA: 47 Strategic Projects designated May 2024 first list (Umicore-promoted GePETO + ReGAIN included for germanium processing + substitution); second-round >160 applications (75 battery, 21 RE-magnet) under assessment since 19 Jan 2026. EU Battery Regulation 2023/1542: enters force 17 Aug 2023; Battery Passport hard date 18 Feb 2027 with operational guidelines published Aug 2025; carbon-footprint phase-in 2025-2026; recycled-content requirements from 2028; recycling-efficiency thresholds 31 Dec 2027 (90% Ni/Co/Cu/Pb, 50% Li). IRA Section 30D: 70% battery-component domestic-content threshold live 1 Jan 2026, stepping 80% 2027 / 85% 2028 / 90% 2029; FEOC 25% ownership threshold per May 2024 Final Rule with 'impracticable-to-trace' transition for graphite, electrolyte salts, trace minerals expiring 31 Dec 2026. OBBBA (Jul 2025): codified FEOC into statute via Material Assistance Cost Ratio (MACR) mechanism for §45Y/48E/45X; IRS Notice 2026-15 (12 Feb 2026) issued MACR framework + safe harbours, comment window closed 30 Mar 2026, proposed regulations expected Q3 2026. NDAA Section 842 (FY26, enacted 23 Dec 2025): prohibits DoD procurement of advanced batteries from FEOCs with phased compliance 1 Jan 2028 (new contracts), 1 Jan 2029 (standard), 1 Jan 2031 (legacy); DFARS implementing rule pending. US tariff stack: Section 301 cumulative ~127.2% on Chinese CAM as of Q3 2025; non-EV lithium-ion batteries stepping to 25% on 1 Jan 2026, natural graphite to 25% on 1 Jan 2026 per FRN 89 FR 76581. MOFCOM: Aug 2023 germanium / gallium licensing regime operational; Sept 2024 antimony controls; Oct 2025 MOFCOM 55-58 + 61-62 (rare-earth / permanent-magnet / battery-graphite) package SUSPENDED via MOFCOM/GAC Announcement 70/2025 through 10 Nov 2026 post-Busan. USTR: Section 301 overcapacity investigations launched 11 Mar 2026 across 16 economies; hearings 5-8 May 2026; remedy target 24 Jul 2026; framing remains structural. Asian compliant-capacity build inside perimeter: EcoPro BM Debrecen 54 kt/yr live 1 Dec 2025 (NCA / NCM high-nickel), expanding to 108 kt/yr in 2026; LG Chem Tennessee in commissioning H1 2026 at half-original-scale (~60 kt/yr by 2027); POSCO Future M Bécancour Quebec announcements pending; SK On + Sumitomo Metal Mining additional moves under tracking. Compliant-CAM ASP premium estimated 8-15% per Platts CAM Daily indications. Umicore FY2025: BMS revenue EUR 436m at -19.5% Adjusted EBIT margin (EUR -91m), capital employed EUR 2.0bn (56% of group), -4.8% ROCE; WC build EUR 261m; Specialty Materials EUR 76m EBIT at 13.6% margin / 11.1% ROCE; Catalysis EUR 383m EBIT at 23.0% / 40.8% ROCE; Recycling EUR 296m EBIT at 31.2% (208% reported ROCE distorted by gold leaseback). Convergence stream: compliance premium 'degrades from competitive advantage to table stakes within 3-5 years'.
baseline_research_note.contradictions_in_evidence: Several genuine cross-stream tensions exist on the compliance-mandate-vs-uptake split. (1) Strategic stream A4 (regulatory premium) is rated VALIDATED with net-positive signal score +4.44 — supportive of moat-persistence — but the failure scenarios explicitly include 'Governments soften localization rules; OEMs prioritize lowest-cost supply amid EV affordability push; ESG/traceability becomes table-stakes with no premium' — i.e. the validation is on existence-of-framework, not on premium-translation. The strategic stream therefore provides asymmetric evidence that does NOT distinguish Layer 1 from Layer 2. (2) Macro stream T4 deep-read explicitly separates P(framework persists through 2030) ~0.70-0.80 from P(framework translates into >5% sustained ASP premium for incumbent Western suppliers through 2030) ~0.35-0.45 — directly identifying the wedge that consensus mis-prices; this is the load-bearing insight underwriting B4. (3) Macro stream alternate scenario probabilities {moat_persists_and_translates: 0.30, premium_erodes_via_localisation: 0.40, treasury_softens: 0.20, enforcement_deepens: 0.10} are inconsistent with sell-side consensus closer to {persists_translates: 0.50, erodes: 0.25, softens: 0.15, deepens: 0.10} — our distribution shifts 20pp from 'persists_translates' into 'erodes', a quantifiable wedge but creates dependence on the localisation-pace track being read accurately. (4) Position-map pos_capability_integrated_data_traceability flags 'There is no moat, only table stakes' (Glencore, Li-Cycle, Aurubis, OEM closed-loop programs all pursuing similar circularity) — directly contradicting the strategic stream's 'integrated CAM + recycling supports circular supply and customer stickiness' framing. The 150 kt EU battery recycling plant deferral to 2032+ confirms the macro view that the integrated story is partial today. (5) CRMA Strategic Project status: Umicore's GePETO + ReGAIN are on the May 2024 first list — operationally significant but does NOT extend to CAM/pCAM and does NOT by itself convert to ASP premium. Equity-market commentary often conflates designation with premium. (6) Specialty / germanium asymmetry: pos_technology_germanium_franchise notes the policy premium is 'capturable but time-limited' — Western substitution feasible 3-5 years once scrap-recovery and primary routes qualify (5N Plus, Teledyne, AXT, JX Advanced Metals). Macro: 'treat as monetise-now niche, not permanent moat'. The Specialty perimeter's compliance pricing power is therefore structurally insulated through 2027-2028 but begins to fade beyond the resolution horizon — supportive for B4 within the 2028 window but with natural decay. (7) Buyer-power renegotiation: POSCO/GM/Ultium precedent visible directly in Umicore FY2025 EUR 261m WC build and EUR -91m BMS EBIT — strategic stream A2 (contract protection mechanisms) is rated MIXED. Convergence stream verdict: the compliance premium 'degrades from competitive advantage to table stakes within 3-5 years' lands within the 2028 horizon mathematically. (8) USTR overcapacity track vs Busan truce: dual-track state means escalation pipeline coexists with tactical truce, creating asymmetric optionality (Busan extension preserves stack; Busan lapse triggers 2025 MOFCOM snap-back; only Phase-2 carve-out below 100% is materially negative).
open_questions: What is the actual realised ASP premium for compliant-CAM vs Chinese-origin per Platts CAM Daily (current proxy 8-15%, not directly published)? Resolving this with published quarterly indications would re-rate B4 Layer 2 reading materially.

Will Treasury allow the 'impracticable-to-trace' transition to expire on schedule end-2026, AND will the Q1 2027 IRS hard-edge tracing guidance be issued? The single most consequential calendared decision for B4 Layer 1 in the 2026 window.

Will the Q3 2026 Treasury proposed regulations under OBBBA preserve MACR thresholds and tighten transshipment safe harbours, or widen safe harbours to Korea / Mexico / Morocco? T3 → B4 propagation re-rates 18-24 months on the latter.

Will the 24 Jul 2026 USTR overcapacity remedy extend Section 301 coverage upstream (pCAM, separators, processing equipment) or to transshipment corridors? T3 central-path scenario probability 0.55 supports the upside path but consensus under-prices it.

Will the 10 Nov 2026 Busan / MOFCOM-70 expiry produce snap-back of the 2025 MOFCOM 55-62 package, status-quo extension, or Phase-2 carve-out below 100% on CAM? The Phase-2 carve-out outcome (T3 8% probability) is the only outcome that materially erodes B4 Layer 1.

Will the EU Battery Passport (Feb 2027) produce at least one DG GROW or member-state market-access denial / penalty case with monetary threshold in H1-H2 2027? Absence through H2 2027 is direct table-stakes confirmation.
Will the DFARS Section 842 implementing rule pull forward to 2027 ahead of 1 Jan 2028 statutory date? Pull-forward is the leading indicator of defence-tier premium channel materialising.

Will Asian compliant-capacity inside the EU/US perimeter exceed the 250 kt/yr T4-bear threshold by year-end 2027? Tracking EcoPro Hungary Phase 2 (108 kt/yr planned 2026), LG Chem TN ramp pace, POSCO Future M Quebec announcement, SK On / Sumitomo additional moves.

Will the MOFCOM Aug 2023 germanium / gallium regime persist OR be voluntarily suspended as Phase-2 goodwill gesture? The single largest determinant of Specialty Materials compliance pricing power and the most-insulated B4 monetisation node.

Will major fleet operators (Ayvens, Element, ARI / Holman) publish RFP language explicitly excluding non-FEOC-compliant batteries with attestation requirement, and will OEM Q-call commentary reference passport-driven re-sourcing decisions? Direct read on Layer 2 commercial monetisation rate.
Will the Umicore-promoted GePETO + ReGAIN CRMA Strategic Project designations advance through EIB / EIF funding disbursement by year-end 2027? Institutional infrastructure operational signal.

factual_corrections: CRMA Strategic Project status: Umicore's GePETO (germanium processing) + ReGAIN (germanium substitution) are on the May 2024 first list of 47 Strategic Projects. Do not invent additional designations. Second-round >160 applications (75 battery, 21 RE-magnet) are under assessment since 19 Jan 2026 but Umicore-specific second-round designations are not confirmed at May 2026.

EU Battery Passport hard date is 18 Feb 2027 per Regulation 2023/1542 Article 77; operational guidelines published Aug 2025; CRMA Strategic Project status is a designation but does NOT by itself convert to ASP premium.

Treasury 'impracticable-to-trace' transition rule per May 2024 Final Rule covers graphite, electrolyte salts, certain trace mineral inputs through end-2026 (2026-12-31). Q1 2027 hard-edge FEOC tracing guidance is conditional on the expiry path.
USTR Section 301 overcapacity-investigation remedy target date is 24 Jul 2026; investigations launched 11 Mar 2026 across 16 economies; hearings 5-8 May 2026.

10 Nov 2026 is the Busan / MOFCOM-70 expiry date (MOFCOM 55-58 + 61-62 suspended via MOFCOM/GAC Announcement 70/2025 through 10 Nov 2026); the Aug 2023 germanium / gallium regime is DISTINCT and was untouched by Busan.

EcoPro BM Debrecen 54 kt/yr live 1 Dec 2025; expansion to 108 kt/yr planned 2026; LG Chem Tennessee in commissioning H1 2026 at half-original-scale (~60 kt/yr by 2027). Asian compliant capacity bear threshold per T4 is cumulative >250 kt/yr inside EU + US by year-end 2027.

FY26 NDAA Section 842 enacted 23 Dec 2025 prohibits DoD procurement of advanced batteries from FEOCs with phased compliance: 1 Jan 2028 (new contracts), 1 Jan 2029 (standard), 1 Jan 2031 (legacy programmes); DFARS implementing rule pending.

Compliance-mandate-vs-uptake split is the load-bearing insight from T4 — moat persistence (Layer 1) and moat monetisation (Layer 2) are separable; consensus systematically conflates them; the wedge IS the alpha. B4's resolution-true definition requires BOTH layers to clear.
Convergence stream verdict: compliance premium 'degrades from competitive advantage to table stakes within 3-5 years' lands within the FY2028 resolution horizon mathematically.

Position-map pos_capability_integrated_data_traceability flags 'There is no moat, only table stakes' (Glencore, Li-Cycle, Aurubis, OEM closed-loop programs all pursuing similar circularity) — Recycling/Catalysis traceability provides Layer 2 monetisation only as table stakes, not as premium driver.

cross_segment_TL_bleed_to_other_bets.directionality_rule: Per T4 propagation rule for B1 channel 'compliant_cam_asp_premium' (T4 weight 0.6 to B1) — when B4 Layer 2 erodes (compliant-CAM ASP premium <5% sustained for >2 quarters), B1 conversion margin compresses below break-even structurally and Q3 2027 BMS proof-gate becomes mathematically unreachable on the contracted book. When B4 Layer 2 holds at 5-10% (B4 central path), B1 reads its central-path 'partial_book_translation_proof_gate_marginally_clears' scenario. When B4 enforcement-teeth episode fires, B1 reads its 'regulatory_underwriting_premium_widens_marginal_break_even' scenario. B4 → B1 propagation is symmetric on direction but asymmetric on magnitude — the bear case for B4 (40% probability) IS the central-bear path for B1.
cross_segment_TL_bleed_to_other_bets.step5_propagation_rewiring_note: B4 → B1 premium-pricing channel weight 0.6 is the load-bearing cross-segment TL transmission for the Step 5 propagation rewire. T4 → B1 explicit T4 entry already carries this in B1's theme_dependencies (T4 weight 0.6 to B1, channel 'compliant_cam_asp_premium'). B4-as-bet thereby aggregates the T4 reading into a single bet-level signal that B1 must read; downstream rewire should ensure B4 TL changes propagate to B1 directly (not just via T4) to avoid double-counting on segment-shared transmission.

cross_segment_TL_bleed_to_other_bets.channel: Specialty premium driven by China germanium / antimony / gallium export-control regime
cross_segment_TL_bleed_to_other_bets.directionality_rule: Per T4 propagation_to_bets_seed entry to B5 (weight 0.4) — Specialty premium is anchored more on T3 (decoupling permanence) than T4, but compliance-moat enforcement on critical minerals (CRMA strategic-project designations on germanium recycling — Umicore's GePETO + ReGAIN — and MOFCOM Aug 2023 regime persistence) overlaps the demand-side monetisation channel for defence/space-solar applications.
