When B4 Layer 1 (regulatory persistence) widens via T4 enforcement-teeth + T3 USTR overcapacity remedy, B5 reads the +1 magnitude reinforcement (NDAA 842 + DFARS implementing rule pull-forward + CRMA funding disbursement); when Layer 1 compresses via Phase-2 carve-out or MOFCOM germanium voluntary suspension, B5 reads the -2 magnitude erosion (this is the 'moat fragments / asymmetric' scenario for B4). B4 → B5 propagation magnitude is smaller than B4 → B1 because B5 is structurally insulated by T3 alone with B4 contributing only marginal demand-pull through compliance enforcement.
cross_segment_TL_bleed_to_other_bets.step5_propagation_rewiring_note: B4 → B5 premium-channel weight 0.4 is the secondary cross-segment TL transmission for the Step 5 propagation rewire. T4 → B5 explicit weight 0.4 entry in T4.propagation_to_bets_seed already carries this in any B5 theme_dependencies. The rewire should ensure that B4-bear-on-Specialty-perimeter (the 'moat fragments / asymmetric' scenario, 6% probability) propagates to B5 directly, since this is the rare scenario where B4's TL erosion concentrates on the Specialty node rather than the BMS node — failing to wire this would leave B5 reading T4 only (not B4), under-counting the joint-asymmetric-failure mode.

bets.label: Specialty Materials premium pricing power through FY2028
bets.segment: Specialty Materials (Cobalt & Specialty Materials, Electro-Optic Materials including the germanium franchise, Metal Deposition Solutions)

intermediate_gates.name: Busan / MOFCOM-70 expiry — load-bearing dated breakage gate
intermediate_gates.what_resolves: MOFCOM/GAC Announcement 70/2025 (which on 7 Nov 2025 suspended MOFCOM 55-58 and 61-62 — rare-earth / permanent-magnet / battery-grade graphite controls — as part of the Busan truce) expires 10 Nov 2026. The single most consequential dated event for B5 is whether China simultaneously uses this expiry to (i) extend MOFCOM 70 / status-quo (T3 0.20 probability — B5 holds, germanium / gallium / antimony Aug 2023 regime untouched and operational), (ii) let the suspension lapse without renewal triggering snap-back of the 2025 layer AND extending the 2023 regime to additional metals tungsten / indium / niobium (T3 'mofcom_snapback_post_busan' escalation — B5 widens, germanium spot premia anchored, Specialty FY2028 EBIT margin >15% achievable), or (iii) negotiate a Phase-2 carve-out paired with VOLUNTARY relaxation of the August 2023 germanium / gallium licensing regime as a Chinese goodwill gesture (T3 alternate scenario 0.08-0.10 probability — directly fires the -2 magnitude rule on B5 per T3 propagation_to_bets_seed entry; Western feedstock-scarcity premium evaporates, Specialty franchise economics compress to commodity floor). Pre-positioning signals readable from September 2026 via MOFCOM monthly licensing-volume bulletins, Reuters / Bloomberg trade desk leaks on summit-channel communiques, and CRU germanium price-feed tape. The 10 Nov 2026 gate is what converts B5 from currently-observable persistence into a binary observable on the load-bearing macro driver.

intermediate_gates.name: MOFCOM August 2023 germanium / gallium / antimony approval-rate trajectory through Q3 2026
intermediate_gates.what_resolves: Distinct from the Busan / MOFCOM-70 layer above, the Aug 2023 germanium / gallium regime (extended Sept 2024 to antimony) has its OWN observable trajectory via MOFCOM monthly export-licence bulletins. Per the published industry tracking, applications from buyers in Japan, South Korea, the Netherlands and the United States have experienced longer processing times and lower approval rates than applications from Southeast Asia / Middle East buyers — the de-facto Western feedstock-scarcity mechanism that anchors current pricing. By end-Q3 2026 the cumulative approval-rate trend reads either as (a) holding or tightening (Western queue lengths sustained or extending; Chinese export volumes to Japan/Korea/EU below trailing 24-month averages — B5 supportive), or (b) loosening as Phase-2 pre-positioning (Western queue lengths shortening; export volumes recovering toward 2022 baselines — B5 prior re-anchors below 0.50 ahead of the formal 10 Nov 2026 decision). This is the leading-indicator gate that pre-resolves a meaningful portion of the Busan / MOFCOM-70 binary.

intermediate_gates.name: USTR Section 301 overcapacity-investigation remedy determination
intermediate_gates.what_resolves: T3-anchored gate (T3 weight 0.7 to B5 underwriter) — USTR remedy on or around 24 Jul 2026 either (i) extends Section 301 coverage upstream (covering germanium-bearing semiconductor inputs, indium / gallium intermediates, optical-fibre preform inputs) or to transshipment corridors used to circumvent MOFCOM controls — supportive of B5's Western feedstock-scarcity narrative AND the defence/space-solar demand-pull layer because it widens the wall around domestic compliant supply; or (ii) closes the investigation without remedy or with carve-out language, signalling the structural escalation pipeline supporting B5 has stalled. Hearings 5-8 May 2026 are the determinative pre-remedy block. Reading rule: extension upstream into critical-minerals adjacencies = +0.5 magnitude on B5 prior; carve-out language = -0.5 magnitude.

intermediate_gates.name: DFARS Section 842 implementing rule publication (NDAA defence-demand-pull pillar)
intermediate_gates.what_resolves: FY26 NDAA Section 842 (enacted 23 Dec 2025) prohibits DoD procurement of advanced batteries from FEOCs with phased compliance dates 1 Jan 2028 (new contracts), 1 Jan 2029 (standard), 1 Jan 2031 (legacy). The 95% non-FEOC cost threshold for the assembly exemption is the operative compliance gate. Specialty Materials' direct exposure runs through the germanium-substrate-anchored space-solar (CPV) supply chain to defence space-system primes (Lockheed, RTX, Northrop, Boeing satellite divisions) and the IR-optics / thermal-imaging supply chain to the ground-defence primes — neither is a battery-end-item but both are downstream of FEOC-sensitive critical-mineral inputs that DoD acquisition policy increasingly treats as adjacencies. DFARS implementing rule pull-forward (publication ahead of Jan 2028 statutory date) signals defence-demand pull-forward into the Specialty perimeter — widening the premium-tier monetisation channel via DoD pilot procurements specifying FEOC-clean germanium / gallium / antimony inputs. Slippage past 2027 confirms commercial monetisation lag. Reading rule: +1 magnitude on pull-forward (T4 escalation 'ndaa_section_842_demand_pull_acceleration' fires); -0.5 on slippage.

intermediate_gates.name: Western germanium spot-price premium vs China domestic — 4-quarter tape through FY2027
intermediate_gates.what_resolves: The single most readable monetisation indicator for the load-bearing Electro-Optic franchise. Public industry tracking (Argus Metals, Fastmarkets germanium prices, ScrapMonster, The Oregon Group critical-minerals tracker) prints Western in-warehouse germanium materially above China domestic ~$1,949/kg as of Q1 2026 — the structurally elevated Western premium IS the franchise's pricing-power expression. The 4-quarter rolling tape through FY2027 reads either (i) sustained at or above current >150% wedge consistent with MOFCOM Aug 2023 regime persistence (B5 supportive — Specialty FY2028 EBIT margin holds 13-15%), (ii) gradual compression toward 50-100% premium consistent with Western substitution timeline progressing (5N Plus / Teledyne / AXT / JX Advanced Metals scrap-recovery and primary qualification advancing — B5 central path slow-decay), or (iii) sharp compression to <50% premium consistent with regime relaxation under Phase-2 framework (B5 bear scenario realised; Specialty FY2028 EBIT margin compresses to 8-10%). The premium tape is the price-discovery channel that reveals which of the three dated regulatory paths is actually firing on the operating line.

bets.prior_rationale: B5 is the only one of the seven Crow's Nest bets where the load-bearing macro theme (T3 prior 0.66, holding with upward bias) anchors the prior ABOVE 0.50, with T4 (prior 0.42, ambiguous) acting as a smaller secondary demand-pull. Six observable inputs anchor the prior at 0.55. (1) T3 (weight 0.7, load-bearing per T3 propagation_to_bets_seed entry to B5) is at 0.66 with upward bias because the structural escalation pipeline (USTR overcapacity track, OBBBA-codified FEOC, IRS Notice 2026-15 MACR mechanism) coexists with the Busan tactical truce — and CRITICALLY the August 2023 germanium / gallium regime (the operative T3 sub-component for B5) is DISTINCT from the suspended October 2025 MOFCOM 55-62 package and was UNTOUCHED by Busan / MOFCOM-70. The T3 deep-read explicitly notes 'MOFCOM's August 2023 germanium / gallium licensing regime — distinct from the suspended 2025 package — remains fully operational.' This is the single most important factual asymmetry for B5 vs B4: B4's BMS-perimeter ASP-translation rate is being compressed in real time by Asian-localiser CAM capacity inside the perimeter; B5's Specialty-perimeter pricing power is being SUSTAINED in real time by an export-licensing regime that is currently operational and has only one specific path to break (voluntary suspension as Phase-2 goodwill gesture in Busan-2 deal). T3 central-path scenario probability 0.55 'structural decoupling holds with extension' + 0.20 'Busan renewed status quo' + 0.15 'mofcom snapback post Busan' = 0.90 cumulative supportive; only the 0.08-0.10 'Phase-2 carve-out below 100% on CAM with germanium goodwill suspension' scenario fires the -2 magnitude rule on B5. (2) Hard-data anchor: as of March 2026, public industry tracking shows Western in-warehouse germanium at ~$5,475/kg vs China domestic ~$1,949/kg — a 181% premium. This is not a forecast; this is an OBSERVED operating-line price reality that translates directly into Specialty Materials FY2025 EUR 76m EBITA at 13.6% margin / 11.1% ROCE (segment financials confirmed). The franchise is monetising the regime today; the question for FY2028 is whether the monetisation persists, not whether it materialises. (3) Specialty FY2025 baseline: revenue EUR 558m (+4.0% YoY), revenue mix 15.7% of group, EBITA EUR 76m (13.6% margin, 13.1% mix of group EBITA, 11.1% ROCE). Modest contributor to group EBITA but with high asymmetric upside if defence procurement scales — and CRITICALLY, with low single-segment-impairment downside because the EUR 558m revenue / EUR 76m EBITA scale is too small to anchor a board-level capital-discipline event (unlike BMS's EUR 2.0bn capital base where a EUR 400-800m second impairment is base-case bear). (4) T4 (weight 0.4, secondary demand-pull) is at 0.42 with 'moat-persists-premium-eroding' trajectory — but T4's erosion is concentrated on the BMS perimeter via Asian-localisation oversupply (EcoPro Hungary 108 kt/yr, LG Chem TN 60+ kt/yr, POSCO Future M Quebec). T4's compression channel does NOT directly hit Specialty: there is no Asian-localiser ramping germanium-substrate or GeCl4 capacity inside the EU/US perimeter at scale, and the Western-qualified peer set (5N Plus, Teledyne, AXT, JX Advanced Metals) is not Asian-promoter-controlled. T4 contributes secondary demand-pull via NDAA Section 842 + DFARS Section 842 implementing rule + CRMA Strategic Project status (Umicore-promoted GePETO germanium processing + ReGAIN germanium substitution on the May 2024 first list of 47 designations) + ESG-fund / fleet-RFP attestation language — each is real but each is asymmetric on the upside. (5) Position-map factual anchor: pos_technology_germanium_franchise notes 'few Western producers of qualified high-purity germanium substrates; Hoboken feedstock access is a structural advantage over mining-exposed competitors; long qualification cycles in space solar, IR optics, fiber preforms make respecification costly. Advantaged.' Macro stream's recommended action is 'protect the germanium franchise; scale selectively elsewhere.' The decision Umicore actively made was to RETAIN this franchise — the active position is well-defended. (6) Time-limited caveat that pulls the prior below 0.65: Macro's verdict on the franchise is 'capturable but time-limited — Western substitution feasible 3-5 years once scrap-recovery and primary routes qualify ... treat as monetise-now niche, not permanent moat.' The 3-5 year horizon mathematically lands inside the 2028 resolution window at its lower bound. Western substitution capacity at 5N Plus + Teledyne + AXT + JX Advanced Metals is advancing through scrap-recovery scaling and primary-route qualification — each year the Western substitution timeline progresses, the structural premium compresses by a measurable amount even WITHOUT MOFCOM regime relaxation. Net-net the prior settles at 0.55 — clearly above 0.50 because (a) the load-bearing macro driver T3 is at 0.66 with upward bias and the operative regime (Aug 2023) is fully untouched by the Busan truce, (b) the franchise is monetising the regime TODAY at a 181% Western-vs-China spot premium translating directly into FY2025 13.6% EBITA margin, (c) there is no Asian-localiser inside-the-perimeter erosion vector parallel to BMS's EcoPro / LG Chem / POSCO compression mechanism, (d) the segment-scale (EUR 558m revenue / EUR 76m EBITA) is too small to trigger the board-level kill-criteria mechanism even in the bear scenario; but well below 0.70 because (i) the Macro 3-5 year Western substitution timeline lands inside the resolution horizon at its lower bound mathematically, (ii) the Phase-2 carve-out scenario with voluntary germanium suspension is a real (8-10%) tail that fires the -2 magnitude rule via T3 propagation, (iii) defence demand-pull (NDAA 842) is dated to 2028+ and may not be visible in operating-line monetisation by FY2028 if DFARS implementing rule slips, and (iv) B5 inherits a secondary cross-segment bleed from B4 weight 0.4 such that the BMS-perimeter compliance-moat erosion narrative could mechanically pull broker / ESG-fund framing of Specialty pricing power down via brand-level read-across even when the Specialty-specific operating reality is intact. The 0.55 prior reflects the asymmetry: Specialty IS the most-insulated B-bet across the seven, but insulation does not mean impervious — the Phase-2 goodwill-suspension tail and the Western-substitution-timeline slow-decay path combine to keep the prior meaningfully below the 0.66 T3 anchor.
bets.thesis_resolves_true: By FY2028 Specialty Materials sustains revenue of EUR 600-750m (FY2025 EUR 558m baseline +5-8% CAGR) with EBITA in EUR 80-120m range, EBITA margin sustained at 13-16%, and ROCE held above 11% — AND the Western feedstock-scarcity premium for germanium / gallium / antimony inputs holds at or above 100% vs China domestic on a sustained basis through Q4 2027 per public industry tape (currently 181% Western premium per March 2026 industry tracking). The path requires: (i) MOFCOM Busan / MOFCOM-70 expiry on 10 Nov 2026 produces extension status quo OR snap-back of the 2025 MOFCOM 55-62 layer rather than Phase-2 carve-out with voluntary germanium suspension (T3 cumulative 0.90 supportive vs 0.08-0.10 break path), (ii) the August 2023 germanium / gallium / antimony regime continues operating with current approval-rate asymmetry (Western buyers facing longer processing times and lower approval rates than Southeast Asian / Middle East buyers) through 2028, (iii) Western substitution timeline (5N Plus, Teledyne, AXT, JX Advanced Metals) progresses but at a pace consistent with the upper-end of Macro's 3-5 year horizon — i.e. material qualification in 2028-2029 rather than 2027 — preserving Umicore's Hoboken-feedstock-anchored complex-feed advantage at least through the resolution date, (iv) the defence/space-solar demand-pull from NDAA Section 842 + DFARS implementing rule materialises with at least one DoD pilot procurement specifying FEOC-clean germanium / gallium inputs in 2027, anchoring premium-tier defence channel monetisation, (v) Umicore-promoted GePETO + ReGAIN CRMA Strategic Project designations advance through EIB / EIF funding disbursement signalling EU institutional infrastructure operational support, (vi) USTR Section 301 overcapacity remedy on 24 Jul 2026 extends coverage upstream into critical-minerals adjacencies (germanium-bearing semiconductor inputs, indium / gallium intermediates, optical-fibre preform inputs) widening the wall around Western compliant supply, (vii) Hoboken complex-feed continuity holds through the 2026 maintenance shutdown without unplanned operational interruption (germanium-bearing residues feedstock channel into Specialty preserved), and (viii) the cobalt specialty chemistry and electroplating sub-franchises (the non-germanium parts of Specialty) at minimum hold at FY2025 contribution levels — they are NOT load-bearing but their failure would compress segment margin even with germanium franchise intact. Resolution-true does NOT require Specialty to scale to a foundation-business contributor (Catalysis EUR 383m / Recycling EUR 296m vs Specialty EUR 76m baseline); it requires the segment to defend its current 13.6% margin profile and hold open the asymmetric upside if defence procurement scales materially. The bet is calibrated to franchise-defence-with-optional-defence-upside, NOT to a doubling of segment EBITA.
bets.thesis_resolves_false: The bet resolves false in any of three structurally different ways. Most likely break path: a slow-decay where the Busan / MOFCOM-70 expiry produces extension status quo (no Phase-2 carve-out) BUT the Western substitution timeline progresses at the FAST end of Macro's 3-5 year horizon — 5N Plus expands its Trail BC germanium operations and Saint-Augustin Quebec antimony processing to material 2027-2028 qualified output, Teledyne brings new primary-route germanium capacity online, AXT Asia operations re-route Western-qualified material at lower premium pricing, and JX Advanced Metals Japan ramps competing Specialty-grade germanium / gallium output — collectively compressing the Western-vs-China spot premium from current 181% toward 50-80% range by H2 2027. Specialty FY2027-2028 EBITA margin compresses from 13.6% to 9-11% as franchise pricing power erodes ahead of any regulatory shift; segment EBITA falls to EUR 50-65m on flat revenue. The MOFCOM regime is still operating, but the Western substitution capacity has caught up enough that the franchise-rent has decayed structurally. Less likely but more abrupt: the MOFCOM Phase-2 voluntary suspension scenario (T3 alternate scenario 0.08-0.10 probability) — the 10 Nov 2026 Busan / MOFCOM-70 expiry produces a Phase-2 framework deal where China voluntarily suspends or materially loosens the August 2023 germanium / gallium / antimony licensing regime as a goodwill gesture, paired with managed-trade quotas on CAM / precursors / battery-graphite below the 100% line. The T3 -> B5 propagation rule fires at full magnitude (-2): germanium spot premia compress sharply (Western warehouse pricing collapses toward $2,500-3,000/kg from current $5,400+, ~30-50% premium not 181%), Umicore Electro-Optic franchise economics erode rapidly through H1 2027 as inventory cycles unwind at lower realised prices, Specialty FY2028 EBITA margin compresses to 7-9%, segment EBITA prints EUR 35-50m. The 'moat fragments / asymmetric Specialty collapse' scenario in B4 fires concurrently. Tail-but-non-trivial: a defence-demand-pull failure path where MOFCOM regime persistence holds AND Western substitution is slow BUT the NDAA Section 842 / DFARS implementing rule slips materially past 2028 statutory date OR DoD pilot procurements rely broadly on the COTS exemption and the RDT&E exemption rather than activating new FEOC-clean specifications, AND the space-solar demand cycle (Boeing / RTX / Northrop satellite programmes, Starlink-class commercial constellations) experiences a synchronised slowdown — the asymmetric upside that the prior is partly anchored on fails to materialise; segment continues at FY2025 baseline EUR 76m EBITA in nominal terms but in real terms loses ~5-7% to indexation/cost stack, and the FY2028 monetisation reality reads as 'franchise defence successful, asymmetric upside absent' rather than 'franchise defence successful with capturable upside'. Resolution-true definition requires both layers (regime persistence + measurable monetisation) and the defence-demand-pull failure makes the upper-band resolution-true unreachable while keeping the lower-band marginal — the resolution is technically satisfied at the floor but the bet's calibration to franchise-defence-with-optional-defence-upside fails at the optional-upside leg. In all three false paths, Umicore's stated 'protect the germanium franchise; scale selectively elsewhere' decision either (a) succeeds at protecting the franchise but the franchise itself becomes commodity-with-attestation by 2028 (slow-decay), (b) fails because the regulatory underwriter is voluntarily withdrawn (Phase-2 goodwill), or (c) succeeds but never captures the asymmetric defence-procurement upside that justified the active retention.

transmission_mechanisms.reading_rule: Reading T3 named-watch metric 'MOFCOM germanium / gallium / antimony export-licence approval rate (Aug-2023 regime)'. Per T3 propagation rule for B5 (weight 0.7, channel 'Direct: MOFCOM Aug-2023 germanium / gallium licensing regime drives the Western feedstock scarcity premium captured in Electro-Optic'): '+1 magnitude per MOFCOM tightening or extension to additional metals; -2 magnitude on regime suspension as Phase-2 goodwill'. The Aug 2023 regime is DISTINCT from the suspended 2025 MOFCOM 55-62 package and was untouched by Busan / MOFCOM-70 — the operative regime for B5 is fully operational as of May 2026. Bull threshold: MOFCOM monthly bulletins show approval-rate to Western buyers (Japan, South Korea, Netherlands, US) tightening through Q3 2026 OR licence scope extends to tungsten, indium, niobium. Bear threshold: voluntary suspension or material loosening as Phase-2 goodwill gesture in Busan-2 deal post 10 Nov 2026 expiry — fires the -2 magnitude rule directly.

T3 dated gate: 10 November 2026 Busan / MOFCOM-70 expiry. The 2025 MOFCOM 55-58 + 61-62 package (rare-earth / permanent-magnet / battery-graphite) is currently SUSPENDED via MOFCOM/GAC Announcement 70/2025. Three paths: (i) extension status quo (T3 0.20 — B5 holds, 2023 regime untouched), (ii) snap-back of 2025 layer + 2023 regime extends to additional metals (T3 'mofcom_snapback_post_busan' escalation — B5 widens, +1 magnitude), or (iii) Phase-2 framework deal with managed-trade quotas paired with VOLUNTARY relaxation of August 2023 germanium / gallium regime (T3 alternate scenario 0.08-0.10 — fires the -2 magnitude rule on B5). T3 cumulative 0.90 supportive vs 0.08-0.10 break path. The 2025 layer is asymmetric upside-only for B5 (its snap-back would be incremental supportive but its continued suspension is neutral); the binary is on whether the August 2023 regime survives or is voluntarily withdrawn.

T3 dated gate: USTR Section 301 overcapacity-investigation remedy by 24 Jul 2026 (16 economies investigated since 11 Mar 2026; hearings 5-8 May 2026). For B5 specifically — distinct from the BMS-perimeter pCAM / separators / processing-equipment extension that anchors the B4 / B1 reading — the relevant extension is into critical-minerals adjacencies: germanium-bearing semiconductor inputs, indium / gallium intermediates, optical-fibre preform inputs, and antimony-bearing flame-retardant / lead-acid-battery inputs. Reading rule: extension into these adjacencies = +0.5 magnitude on B5 (widens wall around Western compliant supply); coverage limited to existing CAM / battery-cell scope = neutral; carve-out language = -0.5 magnitude. Per T3 central-path scenario probability 0.55, the extension path is meaningfully more likely than consensus.

Reading the Macro time-limited caveat directly: 'capturable but time-limited — Western substitution feasible 3-5 years once scrap-recovery and primary routes qualify (5N Plus, Teledyne, AXT, JX Advanced Metals) ... treat as monetise-now niche, not permanent moat.' This is the slow-decay channel that operates INSIDE T3 persistence — a structural compression of franchise rent independent of the regulatory regime. Reading rule: each named Western competitor reaching Specialty-grade germanium / gallium qualification at scale = -0.5 magnitude on B5 (e.g. 5N Plus Trail BC expansion + Saint-Augustin antimony scale-up + Teledyne primary-route germanium + AXT Asia re-routing + JX Advanced Metals Japan growth). Bull: Western substitution stalls (5N Plus capacity slippage, Teledyne primary-route delays, qualification cycles extending). The 4-quarter Western-vs-China spot premium tape per Argus / Fastmarkets / The Oregon Group is the direct read on this channel — 181% premium March 2026 vs threshold of >100% for B5 supportive central path.

Per T4 propagation_to_bets_seed entry to B5 (weight 0.4): 'Specialty premium is anchored more on T3 (decoupling permanence) than T4, but compliance-moat enforcement on critical minerals (CRMA strategic-project designations on germanium recycling, MOFCOM regime persistence) overlaps the demand-side monetisation channel for defence/space-solar.' Reading T4 escalation 'ndaa_section_842_demand_pull_acceleration': DFARS amendment publishing FEOC-battery exclusion language ahead of 1 Jan 2028 statutory date; major defence prime (Lockheed, RTX, GD, Northrop) Q-call commentary on compliant-mineral sourcing premium for satellite / space-solar / IR-optics programmes; Federal Register notice of DoD pilot procurement specifying FEOC-clean germanium / gallium inputs. The 95% non-FEOC cost threshold for the assembly exemption is the operative compliance gate. Reading rule per T4 directionality: 'TL up → Specialty premium reinforced by enforcement teeth; TL down → Specialty premium remains anchored to T3 alone, with compliance contributing only marginal demand-pull'. Pull-forward = +1 magnitude on B5 defence-tier monetisation channel; slippage past 2028 = -0.5 magnitude (B5 anchors on T3 alone, central-path resolution-true at the floor not upper band).

T4 named-watch metric 'CRMA Strategic Project designations approved + funding disbursed (count, EUR m)' applied to Umicore-promoted designations. Per T4 propagation rule: GePETO (germanium processing) + ReGAIN (germanium substitution) are on the May 2024 first list of 47 Strategic Projects. CRITICAL CALIBRATION: do not invent additional designations. Second-round >160 applications under assessment since 19 Jan 2026 but Umicore-specific second-round designations are not confirmed at May 2026. Reading rule: GePETO + ReGAIN advancing through EIB / EIF funding disbursement by year-end 2027 = +0.5 magnitude on B5 (institutional infrastructure operational signal supporting the 'protect the germanium franchise' active position); designations stall at announcement stage with no funding disbursement = -0.5 magnitude (signals EU institutional infrastructure non-operational, B5 falls back to MOFCOM-anchored T3 channel only). CRMA designation reads on Layer 1 (legal infrastructure) not Layer 2 (commercial monetisation) — supportive but does not by itself convert to ASP.

T4 named indicator 'Fleet RFP language requiring FEOC-free / Battery-Passport-ready batteries (% of EU+US fleet RFPs)' has limited direct applicability to Specialty Materials (germanium substrates, GeCl4, electroplating chemistry, cobalt specialty are NOT battery-EV-fleet inputs). The transmission to B5 is via the analogous attestation-language channel for critical-minerals procurement: ESG-fund / institutional-buyer mandates for traceable, FEOC-clean germanium / gallium / antimony in space-solar (Starlink, Eutelsat, satellite insurers' due-diligence requirements), IR-optics (commercial thermal imaging, FLIR / Teledyne FLIR procurement specifications), and optical-fibre preforms (Corning / Prysmian / OFS supply-chain disclosures). Reading rule: each major institutional buyer publishing FEOC-clean attestation language for critical-minerals inputs = +0.25 magnitude on B5 secondary monetisation channel. This channel is structurally smaller than the defence-procurement channel (smaller addressable market) but is more leading on premium-realisation.

CRITICAL DIFFERENTIATOR vs B4: T4's load-bearing erosion channel — Asian-localiser CAM capacity ramping inside the EU/US perimeter (EcoPro Hungary 108 kt/yr, LG Chem TN 60+ kt/yr, POSCO Future M Quebec) — does NOT directly hit Specialty. There is no Asian-promoter ramping germanium-substrate, GeCl4, electroplating-chemistry, or cobalt-specialty-chemistry capacity inside EU/US at scale. The Western-qualified peer set (5N Plus Canada, Teledyne US, AXT US-listed-China-based, JX Advanced Metals Japan) is not Asian-promoter-controlled and is structurally Western-jurisdiction-domiciled. Reading rule: the T4 erosion-via-Asian-localisation scenario fires on B4 (40% probability) and B1 (T4 weight 0.6 to B1) but does NOT propagate directly to B5. This insulation IS the structural basis for B5's higher prior than B4 despite both bets reading T4 in their dependency stack — T4's load-bearing transmission channel is mechanically inapplicable to the Specialty perimeter.

scenarios.name: central_path_mofcom_aug_2023_regime_persists_premium_holds_franchise_defence_clears
scenarios.narrative: Through end-2027 the August 2023 MOFCOM germanium / gallium / antimony licensing regime persists at full operational aperture, distinct from any Busan / MOFCOM-70 dynamics. The 10 Nov 2026 Busan / MOFCOM-70 expiry resolves via extension status quo (T3 0.20) or snap-back of the 2025 layer (T3 'mofcom_snapback_post_busan' channel) — neither path involves voluntary suspension of the August 2023 regime. Western in-warehouse germanium spot premium vs China domestic compresses from current 181% toward ~100-130% by end-2027 as Western substitution timeline progresses at the upper-end of Macro's 3-5 year horizon (5N Plus Trail BC ramps but at slower pace; Teledyne primary-route germanium qualification slips into 2028; AXT remains constrained by Asia-side regulatory friction; JX Advanced Metals Japan adds capacity at premium-tier pricing). USTR Section 301 overcapacity remedy on 24 Jul 2026 extends coverage upstream including into germanium-bearing semiconductor inputs and optical-fibre preform inputs — supportive widening of the Western compliant-supply wall. DFARS Section 842 implementing rule published Q3-Q4 2027 ahead of 1 Jan 2028 statutory date with at least one DoD pilot procurement RFP specifying FEOC-clean germanium / gallium inputs (defence-tier premium channel anchored). Umicore-promoted GePETO + ReGAIN advance through EIB / EIF funding disbursement (institutional infrastructure signal). Specialty FY2026 EBITA holds at ~EUR 75-85m; FY2027 EUR 80-95m; FY2028 EUR 85-110m; revenue grows from EUR 558m to EUR 620-700m at 13-15% EBITA margin sustained. ROCE holds at 11-13%. The 'capturable but time-limited' niche delivers franchise defence successfully through the resolution date; defence-tier upside materialises modestly. Hoboken complex-feed continuity holds through the 2026 maintenance shutdown without unplanned interruption.
scenarios.what_makes_this_path_break: Phase-2 carve-out scenario fires at 10 Nov 2026 with voluntary germanium suspension (T3 0.08-0.10); OR Western substitution pace accelerates beyond upper end of Macro 3-5 year range (5N Plus + Teledyne both reach Specialty-grade qualification at scale by H2 2027); OR DFARS Section 842 implementing rule slips beyond 2028 with broad COTS exemption (defence-tier upside fails to materialise); OR Hoboken maintenance shutdown extends to operational interruption affecting germanium-bearing residues feedstock continuity.

scenarios.name: regime_persists_widening_premium_defence_pull_forward_upside_materialises
scenarios.narrative: The asymmetric upside path. Busan / MOFCOM-70 expiry produces snap-back of the 2025 MOFCOM 55-58 + 61-62 package (rare-earth / permanent-magnet / battery-graphite controls reinstate) AND the August 2023 regime extends to additional metals (tungsten, indium, niobium) — T3 'mofcom_snapback_post_busan' escalation fires at full magnitude; T3 -> B5 propagation reads +1 magnitude. USTR Section 301 overcapacity remedy on 24 Jul 2026 extends coverage into critical-minerals adjacencies materially. DFARS Section 842 implementing rule pull-forward to Q1-Q2 2027 with first DoD pilot procurement RFP under Section 842 language specifying FEOC-clean germanium / gallium / antimony inputs in space-solar / IR-optics / optical-fibre programmes; major defence prime (Lockheed, RTX, GD, Northrop, Boeing satellite divisions) Q-call commentary references compliant-mineral sourcing premium for satellite programmes. Western germanium spot premium vs China domestic widens from current 181% toward 200-250% range as MOFCOM tightening propagates (longer processing times, lower approval rates for Western buyers). Western substitution timeline slips materially (5N Plus capacity slippage, Teledyne primary-route delays, qualification cycles extending). CRMA GePETO + ReGAIN funding disbursement accelerates with member-state co-financing. ESG-fund / institutional-buyer attestation language on FEOC-clean germanium / gallium / antimony becomes standard in space-solar and IR-optics procurement. Specialty FY2026 EBITA EUR 80-90m; FY2027 EUR 100-130m; FY2028 EUR 130-180m on revenue scaling to EUR 720-820m at 17-22% EBITA margin. ROCE expands to 16-22%. Specialty becomes an asymmetric upside contributor to group EBITA.

key_anchors_FY2028.mofcom_aug_2023_regime_status: extended; 2025 layer snap-back; coverage to tungsten/indium/niobium

scenarios.what_makes_this_path_break: Busan extension renewed without 2025-layer snap-back (T3 status-quo 0.20); OR DFARS Section 842 implementing rule slips and DoD relies on COTS exemption broadly; OR space-solar / IR-optics demand-cycle synchronised slowdown; OR Western substitution pace accelerates faster than expected (Macro 3-5 year horizon hits at the lower bound 2027).

scenarios.name: slow_decay_western_substitution_progresses_franchise_rent_compresses
scenarios.narrative: MOFCOM August 2023 regime persists (no Phase-2 voluntary suspension) AND Busan / MOFCOM-70 resolves via extension status quo — but Western substitution timeline progresses at the FAST end of Macro's 3-5 year horizon. 5N Plus expands Trail BC germanium operations and Saint-Augustin Quebec antimony processing to material 2027-2028 qualified output; Teledyne brings new primary-route germanium capacity online ahead of plan; AXT Asia operations re-route Western-qualified material at lower premium pricing; JX Advanced Metals Japan ramps competing Specialty-grade germanium / gallium output; collectively the qualification cycles complete by H2 2027 with Specialty-grade alternatives entering preferred-supplier lists at premium prices but at narrowing spread. Western-vs-China spot premium compresses from current 181% toward 50-80% range by end-2027 — franchise pricing power erodes ahead of any regulatory shift. DFARS Section 842 implementing rule arrives at statutory date Jan 2028 but DoD pilot procurements distribute volumes across the broader Western-qualified peer set rather than concentrating on Umicore. CRMA GePETO + ReGAIN advance but at funding pace consistent with broader EU institutional-infrastructure rollout (no Umicore-specific differentiator). Specialty FY2026 EBITA EUR 70-78m; FY2027 EUR 60-72m; FY2028 EUR 50-70m; revenue grows nominally to EUR 580-650m but margin compresses to 9-11%. ROCE compresses to 7-10%. The 'capturable but time-limited' niche resolves on the time-limited side; franchise defence is technically successful (segment continues operating, no impairment) but the rent has decayed structurally; B5 resolves false at the floor — Specialty does not deliver the FY2028 EBITA margin profile that the bet requires.

key_anchors_FY2028.mofcom_aug_2023_regime_status: operational; Western substitution caught up materially

scenarios.what_makes_this_path_break: 5N Plus or Teledyne capacity slippage; OR primary-route qualification cycles extending into 2029-2030; OR MOFCOM tightening extends the regime to additional metals (preserves franchise rent on broader scope even as germanium narrows); OR DFARS Section 842 implementation concentrates DoD volumes on Umicore-specific FEOC-clean germanium specification; OR China domestic germanium pricing structurally rises (closing the wedge from below rather than from above).

scenarios.name: tail_mofcom_phase_2_voluntary_germanium_suspension_premium_evaporates
scenarios.narrative: The load-bearing tail scenario. The 10 Nov 2026 Busan / MOFCOM-70 expiry produces a Phase-2 framework deal where China VOLUNTARILY suspends or materially loosens the August 2023 germanium / gallium / antimony licensing regime as a goodwill gesture, paired with managed-trade quotas on CAM / precursors / battery-graphite below the 100% line. T3 alternate scenario fires at 0.08-0.10 probability per T3 forward_projection; the T3 -> B5 propagation rule fires at full -2 magnitude per T3 propagation_to_bets_seed entry to B5. Headline framing shifts from 'structural decoupling' to 'managed competition'. Germanium spot premia compress sharply: Western warehouse pricing collapses from current $5,400+ toward $2,500-3,000/kg (~30-50% premium not 181%) within 6-12 months as inventory cycles unwind at lower realised prices. Umicore Electro-Optic franchise economics erode rapidly through H1 2027 and into H2 2027. Defence-tier premium channel partially holds (NDAA Section 842 statute persists; DoD procurement still requires FEOC-clean specifications) but addressable market shrinks materially. Western substitution timeline accelerates because price-economics no longer support premium qualification investment from 5N Plus / Teledyne. CRMA GePETO + ReGAIN designations remain nominal but funding-disbursement urgency drops. Specialty FY2026 EBITA EUR 65-75m (initial inventory cycle holds); FY2027 EUR 35-50m (compression bites); FY2028 EUR 30-50m at 7-9% EBITA margin on revenue compressing to EUR 480-560m. ROCE compresses to 4-7%. The 'moat fragments / asymmetric Specialty collapse' scenario in B4 fires concurrently — B4-bear-on-Specialty-perimeter propagates directly to B5. The premium evaporates exactly as the brief warned. Umicore's stated 'protect the germanium franchise' decision proves correct in intent (the franchise is still operational) but impotent in execution (the regulatory underwriter has been voluntarily withdrawn). Portfolio implications: deposition and cobalt specialty conversion sub-franchises become formal pruning candidates; Specialty's 'contained optionality' framing converts to 'contained legacy' framing at the group level.

key_anchors_FY2028.mofcom_aug_2023_regime_status: voluntarily suspended or materially loosened as Phase-2 goodwill

scenarios.what_makes_this_path_break: Phase-2 negotiations collapse and 10 Nov 2026 expiry resolves via extension or snap-back; OR China retains August 2023 regime even within Phase-2 framework (carving out CAM / battery materials but not germanium / gallium / antimony); OR DFARS Section 842 implementing rule pull-forward concentrates DoD demand on the surviving Western suppliers (Umicore + 5N Plus + Teledyne) at sustained premium-tier pricing despite spot-market compression; OR space-solar demand surge (NASA Mars programme acceleration, Eutelsat / Starlink constellation expansion) absorbs Western inventory at premium prices independent of MOFCOM dynamics.

scenarios.name: tail_grand_bargain_full_premium_collapse_specialty_re_scoped
scenarios.narrative: Tail-but-non-trivial: a negotiated grand bargain (T3 'negotiated_grand_bargain' 0.02 probability scenario, slightly upweighted here for B5-specific exposure given Specialty is the most-direct germanium-and-gallium-channel beneficiary). The grand bargain restructures the entire critical-minerals stack: managed-trade quotas paired with Chinese unilateral relaxation of germanium / gallium / antimony controls AND partial Section 301 rollback on critical-minerals adjacencies AND OBBBA MACR safe-harbour widening via legislative trade-relief. Headline framing shifts from 'structural' to 'managed competition' across ALL bilateral channels simultaneously. Germanium spot premia normalise toward Asian floor (~$2,000-2,500/kg Western warehouse, <30% premium vs China domestic). Defence-tier premium channel partially holds via NDAA Section 842 statute but DFARS implementing rule slips and DoD relies broadly on COTS exemption. Western substitution timeline becomes irrelevant (no premium to substitute). CRMA Strategic Project designations remain nominal. Specialty FY2026-2028 EBITA progressively compresses: FY2026 EUR 55-65m, FY2027 EUR 25-40m, FY2028 EUR 15-30m at 4-6% EBITA margin on revenue compressing to EUR 420-500m. ROCE drops to 2-4%. Hoboken complex-feed advantage on germanium-bearing residues becomes operational-only (no premium to capture on the recovered material). The contested tail (deposition, cobalt conversion) becomes immediate portfolio-pruning material; Specialty re-scoped to 'germanium franchise legacy with pricing-power gone' framing. Resolution-true is unreachable across all bands. The B4 'tail_grand_bargain_full_premium_collapse' scenario fires concurrently — B5 inherits the cross-segment failure mode.
scenarios.what_makes_this_path_break: Base-rate frequency of grand-bargain industrial-policy reversal within 18-30 months is structurally very low (T3 ~0.02 consensus, our read maintained at ~0.05 for B5 because Specialty's direct germanium/gallium exposure makes the grand bargain mechanically more impactful even if equally improbable); requires simultaneous political alignment in Beijing AND Washington that is contradicted by USTR overcapacity-investigation framing language and OBBBA codification of FEOC into statute (raising friction cost of legislative reversal materially).

named_watch_metrics.metric: MOFCOM germanium / gallium / antimony export-licence approval-rate trajectory (August 2023 regime)
named_watch_metrics.source: MOFCOM monthly bulletins (mofcom.gov.cn); USGS Mineral Commodity Summaries; CRU germanium / gallium price feeds; The Oregon Group critical-minerals tracker; Argus Metals germanium platform; Fastmarkets minor metals (germanium); ScrapMonster minor metals tracker
named_watch_metrics.current_value: August 2023 regime in force; specific licence-grant counts not publicly disclosed by MOFCOM; industry-tracking proxy: applications from Japan, South Korea, Netherlands, US buyers face longer processing times and lower approval rates than applications from Southeast Asia / Middle East buyers (the de-facto Western feedstock-scarcity mechanism). Sept 2024 antimony controls extended the regime. Distinct from suspended 2025 MOFCOM 55-62 package.
named_watch_metrics.threshold_implications: Bull (T3 escalation 'mofcom_snapback_post_busan' analogue for Aug 2023 regime): approval rate to Western buyers tightens further OR licence scope extends to tungsten / indium / niobium (+1 magnitude on B5 prior). Bear (T3 alternate scenario 'phase_2_carveout_framework' fires the -2 magnitude rule per T3 propagation_to_bets_seed entry to B5): voluntary suspension or material loosening as Phase-2 goodwill gesture in Busan-2 deal post 10 Nov 2026 expiry. Critical gate: cumulative Western-vs-China spot premium 4-quarter rolling average through FY2027 sustained >100% = B5 central path supportive; <50% sustained = B5 bear scenarios firing.

named_watch_metrics.metric: Western germanium spot-price premium vs China domestic (Argus / Fastmarkets / The Oregon Group tape)
named_watch_metrics.source: Argus Metals germanium prices platform; Fastmarkets minor metals germanium prices (cost per kg, market data); ScrapMonster minor metals germanium tracker; rare-earth-mining.com germanium price index; price-watch.ai germanium prices; Mordor Intelligence germanium market analysis; tradingeconomics.com germanium commodity index
named_watch_metrics.current_value: March 2026: Western in-warehouse germanium ~$5,475/kg (US) and ~$5,425/kg (Europe); China domestic ~$1,949.19/kg; Western premium ~181%. Analyst expectation per industry tracking: China domestic $1,800-2,100/kg through Q3 2026; Western warehouse >$5,000/kg absent regime relaxation.
named_watch_metrics.threshold_implications: Bull: sustained >150% Western premium for >2 quarters (regime tightening + Western substitution slow; B5 enforcement-teeth analogue widens). Bear: sustained <50% Western premium for >2 quarters (regime relaxation OR Western substitution caught up; B5 slow-decay or tail scenario firing). Critical gate: 4-quarter rolling average through FY2027 = leading indicator of which regulatory path is firing on the operating line. The 4-quarter tape is the price-discovery channel that reveals whether regime persistence is translating into franchise rent or whether Western substitution / regime relaxation has compressed the rent.

bets.breakage_shape: When B5 breaks, two structurally distinct break-shapes are most likely. PRIMARY break-shape — slow-decay over 18-24 months: through 2026 the August 2023 MOFCOM regime persists and 10 Nov 2026 Busan / MOFCOM-70 expiry resolves via extension status quo (no Phase-2 voluntary suspension); the regulatory underwriter holds. BUT through 2027 the Western substitution timeline accelerates at the fast end of Macro's 3-5 year horizon: 5N Plus Trail BC germanium operations + Saint-Augustin Quebec antimony processing reach material 2027 qualified output; Teledyne primary-route germanium qualification completes ahead of plan; AXT Asia operations re-route Western-qualified material at lower premium pricing; JX Advanced Metals Japan adds competing Specialty-grade output. The Western-vs-China spot premium tape compresses from 181% (March 2026) toward 80-100% by mid-2027, then toward 50-70% by H2 2027. Specialty FY2027 EBITA prints EUR 60-72m (down from FY2025 EUR 76m); FY2028 prints EUR 50-65m at 9-11% margin — segment continues operating, no impairment, no board-level kill-criteria event, but the franchise rent has decayed structurally and the bet's calibrated resolution-true margin profile (13-16% sustained) is unreachable. B5 resolves false at the floor; the technical operating result is intact but the bet's calibration to franchise-defence-with-asymmetric-defence-upside fails because the franchise rent itself has compressed before defence demand can backfill. SECONDARY break-shape — sharper compression over 6-9 months around the 10 Nov 2026 Busan / MOFCOM-70 expiry: the Phase-2 voluntary germanium suspension scenario (T3 0.08-0.10) fires; the regulatory underwriter is voluntarily withdrawn as a Chinese goodwill gesture in the Phase-2 framework. The T3 -> B5 propagation rule fires at full -2 magnitude. Western warehouse germanium pricing collapses from $5,400+ toward $2,500-3,000/kg within 6-12 months as Chinese export volumes recover and inventory cycles unwind at lower realised prices. Specialty FY2027 EBITA prints EUR 35-50m (compression bites in H1 2027); FY2028 EUR 30-50m at 7-9% margin. Defence-tier premium channel partially holds via NDAA Section 842 statute persistence but addressable market shrinks materially. Western substitution accelerates because price-economics no longer support premium qualification investment. CRMA GePETO + ReGAIN designations remain nominal but funding-disbursement urgency drops. The 'moat fragments / asymmetric Specialty collapse' scenario in B4 fires concurrently — the rare two-node simultaneous break that B4 explicitly maps via its B5 cross-segment TL bleed. Umicore's stated 'protect the germanium franchise; scale selectively elsewhere' decision proves correct in intent (the franchise is still operational, no impairment) but impotent in execution (the regulatory underwriter has been voluntarily withdrawn). Portfolio implications: the contested tail (deposition, cobalt specialty conversion sub-franchises) becomes immediate portfolio-pruning material; Specialty's 'contained optionality' framing converts to 'contained legacy' framing at the group level. TAIL break-shape — full collapse via grand bargain: a negotiated grand bargain (5% probability) restructures the entire critical-minerals stack with managed-trade quotas paired with Chinese unilateral relaxation across germanium / gallium / antimony AND partial Section 301 rollback on critical-minerals adjacencies. Germanium spot premia normalise toward Asian floor (~30% premium vs China domestic). Specialty FY2028 EBITA compresses to EUR 15-30m at 4-6% margin. Resolution-true unreachable across all bands. The B4 'tail_grand_bargain_full_premium_collapse' scenario fires concurrently — B5 inherits the cross-segment failure mode at full magnitude. In every break path, the bet's resolution-true definition is not satisfiable because the load-bearing channel (Western feedstock-scarcity premium captured in the germanium franchise) has either decayed structurally (slow-decay) or evaporated regulatorily (Phase-2 voluntary suspension or grand bargain) — even when the segment continues operating, the franchise rent that the bet is calibrated on does not survive in monetisable form to FY2028.

baseline_research_note.claim_under_test: Specialty Materials sustains FY2025 profile of EUR 558m revenue / EUR 76m EBITA / 13.6% margin / 11.1% ROCE through FY2028 AND captures a maintained or widening Western feedstock-scarcity premium driven by China's August 2023 MOFCOM germanium / gallium / antimony export-control regime + downstream defence/space-solar demand-pull (NDAA Section 842, DoD Section 842 phased dates 2028/2029/2031). The germanium franchise is the load-bearing component — if the MOFCOM regime is suspended or carved out, the Specialty premium compresses rapidly. Resolution requires both Layer 1 (regime persistence) AND Layer 2 (measurable monetisation via Western-vs-China spot premium tape) to clear, with optional asymmetric upside via DFARS Section 842 implementing rule pull-forward + DoD pilot procurement specifying FEOC-clean germanium / gallium inputs.
baseline_research_note.what_is_known_today: China August 2023 germanium / gallium licensing regime in force; September 2024 antimony controls extended the regime; both DISTINCT from the October 2025 MOFCOM 55-58 + 61-62 package which was suspended via MOFCOM/GAC Announcement 70/2025 through 10 Nov 2026 (Busan truce). The August 2023 regime was UNTOUCHED by Busan and remains fully operational. Public industry tracking (March 2026): Western in-warehouse germanium ~$5,475/kg vs China domestic ~$1,949/kg = 181% Western premium; in-warehouse Europe ~$5,425/kg. China has not published licence approval-rate data, but industry tracking indicates applications from Japan, South Korea, Netherlands, US buyers face longer processing times and lower approval rates than applications from Southeast Asia / Middle East buyers — the de facto Western feedstock-scarcity mechanism. Analyst expectation: China domestic germanium $1,800-2,100/kg through Q3 2026; Western warehouse >$5,000/kg absent regime relaxation. Umicore Specialty Materials FY2025: revenue EUR 558m (+4.0% YoY), revenue mix 15.7% of group, EBITA EUR 76m (13.6% margin, 13.1% mix of group EBITA, 11.1% ROCE) — confirmed via segment_analysis. Germanium franchise products: high-purity germanium substrates and wafers (CPV / space solar), GeCl4 (optical fibre preforms), germanium optics (thermal imaging / IR). Western-qualified peer set: 5N Plus (Canada, Trail BC + Saint-Augustin Quebec), Teledyne (US), AXT (US-listed China-based), JX Advanced Metals (Japan). Hoboken complex-feed feedstock advantage: germanium-bearing residues recycled into Specialty via the integrated Catalysis-Recycling-Specialty PGM/critical-minerals loop. CRMA Strategic Project status: Umicore-promoted GePETO (germanium processing) + ReGAIN (germanium substitution) on May 2024 first list of 47 designations; second-round assessment since 19 Jan 2026 but Umicore-specific second-round not confirmed. NDAA Section 842 enacted 23 Dec 2025: prohibits DoD procurement of advanced batteries from FEOCs with phased dates 1 Jan 2028 (new contracts), 1 Jan 2029 (standard), 1 Jan 2031 (legacy); 95% non-FEOC cost threshold for assembly exemption; DFARS implementing rule pending. USTR Section 301 overcapacity investigation: 16 economies, hearings 5-8 May 2026, remedy target 24 Jul 2026. Macro central-path verdict: 'protect the germanium franchise; scale selectively elsewhere' as Umicore's recommended action; segment is 'too small to offset Battery Materials drag at the group level. Worth defending and selectively investing in, but not a replacement for the Battery Materials decision.' Macro time-limited caveat: 'capturable but time-limited — Western substitution feasible 3-5 years once scrap-recovery and primary routes qualify ... treat as monetise-now niche, not permanent moat.' The contested tail (deposition, cobalt conversion) is a portfolio-pruning candidate per Macro.
baseline_research_note.contradictions_in_evidence: Several genuine cross-stream tensions exist on the franchise-rent durability question. (1) Macro stream simultaneously calls germanium franchise pricing power 'persistent' (Aug 2023 regime untouched by Busan) AND 'time-limited' (Western substitution 3-5 years) — these are not contradictory but the resolution-window mathematics is sensitive to which end of the 3-5 year range fires. The 2028 horizon catches the lower bound of substitution mathematically. (2) T3 (load-bearing) prior 0.66 with upward bias supports B5 strongly through the regulatory persistence channel; T4 (secondary) prior 0.42 with erosion trajectory provides only modest demand-pull support — but T4's erosion mechanism (Asian-localisation inside perimeter) is structurally inapplicable to Specialty. The two-input prior structure is therefore asymmetric in B5's favour (vs B4 where both T3 and T4 erosion vectors apply via different channels). (3) Specialty FY2025 11.1% ROCE is solid but materially below Catalysis (40.8% ROCE, EUR 383m EBITA) and Recycling (208% reported / mid-20s normalised, EUR 296m EBITA) — Specialty is positioned as 'contained optionality' not 'foundation business'. CORE plan March 2025 did NOT classify Specialty as a foundation business equal to Catalysis or Recycling, signalling the implicit active position is 'protect the germanium franchise; scale selectively elsewhere' rather than 'invest aggressively'. The contested tail (deposition, cobalt conversion) is a portfolio-pruning candidate. (4) Convergence stream notes Specialty is 'too small to offset Battery Materials drag at the group level. Worth defending and selectively investing in, but not a replacement for the Battery Materials decision.' This calibrates the bet's resolution-true definition appropriately — B5 does NOT need Specialty to scale to a foundation contributor; it needs the segment to defend its 13.6% margin profile and hold open the asymmetric defence-procurement upside. (5) Cross-segment TL bleed from B4 weight 0.4 to B5: B4 was authored before B5; B4's metadata.cross_segment_TL_bleed_to_other_bets explicitly declares 'B4 → B5 premium-channel weight 0.4 is the secondary cross-segment TL transmission for the Step 5 propagation rewire ... the rewire should ensure that B4-bear-on-Specialty-perimeter (the 'moat fragments / asymmetric' scenario, 6% probability) propagates to B5 directly, since this is the rare scenario where B4's TL erosion concentrates on the Specialty node rather than the BMS node — failing to wire this would leave B5 reading T4 only (not B4), under-counting the joint-asymmetric-failure mode.' This is a REVERSE-COUPLING that B5 must explicitly honour in metadata. The B4-asymmetric-Specialty-collapse scenario (6% probability) IS structurally a B5 break-path, regardless of whether B5 reads T3+T4 directly. (6) NDAA Section 842 / DFARS implementing rule slippage risk: statute is enacted but DFARS rule is pending; pull-forward vs slippage is observable but currently neutral velocity per T4 prediction-layer. Defence-tier premium materialisation by FY2028 is uncertain even with statute live. (7) CRMA Strategic Project advancement: GePETO + ReGAIN are on the first list but funding disbursement (EIB / EIF) timeline is phased and not confirmed at site/project level for Umicore. Designation is supportive but not by-itself ASP-converting.

They are NOT load-bearing but their failure would compress segment margin even with germanium franchise intact; segment-level disclosure on these sub-franchises is limited.

Position-map flags this as a transparency precondition; non-implementation is a governance signal.
