scenarios.name: central_path_wc_release_capex_on_plan_conversion_marginally_clears
scenarios.narrative: T5 base case 'regime_holds_supportive' (50% T5 prob) maps onto B7 with a modest WC release: PGM/gold cycles moderate gradually through 2026-2027 (gold softens to EUR 3,800/oz, Pt deficit narrows toward 150 koz by 2028, Russian containment intact) — cumulative WC release of EUR 100-200m across FY2026-FY2028 versus FY2025 EUR 261m build. Hoboken EUR 400m capex programme on schedule with EUR ~250m spent by H1 2027, payback path intact 2030-2031; FY2027 group capex prints in the EUR 425-450m band, FY2028 in the EUR 400-425m band — inside discipline envelope. EUR 100m efficiency program FY2025 base sustained AND extended by EUR 50-75m incremental cumulative through FY2027 via continuation pipeline (Catalysis automation + Recycling process improvements + Group-level procurement). BMS Q3 2027 proof-gate marginally clears (per B1 central-path scenario, 25% probability) — reduces forward BMS funding requirement and stops incremental WC build on under-realised volumes. Dividend held at EUR 0.50 (~EUR 120m/year). Net debt / EBITDA stays inside 1.5-1.7x band. FY2026 group FCF EUR 250-300m on EBITDA ~EUR 920m = 27-33% conversion; FY2027 FCF EUR 350-450m on EBITDA ~EUR 980m = 36-46%; FY2028 FCF EUR 420-520m on EBITDA ~EUR 1,000m = 42-52% — bet clears at the lower end of the 40-60% target band.
scenarios.what_makes_this_path_break: T5 'russian_pgm_normalisation_shock' (10% T5 prob) firing in 2027 — pulls toward 'foundation_compression_correlated_break' scenario via simultaneous WC unwind pulse + leaseback margin-call mechanics + foundation EBITDA compression. OR Hoboken EUR 400m capex over-run >15% with schedule slippage >9 months — pulls toward 'capex_overrun_wc_spike_concurrent_stress'. OR T2 escalation 'second_brent_or_ttf_shock' + 'carbon_market_stress' fire concurrently — Hoboken/Olen/Nysa EBITDA compresses by EUR 30-60m/year absorbing the residual conversion improvement.

scenarios.name: wc_release_overshoots_efficiency_extends_conversion_clears_strongly
scenarios.narrative: Upside-skew path: T5 'gold_correction_partial_compression' (20% T5 prob) materialises modestly — gold drifts to EUR 3,500/oz, Pt deficit narrows but stays positive, Russian containment intact — cumulative WC release of EUR 250-400m across FY2026-FY2028 (i.e. full unwind of FY2025 build plus moderate further release). EUR 100m efficiency program FY2025 base extends by EUR 100-150m cumulative through FY2027 (Catalysis automation + Recycling process + procurement consolidation deliver durably). Hoboken EUR 400m capex programme on schedule with no over-run. BMS Q3 2027 proof-gate clears comfortably (per B1 'regulatory_underwriting_premium_widens' upside scenario) — BMS WC drag releases as call-offs exceed 170 GWh 2027. Dividend held at EUR 0.50 with modest market-friendly increase to EUR 0.65 in FY2028 if cash-conversion runs ahead of plan. Net debt / EBITDA falls to 1.2-1.4x by FY2028 — leverage room re-opens. FY2027 FCF EUR 480-580m on EBITDA ~EUR 1,000m = 48-58%; FY2028 FCF EUR 550-650m on EBITDA ~EUR 1,050m = 52-62% — bet clears comfortably and the CORE pivot is fully validated in the cash flow statement.
scenarios.what_makes_this_path_break: Gold-correction is sharper than partial (T5 'gold_correction_to_2500_eur_oz' triggers above EUR 3,300/oz threshold with two consecutive months below) AND Russian normalisation concurrent — pulls toward 'foundation_compression_correlated_break' as the gold-tailwind unwind compresses Recycling EBITDA below EUR 200m FY2027 even while WC releases. OR efficiency program FY2026 contribution prints below EUR 30m incremental (Strategic A8 'durability beyond 2025 unproven' fires) — pulls toward 'slow_grind_sub_30pct_conversion'.

scenarios.name: slow_grind_sub_30pct_conversion_envelope_breach_dividend_held
scenarios.narrative: The most likely break-shape. T5 base case extends with a slower WC release (PGM/gold prices stay elevated through 2027 — Pt deficit confirmed 5th consecutive year, Russian containment tightens further on EU 21st sanctions package, gold holds EUR 3,800-4,100/oz); cumulative WC release of EUR 0-100m across FY2026-FY2028 — i.e. only a small fraction of the FY2025 EUR 261m build releases. EUR 300m Hoboken capex concentration 2027-2028 lands inside envelope but absorbs most of the foundation cash flow that would otherwise convert. EUR 100m efficiency program does not extend incrementally beyond FY2025 base (Strategic A8 durability concern realises) — FY2026 incremental contribution is +EUR 20-40m, FY2027 plateau, FY2028 give-back of EUR 10-20m as Belgian wage indexation + ETS auction creep above EUR 90 absorb gains. BMS Q3 2027 proof-gate clears marginally (per B1 central-path) but with WC drag continuing through 2027. T2 cost-floor escalation modestly: ETS hits EUR 90 sustained 2026-2027, Belgian indexation 2% step + cent-index reform compounds; Hoboken/Olen EBITDA compresses by EUR 20-40m/year. FY2026 FCF EUR 200-260m on EBITDA ~EUR 900m = 22-29% conversion; FY2027 FCF EUR 250-330m on EBITDA ~EUR 950m = 26-35%; FY2028 FCF EUR 290-380m on EBITDA ~EUR 980m = 30-39% — improving from trough but missing the 40-60% target band. Dividend held at EUR 0.50 by management discipline but with no further increase. Net debt / EBITDA stays at 1.6-1.8x. The franchise SURVIVES but the CORE-pivot promise of 40-60% cash conversion does NOT show up in the cash flow statement by FY2028.
scenarios.what_makes_this_path_break: T5 'russian_pgm_normalisation_shock' fires within window — pulls toward 'foundation_compression_correlated_break' via PGM-loop inventory mark-down + WC unwind pulse + leaseback margin-call mechanics. OR Hoboken EUR 400m programme on track AND efficiency program extends meaningfully — pulls back toward 'central_path'. OR PGM moderation overshoots the slow-grind glide via Q4 2026 gold spot break below EUR 3,500/oz — pulls toward 'wc_release_overshoots'.

scenarios.name: capex_overrun_wc_spike_concurrent_stress
scenarios.narrative: Downside concurrent stress. Hoboken EUR 400m programme over-runs by 15-25% (EUR 460-500m total spend), schedule slips by 9-12 months pushing payback to 2032-2033 — FY2027 capex prints EUR 480-520m, FY2028 prints EUR 450-500m. AND a PGM/gold rally extends WC consumption above EUR 250m/cycle envelope through 2026-2027 (T5 'regime_holds_supportive' extends + EU 21st sanctions package tightens Russian containment further) — incremental WC build of EUR 100-200m through FY2026-2027 vs FY2025 baseline. AND BMS Q3 2027 proof-gate is missed (per B1 'slow_burn_proof_gate_missed' 40% probability) forcing structured-partnership transaction costs in late 2027 (one-off cash drain EUR 100-200m). T2 'second_brent_or_ttf_shock' fires concurrently — Hoboken/Olen EBITDA compresses by EUR 40-60m/year. FY2026 FCF EUR 100-180m on EBITDA ~EUR 880m = 11-20% conversion (no improvement); FY2027 FCF EUR 50-150m on EBITDA ~EUR 920m = 5-16% (regression); FY2028 FCF EUR 150-280m on EBITDA ~EUR 950m = 16-29% (partial recovery as transaction costs lap). Dividend cut from EUR 0.50 back to EUR 0.25 or omitted in 2027-2028 — third cut signals balance-sheet stress; market reads CORE pivot as failed. Net debt / EBITDA breaches 1.8x and approaches 2.0x in FY2027 trough — second leverage breach forces credit-rating review.
scenarios.what_makes_this_path_break: Hoboken EUR 400m programme execution discipline holds (no over-run, schedule on plan) — independently pulls back toward central_path even with PGM rally extending. OR BMS Q3 2027 proof-gate clears AND PGM moderates concurrently — pulls toward central_path. OR board executes additional one-off cash lever (asset sale, JV monetisation, sub-portfolio carve-out beyond gold leaseback) — but per CFO non-negotiable 'one-time levers are exhausted', a successful additional one-off lever would be a meaningful surprise.

scenarios.name: foundation_compression_correlated_break_gold_cliff_b3_contagion
scenarios.narrative: Tail correlated-break. Gold corrects past USD 1,800-2,000/oz CFO-stress threshold within bet horizon (Fed pivot to sustained higher-for-longer real rates above 2.5%, EUR/USD strengthening past 1.25 amplifying EUR-denominated correction, coordinated central-bank gold sales). EUR 525m sale-and-leaseback faces margin call — board forced into reverse-leaseback transaction at unfavourable terms (one-off cash drain EUR 200-400m on unwind cost; lease-cost drag continues until full unwind). SIMULTANEOUS T5 'russian_pgm_normalisation_shock' (Ukraine ceasefire framework + OFAC sectoral relief firing concurrently with the gold correction trigger): Pt re-prices to USD 1,500/oz, Pd to USD 1,000/oz, loaded-catalyst inventory marks down across PGM loop, WC unwind partial offset but inventory mark-down compresses Recycling EBITDA below EUR 180m AND Catalysis margin compresses 200-400 bps via PGM revaluation. Combined foundation EBITDA drops below EUR 500m FY2027 — INSUFFICIENT to fund BMS patience capital through Q3 2027 proof-gate; B1 tail scenarios accelerate (forced structured-partnership in 2026-Q1 2027 pre-emptively). Dividend cut to EUR 0 (omission). Net debt / EBITDA breaches 2.0x, approaches 2.5x in FY2027 trough — second-grade credit downgrade triggers covenant negotiation. FY2027 FCF prints EUR -50 to -150m (negative on leaseback unwind cost + structured-partnership transaction costs); FY2028 partial recovery to EUR 100-250m as one-offs lap but the conversion-ratio is meaningless or strongly negative. The bet definitively resolves false — and resolves false in the worst form because B3, B7, and B1 break simultaneously.
scenarios.what_makes_this_path_break: Gold holds above USD 3,800/oz / EUR 3,300/oz — sale-and-leaseback margin-call mechanics do NOT fire. OR Russian containment holds (no Ukraine ceasefire framework, OFAC sectoral relief blocked) — PGM-loop inventory mark-down does NOT trigger. The path is conditional on simultaneous gold + Russian + Fed-policy stress; each individually has 10-25% probability but the joint occurrence inside an 18-30 month window is genuinely tail-shaped.

named_watch_metrics.metric: Group FCF / EBITDA conversion ratio (quarterly trailing-12-months and annual)
named_watch_metrics.source: Umicore quarterly trading updates and annual results; Convergence-prescribed segment-level FCF disclosure starting 24 April 2026 trading update; cross-checked against group operating cash flow ex-gold-leaseback per pos_balance_sheet_gold_leaseback adjustment
named_watch_metrics.current_value: FY2025 reported ~17% (FCF EUR 145m / EBITDA EUR 847m); underlying ex-gold-sale ~38% (operating cash EUR 324m / EBITDA EUR 847m using underlying numerator) but underlying FCF after capex and WC much weaker. FY2024 ~12% (FCF EUR 93m / EBITDA EUR 763m).
named_watch_metrics.threshold_implications: Bull: trailing-12M conversion >35% sustained for two consecutive quarters before Q3 2027; FY2026 full-year conversion >25%; FY2027 >35%; FY2028 in target 40-60% band. Bear: FY2026 conversion stays sub-25% with WC delta still positive (build continuing); OR FY2027 prints below 30% (path-to-target broken); OR any single quarter prints negative FCF on operational basis (excluding lapping effects of FY2024 Hoboken investment cycle). Critical gate: FY2028 conversion in 40-60% band = bet-truth resolution.

named_watch_metrics.metric: Working capital delta vs FY2024 base (segment-level if disclosed)
named_watch_metrics.source: Umicore quarterly cash flow statement and balance sheet; segment-level WC disclosure if implemented per Convergence-prescribed transparency
named_watch_metrics.current_value: FY2025 group WC build EUR 261m (FY2025 working_capital_m EUR 1,522m vs FY2024 EUR 1,261m per yfinance basis; AR-bridge methodology gap noted)
named_watch_metrics.threshold_implications: Bull: cumulative WC release of EUR 100-300m across FY2026-FY2028 versus FY2025 baseline (mean-reverting on PGM/gold price moderation). Bear: WC build of additional EUR 100-250m through FY2026-2027 if PGM/gold rally extends OR BMS volumes ramp ahead of customer cash-cycle. Critical gate: cumulative WC release rather than build over 2026-2028 period. Sub-bear: any single quarter WC build >EUR 100m in 2026-2027 = direct read-across to T5 escalation 'regime_holds_supportive_extends' or BMS volume drag.

named_watch_metrics.metric: Total group capex vs implicit envelope (FY2025 EUR 310m base; FY2027-2028 envelope ~EUR 425-475m)
named_watch_metrics.source: Umicore quarterly trading updates capex disclosure; capital expenditure tracking against Hoboken EUR 400m programme + BMS Nysa+Korea maintenance + Catalysis modernisation
named_watch_metrics.current_value: FY2025 EUR 310m (down 44% YoY from EUR 555m FY2024). Hoboken EUR 400m programme is concentrated EUR ~300m in 2027-2028.
named_watch_metrics.threshold_implications: Bull: FY2026 capex EUR 350-400m, FY2027 EUR 425-450m, FY2028 EUR 400-425m — discipline holds within envelope. Bear: any single year capex >EUR 475m through bet horizon (signals over-run on Hoboken OR incremental BMS capital injection beyond Nysa + Korea maintenance). Critical gate: cumulative FY2026-FY2028 capex below EUR 1,300m. Sub-bear: capex run-rate >EUR 110m/quarter for two consecutive quarters in 2026 before the Hoboken concentration kicks in = early signal of envelope breach.

named_watch_metrics.metric: Efficiency program incremental contribution (cumulative beyond FY2025 EUR 100m base) and EBITDA-to-cash flow-through
named_watch_metrics.source: Umicore quarterly trading updates efficiency commentary; H1/H2 progress reports against original EUR 70m FY2025 target now extended; Strategic A8 verdict tracking
named_watch_metrics.current_value: FY2025 EUR 100m EBITDA contribution achieved (ahead of EUR 70m target); H1 2025 progress ~EUR 50m. FY2026 incremental target not separately disclosed.
named_watch_metrics.threshold_implications: Bull: FY2026 incremental contribution EUR 30-50m on top of FY2025 base, sustained run-rate; FY2027 cumulative EUR 150-200m beyond FY2025 base. Bear: FY2026 contribution flat-to-down vs FY2025 base (Strategic A8 'durability beyond 2025 unproven' fires) — efficiency gains absorbed by Belgian wage indexation + Polish wage convergence + ETS auction creep. Critical gate: EBITDA-to-FCF flow-through ratio above 80% on the incremental EUR 50-100m efficiency contribution (i.e. savings convert to cash, not absorbed by WC or maintenance capex deferral).

named_watch_metrics.metric: Dividend per share defence at EUR 0.50 partial-restoration level
named_watch_metrics.source: Umicore annual general meeting dividend declarations; quarterly trading updates on capital allocation policy
named_watch_metrics.current_value: FY2025 dividend EUR 0.25 per share; proposed partial restoration to EUR 0.50 gross annual dividend for FY2025 (paid in 2026); pre-crisis EUR 0.80
named_watch_metrics.threshold_implications: Bull: dividend held at EUR 0.50 through FY2027-2028 with no further cuts (conservation discipline holds); modest restoration toward EUR 0.65 in FY2028 if cash conversion runs ahead of plan signals strong cash-flow improvement. Bear: third dividend cut below EUR 0.50 within bet horizon (signals balance-sheet stress, breaks CORE-pivot credibility narrative); dividend omission in any single year = severe bear (Strategic A4 / pos_balance_sheet_dividend_signal capital-preservation discipline fully broken). Critical gate: dividend EUR 0.50 sustained through FY2028 with FY2028 FCF coverage >2.0x (FCF >EUR 240m vs ~EUR 120m dividend cash outflow at 240m shares).

named_watch_metrics.metric: Net debt / EBITDA leverage ratio post-gold-leaseback adjustment
named_watch_metrics.source: Umicore quarterly net debt disclosure + trailing-12M EBITDA; Convergence CFO non-negotiable 'one-time levers are exhausted' commitment tracking
named_watch_metrics.current_value: FY2025 1.6x (net debt EUR 1,357m / FY2025 EBITDA EUR 847m); FY2024 1.87x; gearing ratio 37.4%; cash EUR 1,559m. Convergence stress-test reference: gold correction to USD 1,800-2,000/oz
named_watch_metrics.threshold_implications: Bull: leverage stays inside 1.4-1.7x band through bet horizon (no second leverage breach); falls below 1.5x in FY2028 on cash-conversion improvement. Bear: leverage breaches 1.8x in any single quarter of 2026-2027 (signals operational cash-conversion failure absent further one-off levers); breach of 2.0x = covenant negotiation territory (most facilities at major European industrials cluster around 2.0-2.5x covenant); approach toward 2.5x = credit-rating downgrade trigger. Critical gate: FY2028 net debt / EBITDA below 1.7x with cash-conversion improvement underwriting the leverage floor (not balance-sheet engineering).

named_watch_metrics.metric: Hoboken EUR 400m capex programme cumulative spend and schedule adherence
named_watch_metrics.source: Umicore Recycling segment disclosure on Hoboken capex run-rate; mid-cycle progress reports against EUR ~300m 2027-2028 concentration plan and 2030-2031 payback expectation
named_watch_metrics.current_value: EUR 400m envelope committed 2025-2028; concentration EUR ~300m in 2027-2028; expected 2030-2031 payback (6-year)
named_watch_metrics.threshold_implications: Bull: cumulative spend tracking ~EUR 200-250m by H1 2027 (on schedule); first throughput uplift evidence on commissioned capacity by H2 2027; payback path to 2030-2031 intact. Bear: cost over-run >15% (cumulative spend >EUR 460m by FY2028 vs EUR 400m plan); schedule slippage >9 months (payback into 2032-2033) directly compresses FY2027-2028 cash conversion AND re-rates B3 normalised ROCE recovery path. Critical gate: schedule adherence in 2027 H1 + cumulative spend within EUR 460m by FY2028 even if 15% over-run materialises (i.e. soft-bear absorbed without hard-break).

expected_projection_topics: Group FCF / EBITDA conversion trajectory through FY2028 (post Q1 2026 disclosure implementation, with explicit gold sale-and-leaseback adjustment)
Working capital delta normalisation as PGM/gold cycles moderate vs T5 escalation paths (rally extends, Russian normalisation shock, gold correction)
Capex envelope discipline through Hoboken EUR 400m concentration (FY2027-2028) alongside BMS Nysa+Korea maintenance + Catalysis modernisation
Efficiency program FY2026-2027 incremental contribution and EBITDA-to-cash flow-through (Strategic A8 durability test)
Dividend defence at EUR 0.50 partial-restoration level vs further cut / restoration scenarios under cash-conversion stress
Net debt / EBITDA leverage trajectory post-gold-leaseback (CFO non-negotiable 'one-time levers are exhausted')
BMS Q3 2027 proof-gate interaction with cash-allocation decision (clearing reduces forward funding requirement; missing triggers structured-partnership transaction costs)
falsification_criteria: FY2028 group FCF / EBITDA conversion prints below 35% (decisively missing the 40-60% target band) — the bet's load-bearing readable signal failing.

Cumulative FY2026-FY2028 working capital delta is positive (further build) rather than negative (release), indicating the metal-management capability stress-test (pos_capability_metal_management_hedging Strategic A7 'AT RISK') has structurally failed.

Total group capex breaches EUR 475m in any single year through bet horizon, signalling Hoboken EUR 400m over-run OR incremental BMS capital injection beyond Nysa + Korea maintenance — capital discipline broken.

Hoboken EUR 400m programme over-runs by >15% (cumulative spend >EUR 460m) AND schedule slippage >9 months pushing payback into 2032-2033 — execution risk materialising in the same window as PGM/gold WC volatility.
Dividend cut below EUR 0.50 partial-restoration level (third cut) OR dividend omission in any single year through FY2028 — capital-preservation discipline failed; CORE-pivot credibility broken.
Net debt / EBITDA breaches 1.8x in any single quarter of FY2026-2027 OR approaches 2.0x in FY2027-2028 — second leverage breach forces credit-rating review and covenant-negotiation territory.

EUR 525m gold sale-and-leaseback structure is unwound prematurely (margin call OR voluntary unwind under stress) before resolution date AT a net cash-out cost above EUR 100m — the load-bearing one-off lever fails on the structural-fragility flagged in pos_balance_sheet_gold_leaseback.

Foundation cushion (combined Catalysis + Recycling Adjusted EBIT) drops below EUR 500m for FY2027 — insufficient to fund BMS patience capital through Q3 2027 proof-gate AND mechanically compresses the cash-conversion numerator below the path-to-40%.

Q1 2026 segment-level FCF disclosure (24 April 2026 trading update) is NOT implemented OR is implemented but does NOT cleanly strip the EUR 525m gold sale from underlying cash — governance failure mode that prevents the market from independently auditing the CORE pivot, which itself functionally falsifies the bet's transparency precondition.
bets.breakage_shape: When B7 breaks, it does NOT typically break sharply on a single date. The most likely break-shape is a 24-30 month slow grind through 2026-2028: FY2026 prints group FCF in EUR 200-260m range on EBITDA ~EUR 900m (conversion 22-29%, NO meaningful improvement from FY2025 trough once gold-leaseback comparator drops out); WC fails to release at central-path pace (PGM/gold prices stay elevated, Pt deficit confirmed 5th year); FY2027 capex prints near or above EUR 450m as Hoboken concentration absorbs cash; efficiency program does not extend incrementally beyond FY2025 base; FY2027 cash conversion improves only to 26-35% (still below the 40% threshold); BMS Q3 2027 proof-gate clears marginally but with WC drag continuing; FY2028 prints conversion 30-39% — improving but missing the target band, dividend held at EUR 0.50 by management discipline but with no further increase, the CORE-pivot promise of 40-60% does NOT show up in the cash flow statement. The franchise SURVIVES at this break-shape; what breaks is the credibility of CORE as a 'cash generation pivot' rather than a holding pattern. The sharper alternative break-shape compresses this to 6-12 months around a single inflection event — a Hoboken capex over-run announced in 2027 OR a PGM rally + Russian sanctions tightening combination that pushes WC build past EUR 350m in 2026, OR a gold correction below EUR 3,300/oz triggering leaseback margin-call mechanics. The tail-correlated break-shape (foundation_compression_correlated_break) is the genuinely worst form: gold cliff + Russian normalisation + Fed real-rates pivot fire concurrently within 18-30 months, FY2027 FCF prints negative on leaseback unwind + structured-partnership transaction costs, B3 + B7 + B1 break simultaneously and the foundation cushion that underwrites the entire CORE pivot collapses inside one fiscal year. Either path culminates in a forced re-baselining of the CORE strategy in 2027-2028 rather than a clean 40-60% conversion print — likely accompanied by a third dividend cut, leverage covenant negotiation, and either a sub-portfolio sale or accelerated BMS structural action.

baseline_research_note.claim_under_test: Group cash conversion (FCF / EBITDA) recovers from FY2025 sub-20% trough (EUR 145m FCF on EUR 847m EBITDA = ~17%, with underlying ex-gold-leaseback operating cash EUR 324m vs EUR 869m FY2024 = -63% YoY) toward a 40-60% range by FY2028, driven by (i) WC normalisation as PGM/gold cycles moderate (versus FY2025 EUR 261m WC build), (ii) capex envelope discipline holding inside ~EUR 425-475m FY2027-2028 even with EUR 300m Hoboken concentration, (iii) Hoboken EUR 400m programme on plan with no over-run, (iv) dividend defence at EUR 0.50/share level (conservation, not generosity), (v) efficiency program EUR 100m FY2025 base extended with incremental EUR 50-100m cumulative through FY2027 — all while underwriting BMS through Q3 2027 proof-gate without breaching the implicit 1.5-1.8x leverage band.
baseline_research_note.what_is_known_today: FY2025 reported group EBITDA EUR 847m, EBITA EUR 579m, EBITA margin 16.5% (down from 25.1% peak FY2021 and recovered from the FY2024 distortion). Reported FCF EUR 145m on capex EUR 310m (down 44% YoY from EUR 555m FY2024 reflecting CORE plan capital discipline). Underlying operating cash flow ex-gold sale-and-leaseback ~EUR 324m vs EUR 869m prior year — the reported EUR 849m operating cash line includes EUR 525m one-time gold inventory sale proceeds. WC up EUR 261m FY2025 (visible failure mode of the metal-management capability per pos_capability_metal_management_hedging Strategic A7 verdict 'AT RISK with net signal score -2.25'). Net debt EUR 1,357m (down from EUR 1,425m FY2024) at 1.6x leverage (down from 1.87x) — improvement underwritten by the EUR 525m gold sale; without it leverage would likely have deteriorated. Dividend halved EUR 0.80 -> EUR 0.25 with proposed partial restoration to EUR 0.50 (~EUR 120m/year cash outflow at 240m shares). Foundation cushion: Catalysis EUR 383m + Recycling EUR 296m = EUR 679m FY2025 Adjusted EBIT plus Specialty EUR 76m = EUR 755m; BMS drag EUR -91m + Corporate EUR -84m. CORE plan March 2025 narrowed BMS capex to Nysa + Korea, deferred 150 kt European battery recycling plant to 2032+, halved dividend, delivered EUR 100m FY2025 efficiency ahead of EUR 70m target. Hoboken EUR 400m 2025-2028 capex programme committed with EUR ~300m concentrated in 2027-2028 and 6-year payback into 2030-2031. FY2026 brings scheduled Hoboken maintenance shutdown (one-off EBITDA hit EUR ~30-60m). Convergence Share Price Analyst non-negotiable: quarterly segment-level FCF disclosure starting 24 April 2026 trading update (already crossed at as_of). Convergence CFO non-negotiable: 'one-time levers are exhausted'. Trajectory stream year-by-year status-quo path implies group EBITDA in the EUR 950-1,050m band through FY2026-2028 (Catalysis 376/363/347 + Recycling normalising 270-300 + BMS recovering -90 -> -30 to +30 + Specialty ~80) — i.e. for 40-60% conversion the FCF trajectory needs to land in EUR 380-630m by FY2028 vs FY2025 EUR 145m base.
baseline_research_note.contradictions_in_evidence: Several genuine cross-stream tensions exist. (1) Reported vs underlying cash-conversion: financial stream prints reported FCF EUR 145m; convergence earnings-quality verdict 'mixed' precisely on the gold leaseback masking weaker underlying cash; macro stream confirms 'underlying operating cash flow was roughly EUR 324m — down 63% from EUR 869m in the prior year'. The bet must be calibrated on underlying not reported — which means resolution is consistent with the reported FY2025 EUR 145m being an unrepresentative trough that recovers as the gold-leaseback masking unwinds. (2) WC build is a one-off PGM cycle vs structural failure: pos_capability_metal_management_hedging Strategic A7 verdict 'AT RISK' frames FY2025 WC build EUR 261m as visible failure mode; T5 propagation rule frames it as cyclical pricing-driven WC build at upper envelope, releasable on PGM/gold price moderation. The first framing implies structural break (negative for B7); the second implies mean-reverting cycle (positive for B7). The two-sidedness is the load-bearing variable — if PGM/gold moderate without Russian normalisation shock, B7 clears; if PGM/gold rally extends, B7 fails. (3) Capex envelope discipline vs Hoboken concentration: CORE plan capex envelope cut to EUR 310m FY2025 is observable discipline; but the EUR 300m Hoboken concentration in 2027-2028 mechanically pushes group capex back up even with BMS held flat. The discipline is real for FY2025-2026 but the FY2027-2028 envelope is the binding test, not the FY2025 print. (4) Dividend signal: pos_balance_sheet_dividend_signal partial restoration to EUR 0.50 is read by some as 'capital-preservation discipline weakening' (negative governance signal) and by others as 'modest restoration consistent with cash-flow improvement' (positive cash-flow signal). The Share Price Analyst notes 'the partial restoration to EUR 0.50 leaves the dividend well below pre-crisis (EUR 0.80) — the market reads it as balance-sheet repair takes precedence over returns'. (5) Efficiency program durability: Strategic A8 verdict 'MIXED. Real delivery in 2025 but durability beyond 2025 unproven; weak cash conversion suggests EBITDA gains not yet flowing through to FCF'. The FY2025 EUR 100m EBITDA contribution did NOT translate into proportional FCF — meaning the conversion-ratio mechanic is broken on the efficiency-side. (6) BMS Q3 2027 proof-gate interaction: positive proof-gate clearing reduces B7 funding requirement; missed proof-gate triggers structured-partnership transaction costs. Either resolution is informative for B7 but in different directions, and the timing (Q3 2027) gives only one quarter's read before FY2027 results print.
open_questions: What is Hoboken's specific share of the FY2025 EUR 261m group WC build vs BMS share vs Catalysis share? Without segment-level WC waterfall the PGM-cycle vs BMS-volume-build attribution is ambiguous and the FY2026-2027 WC release/build forecast is not bottoms-up estimable.

Has Q1 2026 trading update on 24 April 2026 actually delivered the Convergence-prescribed segment-level FCF disclosure, and does it cleanly strip the EUR 525m gold sale from underlying cash? This is the readable signal between as_of (2026-05-04) and FY2026 results.

What is the actual margin-call trigger price embedded in the EUR 525m gold sale-and-leaseback contract? CFO stress-test references USD 1,800-2,000/oz but the contract-specific trigger is not disclosed; resolving it would re-rate the tail-scenario probability materially.

Is the Hoboken EUR 400m programme phased to deliver throughput uplift before the EUR 300m 2027-2028 cash absorption peaks, or does the cash-out-then-revenue-in lag mean FY2027-2028 will be the worst conversion years even on plan?

What is the FY2026-2027 incremental BMS funding requirement under Macro's central case (call-off slippage 6-18 months)? The implicit BMS capital ceiling at EUR 2.0bn is cumulative; an additional EUR 100-300m WC build before Q3 2027 mechanically compresses cash conversion in the same window.

Is the EUR 0.50 partial-restoration dividend sustainable through FY2027 even on the slow-grind path, or does the FY2027 cash-conversion stress force a third cut? The signalling cost of a third cut is severe given EUR 0.80 -> EUR 0.25 -> EUR 0.50 trajectory already telegraphs balance-sheet repair priority.

Has the board reviewed reverse-leaseback / unwind options for the EUR 525m gold structure under stress scenarios? CFO non-negotiable 'one-time levers are exhausted' + pos_balance_sheet_gold_leaseback ongoing lease costs on recirculating inventory reducing future EBITDA collectively imply NO graceful exit at current spot — the structure is contingent on the regime continuing.

Does the FY2025 EUR 100m efficiency program have a documented FY2026-2027 follow-on initiative pipeline, or was FY2025 the peak run-rate year? Strategic A8 'durability beyond 2025 unproven' is the explicit transparency gap.

factual_corrections: Sub-20% FCF conversion FY2025 is the starting point; CORE 'cash generation pivot' is NOT yet visible in reported numbers (EUR 145m FCF / EUR 847m EBITDA = ~17%, pos_balance_sheet_gold_leaseback masks underlying weakness).

EUR 525m gold sale-and-leaseback is a one-off cash event, not recurring conversion (pos_balance_sheet_gold_leaseback). Underlying operating cash flow ex-gold ~EUR 324m vs EUR 869m prior year (-63% YoY).
FY2025 WC build EUR 261m is the visible failure mode of the metal-management/hedging capability (pos_capability_metal_management_hedging Strategic A7 'AT RISK with net signal score -2.25').

Hoboken EUR 400m capex 2025-2028 programme is the single largest forward capex commitment, with EUR ~300m concentrated 2027-2028 and 6-year payback into 2030-2031 (pos_strategic_commitment_hoboken_400m_capex). Note: this is for the existing Cu/PGM circuit, NOT for battery recycling (150 kt European battery recycling plant deferred to 2032+).

Convergence CFO non-negotiable: 'one-time levers are exhausted' — next leverage / cash-conversion improvement must come from operational discipline not balance-sheet engineering. Stress-test reference: gold correction to USD 1,800-2,000/oz.

Belgian (Hoboken, Olen) and Polish (Nysa) sites sit OUTSIDE German Industriestrompreis EUR 50/MWh relief approved 16 April 2026 — asymmetric national disadvantage compounds capex execution stress in 2027-2028.

Quarterly segment-level FCF disclosure was Convergence-prescribed for 24 April 2026 Q1 trading update (pos_governance_segment_fcf_disclosure_commitment); already crossed at as_of 2026-05-04. Implementation status itself is a leading governance signal.

Efficiency program EUR 100m FY2025 contribution achieved ahead of EUR 70m target (Strategic A8 verdict 'MIXED. Real delivery in 2025 but durability beyond 2025 unproven; weak cash conversion suggests EBITDA gains not yet flowing through to FCF') — the FY2025 EBITDA contribution did NOT translate proportionally to FCF, which is a direct conversion-ratio failure mode.

bet_to_bet_dependencies.direction: B1 -> B7 (BMS funding requirement compresses cash conversion); B7 -> B1 (cash-conversion failure constrains BMS patience capital)
bet_to_bet_dependencies.coupling_mechanism: B1 BMS capital-employed ceiling (~EUR 2.0bn, pos_strategic_commitment_bms_capital_ceiling) is the single largest direct draw on group cash. BMS losses (FY2025 EUR -91m EBIT) plus BMS WC build (under-realised contracted volumes accumulating inventory) mechanically compress B7 cash conversion. Conversely, if B7 fails (cash conversion stays sub-25% through FY2027), the foundation cushion's ability to fund BMS patience capital through Q3 2027 proof-gate is compressed — B1 tail scenarios (premature kill-criteria firing) accelerate. The BMS proof-gate Q3 2027 outcome is a load-bearing intermediate gate for B7 because clearing reduces forward BMS funding requirement and stops incremental WC build on under-realised volumes; missing triggers structured-partnership transaction costs that themselves are cash-flow distortive in late 2027 / early 2028.
bet_to_bet_dependencies.joint_probability_note: Central-path scenarios are positively correlated: if B1 central-path clears (proof-gate marginal clear) at 25% B1 probability, B7 central-path conditional probability rises modestly (joint ~0.10-0.15). Break paths are also correlated: B1 'slow_burn_proof_gate_missed' (40% B1 prob) intersects with B7 'slow_grind_sub_30pct' AND 'capex_overrun_wc_spike' scenarios because BMS WC build + structured-partnership transaction costs compound cash-conversion stress.

bet_to_bet_dependencies.bet_label: Recycling franchise (Hoboken complex-feed PGM refining) durability + monetisation through FY2028
bet_to_bet_dependencies.direction: B3 -> B7 (foundation EBITDA supplies conversion-ratio numerator; gold leaseback margin-call triggers correlated cash drain); B7 -> B3 (capex envelope discipline determines Hoboken EUR 400m payback path)
bet_to_bet_dependencies.coupling_mechanism: B3 directly supplies the Recycling EBITDA component of the foundation cushion that funds the conversion-ratio numerator. The EUR 525m gold sale-and-leaseback structure (pos_balance_sheet_gold_leaseback) is the single largest contingent cash-drain risk for B7 — margin call at gold below USD 1,800-2,000/oz CFO-stress threshold would trigger reverse-leaseback transaction at unfavourable terms (one-off cash drain EUR 200-400m) AT THE SAME TIME as Recycling EBITDA compresses on gold-tailwind reversal. T5 'russian_pgm_normalisation_shock' breaks BOTH B3 (PGM-loop inventory mark-down + WC unwind pulse) AND B7 (correlated cash drain in single window) simultaneously. Conversely, B7 cash-conversion discipline determines whether Hoboken EUR 400m programme can execute on schedule (EUR ~300m 2027-2028 concentration); if B7 fails the capex envelope discipline (>EUR 475m single year), B3 payback path slips from 2030-2031 to 2032-2033 and B3 normalised ROCE recovery breaks.
bet_to_bet_dependencies.joint_probability_note: Gold-cliff tail is the single most correlated joint break: B3 'gold_cliff_margin_call_b7_contagion' (low probability tail) is the same event as B7 'foundation_compression_correlated_break_gold_cliff_b3_contagion' (10% B7 prob). The labels are different but the underlying state is one event. WC dynamics under T5 propagation are direct B3-B7 channel: PGM rally extending = WC build (negative both bets); PGM moderation = WC release (positive both bets).

bet_to_bet_dependencies.bet_label: Optionality cushion preservation (foundation cushion underwriting BMS patience capital)
bet_to_bet_dependencies.direction: B6 -> B7 (optionality cushion preservation correlates with cash-conversion discipline); B7 -> B6 (WC release improves optionality cushion)
bet_to_bet_dependencies.coupling_mechanism: B6 (per brief framing as 'whether the optionality holds') and B7 (per brief framing as 'did CORE actually deliver?') are conceptually distinct but mechanically coupled. B6 tracks whether the foundation cushion + balance-sheet capacity to fund BMS patience capital is preserved through bet horizon; B7 tracks whether the cash conversion that REPLENISHES that cushion shows up in the cash flow statement. If B7 fails (cash conversion stays sub-25% through FY2027), the cushion's replenishment rate is below its draw rate (BMS losses + Hoboken capex + dividend) and B6 tail scenarios accelerate. Conversely, if B7 clears via WC release in 2026-2027, B6 optionality cushion strengthens because the released cash directly reduces leverage AND adds to the foundation cushion's balance-sheet capacity. The brief explicitly notes 'B7 affects B6 (WC release improves optionality)' — the reverse is also true via dividend / capex re-allocation flexibility.
bet_to_bet_dependencies.joint_probability_note: Central-path scenarios for B6 and B7 are strongly positively correlated (both rely on PGM moderation + capex discipline + efficiency continuation). Break paths share the same correlated stress triggers (gold cliff + PGM rally + capex over-run). Treat B6-B7 as nearly co-resolving on the central path; divergence only emerges in tail scenarios where B6 cushion compresses faster than B7 cash conversion (e.g. foundation EBITDA hit before WC normalises) or vice versa (e.g. WC release happens but BMS losses widen disproportionately, draining the cushion faster than B7 replenishes it).

position_map.summary: Umicore is functionally two companies sharing one balance sheet: a foundation of cash-compounding businesses (Catalysis at 40.8% ROCE, Recycling at distorted 208% ROCE / normalised high-teens to mid-twenties, Specialty Materials at 11.1% ROCE) generating roughly EUR 755m of FY2025 Adjusted EBIT, and a Battery Materials Solutions division consuming 56% of group capital employed (EUR 2.0bn) at -4.8% ROCE. The active strategic position taken in March 2025 (CORE plan) is one of capital preservation, not growth: capex was cut to EUR 310m, the dividend halved from EUR 0.80 to EUR 0.25, the 150 kt European battery recycling plant deferred from 2026 to 2032-at-earliest, and the residual BMS capex narrowed to Nysa (Poland) and Korea. The most load-bearing decisions Umicore actively chose are: (i) committing EUR ~2bn of capital to a nickel-rich NMC cathode footprint (now stranded by LFP migration), (ii) committing EUR 400m through 2028 to defend the Hoboken complex-feed PGM smelter-refinery, (iii) executing the EUR 525m gold-inventory sale-and-leaseback to bridge near-term cash, and (iv) anchoring the BMS book around the IONWAY/PowerCo JV (Salzgitter), AESC North America (Loyalist, Ontario), ACC, and SK On.
factual_corrections_flagged: Multi-chemistry portfolio claim is unsupported. Macro Analyse segment_views/cathode_and_precursor_materials.written_analysis.key_takeaways explicitly states: 'Umicore's chemistry exposure is nickel-rich NMC, manganese-rich variants, and solid-state development work — the evidence shows no material disclosed LFP/LMFP position.' The EUR ~2bn BMS capital base is concentrated in nickel-rich NMC cathodes (NMC 622, 811 and Ni-rich variants) plus pCAM. Umicore is a single-chemistry-family bet, not a chemistry-diversified one.

The 'integrated CAM + Recycling closed-loop' moat is overstated for batteries. Umicore itself deferred the large 150 kt European battery recycling plant from 2026 to 2032-at-earliest (Macro Analyse, segment_views/cathode_and_precursor_materials), so end-of-life battery loop economics are explicitly NOT bankable this decade. Closed-loop today is a commercial enabler (qualification, traceability) rather than a profit pool.

The 'localised Western cost curve' claim is strategically necessary but economically unverified. Macro Analyse states APAC capacity is still 'doing economic work that the localization thesis said Europe/North America would do' and Umicore still depends on CNGR and Eco&Dream for precursor (pCAM) supply, so the Nysa/Loyalist localisation story is partial, not standalone-competitive.

Recycling's 208% reported ROCE is artefactual and should not be cited as durable. The EUR 525m gold-inventory sale-and-leaseback collapsed Recycling year-end capital employed to -EUR 37m; Macro Analyse and earnings_quality both estimate normalised ROCE at high-teens to mid-twenties.

The CORE 'cash generation pivot' is not yet visible in cash. FY2025 underlying free cash flow was only EUR 145m on EUR 847m EBITDA (sub-20% conversion), working capital increased EUR 261m, and reported operating cash flow includes the EUR 525m one-time gold sale; strip it out and underlying OCF fell from EUR 869m to EUR ~324m.

Hyundai Changshu PEM, Loyang Singapore, and Cheonan Korea are NOT named in any of the four input streams. The disclosed Korean BMS base is referenced generically as 'Korea' (existing nickel-rich CAM base). The North America BMS site is named Loyalist, Ontario (Canada), not a US site. Position-components below cite only the named sites in the streams.

components.label: Catalysis segment — autocatalyst cash engine
components.the_decision_umicore_made: Umicore actively chose to position Catalysis as a 'foundation business' under the March 2025 CORE plan — managed for maximum cash extraction with low incremental capex on a depreciated brownfield footprint, NOT reinvested for growth. Combined with peers (JM, BASF) in cash-harvest mode, the active stance is to preserve oligopoly share in gasoline TWC + GPF and heavy-duty SCR while monetising Euro 7 / EPA 2027 content uplift, then redeploy proceeds to higher-return uses elsewhere in the group.

citations.snippet: Catalysis: revenue 1668, revenue_yoy 0.1, revenue_mix_pct 46.8, ebita 383, ebita_margin 23.0, ebita_mix_pct 66.1
Catalysis remains the earnings anchor: FY2025 Adjusted EBIT of EUR 383 million on revenues of EUR 1,668 million (23.0% margin), with ROCE of 40.8%.
Harvest aggressively - monetize the cash annuity while it lasts and redirect proceeds to higher-return uses elsewhere in the group
Catalysis delivered EUR 383 million EBIT at 23% margin and 40.8% ROCE on essentially flat revenues. It is the company's earnings anchor and primary cash generator.

what_it_exposes_to: ICE light-vehicle production secular decline (2-4% per year through 2030)
EU 2035 ICE phase-out / regulatory framework
US hybrid market share trajectory (currently 19.7% Q4 2025)
Euro 7 and EPA 2027 emissions standards content uplift
PGM (palladium, platinum, rhodium) price volatility and pass-through
Three-way oligopoly competitive stress (JM dismemberment, BASF ECMS carve-out)
components.notes: The 40.8% ROCE is a wasting asset by management's own framing — Catalysis runway is durable into early 2030s but has a defined endpoint. Convergence debate Round 2 explicitly framed Catalysis as 'a runway, not an ice cube' but also as a non-renewable resource that must fund the BMS bet.

components.label: Recycling segment — Hoboken-anchored PGM refining
components.the_decision_umicore_made: Umicore actively chose to position Recycling as the second 'foundation business' under CORE — to be defended (via the EUR 400m Hoboken capex programme) and selectively scaled (EUR 400m by 2030 with ~EUR 300m in 2027-2028, ~6-year payback). The active capital decision was NOT to expand into battery recycling in the near term: the planned 150 kt European battery recycling plant was deferred from 2026 to 2032-at-earliest.

citations.snippet: Recycling: revenue 947, revenue_yoy 4.4, revenue_mix_pct 26.6, ebita 296, ebita_margin 31.2, ebita_mix_pct 51.1

Recycling delivered Adjusted EBIT of EUR 296 million on revenues of EUR 947 million (31.2% margin), with an extraordinary ROCE of 208% due to very low capital employed (EUR -37 million at year-end, reflecting the gold inventory sale).

Hoboken precious-metals refining — Umicore's highest-quality franchise by ROCE. EUR 296M Adjusted EBIT on revenues of EUR 947M (31.2% margin) in FY2025. Reported 208% ROCE is distorted by a one-time EUR 525M gold-inventory sale that reduced year-end capital employed to -EUR 37M; normalized ROCE is in the high-teens to mid-twenties.
The 208% ROCE is distorted by the gold inventory sale, which reduced Recycling's capital employed to -EUR 37 million at year-end.

what_it_exposes_to: Gold price (currently >EUR 4,500/oz; correction to EUR 3,000/oz could remove EUR 100-150m EBITDA)
Platinum structural deficit (3rd consecutive year per Johnson Matthey)
PGM and precious metals price cycle generally
PGM scrap feedstock competition from Glencore, Trafigura, Aurubis, Heraeus
Hoboken social licence to operate (lead-standard 2 μg/dl dispute flagged in Macro)
Hoboken 2026 scheduled smelter maintenance shutdown
EU CRMA secondary supply tightening within Europe
components.notes: ROCE optics will mechanically normalise as the EUR 400m capex cycle rebuilds the capital base. The gold sale-and-leaseback brings ongoing lease costs that drag future EBITDA. Recycling's contribution is the single largest enabler of the CORE strategy's viability; if gold corrects, the cash cushion that makes BMS patience affordable evaporates.

components.label: Battery Materials Solutions — loss-making nickel-rich NMC bet

concrete_facts.chemistry_focus: nickel-rich NMC (622, 811, Ni-rich variants), manganese-rich, solid-state development

components.the_decision_umicore_made: Umicore actively chose under the March 2025 CORE plan to NOT expand BMS — capex narrowed to Nysa and Korea only, the European recycling plant deferred, BMS capital employed ceiling implicit at EUR ~2bn, and the original RISE growth strategy explicitly abandoned. The active stance is 'restore value' from existing footprint via utilisation of contracted volumes rather than new capacity. The EUR 1.7bn FY2024 impairment was the formal acknowledgement that the prior NMC growth thesis was over-built relative to the chemistry mix the market actually demands.

citations.snippet: Battery Materials Solutions: revenue 436, revenue_yoy 13.0, revenue_mix_pct 12.2, ebita -91, ebita_margin -19.5, ebita_mix_pct -15.7

Battery Materials Solutions is the problem child: EUR -91 million Adjusted EBIT on EUR 436 million revenues (-19.5% margin). Capital employed of EUR 2,003 million means this segment ties up 56% of group capital employed while generating negative returns (-4.8% ROCE).

EUR 2.0 billion of capital employed in Battery Materials Solutions (56% of group capital employed) against EUR 436 million revenue, EUR -91 million Adjusted EBIT, -19.5% margin, and -4.8% ROCE — the legacy of the FY2024 EUR 1.7 billion impairment.
Umicore's chemistry exposure is nickel-rich NMC, manganese-rich variants, and solid-state development work — the evidence shows no material disclosed LFP/LMFP position.

Umicore's contracted cathode active materials (CAM) order book (190 GWh for 2027; 270 GWh for 2030) remains largely intact and converts into physical call-offs from 2026-2028 at a pace sufficient to lift utilization.

what_it_exposes_to: LFP / LMFP chemistry substitution accelerating in European mass-market platforms
Chinese integrated CAM cost advantage (~80% global capacity in China per macro context)
US-China trade barriers (US 127% tariffs on Chinese CAM imports)
EU Battery Regulation: carbon-footprint thresholds, recycled-content mandates 2028, recycling-efficiency 2027
OEM EV demand growth (2026 expected to lose pace per Reuters, in macro)
Customer ramp slippage (AESC, PowerCo/IONWAY, ACC, SK On)
Precursor (pCAM) cost — dependence on CNGR and Eco&Dream
WACC / discount rate (impairment trigger)
components.notes: Critical: a single-chemistry-family bet, NOT a multi-chemistry portfolio. FY2024 revenue fell 30% YoY despite the same contract book being in place — contracted GWh and realised GWh are not the same number. Macro segment scorecard rates Position Sustainability 'fragile' (3/10) and Market Trajectory 'declining' (4/10). A second impairment of EUR 400-800m is the bear-case base path, not a tail risk.

components.label: Specialty Materials — germanium / electro-optic franchise

concrete_facts.core_franchises: germanium substrates, GeCl4, electroplating chemistry, cobalt specialty chemicals

components.the_decision_umicore_made: Umicore actively chose to retain and protect the germanium / electro-optic franchise (sitting on the right side of China's August 2023 export-control regime) while NOT classifying Specialty Materials as a foundation business equal to Catalysis or Recycling under CORE. The implicit active position is 'protect the germanium franchise; scale selectively elsewhere' — the segment is too small (15.7% of ex-metal revenue) to materially reshape the investment case but is being defended as a pricing-power niche.

EUR 76M Adjusted EBIT at 13.6% margin and 11.1% ROCE in FY2025 on EUR 558M revenues. Germanium franchise sits on the right side of China's August 2023 export-control regime.

components.label: CORE strategic plan (March 2025)
components.the_decision_umicore_made: Umicore actively replaced the RISE 2030 growth strategy with the CORE plan in March 2025 — pivoting from capacity-led growth to capital preservation, cash generation, and value recovery. The four concrete active commitments: (i) capex envelope cut to EUR 310m, (ii) dividend halved EUR 0.80 → EUR 0.25, (iii) BMS capex narrowed to Nysa + Korea only, (iv) the planned 150 kt European battery recycling plant deferred from 2026 to 2032-at-earliest.

citations.snippet: Umicore launched its CORE strategy in March 2025, replacing the 2030 RISE strategy that was effectively abandoned after the Battery Materials impairment. The CORE strategy focuses on: reinforcing leadership in foundation businesses (Catalysis, Recycling, Specialty Materials), restoring value in Battery Materials, and maintaining capital discipline.

The March 2025 CORE strategy reset is the most important management action in this activity since the FY2024 impairment. It replaced RISE's growth ambition with a 'restore value' framing, narrowed capex to Nysa and Korea plus maintenance, delayed the large European recycling plant from 2026 to 2032-at-earliest, achieved the EUR 100 million FY2025 efficiency target, and cut the dividend from EUR 0.80 to EUR 0.25.
The CORE strategy (March 2025) represents a necessary pivot from the failed RISE growth plan to cash generation and capital discipline.

what_it_exposes_to: Capital markets perception of strategic credibility
Foundation-business cash generation durability (gold price, Catalysis margins)
BMS customer ramp timing
Dividend signalling and shareholder pressure
Capex execution discipline

aggregation_rules.anti_double_count_rule: When a theme T propagates DIRECTLY to bet B with weight w_t, and projections P anchored under T also propagate to B with weights w_p, the bundle deriver picks projection-level evidence (w_p × P_TL) over the direct theme seed (w_t × T_TL) — the direct theme weight is the fallback aggregate when no projections exist under T covering bet B's transmission channel.
aggregation_rules.bet_TL_aggregation_formula:
bet_TL ≈ normalize(
  Σ over (T,B) where no projection covers the channel: w_TB × theme_T_TL,
  Σ over (P,B) where projection P covers a transmission channel: w_PB × projection_P_TL,
  Σ over (B_src,B): w × bet_B_src_TL
)
aggregation_rules.projection_TL_aggregation_formula:
projection_TL ≈ blend(parent_theme_TL, evidence_velocity, manual_anchor)
Coherent with theme TL but allows independent measurable evidence to drift it.
aggregation_rules.channel_coverage_check: For each (theme T, bet B) pair with a direct seed weight, list which projections under T cover the same transmission channel — when coverage exists, use projections; when partial, blend; when absent, fall back to direct seed.

status_quo_outlook_v2.headline: Under the v2 7-bet structure as of cycle 2026-05-04, Umicore's FY2028 path is bracketed by B1 BMS at TL 0.30 falling — the load-bearing break-risk — vs the B2+B3 foundation cushion at TL ~0.63 — what buys patience capital. The other four bets sit between 0.40 (B4 compliance moat eroding via Asian-localiser CAM oversupply inside the EU/US perimeter) and 0.55 (B5 Specialty germanium franchise insulated by the Aug-2023 MOFCOM regime; B6 corporate optionality holding on a 1.6x leverage / EUR 1.56bn cash baseline). B7 cash-conversion sits at 0.45 (Contested), needing a 23-43pp lift from FY2025's ~17% trough. The dominant tension is whether the EUR 679m Catalysis+Recycling foundation cushion durably finances ~EUR 100-200m of incremental BMS losses through Q3 2027 without firing premature kill criteria.

bet_anchored_outlook.label: BMS NMC viability through FY2028
bet_anchored_outlook.narrative_60_100_words: B1 is the load-bearing break-risk. T1 (chemistry transition) at prior 0.78 falling and T6 (Western EV demand) premium slice deteriorating one-way pull against the contracted 190 GWh 2027 / 270 GWh 2030 book. FY2025 BMS printed EUR 436m revenue at -19.5% Adjusted EBIT and EUR 2.0bn capital employed = 56% of group, after the FY2024 EUR 1.7bn impairment. Most-likely path (40%) is slow-burn slippage at the upper end of Macro's 6-18 month range, Q3 2027 proof-gate missed, structured partnership initiated, second impairment EUR 400-800m. Central path (25%) marginally clears via positive segment FCF on the AESC + Korean premium NMC sub-book.
bet_anchored_outlook.what_breaks_30_words: Q3 2027 BMS proof-gate missed; second impairment EUR 400-800m; foundation cushion absorbs 12-18 months of stress before kill-criteria fire.

bet_anchored_outlook.label: Catalysis cash-engine durability through FY2028
bet_anchored_outlook.narrative_60_100_words: FY2025 Catalysis printed EUR 1,668m revenue / EUR 383m EBITA / 23.0% margin / 40.8% ROCE — 66.1% of group segment EBITA on a largely depreciated brownfield footprint. Trajectory-stream status-quo lands FY2028 at EUR 1,545m / EUR 347m EBITA, comfortably inside the +/-15% bet envelope. T6 hybrid persistence (EU HEV 38.6%, Toyota's 6.7m unit 2028 commitment) and T5 PGM regime support (current contribution +0.6 combined) outweigh T2 EU-cost drag (-0.1). Modal scenario (40%) delivers FY2028 EBITA EUR 320-380m. Two-sided risk: BOTH faster pure-BEV adoption AND slower HEV adoption hurt; envelope-breach scenario sits at 13%.
bet_anchored_outlook.what_breaks_30_words: ICE production decline accelerates to 6-8% CAGR with US HEV failing to lock above 19%; FY2028 EBITA falls below EUR 326m; foundation arithmetic breaks BMS cross-subsidy threshold.

bet_anchored_outlook.label: Recycling franchise (Hoboken) durability + monetisation through FY2028
bet_anchored_outlook.narrative_60_100_words: FY2025 Recycling printed EUR 296m EBITA on EUR 947m revenue (31.2% margin, 51.1% of group EBITA). Five of six T5 layers supportive today: Pt 4th-year deficit, Russian PGM containment intact, gold ~EUR 4,000/oz spot ~EUR 500 above CFO-stress, refiner pricing power intact (Aurubis Q1 25/26 PGM volume +27%), WSR digital procedures live 21 May 2026. Modal scenario (45%) holds normalised EBITA at EUR 250-350m. The 208% reported ROCE is artefactual (collapsed CE from EUR 525m gold sale-and-leaseback); normalised high-teens / mid-twenties. Hoboken EUR 400m capex (~EUR 300m in 2027-2028) is the load-bearing capex envelope concurrent with stress window.
bet_anchored_outlook.what_breaks_30_words: Gold corrects past USD 1,800-2,000/oz triggering leaseback margin-call concurrent with Russian PGM normalisation; Recycling EBITDA compresses 25-40%; B7 cash-conversion stress fires; foundation cushion below EUR 600m breaks BMS patience-capital arithmetic.

bet_anchored_outlook.label: Compliance moat half-life (cross-segment) through FY2028
bet_anchored_outlook.narrative_60_100_words: Two-layer asymmetric structure: T3 anchors Layer 1 (legal persistence ~70-80%) but T4 anchors Layer 2 (commercial monetisation ~35-45%). Asian compliant-CAM capacity inside the EU/US perimeter is the dominant erosion vector — EcoPro Debrecen 54 kt/yr live Dec 2025 scaling to 108 kt/yr in 2026, LG Chem Tennessee H1 2026 (~60 kt/yr by 2027), POSCO Future M Quebec pending. Bear threshold cumulative >250 kt/yr by year-end 2027 collapses ASP premium to commodity-with-attestation. Modal scenario (40%) is 'moat persists / premium erodes to cost-plus' — Layer 1 holds, Layer 2 compresses, Specialty germanium niche stays insulated, BMS proof-gate becomes mathematically unreachable.
bet_anchored_outlook.what_breaks_30_words: Asian compliant-CAM cumulative >250 kt/yr inside EU/US by end-2027 OR Treasury extends impracticable-to-trace beyond end-2026; compliant-CAM ASP premium drops below 5% sustained for >2 quarters.

bet_anchored_outlook.label: Specialty Materials premium pricing power through FY2028
bet_anchored_outlook.narrative_60_100_words: Most-insulated of the seven bets. T3 load-bearing at 0.66 with upward bias — the operative August-2023 MOFCOM germanium/gallium licensing regime is DISTINCT from the suspended 2025 MOFCOM 55-62 package and was UNTOUCHED by Busan / MOFCOM-70. March 2026 Western germanium spot ~$5,475/kg vs China domestic ~$1,949/kg = 181% observed premium translating into FY2025 EUR 76m EBITA at 13.6% margin. No Asian-localiser germanium-substrate ramping inside the perimeter. Modal scenario (45%) holds FY2028 EBITA at EUR 80-120m. Phase-2 voluntary suspension tail at 8-10% probability fires the -2 magnitude rule. Western substitution timeline (5N Plus, Teledyne, AXT) at fast-end of Macro's 3-5 year horizon is the slow-decay break path.
bet_anchored_outlook.what_breaks_30_words: MOFCOM Phase-2 voluntary suspension of Aug-2023 germanium/gallium regime as Busan-2 goodwill OR Western substitution capacity catches up by H2 2027 compressing premium toward 50-80%.

bet_anchored_outlook.label: Corporate optionality preservation through FY2028 BMS resolution window
bet_anchored_outlook.narrative_60_100_words: Pure cushion bet, no direct theme propagation — strong bet-to-bet coupling: 1.0 to B1, 0.9 to B2, 0.85 to B3, 0.8 to B7. FY2025 baseline supportive: net debt EUR 1,357m, 1.6x leverage (gold-sale-flattered), EUR 1,559m cash, partially-restored EUR 0.50 dividend. Heaviest defensive levers (dividend cut, capex cut, 150 kt EU recycling deferral to 2032+) already pulled. Three pending governance gaps: kill criteria not formalised, segment FCF disclosure implementation, quarterly cash gates. Modal path (40%) preserves optionality. Tail-governance failure scenario (5%) compresses cushion pre-Q3 2027 forcing premature kill criteria. The cushion is materially likelier to hold than break per individual bet, but joint-stress correlation pulls below 0.65.
bet_anchored_outlook.what_breaks_30_words: Joint B2 envelope-breach + B3 gold-cliff + B7 conversion-stress firing in correlated stress regime; second dividend cut signals erosion; BMS partnership forced rather than chosen.

bet_anchored_outlook.label: Group cash-conversion bridge from sub-20% (FY2025) toward 40-60% by FY2028
bet_anchored_outlook.narrative_60_100_words: FY2025 trough severe: FCF EUR 145m on EBITDA EUR 847m = ~17% conversion; underlying operating cash flow ex-gold-sale EUR 324m (-63% YoY); WC build EUR 261m. The CORE 'cash generation pivot' is NOT yet visible. Target requires 23-43pp recovery. Capex envelope discipline real (FY2025 capex EUR 310m down 44% YoY) but EUR 300m Hoboken concentration 2027-2028 is binding constraint. Belgian/Polish sites outside German Industriestrompreis EUR 50/MWh relief. Two correlated modal scenarios at 30% each: slow-grind sub-30% conversion through FY2028 vs central recovery to 40-50%. Tail capex-overrun + WC-spike + BMS impairment break path concurrent at 15%.
bet_anchored_outlook.what_breaks_30_words: Hoboken capex over-runs 15-25% concurrent with PGM/gold-driven WC build above EUR 250m/cycle and BMS proof-gate miss; FY2027-2028 conversion stays sub-25%; second dividend cut.

joint_distribution_risks.scenario: B3 gold-cliff + B7 cash-conversion stress fire concurrently (gold past USD 1,800-2,000/oz triggers leaseback margin-call AND PGM normalisation marks down inventory AND foundation EBITDA compresses)
joint_distribution_risks.impact_one_sentence: B3-B7 coupling at 0.5 plus simultaneous foundation-cushion erosion forces reverse-leaseback or accelerated capex-pause and pre-empts BMS Q3 2027 proof-gate via premature kill-criteria firing — exactly the B6 tail-governance break-path.

joint_distribution_risks.scenario: B1 BMS proof-gate miss + B4 compliance-moat ASP-premium collapse below 5% sustained (Asian-localiser cumulative >250 kt/yr inside perimeter by end-2027, replay of POSCO/GM/Ultium renegotiation precedent at scale)
joint_distribution_risks.impact_one_sentence: B4-to-B1 coupling at 0.6 makes the proof-gate mathematically unreachable on the contracted book and forces structured partnership / partial divestiture at distressed terms before the foundation cushion can absorb the second EUR 400-800m impairment cleanly.

joint_distribution_risks.scenario: B2 envelope-breach + B3 slow-grind margin compression (Catalysis FY2028 EBITA below EUR 326m AND Recycling normalised EBITA below EUR 230m on gold 15-25% correction + trader-incursion + Belgian wage indexation lifting Hoboken cost floor)
joint_distribution_risks.impact_one_sentence: Combined foundation cushion drops below EUR 580m through FY2027 — direct break of the B6 patience-capital arithmetic that funds BMS through the proof-gate; cascades to B1 tail-governance scenario from 5% into the 12-15% range.

joint_distribution_risks.scenario: B1 chemistry cliff + B4 compliance-moat persists but premium erodes (premium-OEM platform LFP/LMFP confirmation for non-entry trim before 2028 + Korean cell-maker LFP second-wave conversions in 2027-2028)
joint_distribution_risks.impact_one_sentence: 270 GWh 2030 book renegotiated POSCO/GM/Ultium-style; IONWAY capex partially stranded; BMS formally re-scoped to surviving offtakes (~50-130 GWh effective volume); second impairment EUR 700-1,200m; foundation cushion still funds wind-down but optionality envelope (B6) compresses materially.

joint_distribution_risks.scenario: B6 tail-governance failure (foundation cushion compresses pre-Q3 2027 from concurrent B2 ROCE wall + B3 gold correction + Hoboken capex slippage + T2 second energy shock)
joint_distribution_risks.impact_one_sentence: Patience capital runs out; board fires kill criteria pre-emptively in 2026-Q1 2027 to protect group ROCE; BMS forced into structured partnership / sale BEFORE proof-gate matures; this is B6 break itself firing through the four-bet correlated-stress channel and is the lowest-probability but highest-severity joint risk in the distribution.

factual_corrections_carried_forward: BMS chemistry concentrated nickel-rich NMC, not multi-chemistry
Closed-loop CAM + Recycling moat overstated — 150 kt European battery recycling deferred to 2032+
Recycling 208% ROCE artefactual; normalised high-teens / mid-20s
Western cost curve unverified — pCAM still externally sourced from CNGR / Eco&Dream
CORE cash generation pivot not yet visible — sub-20% FCF conversion FY2025
Refused to fabricate Hyundai Changshu PEM, Loyang Singapore, Cheonan Korea
status_quo_outlook_v2.narrative_md:
## 1. Headline — the dominant tension

Umicore's FY2028 path is structurally bracketed. On one side: B1 (BMS NMC viability) at TL 0.30 falling — the load-bearing break-risk, with a 0.32 prior reflecting that T1 chemistry-transition pace at prior 0.78 falling and T6 Western premium-EV demand are pulling one-way against the contracted 190 GWh 2027 / 270 GWh 2030 offtake book. On the other side: B2 + B3 (Catalysis + Recycling) at TLs 0.62 and 0.65 respectively, providing the EUR 679m FY2025 Adjusted EBIT foundation cushion that buys ~12-18 months of patience capital for the Q3 2027 BMS proof-gate. Everything else — B4 compliance moat at 0.40, B5 Specialty at 0.55, B6 optionality at 0.55, B7 cash conversion at 0.45 — sits between the foundation and the break-risk. The dominant question is whether the cushion holds long enough, governance formalises kill criteria fast enough, and the proof-gate clears on a sub-set of the contracted book.

## 2. The bet-spine framing — why 7 bets organise the picture

The prior version of this outlook tried to reason from themes downward and timed out. The 7-bet structure organises the picture better because it makes the multiplicative joint-distribution risk visible. B1 has many ways to break and few to clear simultaneously (chemistry shift, demand cliff, compliance commoditisation, IONWAY/PowerCo restructuring, AESC slippage, second impairment). B2 + B3 together print EUR 679m of segment EBITA today on largely depreciated brownfield footprints — the cushion is delivered, not promised. B4 is the cross-segment moat that ASP-translates the regulatory framework into pricing power. B5 is the most insulated (Aug-2023 MOFCOM regime untouched by Busan). B6 is the cushion bet — no direct theme propagation but coupling weights of 1.0 / 0.9 / 0.85 / 0.8 to B1/B2/B3/B7. B7 is the cash-conversion bridge — the binding constraint on whether reported numbers reflect the CORE plan as advertised.

## 3. BMS as load-bearing break-risk

B1's prior of 0.32 is anchored by five inputs: T1 (one-way), T6 (premium slice deteriorating in real time — BMW BEV+PHEV -50% YoY US Q1 2026, Mercedes EU BEV +7% off low base, Audi/VW BEV-mix repeatedly cut), T4 (compliance moat at widest legal aperture but Asian compliant capacity ramping inside the perimeter — EcoPro Debrecen 54 kt/yr live Dec 2025 scaling to 108 kt/yr in 2026, LG Chem Tennessee H1 2026), the FY2025 financial baseline (EUR 436m revenue, -19.5% Adjusted EBIT, EUR 2.0bn capital employed, FY2024 EUR 1.7bn impairment), and structural constraints (pCAM still externally sourced from CNGR/Eco&Dream, 2028 capacity-vs-commitment mismatch of 115 GWh capacity vs 133 GWh commitments). The most-likely scenario at 40% is slow-burn slippage at the upper end of Macro's 6-18 month range — 2027 call-offs land closer to 130-150 GWh than 190 GWh, BMS prints a third year of EUR -50 to -120m EBIT, Q3 2027 proof-gate is missed, structured partnership or partial divestiture is initiated, and a second EUR 400-800m impairment is recognised. Central-path resolution-true at 25% requires ~150-170 GWh physical call-offs, IONWAY/PowerCo ramp without chemistry pivot, AESC stabilising near 2026 commitment, and the compliant-CAM ASP premium holding at 6-8%.

## 4. Foundation cushion (B2+B3) as the buy-time mechanism

This is what makes everything else possible. B2 (Catalysis) at TL 0.62 delivers FY2025 EUR 1,668m revenue / EUR 383m EBITA / 23.0% margin / 40.8% ROCE — a wasting asset with the endpoint into the early 2030s, not by FY2028. The trajectory-stream status-quo path lands FY2028 inside the +/-15% bet envelope. B2's two-sided risk is genuine — both faster pure-BEV adoption AND slower HEV adoption hurt — but T6 hybrid persistence (EU HEV 38.6% Q1 2026, Toyota's 6.7m unit hybrid commitment by 2028) and T5 PGM regime support the volume floor and the integrated PGM loop with Hoboken. B3 (Recycling) at TL 0.65 delivers EUR 296m FY2025 EBITA on EUR 947m revenue (31.2% margin) anchored by five of six T5 layers supportive: Pt 4th-year deficit, Russian PGM containment intact (Norilsk Q1 2026 Pd -18% / Pt -26% YoY), gold ~EUR 500/oz cushion above CFO-stress, refiner pricing power intact, WSR digital procedures live 21 May 2026. The 208% reported ROCE is artefactual — collapsed CE from the EUR 525m gold sale-and-leaseback — and normalises into the high-teens / mid-twenties. The cushion is real, observable, and delivered today; it is not a promise. The fragility is concentrated in one path: a gold correction past USD 1,800-2,000/oz CFO-stress concurrent with Russian PGM normalisation that compresses both reported EBITDA AND triggers leaseback margin-call mechanics in the same window.

## 5. The middle bets — B4 compliance, B5 Specialty, B6 optionality, B7 cash conversion

B4 at TL 0.40 has the most asymmetric structure: T3 anchors Layer 1 (legal persistence ~70-80%) but T4 anchors Layer 2 (commercial monetisation ~35-45%). The modal scenario (40%) is 'moat persists / premium erodes to cost-plus' — the framework holds but Asian compliant capacity inside the perimeter forces compliant-CAM ASP premium below 5% sustained, making B1's proof-gate path mathematically unreachable. B5 at TL 0.55 is the most insulated bet because the operative August-2023 MOFCOM germanium/gallium regime is distinct from the suspended 2025 MOFCOM 55-62 package — observed 181% Western-vs-China spot premium translating into FY2025 EUR 76m EBITA at 13.6% margin, no Asian-localiser inside-perimeter erosion vector, and the Phase-2 voluntary-suspension tail sits at 8-10%. B6 at TL 0.55 is the cushion bet with no direct theme propagation but heavy bet-to-bet coupling — 1.6x leverage (gold-sale-flattered), EUR 1,559m cash, partially-restored EUR 0.50 dividend, the heaviest defensive levers already pulled. The asymmetry: governance is the pivot — formalised kill criteria + segment FCF disclosure + cash gates clearing in the next 9 months would re-rate B6 toward 0.65; failure pulls toward 0.45. B7 at TL 0.45 is Contested — FY2025 ~17% conversion is severe, the CORE 'cash generation pivot' is NOT yet visible, and the 23-43pp recovery target faces the EUR 300m Hoboken capex concentration in 2027-2028 plus Belgian/Polish sites outside German Industriestrompreis EUR 50/MWh relief.

## 6. Joint distribution risks

Five joint scenarios dominate the multiplicative risk surface. The largest is B1 + B4 breaking together (probability ~0.18): proof-gate miss concurrent with compliance moat ASP collapse forces structured partnership at distressed terms before the cushion absorbs the second impairment. B2 + B3 envelope breach + slow-grind margin compression (~0.10) drops the foundation cushion below EUR 580m FY2027 — direct break of B6 patience-capital arithmetic. B1 chemistry cliff + B4 premium erosion (~0.08) renegotiates the 270 GWh 2030 book POSCO/GM/Ultium-style. B3 + B7 gold-cliff + cash-conversion concurrent stress (~0.06) forces reverse-leaseback or accelerated capex-pause. The B6 tail-governance failure (~0.05) is the lowest-probability but highest-severity scenario — patience capital runs out, kill criteria fire pre-emptively. Critically, these stresses are positively correlated: T6 demand cliff hurts both BMS and Catalysis; gold/PGM cycle hurts both Recycling and the leaseback structure; EU industrial cost lift hits all three EU-located segments. The aggregate joint-failure probability is materially higher than the product of independent failures would suggest.

## 7. The five calendared gates that will move TLs in the next 18 months

The 24 July 2026 USTR overcapacity remedy is the first dated test for B4 + B3 — extension to pCAM/separators/transshipment widens the wall around Loyalist + Hoboken; closure without remedy is mildly negative. The 10 November 2026 Busan/MOFCOM-70 expiry is the binary B5 test — extension status quo or snap-back is supportive, Phase-2 carve-out below 100% with voluntary germanium suspension fires the -2 magnitude rule. The 31 December 2026 Treasury impracticable-to-trace expiry is the B4 + B1 ASP-translation gate — clean expiry with hard-edge tracing widens the premium 15-25%, extension replays the May 2024 soften-via-guidance playbook. The 18 February 2027 EU Battery Passport hard date is the B4 + B1 monetisation activation point — at least one DG GROW penalty case in H2 2027 anchors preferred-supplier procurement language; absence confirms the table-stakes-only erosion. The 30 September 2027 Q3 2027 BMS proof-gate is the load-bearing readable signal converting B1 from path-dependent persistence into a binary observable.

## 8. What would change this outlook

Three positive pivots would re-rate the picture: (a) board formalises binding kill criteria + segment FCF disclosure + quarterly cash gates by Q1 2027 (re-rates B6 toward 0.65 and B1 toward 0.40); (b) Treasury hard-edge tracing guidance Q1 2027 widens compliant-CAM premium to 15-25% (re-rates B4 toward 0.55 and B1 toward 0.42 via T4 enforcement-teeth episode); (c) Korean cell-makers add EUR 500m+ named external Western NMC CAM offtake in any 12-month window AND BMW Neue Klasse follow-on confirms high-nickel NMC for full range (re-rates T1 and B1 directly). Three negative pivots would tip the system into the joint-distribution stress regime: (a) gold corrects past USD 1,800-2,000/oz CFO-stress within 12 months; (b) Asian compliant CAM cumulative crosses 250 kt/yr inside the perimeter by year-end 2027; (c) any premium-OEM (BMW, Audi, Mercedes, Porsche) adopts LFP/LMFP for non-entry trim before 2028. The next four quarterly disclosure cycles — and the five calendared gates above — will be enough to update most of the bet TLs in this outlook materially. Until then, B1 is falling, B2+B3 is the cushion, and B6 is the pivot.

executive_papers_v2.headline_60_words: Umicore enters FY2026 with a load-bearing fracture: BMS (B1, TL 0.30) is breaking on chemistry/demand while a Catalysis+Recycling foundation (B2 0.62, B3 0.65) funds patience capital that the cash-conversion bridge (B7 0.45) and optionality cushion (B6 0.55) must protect. Compliance moat (B4 0.40) erodes; Specialty (B5 0.55) is the only insulated franchise.

per_bet_assessment.status_le_60_words: Most ambiguous of the seven.
