per_bet_assessment.mitigation_le_80_words: Lobby for Treasury clean expiry of impracticable-to-trace and Q1 2027 hard-edge FEOC tracing guidance. Advance GePETO + ReGAIN CRMA Strategic Project funding through EIB/EIF disbursement for institutional underwriting visibility. Push DG GROW to publish first Battery Passport non-compliance penalty cases post Feb 2027. Pre-position fleet operator (Ayvens, Element, ARI) RFP language excluding non-FEOC-compliant batteries. Prepare for segment-by-segment defensible-niche re-scoping if Layer 2 collapses cross-segment.

per_bet_assessment.label: Specialty Materials premium pricing power through FY2028
per_bet_assessment.status_le_60_words: Only B-bet where load-bearing macro (T3 prior 0.66) anchors above 0.50. Western germanium spot ~$5,475/kg vs China domestic ~$1,949/kg = 181% Western premium TODAY. FY2025 EUR 558m revenue, EUR 76m EBITA at 13.6% margin / 11.1% ROCE. The Aug 2023 MOFCOM germanium/gallium regime is DISTINCT from the suspended 2025 layer and was untouched by Busan/MOFCOM-70. Franchise is monetising the regime now.
per_bet_assessment.implication_le_80_words: True resolution requires sustained Western premium >100% vs China through Q4 2027 and 13-16% EBITA margin. Most likely false path: slow-decay as 5N Plus Trail BC + Saint-Augustin antimony + Teledyne primary-route + AXT + JX Advanced Metals catch up — premium compresses toward 50-80% by H2 2027. Tail break: Phase-2 voluntary suspension as Busan-2 goodwill gesture (8-10% probability) fires the -2 magnitude rule via T3, premium collapses to 30-50%, EBITA EUR 35-50m.
per_bet_assessment.effect_le_100_words: FY2026-FY2028 effect: at central path, Specialty contributes EUR 80-120m EBITA — modest but with high asymmetric upside if defence procurement scales (NDAA 842, DFARS implementing rule). Segment scale (EUR 558m revenue) too small to anchor a board-level kill-criteria event. No Asian-localiser inside-the-perimeter erosion vector parallel to BMS. CRMA Strategic Project status (GePETO + ReGAIN) reinforces institutional infrastructure. The 3-5 year Western substitution timeline lands inside resolution horizon at lower bound — slow-decay risk mathematically real.
per_bet_assessment.mitigation_le_80_words: Track MOFCOM monthly germanium/gallium licensing bulletins — Western buyer approval-rate trajectory through Q3 2026 pre-resolves a meaningful portion of Busan / MOFCOM-70 binary. Advance DoD pilot procurement specifying FEOC-clean germanium/gallium inputs — anchor defence-tier premium channel. Defend Hoboken-feedstock advantage by sustaining complex-feed throughput. Retain Specialty franchise (active CORE decision); resist value-destructive divestiture pressure. Position GePETO + ReGAIN through EIB/EIF funding disbursement.

per_bet_assessment.label: Corporate optionality preservation through the FY2028 BMS resolution window
per_bet_assessment.status_le_60_words: B6 is the cushion bet — no direct theme propagation; prior is mechanically the joint probability that balance-sheet + governance + strategic flexibility hold through the FY2026-FY2028 stress window. FY2025 leverage 1.6x, cash EUR 1,559m, dividend partially restored EUR 0.50 (from EUR 0.25 trough). EUR 525m gold leaseback bought time but flatters underlying cash. Three governance gaps still pending: kill criteria, segment FCF disclosure follow-through, quarterly cash gates.
per_bet_assessment.implication_le_80_words: True resolution requires the optionality envelope to be preserved long enough that BMS resolution is a CHOSEN board action rather than forced firesale. Most likely false path: slow-burn cushion erosion as B2/B3 partial stress, lost gold-leaseback comparator, and Hoboken EUR 300m capex concentration combine to drift leverage to 2.0-2.3x and force second dividend cut. Tail: B1 tail-governance-failure scenario IS B6 break (5% probability) — cushion runs out pre-Q3 2027, kill criteria fired pre-emptively.
per_bet_assessment.effect_le_100_words: FY2026-FY2028 effect: joint-distribution coupling concentrates risk. B6 has highest bet-to-bet weights (B1 1.0, B2 0.9, B3 0.85, B7 0.8) — break paths are positively correlated (T6 demand cliff, gold/PGM cycle, EU industrial cost lift). Belgian 30% mandatory-bid rule shields against hostile stake-build but NOT friendly carve-out at distressed valuation. Hoboken EUR 400m capex hits exactly during stress window forcing internal capital competition. CORE 'cash generation pivot' not yet visible (FY2025 FCF conversion ~17%).
per_bet_assessment.mitigation_le_80_words: Formalise binding board kill criteria pre-Q1 2027 — Convergence Round 2 unanimous demand. Land Q1 2026 segment FCF disclosure cleanly with gold-leaseback gain reconciled. Operationalise quarterly cash gates on every business unit. Hold dividend at EUR 0.50 (no second cut as defensive lever already pulled). Track Belgian FSMA disclosure thresholds (5/7.5/10/15/20%) for concert-party stake-build patterns. Pre-position structured-partnership counterparties for BMS so any S-3-1 forced-decision transacts at Umicore's timeline.

per_bet_assessment.label: Group cash-conversion bridge from sub-20% (FY2025) toward 40-60% by FY2028
per_bet_assessment.status_le_60_words: FY2025 starting point severe: FCF EUR 145m / EBITDA EUR 847m = ~17% conversion; underlying operating cash flow ex-gold EUR 324m (-63% YoY); WC build EUR 261m. Leverage improvement to 1.6x was gold-leaseback-driven. CORE 'cash generation pivot' NOT yet visible. The 40-60% FY2028 target requires 23-43pp recovery in conversion ratio over three years. T5 -0.2 contribution today (WC at upper envelope); T2 -0.2 (ETS rallying, Belgian indexation due May/June 2026).
per_bet_assessment.implication_le_80_words: True resolution requires WC normalisation as PGM/gold cycles moderate (cumulative release EUR 100-300m FY2026-FY2028), capex inside EUR 425-475m envelope even with EUR 300m Hoboken concentration, EUR 100m efficiency run-rate sustained + EUR 50-100m incremental, dividend held at EUR 0.50. Most likely false path: slow-grind sub-30% conversion as PGM/gold stay elevated, Hoboken capex absorbs foundation cash, efficiency program 'durability beyond 2025 unproven' fires (Strategic A8). Tail: gold-cliff B3-coupled break.
per_bet_assessment.effect_le_100_words: FY2026-FY2028 effect: B7 directly gates dividend defence, Hoboken capex commitment, BMS proof-gate funding through Q3 2027. The break paths are correlated with B3 (PGM rally drives both WC build and foundation EBITDA; gold correction triggers both leaseback margin call and foundation compression). Belgian + Polish energy-stack asymmetry (sites OUTSIDE German Industriestrompreis) compounds capex-execution stress in 2027-2028. 'One-time levers exhausted' per CFO non-negotiable — next leverage improvement must come from operational discipline. Failure cascades into B6 (optionality) and B1 (BMS funding) simultaneously.
per_bet_assessment.mitigation_le_80_words: Implement segment FCF disclosure with WC delta by segment (BMS vs Catalysis vs Recycling) at Q1 2026 update. Defend EUR 100m efficiency run-rate AND deliver incremental EUR 50-100m through FY2027 — answer Strategic A8 'durability unproven' verdict. Hold capex inside envelope by phasing Hoboken capex against foundation cash flow. Pre-position metal-management hedging policy reset to neutralise WC drag — pos_capability_metal_management_hedging stress-test failed in FY2025. Resist incremental BMS growth capital.

executive_papers_v2.joint_distribution_view_120_words: The bet_to_bet matrix shows the joint distribution is dominated by three correlated failure modes. (1) B6 to B1/B2/B3/B7 (weights 1.0/0.9/0.85/0.8): the cushion is mechanically the joint survival probability — ANY foundation-segment break fires premature BMS kill criteria. (2) B4 to B1 (0.6) + B7 to B6 (implicit via cash conversion): compliance-premium erosion compresses BMS conversion margin BELOW the 5% threshold needed for Q3 2027 proof-gate, simultaneously as cash-conversion stress drains optionality. (3) B3-B7 gold-cliff coupling: a USD 1,800-2,000/oz gold correction triggers leaseback margin call AND Recycling EBITDA compression AND WC unwind pulse in the same window, breaking foundation cushion mathematics regardless of BMS execution. Stresses are positively correlated (T6 demand cliff hurts BMS+Catalysis; T2 EU cost-stack hits all EU sites).

active.name: Chemistry transition pace
active.thesis: The central question is whether premium nickel-rich NMC chemistry retains a defensible Western profit pool through 2028-2030, or whether LFP/LMFP migrates further up-market into premium-OEM platforms and Korean cell-makers convert enough lines that external Western NMC-CAM producers lose qualified offtake faster than IRA/CRMA localisation policy can compensate. As of Q1 2026, LFP exceeds 50% of global EV battery deployments (SNE Research), CATL holds 42.1% global cell share, and even Mercedes' new MMA platform launched with both LFP (entry CLA) and NMC (long-range CLA) variants — confirming LFP has crossed into Western premium architectures. The unresolved question is whether this is a permanent re-stratification (NMC niches into >€55k long-range only) or whether Korean cell-maker NMC defence + Euro 7 + EU Battery Regulation traceability rebuild a stable mid-premium NMC pool.

watching_authored.name: Sodium-ion stationary-storage commercialisation
watching_authored.thesis: Sodium-ion (Na-ion) reaches commercial cost-and-cycle parity for stationary-storage applications by 2027-2028, capturing 5-15% of incremental EU+US grid-storage GWh that would otherwise have been LFP/Li-ion. Net effect on Western premium-NMC demand floor is indirect but cumulative: Na-ion absorbs the chemistry-agnostic part of demand, leaving Li-ion increasingly tied to mobility-only use cases.
watching_authored.system_claim: EU+US stationary-storage installed-base addition rate reaches >= 8 GWh per year of sodium-ion chemistry by FY2028, sustained over two consecutive calendar years, with at least one investment-grade Western OEM or utility offtake contract for Na-ion CAM in place. (Note: Chinese deployment has already crossed this scale at the announcement level — CATL/HyperStrong 60 GWh deal April 2026 — so the structural question is now whether deployment migrates to Western markets, not whether the chemistry has reached commercial scale.)

current_state.derivation_note: Computed by cycle_outside_in 2026-05-06 from 8 evidence rows.

watching_authored.trend_direction: UP_STEEP — technology and chemistry threshold cleared; Chinese deployment scaling rapidly; question now shifts to Western deployment timing
watching_authored.umicore_translation_hypothesis: Compresses Western Li-cathode demand floor at the chemistry-agnostic margin (stationary storage). Indirect compression on B1 (BMS) volume baseline. On Recycling (B3), opens question of whether sodium-bearing battery streams enter the EOL-feedstock mix and at what economic value vs Li-cells — likely lower per-tonne value but higher volume. No direct exposure on Catalysis (B2) or Specialty Materials (B5).

watching_authored.name: Direct cathode regeneration / hydromet bypass
watching_authored.thesis: Direct cathode regeneration (DCR) — recovering active cathode material from end-of-life cells without dissolving down to elemental metals via hydromet/pyromet — reaches commercial viability for premium-NMC chemistries by 2027-2029. If validated at scale, it bypasses integrated smelter-refiner economics for the EOL-battery feedstock pool, structurally challenging asset lives of integrated facilities like Hoboken-style operations.
watching_authored.system_claim: At least one Western or Korean DCR operator achieves > 5,000 t/yr commercial throughput at premium-NMC quality (cells regenerated meet OEM-tier-1 acceptance) at positive operating margin, with a named OEM offtake contract, by 2028-12-31.

current_state.derivation_note: Computed by cycle_outside_in 2026-05-06 from 13 evidence rows.

watching_authored.trend_direction: UP_STEEP — moving faster than seed thesis. Question shifts from 'will DCR commercialise' to 'will DCR + OEM-acceptance combine to threaten integrated smelter economics within the 2028-2032 window'.
watching_authored.umicore_translation_hypothesis: Direct strategic threat to B3 (Recycling) if DCR captures premium-NMC EOL feedstock; Hoboken impairment-test exposure window 2028-2032 if EOL-feedstock mix shifts. Two-sided: if Umicore itself moves into DCR (technology hedge), the threat reverses into a strategic-positioning advantage. Tangential to B1 (BMS) on the input-cost side — could lower CAM cost stack if Umicore sources regenerated material; tangential to B2 (Catalysis) and B5 (Specialty) — no direct exposure.

active.name: EU industrial cost stack
active.thesis: The EU industrial cost stack is in a fragmented, asymmetrically relieved regime in mid-2026. Five layers move on different clocks: (1) NW European baseload day-ahead power has held the 2024 gain at EUR 32-50/MWh, but 1-year forwards remain EUR 74-95/MWh, embedding persistence of the cost gap into 2027; (2) TTF gas has rebounded to ~EUR 47/MWh on Iran-blockade headlines, well off the EUR 25-35 normalisation band of 2024; (3) EU ETS has rallied to EUR 75.5/tCO2 with 2026 supply cuts of ~8% mechanically tightening the market; (4) CBAM permanent phase started 1 Jan 2026 at EUR 75.36/cert, but actu

current_state.trajectory: two-sided / mildly widening on molecule + carbon side, stable on power day-ahead, asymmetrically relieved by national German scheme

watching_authored.name: Non-EV battery end-market expansion (BESS / mobile robotics / aerial mobility / heavy machinery / portable + power tools / marine)
watching_authored.thesis: Multiple non-EV battery-consuming industries are scaling materially through 2030 — stationary storage / BESS, mobile robotics (humanoid + industrial AGV/AMR + service robots), aerial mobility (drones, UAV, eVTOL), heavy machinery electrification (mining, construction, agriculture), portable electronics + power tools, and marine / inland shipping electrification. Collectively these reshape the cathode-demand mix away from EV-vehicle-dominance through 2030. The strategically load-bearing question for any European battery-materials provider is: per industry, which battery chemistry wins, at what scale, on what timeline, and through which OEM/integrator demand channel? Tracking these industry trajectories independent of any single company's positioning is what makes this an Outside-In driver.
watching_authored.system_claim: By FY2028 the combined non-automotive cathode-demand pool (BESS + mobile robotics + aerial mobility + heavy machinery + portable/power-tools + marine) globally crosses 1,000 GWh/yr CAM-equivalent annual demand, with at least 4 of the 6 named end-markets each independently consuming > 30 GWh/yr CAM-equivalent. Per-industry chemistry-mix is differentiated (BESS: LFP-dominant; robotics/aerial: high-Ni-NMC/solid-state-dominant; marine: LFP-dominant; heavy machinery: LFP/NCMA-mixed; portable: high-Ni-NMC + LCO).

current_state.derivation_note: Computed by cycle_outside_in 2026-05-06 from 4 evidence rows.

watching_authored.trend_direction: UP_BROAD — multiple end-markets simultaneously scaling; chemistry-mix differentiation likely (LFP wins stationary + marine; high-Ni-NMC wins robotics + aerial; solid-state on premium ramp 2028+)
watching_authored.umicore_translation_hypothesis: LIGHT COUPLING ONLY. Outside-In driver is industry-generic — tracking these flows is informative for any European battery-materials provider class. For Umicore specifically: (a) the 195 GWh installed CAM capacity by end-2026 is currently EV-only-positioned with explicit underutilisation flagged on Chinese assets; non-EV diversification is the obvious pressure-release valve but no Umicore IR commitment exists today. (b) Solid-state position via Blue Current investment is partially aligned with the W10 humanoid + eVTOL + premium-auto vector. (c) Compliance moat (B4) is a procurement differentiator for premium-OEM non-auto buyers (humanoid OEMs, eVTOL OEMs, premium robotics). (d) BESS-LFP locking is a structural negative — Umicore's NMC heritage doesn't address LFP-dominant stationary. The Inside-Out coupling — should Umicore restructure CAM capacity for non-auto markets — lives in BC-OI-3, not here.

watching_authored.name: CBAM extension to chemicals, CAM, catalysts and refined PGMs
watching_authored.thesis: EU Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism (CBAM) scope extension beyond steel/aluminium/cement/fertiliser/electricity/hydrogen into downstream chemicals, cathode active materials (CAM), catalysts and refined platinum-group metals lands in legislative form by 2027-2028. If enacted with embedded-emissions methodology favouring EU producers, CBAM neutralises a meaningful share of the EU industrial-cost stack disadvantage and reinforces compliance moats. If carved out or delayed, EU mid-stream materials remain exposed to Chinese cost-position arbitrage.
watching_authored.system_claim: By 2027-12-31 the European Commission tables a binding CBAM scope-extension proposal that explicitly lists at least one of {cathode active materials, refined PGMs, downstream catalysts, base-chemicals intermediates} as in-scope, with a phase-in implementation date no later than 2030-01-01.

current_state.derivation_note: Computed by cycle_outside_in 2026-05-06 from 0 evidence rows.

watching_authored.trend_direction: UP_MODERATE — scope-extension momentum confirmed but chemicals/CAM specifically remain in the 2027-evaluation queue, not the 2025-26 proposal
watching_authored.umicore_translation_hypothesis: Strong upside bias for Umicore if scope extension lands as drafted. B4 (Compliance Moat) materially reinforced — embedded-emissions advantage of EU/Korean integrated production over Chinese imports gets priced via CBAM. B1 (BMS) competitive position improves on Western CAM imports. B3 (Recycling) tangential — embedded-emissions of recycled-derived metals is structurally lower, so any methodology that recognises this is a positive. B5 (Specialty Materials, germanium) — depends on whether refined PGMs vs other refined-metal categories make the cut. Downside scenario (carve-out / delay) leaves cost-stack disadvantage exposed.

active.name: PGM regime + recycling stream capture

watching_authored.name: EU water-stress regulation tightening on metallurgy and process chemistry
watching_authored.thesis: EU water-stress regulation moves from informational (River Basin Management Plans) into binding-quota and permit-conditioning regimes for water-intensive industrial processes by 2027-2029. Hydrometallurgy, electroplating, refining, and cathode-precursor process chemistry face quota tightening, longer permit-renewal cycles, and increased risk of conditional permits. Geography: Belgium-Netherlands cluster (Scheldt-Meuse basin) and Italian/Spanish/Southern-French clusters most exposed; Nordic and German Rhine basins less so.
watching_authored.system_claim: By 2028-12-31 at least one EU member-state competent water authority denies, suspends, or imposes binding-volume conditions on a Tier-1 industrial water permit for a metallurgical or specialty-chemicals facility (capacity > 10,000 t/yr or > 5 GWh equivalent), explicitly citing water-stress-stress designation under the EU Water Framework Directive or successor instrument.

watching_authored.trend_direction: UP_MODERATE — strategy active, court signals reinforcing, no Tier-1 denial yet but approach of binding-instrument phase
watching_authored.umicore_translation_hypothesis: Direct exposure on Hoboken (Scheldt basin), Olen (Albert Canal / Kempen aquifer), Belgian R&D facilities. Indirect on broader EU greenfield expansion ambitions for Recycling and BMS-precursor capacity. Tangential on Korean operations (no EU water-permit exposure). active.name: US-China decoupling permanence
active.thesis: The US-China relationship sits in a layered, dual-track state as of May 2026. Layer 1 (the surface): the Busan truce of October 2025 suspends heightened reciprocal tariffs, halves the fentanyl tariff to 10% and pauses MOFCOM Announcements 55–62 (rare-earth / permanent-magnet / battery-grade graphite controls) until 10 November 2026 via MOFCOM/GAC Announcement 70/2025. Layer 2 (the durable stack, untouched by Busan): USTR Section 301 tariffs from FRN 89 FR 76581 step up on schedule on 1 January 2026 — non-EV lithium-ion batteries to 25%, natural graphite to 25%; the cumulative US tariff rate on

current_state.trajectory: holding with upward bias (escalation pipeline > de-escalation pipeline)

active.name: Compliance-moat half-life
active.thesis: The compliance moat consists of four mutually reinforcing layers, each with a different half-life: (1) the headline tariff wall (~127% on Chinese CAM via Section 301 + MFN + IRA stack) — long-lived because reversal requires explicit USTR action and consumer-price politics has not bitten; (2) the IRA 30D FEOC eligibility regime — partially operational, with the 25% ownership threshold and battery-component domestic-content step-up to 70% in 2026, BUT softened by the 'impracticable-to-trace' carve-out that expires end-2026 and creates a Q1 2027 enforcement-teeth test; (3) the EU CRMA + Battery R

watching_authored.name: CSDDD enforcement scaling: from disclosure to audit-grade
watching_authored.thesis: EU Corporate Sustainability Due Diligence Directive (CSDDD) on a slowed-but-still-active trajectory toward audit-grade supply-chain enforcement, but with material softening signals. April 2025 Stop-the-Clock Directive pushed transposition to July 2027 / application 2028; December 2025 further delay pushed transposition to 26 July 2028 / uniform application 26 July 2029. Germany's BAFA suspended LkSG report review October 2025. Two-sided thesis: the file either (a) recovers in the delayed 2028-30 window with material first-wave penalties (incumbent compliance-moat reinforcement) OR (b) continues to soften via further delays, scope narrowing, or de facto enforcement holiday (compliance-moat thesis weakens substantially, sunk-cost on existing audit infrastructure does not earn its return).
watching_authored.system_claim: By 2030-12-31 at least one EU member-state competent authority issues a public CSDDD enforcement action with monetary penalty > 1% of global turnover or > EUR 50m against a Tier-1-traded materials/chemicals firm, AND at least one private-claim civil judgment under the CSDDD framework establishes Tier-N supplier responsibility precedent. (Horizon revised from 2028-06-30 to 2030-12-31 following December 2025 delay package.)

current_state.derivation_note: Computed by cycle_outside_in 2026-05-06 from 0 evidence rows.

watching_authored.trend_direction: DOWN_MODERATE_TO_FLAT — the directive is alive but the implementation/enforcement train has slowed materially relative to original calendar; net softening over Q1-Q4 2025
watching_authored.umicore_translation_hypothesis: Strongest single positive driver for B4 (Compliance Moat) durability among the seed Watching set. Umicore's existing audit-readiness, traceability investments, and integrated-supply-chain documentation become priceable advantages once the regime tightens. Operational impact: compliance-spend creep across all segments (B1, B2, B3, B5) but at lower magnitude than the moat-reinforcement upside; net positive. Downside scenario (transposition gutted) leaves the existing compliance investment without its competitive return.

watching_authored.name: China LFP technology export restrictions
watching_authored.thesis: China formalises export-control restrictions on LFP/LMFP cathode-active-material technology, process know-how, and/or precursor specifications by end-2027, materially raising the cost-and-time barrier for Western OEMs and cell-makers to build domestic LFP supply chains. Net effect across Umicore's bet stack is asymmetric and structurally tail-positive: it preserves and lengthens the duration of premium-NMC defensibility (B1), strengthens the ASP premium for compliant non-Chinese CAM (B4), and indirectly compresses the LFP-share ceiling in EU passenger BEV (B1.I2 upper-band).
watching_authored.system_claim: China's Ministry of Commerce (MOFCOM) and/or NDRC enacts at least PARTIAL export-licensing restrictions on LFP/LMFP cathode technology, process patents, or precursor know-how by 2027-12-31 — defined as either: (a) formal addition to China's Catalogue of Technologies Prohibited or Restricted from Export, OR (b) case-by-case licensing requirement applied to a named LFP technology transfer to a Western entity.

current_state.derivation_note: Initial prior set at 0.50 reflecting genuine two-sided uncertainty. To be updated by cycle_outside_in once evidence rows accumulate.

watching_authored.trend_direction: RISING — strategic salience is rising as Western LFP capacity build accelerates; China has tooling and historical pattern to enact; no formal action yet
watching_authored.umicore_translation_hypothesis: Cross-bet asymmetric tail-positive. (1) B1 BMS premium-NMC: STRENGTHENS — Western OEMs and cell-makers (LG ES, Samsung SDI, SK On — Umicore's customer base) cannot easily replicate Chinese LFP know-how, locking premium platforms into NMC for longer; +2 to +4pp on B1 confidence. (2) B1.I2 EU LFP ceiling: SUPPORTS — suppresses upper-band of the central path (Western-built LFP capacity grows slower); -3 to -8pp on EU LFP share by 2030 if enacted. (3) B4 compliant CAM ASP premium: STRENGTHENS — non-Chinese CAM compliance becomes more strategically valuable when Chinese chemistries have export-control friction; widens the compliant-premium spread. (4) B2 cobalt/recycling: NEUTRAL-TO-NEGATIVE — if LFP supply chains stay China-bound, Western recycling-feedstock mix stays NMC-heavier, which is structurally Umicore-positive on recycling economics; offsetting the headwind from LFP volume gains in mass-market EU/US fleets. (5) B3 critical minerals: NEUTRAL — LFP technology controls don't directly affect Li/Ni/Co flows, only know-how transfer.

watching_drivers.name: Non-EV battery end-market expansion (BESS / mobile robotics / aerial mobility / heavy machinery / portable + power tools / marine)
watching_drivers.thesis: Multiple non-EV battery-consuming industries are scaling materially through 2030 — stationary storage / BESS, mobile robotics (humanoid + industrial AGV/AMR + service robots), aerial mobility (drones, UAV, eVTOL), heavy machinery electrification (mining, construction, agriculture), portable electronics + power tools, and marine / inland shipping electrification. Collectively these reshape the cathode-demand mix away from EV-vehicle-dominance through 2030. The strategically load-bearing question for any European battery-materials provider is: per industry, which battery chemistry wins, at what scale, on what timeline, and through which OEM/integrator demand channel? Tracking these industry trajectories independent of any single company's positioning is what makes this an Outside-In driver.

system_claim.claim: By FY2028 the combined non-automotive cathode-demand pool (BESS + mobile robotics + aerial mobility + heavy machinery + portable/power-tools + marine) globally crosses 1,000 GWh/yr CAM-equivalent annual demand, with at least 4 of the 6 named end-markets each independently consuming > 30 GWh/yr CAM-equivalent. Per-industry chemistry-mix is differentiated (BESS: LFP-dominant; robotics/aerial: high-Ni-NMC/solid-state-dominant; marine: LFP-dominant; heavy machinery: LFP/NCMA-mixed; portable: high-Ni-NMC + LCO).
system_claim.rationale: 1,000 GWh/yr non-automotive cathode demand by FY2028 = the threshold at which non-automotive end-markets collectively rival automotive in cathode-volume terms (current automotive cathode demand is ~1,500-2,000 GWh-equivalent/yr by 2028 baseline). Below this threshold, non-EV markets remain a niche. Above it, the cathode-supply industry becomes a multi-end-market portfolio rather than an EV-only volume play. The chemistry-differentiation requirement is the strategic-relevance test: if all non-EV markets converge to LFP, the residual NMC-margin pool collapses; if they differentiate (e.g., high-Ni-NMC for robotics/aerial, LFP for stationary/marine, solid-state for premium platforms), incumbents with chemistry-portfolio capabilities retain pricing power.

current_observed_value.narrative: As of 2026-05-06: BESS market is the largest and fastest-growing non-EV cathode-demand pool (421 GWh shipments 2025, 600 GWh forecast 2026), with LFP consolidating 30%+ share outside China and rising. Mobile robotics (humanoid specifically) is in pre-commercial-ramp phase: Tesla Optimus targeting 50,000 units in 2026 at $20-30k each but Q4 2025 commentary acknowledged units are 'primarily for learning, not productive tasks'; Apptronik Apollo deployed at Mercedes-Benz; Agility Digit is the only revenue-generating humanoid (GXO + Toyota + Mercado Libre paying contracts). eVTOL: Joby uses NMC 811 + graphite per Tesla template; Archer 22% cost-reduction by 2026 via NASA collaboration; commercial passenger certification pursued in 2025-26. Marine: $776M 2025 → $893M 2026; Corvus Energy + BYD MoU Dec 2025; LFP-dominant. Heavy machinery: pre-ramp at scale. Portable + power tools: mature, modest growth. The high-level picture: BESS at scale today (LFP-dominant); robotics/humanoid + eVTOL pre-commercial-ramp (NMC-811-dominant); marine LFP-dominant; heavy machinery pre-ramp.
current_observed_value.non_ev_total_addressable_cathode_pool_FY: research_question — composite estimate from multiple sources puts the FY2028 non-automotive cathode pool at approximately 800-1,200 GWh CAM-equivalent depending on humanoid + eVTOL ramp pace; the 1,000 GWh threshold in system_claim is the central scenario

watching_drivers.umicore_translation_hypothesis: LIGHT COUPLING ONLY. Outside-In driver is industry-generic — tracking these flows is informative for any European battery-materials provider class. For Umicore specifically: (a) the 195 GWh installed CAM capacity by end-2026 is currently EV-only-positioned with explicit underutilisation flagged on Chinese assets; non-EV diversification is the obvious pressure-release valve but no Umicore IR commitment exists today. (b) Solid-state position via Blue Current investment is partially aligned with the W10 humanoid + eVTOL + premium-auto vector. (c) Compliance moat (B4) is a procurement differentiator for premium-OEM non-auto buyers (humanoid OEMs, eVTOL OEMs, premium robotics). (d) BESS-LFP locking is a structural negative — Umicore's NMC heritage doesn't address LFP-dominant stationary. The Inside-Out coupling — should Umicore restructure CAM capacity for non-auto markets — lives in BC-OI-3, not here.

watching_drivers.name: Sodium-ion stationary-storage commercialisation
watching_drivers.thesis: Sodium-ion (Na-ion) reaches commercial cost-and-cycle parity for stationary-storage applications by 2027-2028, capturing 5-15% of incremental EU+US grid-storage GWh that would otherwise have been LFP/Li-ion. Net effect on Western premium-NMC demand floor is indirect but cumulative: Na-ion absorbs the chemistry-agnostic part of demand, leaving Li-ion increasingly tied to mobility-only use cases.

system_claim.claim: EU+US stationary-storage installed-base addition rate reaches >= 8 GWh per year of sodium-ion chemistry by FY2028, sustained over two consecutive calendar years, with at least one investment-grade Western OEM or utility offtake contract for Na-ion CAM in place. (Note: Chinese deployment has already crossed this scale at the announcement level — CATL/HyperStrong 60 GWh deal April 2026 — so the structural question is now whether deployment migrates to Western markets, not whether the chemistry has reached commercial scale.)
system_claim.rationale: 8 GWh annual EU+US Na-ion BESS additions = ~5% of projected 2028 Western stationary-storage market. The April 2026 CATL-HyperStrong 60 GWh sodium-ion supply deal (Electrek 2026-04-27) materially compresses the Chinese-to-Western adoption gap; the chemistry threshold question has moved decisively from technology to geography of deployment. A Western utility/OEM Na-ion offtake of the same scale within 24 months is the next decisive escalation gate.

current_observed_value.narrative: As of April 2026, the Chinese sodium-ion industrial base has crossed announcement-scale commercialisation: CATL signed a 60 GWh sodium-ion supply deal with HyperStrong (April 27, 2026), the world's-largest sodium-ion battery order. HiNa Battery operates a Fuyang plant at 1 GWh first-phase capacity targeting 5 GWh, with multiple MW-scale BESS deployed in Shanxi, Guangxi, and Hubei. BYD has a third-generation sodium-ion platform exceeding 10,000 cycles. CATL announced large-scale 2026 deployment across multiple sectors. Western Na-ion deployment remains pre-commercial: Altris (Sweden), Tiamat (France) at pilot scale; no Western utility/OEM offtake of investment-grade scale yet announced. Thesis status: chemistry has crossed the technology threshold; the open question is whether/when Western deployment follows.

watching_drivers.name: Direct cathode regeneration / hydromet bypass
watching_drivers.thesis: Direct cathode regeneration (DCR) — recovering active cathode material from end-of-life cells without dissolving down to elemental metals via hydromet/pyromet — reaches commercial viability for premium-NMC chemistries by 2027-2029. If validated at scale, it bypasses integrated smelter-refiner economics for the EOL-battery feedstock pool, structurally challenging asset lives of integrated facilities like Hoboken-style operations.

system_claim.claim: At least one Western or Korean DCR operator achieves > 5,000 t/yr commercial throughput at premium-NMC quality (cells regenerated meet OEM-tier-1 acceptance) at positive operating margin, with a named OEM offtake contract, by 2028-12-31.
system_claim.rationale: 5,000 t/yr at premium quality = scale at which DCR transitions from validation to material competitor for premium-NMC EOL stream; OEM-tier-1 acceptance + named offtake = the demand-side gate (without it, DCR remains a research curiosity even if technically demonstrated)

current_observed_value.narrative: Direct cathode regeneration has crossed the early-commercial threshold faster than the seed thesis assumed. Princeton NuEnergy (PNE) operates a flagship facility in Chester, South Carolina — described as the US's first commercial-scale Advanced Black Mass and battery-grade CAM production facility (NCM and LFP). 2026 capacity expanding to 15,000 tpa, scalable to 50,000 tpa as market demand grows. Honda and Princeton NuEnergy signed an MOU for plasma-based DCR collaboration. Ascend Elements ships domestically-decarbonised cathode material via Hydro-to-Cathode pCAM synthesis; multi-year offtake with Trafigura for recycled lithium carbonate. Stated economic case: PNE direct recycling achieves 38% cost reduction and 69% lower environmental footprint vs conventional methods. Korean players (POSCO HY Clean Metal, EcoPro CnG) integrating DCR into existing recycling infrastructure. Implication: the technology is no longer pre-commercial; the open question is OEM-acceptance economics + tier-1 quality validation at scale.

watching_drivers.name: CBAM extension to chemicals, CAM, catalysts and refined PGMs
watching_drivers.thesis: EU Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism (CBAM) scope extension beyond steel/aluminium/cement/fertiliser/electricity/hydrogen into downstream chemicals, cathode active materials (CAM), catalysts and refined platinum-group metals lands in legislative form by 2027-2028. If enacted with embedded-emissions methodology favouring EU producers, CBAM neutralises a meaningful share of the EU industrial-cost stack disadvantage and reinforces compliance moats. If carved out or delayed, EU mid-stream materials remain exposed to Chinese cost-position arbitrage.

system_claim.claim: By 2027-12-31 the European Commission tables a binding CBAM scope-extension proposal that explicitly lists at least one of {cathode active materials, refined PGMs, downstream catalysts, base-chemicals intermediates} as in-scope, with a phase-in implementation date no later than 2030-01-01.
system_claim.rationale: CBAM Phase 1 went into definitive form October 2026 covering iron-steel/cement/aluminium/fertiliser/electricity/hydrogen. Article 30 review mandate requires the Commission to assess scope expansion; downstream products and embedded-emissions methodologies are the explicit review topics. The 2027 horizon is the legal deadline for the review report, not the legislation itself; a binding proposal naming specific materials within scope is the threshold that promotes this from speculative to active.

current_observed_value.narrative: CBAM Phase 1 entered the definitive period in 2026 covering iron/steel, cement, aluminium, fertilisers, electricity, and hydrogen. The European Commission's December 17, 2025 proposal (COM(2025) 989 final) extends scope to selected steel- and aluminium-intensive downstream products and adopts implementing legislation for the 2026 compliance phase. Article 30 review is in force; downstream products in cement, fertilisers, hydrogen are explicitly under review. Chemicals and CAM-specific scope expansion is NOT yet in the December 2025 proposal but is signalled for evaluation in a 2027 Commission report. Timeline matches the W3 horizon of 2027-12-31. Indirect emissions extension to existing CBAM goods (steel, aluminium, hydrogen) is also in the 2027 report scope.
current_observed_value.next_legislative_milestone: 2027 — Commission report evaluating CBAM scope further extension to chemicals + indirect emissions on existing goods

watching_drivers.name: CSDDD enforcement scaling: from disclosure to audit-grade
watching_drivers.thesis: EU Corporate Sustainability Due Diligence Directive (CSDDD) on a slowed-but-still-active trajectory toward audit-grade supply-chain enforcement, but with material softening signals. April 2025 Stop-the-Clock Directive pushed transposition to July 2027 / application 2028; December 2025 further delay pushed transposition to 26 July 2028 / uniform application 26 July 2029. Germany's BAFA suspended LkSG report review October 2025. Two-sided thesis: the file either (a) recovers in the delayed 2028-30 window with material first-wave penalties (incumbent compliance-moat reinforcement) OR (b) continues to soften via further delays, scope narrowing, or de facto enforcement holiday (compliance-moat thesis weakens substantially, sunk-cost on existing audit infrastructure does not earn its return).

system_claim.claim: By 2030-12-31 at least one EU member-state competent authority issues a public CSDDD enforcement action with monetary penalty > 1% of global turnover or > EUR 50m against a Tier-1-traded materials/chemicals firm, AND at least one private-claim civil judgment under the CSDDD framework establishes Tier-N supplier responsibility precedent. (Horizon revised from 2028-06-30 to 2030-12-31 following December 2025 delay package.)
system_claim.rationale: Original 2028-06-30 horizon assumed the first member-state transposition deadline of 2026-07-26. With Stop-the-Clock (April 2025) and the December 2025 second-delay package, transposition is now 26 July 2028 and uniform application is 26 July 2029. First material public penalty + first private-claim precedent are the twin gates that turn CSDDD from a paper exercise into an operating risk; the historical EU enforcement pattern (GDPR, MiFID II) is 18-24 months of guidance after application before enforcement scales. That places the realistic first-wave-penalty window at 2030-2031. Below this revised threshold, CSDDD remains a paper exercise relative to the original directive intent; above it, the regime hardens as architecturally designed.

current_observed_value.narrative: MATERIAL SOFTENING SIGNALS observed since seed authoring. April 2025 Stop-the-Clock Directive (EU 2025/794) pushed CSDDD transposition deadline from July 2026 to July 2027 and staged application from 2027 to 2028. December 2025 Parliament-Council-Commission tripartite further postponed transposition to 26 July 2028 (an additional year), with uniform application from 26 July 2029. Scope narrowed in delay package: CSDDD will apply ONLY to EU companies with > 5,000 employees AND > EUR 1.5 billion turnover (vs. earlier larger scope). Germany's BAFA suspended LkSG report review October 2025; only ongoing administrative proceedings continue, and only where violations are 'particularly serious due to extent, scope or irreversible nature'. Penalties under CSDDD remain in design at min 5% global net turnover. The compliance-moat-reinforcement thesis is on a delayed and partially-narrowed trajectory; the file is not dead but is materially weaker in 2026 than in seed-authoring assumptions.

watching_drivers.name: EU water-stress regulation tightening on metallurgy and process chemistry
watching_drivers.thesis: EU water-stress regulation moves from informational (River Basin Management Plans) into binding-quota and permit-conditioning regimes for water-intensive industrial processes by 2027-2029. Hydrometallurgy, electroplating, refining, and cathode-precursor process chemistry face quota tightening, longer permit-renewal cycles, and increased risk of conditional permits. Geography: Belgium-Netherlands cluster (Scheldt-Meuse basin) and Italian/Spanish/Southern-French clusters most exposed; Nordic and German Rhine basins less so.

system_claim.claim: By 2028-12-31 at least one EU member-state competent water authority denies, suspends, or imposes binding-volume conditions on a Tier-1 industrial water permit for a metallurgical or specialty-chemicals facility (capacity > 10,000 t/yr or > 5 GWh equivalent), explicitly citing water-stress-stress designation under the EU Water Framework Directive or successor instrument.
system_claim.rationale: First binding industrial-permit denial for a Tier-1 facility on water-stress grounds is the threshold event that turns water-stress regulation from environmental policy into industrial-siting risk. Below this threshold, regulation is permitting friction; above it, it is a structural constraint on EU process-chemistry expansion.

current_observed_value.narrative: EU Water Resilience Strategy launched May 2025 (CELEX:52025DC0280) — explicitly identifies battery production, semiconductors, hydrogen, microchips, and datacentres as strategic-autonomy sectors that consume large volumes of often ultra-pure water. The strategy is positioned to restore the water cycle and ensure water security; it is currently a strategic framework, not yet binding industrial-quota legislation, but the Actions Tracker is populated. In Flanders specifically, water quality is documented as a 'critical issue in permit procedures' — only 1 of 195 Flemish water bodies achieves 'good' status under WFD; courts have emphasised that permits should be denied where water quality risk exists. Belgian Single Permit Reform 2026 is in force and includes water-permit harmonisation. As of 2026-05-06, no Tier-1 industrial-permit denial citing water-stress has been documented for a major battery/specialty-chemicals facility — but the regulatory pressure trajectory is up.

watching_drivers.name: EU industrial workforce demographic cliff
watching_drivers.thesis: European chemicals, metallurgy, and process-engineering workforce hits a demographic step-change between 2026 and 2030: median age in Belgium/Germany chemicals workforce ~50; large retirement wave concentrated 2026-2032 against shallow replacement pipeline; compounding effect from US/Asia gigafactory and semiconductor-fab talent draw. Net result: wage inflation in heavy-process industry above general CPI sustained, capex-execution timelines extending, and capability-retention risk on knowledge-intensive operations.

system_claim.claim: By 2028-12-31 EU chemicals/metallurgy sector wage inflation exceeds general EU CPI by > 200 basis points sustained over four consecutive quarters, AND at least one Tier-1 EU materials/specialty-chemicals firm publicly attributes a capex-project delay (> 6 months) or a CGU-level operational issue to skills-shortage causes.
system_claim.rationale: Wage-inflation differential > 200 bps sustained = the threshold at which the cost stack changes structurally for EU process chemistry; combined with named-firm attribution of operational issues, the signal moves from generic labour-market commentary into operating-impact territory.

current_observed_value.narrative: Strong real-source confirmation of W6 thesis. ~30% of EU chemical-industry employees are 50 years of age or more, due to retire within the next decade. Younger generations increasingly seek careers in other fields. Student enrolment declining in engineering and business disciplines critical to chemicals. The chemical sector reduced jobs by approximately 15,000 in 2023 and 2024; 20,000 direct positions eliminated overall. Oxford Economics (Cefic 2025 Tipping Point report) projects employment losses could reach 40,000-50,000 positions across the European chemical sector if current trends continue. Wage data (1.6% annual FTE growth vs 2.4% inflation) shows real-wage erosion compounding the retention problem. EU Commission launched Skills Guarantee (November 2025) and Council adopted recommendation on human capital (March 2026); policy-side intervention is in motion but sub-scale relative to the gap.

watching_drivers.name: China LFP technology export restrictions
watching_drivers.thesis: China formalises export-control restrictions on LFP/LMFP cathode-active-material technology, process know-how, and/or precursor specifications by end-2027, materially raising the cost-and-time barrier for Western OEMs and cell-makers to build domestic LFP supply chains. Net effect across Umicore's bet stack is asymmetric and structurally tail-positive: it preserves and lengthens the duration of premium-NMC defensibility (B1), strengthens the ASP premium for compliant non-Chinese CAM (B4), and indirectly compresses the LFP-share ceiling in EU passenger BEV (B1.I2 upper-band).

system_claim.claim: China's Ministry of Commerce (MOFCOM) and/or NDRC enacts at least PARTIAL export-licensing restrictions on LFP/LMFP cathode technology, process patents, or precursor know-how by 2027-12-31 — defined as either: (a) formal addition to China's Catalogue of Technologies Prohibited or Restricted from Export, OR (b) case-by-case licensing requirement applied to a named LFP technology transfer to a Western entity.
system_claim.rationale: IEA Global EV Outlook 2025 flags this signal twice (p.141 and p.153) — the IEA does not flag speculative policy signals lightly, and the dual-mention indicates the report's authors view it as plausible enough to model. China's pattern of using technology and mineral export controls strategically (graphite Dec 2023, gallium and germanium Jul 2023, rare earths processing tech Dec 2023) creates a strong base-rate prior for LFP being added to the same toolkit, particularly given LFP's emergence as a Chinese strategic-industrial signature. Initial prior = 0.50 (genuinely two-sided uncertainty).
system_claim.source: IEA Global EV Outlook 2025 (p.141, p.153) + China MOFCOM Catalogue of Technologies Prohibited or Restricted from Export historical pattern + Reuters / Caixin China industrial-policy reporting

current_observed_value.narrative: As of 2026-05-06: China LFP technology export restrictions are at the PROPOSED stage, flagged twice by IEA in Global EV Outlook 2025 (p.141, p.153) but not yet formally enacted in the MOFCOM Catalogue of Technologies Prohibited or Restricted from Export. China's pattern of strategic export controls — graphite (Dec 2023), gallium/germanium (Jul 2023), rare-earths processing technology (Dec 2023) — establishes a strong base-rate prior. Initial prior probability of partial enactment by 2027-12-31 set at 0.50 to reflect genuine two-sided uncertainty: the IEA mention is meaningful but no public draft regulation, MOFCOM bulletin, or named official statement has yet surfaced.
current_observed_value.current_status: PROPOSED — flagged by IEA GEVO 2025 (p.141, p.153); no formal MOFCOM enactment as of 2026-05-06

falsification_thresholds.event: China MOFCOM formally adds LFP/LMFP cathode technology to the Catalogue of Technologies Prohibited or Restricted from Export
falsification_thresholds.rationale: This is the canonical formal-enactment signal — the system_claim's primary trigger. Direct read of MOFCOM bulletin is Tier 1 evidence.

falsification_thresholds.event: China blocks or imposes case-by-case licensing on a named LFP technology transfer to a Western entity (e.g. Stellantis-CATL Zaragoza expansion, Ford-CATL Marshall MI, any new Western LFP JV)
falsification_thresholds.rationale: Case-by-case licensing without formal catalogue addition is a softer but still-decisive enactment signal; satisfies the OR-clause of system_claim. A single Western LFP project blocked is materially trajectory-positive.

falsification_thresholds.event: China State Council or Politburo Standing Committee public statement framing LFP as a 'strategic battery technology' or equivalent language
falsification_thresholds.rationale: Strategic-language signal historically precedes formal MOFCOM catalogue updates by 6-18 months (graphite precedent: Dec 2022 framing → Dec 2023 formal control)

falsification_thresholds.event: EU Commission and/or US BIS issues a public response anticipating China LFP export controls (e.g. retaliatory tariff threat, CRMA acceleration, IRA review)
falsification_thresholds.rationale: Western counter-positioning is a signal that diplomatic intelligence has assessed enactment as likely; lagging confirmation but useful as a triangulating data point

falsification_thresholds.event: China publicly RESCINDS or formally drops any draft LFP export-control language and signs an LFP technology-transfer cooperation framework with EU or US OEM
falsification_thresholds.rationale: Direct disconfirmation. Would be the most informative falsification signal because it inverts the strategic posture entirely.

falsification_thresholds.event: Western LFP capacity outside China crosses 200 GWh annual nameplate before end-2027 without any China policy intervention
falsification_thresholds.rationale: If Western LFP scale is achieved inside the horizon without China acting, the policy threshold has either passed or was never going to be crossed; downgrades the driver to historical-context

falsification_thresholds.event: China imposes export controls on LMFP specifically (not LFP) — partial scope
falsification_thresholds.rationale: LMFP-only restriction is plausible early-stage variant since LMFP is the next-gen Chinese chemistry; would still satisfy the partial-enactment OR-clause but with narrower scope

watching_drivers.umicore_translation_hypothesis: Cross-bet asymmetric tail-positive. (1) B1 BMS premium-NMC: STRENGTHENS — Western OEMs and cell-makers (LG ES, Samsung SDI, SK On — Umicore's customer base) cannot easily replicate Chinese LFP know-how, locking premium platforms into NMC for longer; +2 to +4pp on B1 confidence. (2) B1.I2 EU LFP ceiling: SUPPORTS — suppresses upper-band of the central path (Western-built LFP capacity grows slower); -3 to -8pp on EU LFP share by 2030 if enacted. (3) B4 compliant CAM ASP premium: STRENGTHENS — non-Chinese CAM compliance becomes more strategically valuable when Chinese chemistries have export-control friction; widens the compliant-premium spread. (4) B2 cobalt/recycling: NEUTRAL-TO-NEGATIVE — if LFP supply chains stay China-bound, Western recycling-feedstock mix stays NMC-heavier, which is structurally Umicore-positive on recycling economics; offsetting the headwind from LFP volume gains in mass-market EU/US fleets. (5) B3 critical minerals: NEUTRAL — LFP technology controls don't directly affect Li/Ni/Co flows, only know-how transfer.

bet_candidates.name: Closed-loop PGM service contracts as a margin-bearing line of business (recycling-as-a-service)
bet_candidates.thesis_one_paragraph: Today Umicore's Recycling segment (B3 — Hoboken franchise) earns volume + margin-share on the PGM cycle, with cash conversion compressed by metal-price volatility (FY2025 working capital absorption EUR 261m). The bet candidate is to migrate part of that revenue base to a recurring service-revenue model: closed-loop PGM contracts where premium OEMs and tier-1 customers pay an audit-grade service fee for traceable take-back, refining, and re-supply of PGMs and battery-cathode metals at OEM-tier-1 quality. Recurring service revenue at scale would (a) compress working-capital seasonality, (b) lock in feedstock against trader competition (Glencore/Trafigura), (c) capture a compliance-moat premium that a CSDDD-tightening regime would amplify, (d) hedge against direct cathode regeneration (W2) by becoming the audit-grade traceability layer that regenerated material has to clear. Becomes a real bet — not a candidate — when (i) at least one investment-grade premium-OEM closed-loop offtake at audit-grade traceability is signed, AND (ii) the business is structured with internal management commitment (capital allocation envelope + dedicated P&L line), AND (iii) regulatory framework either holds (CSDDD scaling) or strengthens (CRMA strategic-projects status).

two_sided_outcomes.upside_case: Audit-grade compliance regime tightens (W4 reverses to UP); DCR pull-side demand materialises (W2 promotes); ≥3 investment-grade OEM contracts signed by FY2028. Net result: structurally higher Recycling EBIT margin durability, lower metal-cycle EBIT volatility, premium-multiple re-rating opportunity.
two_sided_outcomes.downside_case: CSDDD remains delayed/narrowed (W4 softening continues); DCR captures premium OEM offtake without traceability layer (Umicore loses both volume AND margin); commodity-cycle remains the dominant mode. Net result: option value of the bet candidate erodes; sunk cost on services-line investment if pursued opportunistically without trigger conditions met.
two_sided_outcomes.base_case: One investment-grade contract by FY2028; services-line is opportunistic add-on rather than transformative; bet stays at candidate-tier; revisit at FY2029 / 2030 cycles.

estimated_financial_impact_framework.valuation_framework_pointer: Layer 3 of Simulation Engine (when built) — peer-multiple-style framework: services-revenue businesses (vs commodity refiners) trade at 2-3x EBITDA multiple premium; the candidate's option value lies primarily in this re-rating optionality, not in incremental EBIT addition.

what_makes_this_a_candidate_not_a_bet_to: Umicore has not announced a structured 'PGM-as-a-service' product line in IR materials.
Capital-allocation signal absent: no segment-line P&L, no dedicated capex envelope.
Recycling segment today is volume-and-spread driven, not service-fee driven, in published reporting.
No public investment-grade OEM closed-loop offtake at audit-grade traceability terms is in place.

The Hoboken impairment-test exposure window (2028-2032 if W2 progresses) is the asymmetric risk that would drive defensive transition into this bet candidate; without that pressure the inertia of current model dominates.

bet_candidates.name: Battery Materials end-market diversification — strategic capacity reallocation beyond automotive
bet_candidates.thesis_one_paragraph: The radar's Outside-In W10 driver shows multiple non-EV battery-consuming industries scaling materially through 2030 (BESS, mobile robotics including humanoid, aerial mobility, heavy machinery, marine, portable + power tools — collectively a 1,000 GWh/yr+ CAM-equivalent demand pool by FY2028 in the central scenario). Umicore today is positioned as an EV-only CAM supplier (195 GWh installed capacity by end-2026, Battery Materials EBIT below breakeven 2025+2026, 18-month customer-ramp delays, Chinese assets explicitly underutilised per CEO Sap). The bet candidate is to convert part of that EV-positioned capacity into a structured non-automotive CAM business with named-OEM offtake, dedicated P&L line, and per-end-market chemistry portfolio (high-Ni-NMC for robotics + aerial mobility, solid-state via Blue Current for premium platforms, NMC-mix for portable/power tools, and possibly LFP-line tooling for stationary if the chemistry-mix inside Umicore's current architecture allows). Becomes a real bet — not a candidate — when (i) at least one investment-grade non-EV OEM/integrator offtake > 1 GWh/yr is signed, AND (ii) management commits a structured capital envelope with a dedicated reporting view in IR materials, AND (iii) board approves retooling a named CAM facility (Korean / Polish / Chinese / Belgian) toward non-automotive end-markets.

Working-capital profile vs EV-CAM (likely similar or slightly favourable due to less metal-cycle exposure)

two_sided_outcomes.upside_case: By FY2028: 25-30 GWh/yr non-auto offtake locked across 2-3 named OEMs (humanoid + eVTOL + heavy machinery); EUR 600-900m incremental revenue; ASP premium of 10-15% over EV-CAM benchmark; B1 segment-EBITA returns to positive 12 months earlier than current breakeven trajectory; equity re-rating on non-EV diversification narrative.
two_sided_outcomes.downside_case: BESS locks LFP-only and Umicore lacks LFP-line tooling; humanoid-OEM offtake captured by Asian competitors (LG Energy / SK On / EcoPro / POSCO HY) before Umicore's commercial-channel build-out; capex sunk on retooling without volume offtake; Battery Materials EBITA breakeven slips to FY2029 or later.
two_sided_outcomes.base_case: 5-10 GWh/yr non-auto offtake by FY2028 across 1-2 named OEMs (probably solid-state via Blue Current + 1 robotics OEM); incremental but not transformative; bet remains at candidate-tier; revisit at FY2028 / FY2029 cycles.

estimated_financial_impact_framework.valuation_framework_pointer: Layer 3 of Simulation Engine (when built) — peer-multiple-style framework: non-auto-diversified materials businesses (vs EV-only) trade at 1.5-2x EBITDA multiple premium; the candidate's option value lies in this re-rating optionality + the volatility-reduction effect of multi-end-market exposure.

what_makes_this_a_candidate_not_a_bet_to: Umicore's CMD 2025 + FY2025 results frame Battery Materials as an EV-recovery story, not an end-market-pivot story.
No public capital-allocation signal toward non-auto: no segment-line P&L, no dedicated capex envelope, no named non-auto OEM contract disclosed in IR materials.
Blue Current investment is solid-state-focused (covers automotive + non-auto premium platforms); not equivalent to a structured non-auto CAM business unit.

Existing CAM line architecture is high-Ni-NMC-optimised — retooling to LFP for stationary is non-trivial capex; NMC-line for non-auto (robotics, aerial) is technically straightforward but requires customer-channel build-out.
Inertia of EV customer base: existing offtake commitments to ACC, PowerCo, Ionway, SK On, LG Energy presumably constrain how much capacity can be visibly redirected without renegotiation.

coverage_gaps.scope_basis: STEEPLE-pillar coverage assessed against the union of Active drivers (T1-T6) and seeded Watching drivers (W1-W6). Gaps = STEEPLE pillars or sub-axes where neither posture has a tracked driver record.

Reconsider for v2 seed if any of the remaining_subgaps escalates via news flow.
